title: Implement refund and reversal confirmation
description:
## Objective
Create one attributable full-refund relationship for an eligible original transaction and reject partial, over-refund, unmatched, duplicate-role, and incompatible attempts without changing existing state.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-REFUND-CONFIRMATION: Confirm refunds and reversals
- DD-LEDGER-FULL-AMOUNT-REFUND-RELATIONSHIP: Single full-amount refund relationships
- DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY: Immutable facts and append-only history

## Depends On
- bd-xq6: closed; provides RelationshipStore and active-role model
- bd-2ne: closed; provides LedgerMutationExecutor
- bd-84s: closed; provides TransactionStore

## In Scope
- Publish ledger.refund.confirm through the typed CLI contract.
- Require active same-account ZAR transactions with original amount below zero, refund amount above zero, and exact absolute minor-unit equality.
- Store one immutable refund relationship with refund_original and refund_credit roles.
- Reject partial, over-refund, missing, incompatible, inactive, and active-role duplicate attempts atomically.
- Preserve idempotent request and logical-effect replay.
- Verify source immutability and relationship detail/history.

## Out of Scope
- Partial or cumulative refunds.
- Relationship revoke/replace and actuals projection, which remain downstream tasks.
- Any migration or weakening of financial_relationship_roles_are_exclusive_before_insert.
- Inference from descriptions, dates, category, pool, instrument, cardholder, or evidence.

## Interfaces
- Produces: RefundOperationModule and RefundPolicy
- Consumes: RelationshipStore, TransactionStore, LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync

## Files
- src/Tally/Contracts/Ledger/Relationships/RefundContracts.cs (create)
- src/Tally/Domain/Ledger/Relationships/RefundPolicy.cs (create)
- src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Relationships/RefundHandlers.cs (create)
- src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Relationships/RefundOperationModule.cs (create)
- src/Tally/Contracts/Common/ProcessContracts.cs (modify)
- src/Tally/Bootstrap/LedgerServices.cs (modify)
- src/Tally/Cli/OperationRegistry.cs (modify)
- src/Tally/Cli/TallyProcess.cs (modify)
- tests/Tally.Tests/Features/Ledger/Relationships/RefundTests.cs (test)

## Verification
- focused RefundTests
- dotnet build
- dotnet test

## Commit
- feat(ledger): implement full-amount refund confirmation
- Refs: bd-10w

## Context
- lex context bd-10w --json
design: Implement a refund-specific typed input, pure RefundPolicy, transactional confirmation handler, and operation module following the existing transfer vertical slice. RefundPolicy returns stable validation codes for input, lifecycle, account, currency, sign, and exact full-amount eligibility. The handler uses the existing RelationshipStore active-role check and V001 exclusivity trigger unchanged, stores the full positive minor-unit amount, and delegates replay to LedgerMutationExecutor.
acceptance_criteria:
- Exact same-account ZAR negative-original and positive-refund amounts create one active full-amount relationship.
- Smaller partial and larger over-refund credits return LEDGER-REFUND-AMOUNT with no relationship or idempotency record.
- Missing, inactive, different-account, different-currency, invalid-sign, invalid-input, and active-role reuse cases return stable errors without mutation.
- A second active refund for an original returns LEDGER-RELATIONSHIP-ACTIVE-ROLE-CONFLICT; the existing relationship remains the only active one.
- Identical request and logical-effect replay return the original relationship; changed input under one key returns LEDGER-IDEMPOTENCY-001.
- Detail exposes exact roles, amount, currency, active state, actor, time, and history; both source transactions remain unchanged.
- The existing V001 relationship-role exclusivity trigger and RelationshipStore guard are unchanged.
- Focused RefundTests run at least 16 cases with zero failures; full build and test gates pass.
notes: Resolved product correction: Ledger supports one exact full refund only. Existing role-exclusivity trigger and RelationshipStore semantics were verified correct and remain unchanged. Graph design/plan/task contracts were corrected and documentation review converged. Implementation commit e14542ef305f05602500037a8ca61e60362b0ac4 passes RefundTests 27/27, full suite 547/547, build with 0 warnings/errors, format verification, linux-x64 Native AOT publish, and typed published-schema inspection. Self-review PASS for spec conformance and code quality; durable report: docs/reviews/review-work-ledger-refunds-2026-07-22.md; Lex verification record 01KY4734YKSPS2EZCBZX3SM342.
close_reason: Implemented the reviewed full-amount-only refund contract with durable passing verification evidence.

title: Validate local financial-data security
description:
## Objective
Prove every supported local artifact and public process surface protects financial/evidence data, remains provider-neutral, and fails closed without custom cryptography.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Security validation exercises the complete published process and all artifact paths.
- bd-3sx (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-PROCESS-CONTRACT): Consumer requires TallyProcess.RunAsync from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.
- bd-23m (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-STORAGE): Consumer requires IHostArtifactProtection from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.
- bd-fup (TASK-LEDGER-RESTORE-ACTIVATE): Consumer requires AuthoritativeStoreActivator from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.

## In Scope
- Security validation task intentionally has no Implements link: consolidate owner-only permissions, host protection, input/schema privacy, logging, diagnostics, backup, optional-guidance path, and malicious-value checks.
- Produce: ArtifactPermissionsTests
- Produce: DiagnosticCanaryTests
- Produce: HostProtectionEvidenceTests
- Produce: scripts/verify-ledger-security.sh

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT mark ext4 alone as encryption-at-rest evidence, add application users/auth, log DTOs, or implement custom cryptography.
- Do NOT use sanitized unit-only substitutes for real Linux permission/path/process checks.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- Supported-host tests assert 0700 data roots and 0600 DB/WAL/SHM/candidate/manifest/backup/optional-guidance artifacts after create, crash, restore, and evolution paths.
- Host-protection check rejects absent/invalid evidence before production conformance; no custom crypto, key derivation, or encryption algorithm exists.
- Canary descriptions, raw evidence/provider payloads, credentials, full account/payment identifiers, serialized bodies, SQL, unsafe paths, exception text, and stack traces never appear in logs, stderr, expected errors, or diagnostics.
- Malformed/oversized/unknown JSON, mailbox/MIME/message/recipient/schedule/delivery/acknowledgement fields, open evidence metadata, unsafe artifact/guidance paths, symlinks, traversal, permissions, full disk, locks, and corrupt stores fail before partial mutation.
- Published process runs offline under the owner OS identity, opens no listener, and contains no direct Hermes, AgentMail, WhatsApp, or other provider integration.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT mark ext4 alone as encryption-at-rest evidence, add application users/auth, log DTOs, or implement custom cryptography.
- ❌ Do NOT use sanitized unit-only substitutes for real Linux permission/path/process checks.

## Approach
- Security validation task intentionally has no Implements link: consolidate owner-only permissions, host protection, input/schema privacy, logging, diagnostics, backup, optional-guidance path, and malicious-value checks.
- Expected output: ArtifactPermissionsTests
- Expected output: DiagnosticCanaryTests
- Expected output: HostProtectionEvidenceTests
- Expected output: scripts/verify-ledger-security.sh

## Interfaces
- Consumes: IHostArtifactProtection (DD-LEDGER-EMBEDDED-STORAGE)
- Consumes: TallyProcess.RunAsync (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR)
- Consumes: AuthoritativeStoreActivator (DD-LEDGER-CANDIDATE-ACTIVATION)
- Produces: VerifiedLocalSecurityGate (NFR-LEDGER-LOCAL-DATA-PROTECTION)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Security/ArtifactPermissionsTests.cs` (test) — implementation — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Security/DiagnosticCanaryTests.cs` (test) — implementation — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Security/HostProtectionEvidenceTests.cs` (test) — implementation — required
- `scripts/verify-ledger-security.sh` (create) — security gate — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `bash scripts/verify-ledger-security.sh` — exit 0; at least 24 real-host/process security cases run, 0 fail, every artifact mode is compliant, no canary is found, and host-protection evidence is reported

## Commit
- `test(ledger): validate local financial-data security` — one commit per bead; footer `Refs: bd-112`

## Lex references
- DD-LEDGER-EMBEDDED-STORAGE
- EXT-LEDGER-HOST-OS-SECURITY
- NFR-LEDGER-ATOMIC-DURABLE-MUTATIONS
- NFR-LEDGER-LOCAL-DATA-PROTECTION
- PLAN-LEDGER-V1
- SP-LEDGER-05-VERIFICATION
- TASK-LEDGER-CORE-PROCESS-CONTRACT
- TASK-LEDGER-CORE-STORAGE
- TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT
- TASK-LEDGER-GATE-SECURITY
- TASK-LEDGER-RESTORE-ACTIVATE
- TC-LEDGER-LOCAL-DATA-PROTECTION
- Deep context: `lex context bd-112 --json`

title: Verify failure handling and cleanup workflow
description:
## Objective
Prove an owner can recover from or safely terminate every failed ingestion without payload disclosure, source damage, or Ledger deletion.

## Implements
- No direct FR ownership: this is an evidence, integration, security, use-case, or completion gate.
- Proves or constrains NFR-INGEST-LOCAL-DATA-PROTECTION: Protect local statement and ingestion data (satisfies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-ARTIFACT-CLEANUP-CONTRACT: Verify abandon and artifact cleanup (verifies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-FAILURE-STATUS-CONTRACT: Verify safe failure and status reporting (verifies).
- Proves or constrains UC-INGEST-005: Handle and clean up a failed ingestion (covers).

## Depends On
- bd-2jn (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): UC verification exercises published status/abandon/cleanup operations.
- bd-114 (TASK-INGEST-GATE-SECURITY): The workflow consumes the consolidated security canary and permission evidence.

## In Scope
- Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-005 diagnostics, durable status, retry/resume/abandon decisions, and state-checked cleanup.
- Produce: UCIngest005Evidence.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Every failure returns stable code/category, safe IDs, mutation possibility, last durable state, and retry/resume/abandon/correct-source guidance with one stdout result and metadata-only stderr.
- When payload would be needed to explain an error, the result uses safe correlation/record IDs instead and canary data is absent from every output/log.
- Permission-establishment failure persists no sensitive artifact and performs no Ledger mutation.
- Status proves pre-commit failures have no committable manifest/effect and commit-stage failures preserve exact completed outcomes plus unresolved frontier.
- A simulated crash leaves only known durable/recoverable artifacts; next safe startup removes disposable residue and retains required state.
- Cleanup rejects ambiguous/locked/recoverable targets; abandon prevents commit/resume but preserves prior effects; terminal cleanup removes only selected INGEST artifacts and leaves source/Ledger unchanged.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT inspect/delete files directly from the test except through isolated setup/observation helpers; invoke public abandon/cleanup for behavior.
- ❌ Do NOT emit private payload when a case fails.

## Approach
Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-005 diagnostics, durable status, retry/resume/abandon decisions, and state-checked cleanup.
- Constraint: Filesystem observations are restricted to isolated test data roots and source hashes; cleanup never targets the repository fixture directory.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CompleteIngestPublicContract (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: IngestSecurityGateEvidence (NFR-INGEST-LOCAL-DATA-PROTECTION)
- Produces: UCIngest005Evidence (UC-INGEST-005)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/E2E/FailureCleanupWorkflowTests.cs` (test) — UC-INGEST-005 black-box gate — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~FailureCleanupWorkflowTests` -> exit 0; at least 14 diagnostic, permission, pre/post-mutation status, crash residue, retained-for-recovery, abandon, terminal cleanup, source, and Ledger-preservation cases are discovered and 0 tests fail

## Commit
- `test(ingest): verify failure cleanup` — footer `Refs: bd-113`

## Lex references
- NFR-INGEST-LOCAL-DATA-PROTECTION
- PLAN-INGEST-V1
- SP-INGEST-04-VERIFICATION
- TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT
- TASK-INGEST-GATE-SECURITY
- TASK-INGEST-VERIFY-UC-005
- TC-INGEST-ARTIFACT-CLEANUP-CONTRACT
- TC-INGEST-FAILURE-STATUS-CONTRACT
- UC-INGEST-005
- Deep context: `lex context bd-113 --json`

title: Validate INGEST local financial-data security
description:
## Objective
Prove that hostile/malformed input and every success/failure/crash path disclose no source payload and leave only authorized protected state.

## Implements
- No direct FR ownership: this is an evidence, integration, security, use-case, or completion gate.
- Proves or constrains DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY: Memory-only extraction and owner-only payload handling (governed-by).
- Proves or constrains DD-INGEST-DOCUMENT-EXTRACTION: Managed in-process PDF extraction with PdfPig (governed-by).
- Proves or constrains DD-INGEST-STATE-STORE: Separate raw-SQLite ingestion state with retention compaction (governed-by).
- Proves or constrains NFR-INGEST-BOUNDED-PARSING: Bound passive statement processing (satisfies).
- Proves or constrains NFR-INGEST-LOCAL-DATA-PROTECTION: Protect local statement and ingestion data (satisfies).
- Proves or constrains RISK-INGEST-004: Raw statements or extracted temporary data can leak through permissions, logs, crash artifacts, or indefinite retention. (references).
- Proves or constrains RISK-INGEST-007: A malformed or adversarial document can exhaust local CPU, memory, disk, decompression, or parser time, or attempt active-content and external-resource execution. (references).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-ARTIFACT-PROTECTION: Verify source and artifact privacy boundaries (verifies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION-AOT: Verify PdfPig Native-AOT extraction conformance (verifies).

## Depends On
- bd-2jn (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Security is validated against the complete published eight-operation surface.
- bd-1j2 (TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION): Consumes PrivateStatementFixtureSet.
- bd-3l1 (TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION): Consumes IngestArtifactProtection.EnsureOwnerOnly.

## In Scope
- Security gate intentionally has no Implements link: it consolidates personal financial payload, passive parser, permissions, source preservation, diagnostics, cleanup, and resource-bound obligations across the published module.
- Produce: IngestSecurityGateEvidence.
- Produce: Published security boundary tests.
- Produce: Malformed/resource canary tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Published preview receives sourcePath only through private stdin/file JSON; process command line, environment diagnostics, stdout errors, stderr, logs, crash output, and telemetry contain none of the fixture path, canary, row, description, amount, balance, full identifier, request, manifest, or receipt payload.
- INGEST directories are 0700 and database/WAL/SHM/lock/atomic files are 0600 where supported; permission-establishment failure returns stable permission error before sensitive persistence or Ledger mutation.
- Before/after SHA-256 proves every private source unchanged across preview success, malformed/unsupported/resource failure, approval, commit, resume, abandonment, cleanup, cancellation, and injected crash.
- Malformed, scan-only, password-required, ambiguous, active-content/external-reference, decompression, nesting, byte, page, record, memory, and wall-time cases fail closed before mutation and start no network listener, child process, renderer, OCR, or remote fetch.
- Normal extraction creates no temporary; crash/startup cleanup removes only known unlocked disposable INGEST artifacts and leaves no orphan extraction temporary.
- Architecture guards reject HTTP/FastEndpoints/Aspire/PostgreSQL/EF, reflection/plugin loading, private Ledger storage/handler references, payload logging, and source argument options.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT weaken canary assertions, redact after logging, or treat process isolation as sufficient — prevent payload entry at each boundary.
- ❌ Do NOT skip owner-only mode checks on supported hosts or continue after failure.
- ❌ Do NOT persist test fixture paths/content or emit them when a security assertion fails.

## Approach
Security gate intentionally has no Implements link: it consolidates personal financial payload, passive parser, permissions, source preservation, diagnostics, cleanup, and resource-bound obligations across the published module.
- Constraint: Security failures themselves must use metadata-only stable identifiers and never echo the triggering payload.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CompleteIngestPublicContract (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Published INGEST surface
- Consumes: IngestArtifactProtection.EnsureOwnerOnly (DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY) — Permission enforcement
- Consumes: PrivateStatementFixtureSet (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT) — External private fixture injection
- Consumes: PublishedIngestTallyFixture (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Published process boundary
- Produces: IngestSecurityGateEvidence (NFR-INGEST-LOCAL-DATA-PROTECTION) — Consolidated security proof

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Security/IngestSecurityBoundaryTests.cs` (test) — permissions, source, temp, architecture gate — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Security/IngestResourceCanaryTests.cs` (test) — malformed/bounds/payload canaries — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Process/PublishedIngestSecurityTests.cs` (test) — process arguments/streams/network/orphan gate — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~IngestSecurityBoundaryTests|FullyQualifiedName~IngestResourceCanaryTests|FullyQualifiedName~PublishedIngestSecurityTests'` -> exit 0; at least 28 permission, canary, source-hash, malformed/resource, temporary, offline, process, stream, architecture, and crash cases are discovered and 0 tests fail without echoing canary payloads

title: review-documentation: clarify FR-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CORRECTION ac
description:
Module: LEDGER
Entity: FR-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CORRECTION
Field: ac
Fingerprint: c38ae912516a17cd6232ebabc5d97c6514dad6bf09cbac4c0a47951f9dda18e3

Reviewed text:
Given a voided or superseded transaction, when history-inclusive detail is requested, then original facts, evidence and decision history, assignments, actor context, reason, time, retired relationships, and replacement reference where applicable remain queryable.

Why author input is needed:
The weak phrase does not define its own measurable boundary; confirm the intended scope before changing the graph.

Proposed clarification:
Given a voided or superseded transaction, when history-inclusive detail is requested, then original facts, evidence and decision history, assignments, actor context, reason, time, and retired relationships remain queryable, and replacement reference is returned for supersession or explicit null for void.
close_reason: Clarified the reviewed LEDGER acceptance contract through audited Lex graph updates and verified the exact persisted wording.

title: review-documentation: clarify replay path identity scope
description:
Module: INSIGHTS
Entity: TASK-INSIGHTS-IDEMPOTENCY-EXECUTOR
Field: acceptance_checks[0]

Quoted text:
The unique replay identity is operationId plus contractVersion plus keyDigest; the canonical request fingerprint includes every logical input, actor, reason, target or predecessor, selected policies, expected-current fingerprint, and safe normalized path identity where applicable.

Why judgment is required:
The criterion does not name which retained operations require path identity. Confirm whether only Backup and Restore are path-bearing before changing the contract.

Suggested direction:
The unique replay identity is operationId plus contractVersion plus keyDigest; the canonical request fingerprint includes every logical input, actor, reason, target or predecessor, selected policies, and expected-current fingerprint; Backup and Restore additionally include safe normalized path identity.

Review fingerprint: 416f737a7bcead114b58537aacde2e878e5e505dbe74e05fc4567652ca17456d
close_reason: Approved recommendation applied: normalized path identity is limited to Backup and Restore.

title: Implement the V001 relationship and actuals schema fragment
description:
## Objective
Create exact transfer/refund relationship, relationship-lifecycle, and expiry-bound query-snapshot tables supporting category, pool, instrument, cardholder, evidence, and reconciliation filters without implementing financial rules.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On
- bd-23m (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-STORAGE): Consumes LedgerDb and LedgerSchemaFragmentRegistry.

## In Scope
- Schema foundation intentionally has no Implements link: translate financial relationship/lifecycle and ephemeral multi-dimensional actuals snapshots into one independently verified V001 fragment.
- Produce: V001RelationshipActualsSchema
- Produce: LedgerRelationshipActualsSchemaTests real-SQLite constraint tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT infer transfer/refund semantics in SQL, include snapshots in backup inventory, or create catalogue, transaction-fact, recovery, or idempotency tables in this fragment.
- Do NOT use SQLite REAL totals, live offset pagination, EF Core, or mocked SQLite tests.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-195 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-RECONCILIATION-AUTHORITY)
  - bd-2zt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE)

## Success Criteria
- V001RelationshipActualsSchema creates every linked relationship, role, lifecycle, snapshot, item, canonical filter, version, expiry, exact named-total, dimension bucket, and ordering field with required foreign keys and checks.
- Active relationship roles are unique per transaction, source and target differ, lifecycle replacement links are RESTRICT, and refund projections can follow the original current category and pool.
- Snapshot items preserve exact pool/category cells plus explicit uncategorized, unassigned, unknown-attribution, and Reconciliation State membership while using snapshotId plus ordinal.
- Query snapshots are explicitly ephemeral and excluded from Durable Ledger State; applying the fragment twice or injecting a statement failure leaves no partial tables/indexes.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT infer transfer/refund semantics in SQL, include snapshots in backup inventory, or create catalogue, transaction-fact, recovery, or idempotency tables in this fragment.
- ❌ Do NOT use SQLite REAL totals, live offset pagination, EF Core, or mocked SQLite tests.

## Approach

title: Add the V002 statement-authority schema upgrade
description:
## Objective
Represent statement corrections, prior and active transaction identity, dimension carry-forward, relationship replacement, and historical evidence support without rewriting V001.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On
- bd-35j (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION): V002 upgrades the committed V001 evidence and reconciliation tables.
- bd-2i5 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-CATALOGUE): Correction carry-forward references category, payment, and pool catalogue identities.
- bd-2qj (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-TRANSACTIONS): Statement correction references prior, replacement, and supersession transaction records.
- bd-193 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-RELATIONSHIPS-ACTUALS): Statement correction optionally references invariant-preserving relationship and assignment events.
- bd-23m (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-STORAGE): V002StatementAuthoritySchema consumes LedgerSchemaFragmentRegistry and cannot build before the storage foundation.

## In Scope
- Additive schema follow-up for the already-completed V001 evidence fragment; preserve V001 and upgrade both existing and fresh stores to the revised statement-authoritative model.
- Produce: V002StatementAuthoritySchema
- Produce: LedgerStatementAuthoritySchemaTests upgrade and rollback evidence

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT edit or replace V001EvidenceReconciliationSchema.cs — completed migration history is extended through V002.
- Do NOT overwrite the notification-derived fact, move evidence in place, or allow a statement row to confirm two active transactions — rejected by DD-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT.
- Do NOT add raw statement, email, MIME, provider cursor, recipient, or delivery columns — per ADR-CORE-0030 and C18.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-2zt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE)
  - bd-3pa (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-STATEMENT-CORRECTION-EFFECT-WRITER)

## Success Criteria
- V002 migrates a valid V001 store without data loss and expands Reconciliation Decisions to distinguish confirmed existing, corrected from statement, statement-only, ambiguity, exception, owner confirmation, rejection, revocation, and replacement.
- A statement-correction record references prior and active transaction IDs, supersession, category and pool carry-forward events, payment carry-forward or explicit unknown initialization, optional invariant-preserving relationship replacement events, authority basis, actor, reason, and predecessor decision.
- Evidence-link history supports retained historical agent evidence and one active confirming statement target; all new state is append-only, RESTRICT-linked, and excluded from arbitrary metadata or raw payloads.
- Real-SQLite tests prove V001-to-V002 upgrade, fresh composition, invalid reference rejection, immutable history, atomic rollback, and idempotent re-open with observable row and migration-metadata counts.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT edit or replace V001EvidenceReconciliationSchema.cs — completed migration history is extended through V002.
- ❌ Do NOT overwrite the notification-derived fact, move evidence in place, or allow a statement row to confirm two active transactions — rejected by DD-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT.
- ❌ Do NOT add raw statement, email, MIME, provider cursor, recipient, or delivery columns — per ADR-CORE-0030 and C18.

## Approach
- Additive schema follow-up for the already-completed V001 evidence fragment; preserve V001 and upgrade both existing and fresh stores to the revised statement-authoritative model.
- Constraint: The migration is additive from committed V001 and composes under LedgerSchemaFragmentRegistry in one transaction.
- Expected output: V002StatementAuthoritySchema
- Expected output: LedgerStatementAuthoritySchemaTests upgrade and rollback evidence

## Interfaces
- Produces: V002StatementAuthoritySchema (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY) — Additive statement-authority schema fragment
- Consumes: V001EvidenceReconciliationSchema (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECORD-LINK) — Committed predecessor schema
- Consumes: LedgerSchemaFragmentRegistry (DD-LEDGER-EMBEDDED-STORAGE) — Transactional version ordering

- `tests/Tally.Tests/Infrastructure/Storage/LedgerStatementAuthoritySchemaTests.cs` (test) — real SQLite upgrade verification — required — Cover V001 upgrade, fresh store, constraints, rollback, and reopen

title: Read retained report details and explanations
description:
## Objective
GetInsightReportQuery and ExplainInsightReportQuery return only the selected immutable stored evidence without recomputing current producers or mutating history.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-EXPLANATION: Explain report evidence deterministically
  - AC: Given a supported exact metric, when its explanation is inspected, then exact value, window bounds, category or total identity, source evidence references, and arithmetic rule are returned.
  - AC: Given a supported Linear Pace state, when its explanation is inspected, then formula version, elapsed and total days, exact plan and actual inputs, exact comparison result, and uniform-distribution limitation are returned.
  - AC: Given an unavailable, insufficient, not-started, or deferred state, when its explanation is inspected, then the typed reason, evaluated evidence, unmet rule, and permitted next action are returned without an invented result.
  - AC: Given a retained Report Snapshot identity, when Get or Explain is requested, then the exact immutable stored explanation and provenance are returned rather than recomputing current evidence.
  - AC: Given an unknown retained identity, when Get or Explain is requested, then Not Found is returned without leaking other report metadata.
  - AC: Given a deleted retained identity with a Deletion Event, when Get or Explain is requested, then Deleted is returned with metadata-only deletion evidence and no prior report payload.
  - AC: Given explanatory text, when it is compared across identical structured states and policy versions, then it is semantically identical, template-based, channel-neutral, and contains no persuasion, recommendation, translation, or delivery wording.
- FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-HISTORY: Inspect retained report history and status
  - AC: Given a known retained identity, when Get is requested, then the exact immutable Report Snapshot and its complete provenance are returned.
  - AC: Given an unknown retained identity, when Get is requested, then Not Found is returned without other report metadata.
  - AC: Given a deleted retained identity, when Get is requested, then Deleted and metadata-only Deletion Event evidence are returned without report content.
  - AC: Given a bounded List request, when it succeeds, then stable report identity, predecessor identity, Budget Period, trusted creation time, actor, policy versions, content fingerprint, and deletion state are returned without financial payload.
  - AC: Given retained history, when List ordering is inspected, then it is trusted creation time descending followed by stable report identity descending.
  - AC: Given a List request over the released count or output limit, when it is validated, then resource failure is returned rather than silent truncation.
  - AC: Given Status, when it is requested, then store version, integrity state, retained count, deleted count, latest successful backup/verification evidence, and supported policy versions are returned without amounts, merchant text, report bodies, or private paths.
  - AC: Given Get, List, or Status success or failure, when retained history is inspected afterwards, then no Report Snapshot, Deletion Event, or idempotency outcome changed.

## Depends On
- bd-7kn (TASK-INSIGHTS-STATE-FOUNDATION): Both queries use verified metadata and payload read primitives.

## In Scope
- Implement payload-verifying Get and Explain queries for one stable retained identity with explicit NotFound and Deleted outcomes.
- Produce: GetInsightReportQuery
- Produce: ExplainInsightReportQuery
- Produce: Detail read contract tests

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- Get for a known identity verifies report schema, payload fingerprint, and content fingerprint and returns the exact immutable ReportSnapshot plus complete stored provenance.
- Explain for a known identity returns only the selected exact stored explanation and provenance and never calls BUDGET, LEDGER, TimeProvider, or InsightReportCompiler.
- Unknown identity returns NotFound without other report metadata; deleted identity returns metadata-only Deleted evidence with no former payload or explanation.
- Every success and expected failure uses a short read connection and changes no snapshot, deletion event, idempotency outcome, recovery receipt, or status state.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT recompute current evidence, return list metadata for unknown IDs, expand raw transactions, or deserialize unrelated report payloads.
- ❌ Do NOT add a generic repository, HTTP detail endpoint, mutation, cache, or hidden repair on integrity failure — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE and DD-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE.
- ❌ Do NOT return deleted content from a backup or replay reference.

## Approach
Implement payload-verifying Get and Explain queries for one stable retained identity with explicit NotFound and Deleted outcomes.
- Constraint: Queries are read-only and forward CancellationToken.
- Expected output: GetInsightReportQuery
- Expected output: ExplainInsightReportQuery
- Expected output: Detail read contract tests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: InsightsStateStore (DM-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE) — verified selected payload and deletion evidence
- Produces: GetInsightReportQuery.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — insights.report.get handler
- Produces: ExplainInsightReportQuery.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — insights.report.explain handler

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/History/Get/GetInsightReportQuery.cs` (create) — one immutable snapshot read
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/Reports/Explain/ExplainInsightReportQuery.cs` (create) — selected stored explanation read
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/History/InsightReportDetailReadTests.cs` (test) — known, unknown, deleted, corrupt, and no-recompute cases

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~InsightReportDetailReadTests` — exit 0; at least 16 known, unknown, deleted, schema-corrupt, payload-corrupt, selected-explanation, no-recompute, no-leak, no-write, and cancellation cases are discovered across both handlers and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(insights): read retained report details and explanations` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-1a7`

- DD-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE

title: Implement the qualified layout A adapter
description:
## Objective
Produce deterministic layout A statement evidence and normalized source records without a generic template or fallback parser.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-FINANCIAL-NORMALIZATION: Normalize exact source financial facts
  - AC: Given a transaction-bearing source record with complete evidence, when it is normalized, then the candidate preserves the selected account, original description, exact base-10 amount, ZAR, transaction date, optional distinct posting date, adapter version, source fingerprint, source-context record identity, and source reference without precision loss.
  - AC: Given an asset or liability statement movement, when its signed amount is normalized, then positive means the owner economic position improved and negative means it worsened: asset increases and liability reductions are positive; asset decreases and liability increases are negative.
  - AC: Given a source transaction date that includes its year, when it is normalized, then that exact calendar date is the Effective Date.
  - AC: Given a source row with zero monetary movement, when it is accounted for, then it is retained as an explicitly excluded non-transaction record with a stable reason and no zero-amount LEDGER candidate is created.
  - AC: Given a missing or ambiguous amount, sign, currency, date, description, record boundary, account meaning, or provenance field, when normalization is attempted, then the source record is blocked without inventing or editing a value.
  - AC: Given a row date without a year in the qualified yearless layout, when exactly one matching calendar date falls inside the explicit statement period, then that date is the Effective Date; otherwise the batch is blocked.
- FR-INGEST-SOURCE-RECONCILIATION: Reconcile every supported statement before approval
  - AC: Given a qualified statement, when preview completes, then every source record has exactly one accepted-candidate, exact-duplicate, excluded-non-transaction, or blocked outcome with a stable reason.
  - AC: Given any supported initial fixture, when its normalized non-zero movements are summed with its opening economic balance, then the result equals its closing economic balance exactly in minor units.
  - AC: Given a supported variant that supplies running balances, when records are normalized, then every consecutive running-balance transition reconciles exactly to its signed movement.
  - AC: Given a source control that the detected variant exposes, when preview completes, then the manifest records and verifies that control exactly.
  - AC: Given an unexplained transaction-bearing record, amount mismatch, running-balance mismatch, opening/closing mismatch, or ambiguous exclusion, when reconciliation runs, then the manifest remains unapproved and uncommittable.
  - AC: Given a control type that the source does not expose, when preview completes, then the manifest reports that control as unavailable rather than fabricating a value.
- FR-INGEST-VARIANT-QUALIFICATION: Qualify and detect supported statement variants
  - AC: Given any of the three qualified private fixtures, when variant detection runs, then exactly one of the two versioned text-native PDF variants is selected from structural evidence rather than filename or extension.
  - AC: Given the qualified permission-encrypted fixture that is text-extractable without an opening password, when it is previewed twice, then each run selects exactly the fixture manifest variantId, reproduces every expected ordered record and reconciliation control, requires no opening password, OCR, external process, or external fetch, completes within 5 seconds and 256 MiB peak resident memory, and creates no LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a scan-only PDF, opening-password requirement, unsupported tabular export, active-content dependency, external fetch, malformed structure, ambiguous variant, or changed layout, when detection runs, then INGEST returns a stable unsupported or unsafe-source result and creates no committable manifest or LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a source exceeding any advertised adapter bound for size, pages, records, nesting, decompression, memory, or processing time, when preview is attempted, then processing stops with a stable resource-limit result before mutation.
  - AC: Given a proposed new or revised adapter, when it lacks representative fixtures that account for every source record and reconcile every required financial control deterministically, then the variant cannot be advertised as supported.

## Depends On
- bd-1j2 (TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION): The adapter implements IStatementAdapter over PdfDocumentEvidence and uses the private fixture helper.

## In Scope
- Encode only the structural detection, row boundaries, financial semantics, exclusions, and source controls proven by the private layout A fixtures.
- Produce: PdfTextLayoutAStatementAdapter.
- Produce: LayoutAStatementAdapterTests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Descriptor advertises exactly pdf-text-layout-a-v1, its adapter/extraction/manifest versions, media type, and hard limits.
- Probe selects exact_match only from layout A structural evidence and returns no_match for every layout B and unsupported fixture; filename and extension never contribute.
- Extract accounts for every layout A source record in source order, preserves raw evidence privately, distinguishes transaction and non-transaction records, and exposes all variant controls.
- Layout A date, account-class, owner-economic sign, zero-movement exclusion, amount, description, source-reference, and statement-period rules reproduce every private expected fact exactly in integer minor units.
- Two extraction runs over every assigned fixture produce identical ordered evidence and stable source-context positions; malformed or drifted structures fail closed.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT add conditional branches for layout B or future banks — rejected single-parser alternative per DD-INGEST-FORMAT-ADAPTERS.
- ❌ Do NOT use filename, extension, fuzzy matching, user-authored regex/template configuration, or fallback guesses.
- ❌ Do NOT commit private fixtures, expected facts, filenames, extracted text, or payload snapshots.

## Approach
Encode only the structural detection, row boundaries, financial semantics, exclusions, and source controls proven by the private layout A fixtures.
- Constraint: All private expected facts come through PrivateStatementFixtureSet and remain outside source control.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: IStatementAdapter (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Consumes: PdfDocumentEvidence (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Consumes: PrivateStatementFixtureSet (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT)
- Produces: PdfTextLayoutAStatementAdapter (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Pdf/PdfTextLayoutAStatementAdapter.cs` (create) — layout A probe and extraction — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Adapters/LayoutAStatementAdapterTests.cs` (test) — private golden-fixture tests — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~LayoutAStatementAdapterTests` -> exit 0; at least 10 probe, drift, row-accounting, financial-fact, control, and repeatability cases are discovered for every layout A fixture and 0 tests fail

## Commit

- TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION

title: Implement exact money, identifiers, and dates
description:
## Objective
Provide deterministic value types that reject lossy inputs and round-trip accepted money/date values exactly.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-ACTUALS-QUERY: Query exact Ledger actuals
  - AC: Given explicit filters for account, inclusive date range, category, Spend Pool, Payment Instrument, cardholder, Evidence Kind, Reconciliation State, transfer state, refund state, categorization state, Pool Assignment state, and transaction lifecycle state, when actuals are queried, then only transactions matching every supplied filter are returned.
  - AC: Given no lifecycle-state override, when actuals are queried, then each active economic event contributes through exactly one current Canonical Transaction; voided or superseded facts and retired relationships or assignments do not affect default totals.
  - AC: Given any selected transaction set, when Net Account Movement is returned, then it equals the exact base-10 sum of each selected active transaction's Signed Account Amount, with inflows positive and outflows negative.
  - AC: Given a selected transaction set, when External Spend is returned, then it adds positive magnitudes of qualifying active outflows, subtracts active linked refund or reversal magnitudes in their own Effective Date periods, excludes active owned-transfer principal and unrelated inflows, includes separately recorded fees, and never silently chooses a cash-withdrawal policy while OQ-LEDGER-16 is unresolved.
  - AC: Given category grouping with scope exact or subtree, when Budget Actual is returned, then each qualifying active transaction contributes once to its direct category or uncategorized bucket and, for subtree scope, once to each requested ancestor roll-up; pool and pool-by-category totals reconcile exactly without hierarchy double counting.
  - AC: Given an active linked refund or reversal, when grouped actuals are queried, then it offsets the original transaction's current pool and category, including explicit unassigned buckets, without inheriting account, instrument, or cardholder as a pool rule.
  - AC: Given no matching transactions, when actuals are queried, then the Ledger returns a successful empty collection and zero exact totals rather than a not-found error.
  - AC: Given unchanged Ledger state and identical filters, when the query is repeated, then membership, ordering, reconciliation and attribution state, and exact aggregates are identical.
  - AC: Given an approved statement-authoritative correction changes account, amount, or Effective Date, when current actuals are queried, then the superseded notification-derived fact contributes zero and the active statement-derived replacement contributes exactly once under its explicitly carried-forward or corrected dimensions.
- FR-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-RECORDING: Record Canonical Transactions
  - AC: Given an active account, valid original description, exact base-10 signed amount, ZAR currency, transaction date, privacy-safe initial evidence, and Idempotency Identity, when recording succeeds, then one stable Canonical Transaction and one linked Evidence Record are created atomically without precision loss.
  - AC: Given a transaction with a distinct supplied posting date, when it is recorded, then both transaction date and posting date round-trip without substituting one for the other.
  - AC: Given any missing required fact, unsupported currency, zero amount, malformed date, archived account, unsupported Evidence Kind, or forbidden provider payload, when recording is attempted, then a stable validation error is returned and neither transaction nor evidence is created.
  - AC: Given a recorded transaction, when it is read later, then its account identity, original description, signed amount, currency, supplied dates, stable identifier, evidence links, current Reconciliation State, and explicit unknown attribution states round-trip without silently inferring category, pool, instrument, or cardholder.
  - AC: Given a movement on an active owned asset or liability account, when its signed amount is recorded, then a positive value means the owner's economic position improved and a negative value means it worsened: asset increases and liability reductions are positive; asset decreases and liability increases are negative.
  - AC: Given the same logical initial evidence and semantically identical Canonical Transaction facts are submitted through another request identity, when recording is attempted, then the original transaction and evidence result is returned and no duplicate effect is created.
  - AC: Given the same logical initial evidence is submitted with materially different Canonical Transaction facts, when recording is attempted, then a stable conflict is returned and the existing transaction and evidence remain unchanged.
  - AC: Given a Canonical Transaction created through the ordinary recording operation, when its initial state is returned, then its Reconciliation State is recorded-but-unreconciled until a valid deterministic or owner-approved Reconciliation Decision changes it.

## Depends On
- bd-3sx (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-PROCESS-CONTRACT): Value types compile against common validation/result contracts.

## In Scope
- Implement validated ZAR minor units, signed balance delta, Effective Date, exact totals, and ULID primitives.
- Produce: Money, LedgerCurrency, Effective Date, LedgerId, ExactTotals
- Produce: Boundary/property tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT use REAL/double/float/JSON numeric money, decimal-text persistence, or a speculative currency table — rejected per DD-LEDGER-FINANCIAL-REPRESENTATION.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3hy (TASK-LEDGER-ACTUALS-PROJECTION)
  - bd-2zt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE)
  - bd-84s (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTIONS-RECORD-GET)

## Success Criteria
- Canonical -123.45 parses once to Int64 minor units and formats identically without floating point.
- JSON numbers, exponents, negative zero, zero transaction amounts, excessive scale, unsupported currency, and overflow return stable field errors.
- Signed amount follows the resolved owner economic-position delta rule across asset and liability accounts.
- TransactionDate and optional PostingDate round-trip as yyyy-MM-dd; Effective Date is TransactionDate and no timezone is fabricated.
- Identifiers are 26-character ULIDs and total accumulation detects overflow.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT use REAL/double/float/JSON numeric money, decimal-text persistence, or a speculative currency table — rejected per DD-LEDGER-FINANCIAL-REPRESENTATION.

## Approach
- Implement validated ZAR minor units, signed balance delta, Effective Date, exact totals, and ULID primitives.
- Expected output: Money, LedgerCurrency, Effective Date, LedgerId, ExactTotals
- Expected output: Boundary/property tests

title: Generate one ephemeral Insight Report
description:
## Objective
GenerateInsightReportQuery returns one bounded complete report from one frozen instant and one coherent producer result while changing no durable state.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-GENERATION: Generate one deterministic Insight Report
  - AC: Given a valid generation request and coherent evidence, when generation succeeds, then exactly one ephemeral Insight Report is returned and no durable INSIGHTS state changes.
  - AC: Given omitted optional policy selectors, when generation succeeds, then the released default window, formula, Evidence Sufficiency, explanation, and ordering versions are explicit in the result.
  - AC: Given one report, when its content is inspected, then report identity, Budget Period, trusted As-of Instant, exact windows, plan state, exact metrics, every requested signal state, limitations, and Source Provenance are present.
  - AC: Given identical trusted As-of Instant, normalized request values, coherent source identities, and policy versions, when generation is repeated, then semantically identical ordered content and the same content fingerprint are returned.
  - AC: Given categories, metrics, states, and explanations, when report ordering is inspected, then it follows the released deterministic ordering policy and never depends on localized display text.
  - AC: Given one report, when Source Provenance is inspected, then all producer contract versions, authoritative state or snapshot identities, plan revision, category contract, report schema, policy versions, and content fingerprints are present.
  - AC: Given a requested horizon, category count, metric count, or output size over a released limit, when validation occurs, then resource failure is returned before producer reads or report output.
  - AC: Given a successful report, when its claims are inspected, then it describes recorded Tally evidence and does not claim complete bank activity, financial advice, enforcement, or historical source reconstruction.
- FR-INSIGHTS-STRUCTURED-INVOCATION: Invoke Public INSIGHTS Operations non-interactively
  - AC: Given valid structured input through stdin or an approved owner-only file, when a public operation succeeds, then exactly one versioned structured business-result envelope is written to stdout and the process exits with the documented success category.
  - AC: Given an expected validation, Not Found, Deleted, conflict, unavailable, insufficient-evidence, compatibility, integrity, resource, or downstream failure, when invocation completes, then exactly one module-scoped machine-readable error or state envelope is returned with the documented exit category.
  - AC: Given malformed input or a schema-forbidden unknown field, when invocation is attempted, then validation fails before any financial read or retained-state write.
  - AC: Given an unsupported INSIGHTS, BUDGET, or LEDGER contract version, when invocation is attempted, then compatibility fails before financial processing.
  - AC: Given Retain, Restate, Delete, Backup, or Restore without required P1 actor attribution or idempotency identity, when invocation is attempted, then authority validation fails before retained-state change.
  - AC: Given any operation, when it runs, then it requires no interactive prompt, network call, daemon, watcher, scheduler, service, or background Tally process.
  - AC: Given success or expected failure, when stderr, logs, telemetry, crash output, process arguments, and shared temporary locations are inspected, then they contain metadata only and no serialized financial request, report, or response body.

## Depends On
- bd-uh9 (TASK-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): The query implements a published request/result and stable error contract.
- bd-388 (TASK-INSIGHTS-BUDGET-EVIDENCE-CLIENT): Generation consumes the single public BUDGET client.
- bd-3ac (TASK-INSIGHTS-REPORT-COMPILER): Generation delegates all analytical semantics to the compiler.

## In Scope
- Implement the read-only Generate vertical slice from validated structured request through one BUDGET call and deterministic compilation.
- Produce: GenerateInsightReportQuery
- Produce: Ephemeral generation contract tests

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- Generate validates unknown fields, versions, requested policies, rolling windows, category/metric/signal counts, horizon, and output bounds before TimeProvider or producer access.
- A valid request freezes TimeProvider.GetUtcNow exactly once before calling BudgetContractClient.GetInsightEvidenceAsync exactly once, binds the result, and compiles one complete InsightReport.
- Omitted selectors resolve to explicit released default window, formula, sufficiency, explanation, and ordering versions in the result.
- Success writes no ReportSnapshot, idempotency record, recovery metadata, file, or store state; expected failure returns one typed envelope and no partial report.
- CancellationToken is forwarded through every async call and logs contain only operation identity, correlation reference, duration, safe fingerprints, states, and counts.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT combine actions behind a generic Execute or expose Generate over HTTP — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-CLI-OPERATION-CONTRACT.
- ❌ Do NOT embed calculations in the query, call producers more than once, query LEDGER directly, accept a caller clock, or write durable state.
- ❌ Do NOT log, diagnose, cache, or persist the structured financial request, producer result, report body, private path, or merchant text.

## Approach
Implement the read-only Generate vertical slice from validated structured request through one BUDGET call and deterministic compilation.
- Constraint: Use the concrete BudgetContractClient and InsightReportCompiler; do not add interfaces around either.
- Expected output: GenerateInsightReportQuery
- Expected output: Ephemeral generation contract tests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: BudgetContractClient.GetInsightEvidenceAsync (DM-BUDGET-INSIGHTS-READ-CONTRACT) — one producer result
- Consumes: InsightReportCompiler.Compile (DM-INSIGHTS-INSIGHT-REPORT) — pure compilation
- Produces: GenerateInsightReportQuery.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — insights.report.generate handler

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/Reports/Generate/GenerateInsightReportQuery.cs` (create) — ephemeral generation handler
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Reports/GenerateInsightReportQueryTests.cs` (test) — validation, time, one-call, report, and no-write contract

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~GenerateInsightReportQueryTests` — exit 0; at least 18 validation-before-read, single-clock, single-producer-call, defaults, deterministic result, cancellation, stable-failure, no-store-write, no-network, and metadata-only-log cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(insights): generate ephemeral insight report` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-1cr`

## Lex references

- DD-INSIGHTS-CLI-OPERATION-CONTRACT

- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-01-ANALYTICAL-CORE
- TASK-INSIGHTS-GENERATE-REPORT
- TC-INSIGHTS-REPORT-GENERATION-CONTRACT

title: Implement atomic snapshot actuals pagination
description:
## Objective
Keep later pages bound to one category-hierarchy and ledger-generation view despite concurrent writes or reparenting.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-ACTUALS-QUERY: Query exact Ledger actuals
  - AC: Given explicit filters for account, inclusive date range, category, Spend Pool, Payment Instrument, cardholder, Evidence Kind, Reconciliation State, transfer state, refund state, categorization state, Pool Assignment state, and transaction lifecycle state, when actuals are queried, then only transactions matching every supplied filter are returned.
  - AC: Given no lifecycle-state override, when actuals are queried, then each active economic event contributes through exactly one current Canonical Transaction; voided or superseded facts and retired relationships or assignments do not affect default totals.
  - AC: Given any selected transaction set, when Net Account Movement is returned, then it equals the exact base-10 sum of each selected active transaction's Signed Account Amount, with inflows positive and outflows negative.
  - AC: Given a selected transaction set, when External Spend is returned, then it adds positive magnitudes of qualifying active outflows, subtracts active linked refund or reversal magnitudes in their own Effective Date periods, excludes active owned-transfer principal and unrelated inflows, includes separately recorded fees, and never silently chooses a cash-withdrawal policy while OQ-LEDGER-16 is unresolved.
  - AC: Given category grouping with scope exact or subtree, when Budget Actual is returned, then each qualifying active transaction contributes once to its direct category or uncategorized bucket and, for subtree scope, once to each requested ancestor roll-up; pool and pool-by-category totals reconcile exactly without hierarchy double counting.
  - AC: Given an active linked refund or reversal, when grouped actuals are queried, then it offsets the original transaction's current pool and category, including explicit unassigned buckets, without inheriting account, instrument, or cardholder as a pool rule.
  - AC: Given no matching transactions, when actuals are queried, then the Ledger returns a successful empty collection and zero exact totals rather than a not-found error.
  - AC: Given unchanged Ledger state and identical filters, when the query is repeated, then membership, ordering, reconciliation and attribution state, and exact aggregates are identical.
  - AC: Given an approved statement-authoritative correction changes account, amount, or Effective Date, when current actuals are queried, then the superseded notification-derived fact contributes zero and the active statement-derived replacement contributes exactly once under its explicitly carried-forward or corrected dimensions.
- FR-LEDGER-SNAPSHOT-PAGINATION: Preserve query snapshots across pages
  - AC: Given a filtered actuals query requiring multiple pages, when the first page is requested, then the result includes a snapshot-bound opaque cursor, deterministic ordering, evidence and Reconciliation State, current category, pool, instrument and Cardholder Attribution, and totals for the full selected set.
  - AC: Given a valid cursor, when a later page is requested after unrelated Ledger mutations, then membership, state, attribution, and pool, category, and pool-by-category totals remain those of the first-page snapshot.
  - AC: Given a cursor with altered filters, invalid encoding, unsupported version, or expired snapshot, when a page is requested, then a stable cursor error is returned and no partial page or live-state fallback is exposed.
  - AC: Given all pages from one snapshot, when their stable transaction identifiers are combined, then each selected Canonical Transaction appears exactly once and page aggregates reconcile to the first-page full-set totals.

## Depends On
- bd-3hy (TASK-LEDGER-ACTUALS-PROJECTION): Snapshot handler materializes the exact projection and totals.

## In Scope
- Freeze active facts, current category hierarchy, dimensions, evidence, reconciliation, relationships, membership, and exact totals for deterministic pagination.
- Produce: Actuals public contracts/handler/module
- Produce: QuerySnapshotStore
- Produce: Atomic concurrency/crash/cursor tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT recompute ancestry or totals on later pages, hold a transaction across invocations, or silently restart against current hierarchy — per DD-LEDGER-SNAPSHOT-ACTUALS.
- Do NOT include snapshots in backup, restore, or migration durable-state counts.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3ja (TASK-LEDGER-DURABLE-STATE-VERIFIER)
  - bd-3sd (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-RELATIONSHIP-ACTUALS-BUNDLE)

## Success Criteria
- First page materializes the complete ordered item set, exact full-set totals/groups, generation fingerprint, categoryHierarchyFingerprint, and each item's direct category plus frozen ancestry IDs under one BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction.
- Later pages read only the stored snapshot; transaction writes, assignment corrections, statement corrections, or category reparenting cannot change membership, ancestry, ordering, or totals.
- Expired, missing, filter-mismatched, contract-mismatched, generation-mismatched, or hierarchy-mismatched cursors fail explicitly without switching to live state.
- Snapshot cleanup is opportunistic and ephemeral; backup and Durable Ledger State exclude snapshots.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT recompute ancestry or totals on later pages, hold a transaction across invocations, or silently restart against current hierarchy — per DD-LEDGER-SNAPSHOT-ACTUALS.
- ❌ Do NOT include snapshots in backup, restore, or migration durable-state counts.

## Approach
- Freeze active facts, current category hierarchy, dimensions, evidence, reconciliation, relationships, membership, and exact totals for deterministic pagination.
- Expected output: Actuals public contracts/handler/module
- Expected output: QuerySnapshotStore
- Expected output: Atomic concurrency/crash/cursor tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: ActualsOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-RELATIONSHIP-ACTUALS-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: ActualsQueryHandler (DM-LEDGER-RELATIONSHIP-ACTUALS-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: QuerySnapshotStore (DM-LEDGER-QUERY-SNAPSHOT)
- Consumes: ActualsCalculator.Calculate (DM-LEDGER-RELATIONSHIP-ACTUALS-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: ActualsProjectionStore

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ledger/Actuals/ActualsContracts.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Actuals/ActualsQueryHandler.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Storage/Actuals/QuerySnapshotStore.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Actuals/ActualsOperationModule.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Features/Ledger/Actuals/ActualsSnapshotTests.cs` (test) — implementation — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Process/ActualsCursorProcessTests.cs` (test) — implementation — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~ActualsSnapshotTests|FullyQualifiedName~ActualsCursorProcessTests' --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 16 concurrency/crash/stability/cursor cases run and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(ledger): implement atomic snapshot actuals pagination` — one commit per bead; footer `Refs: bd-1dt`

## Lex references
- DD-LEDGER-CATEGORY-HIERARCHY
- DD-LEDGER-SNAPSHOT-ACTUALS

- DM-LEDGER-QUERY-SNAPSHOT

- NFR-LEDGER-AGENT-CONTRACT-STABILITY
- PLAN-LEDGER-V1
- SP-LEDGER-03-RELATIONSHIPS-ACTUALS
- TASK-LEDGER-ACTUALS-PROJECTION
- TASK-LEDGER-ACTUALS-SNAPSHOT

title: Compose the reconciliation operation bundle
description:
## Objective
Integration gate with no Implements link: compose evidence-aware candidate, base apply, statement correction, decision, and coverage modules.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On
- bd-1zm (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-PROJECTION): Bundle consumes ReconciliationProjectionOperationModule.
- bd-37y (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-APPLY): Bundle consumes ReconciliationApplyOperationModule.
- bd-3q4 (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-DECISIONS): Bundle consumes ReconciliationDecisionOperationModule.
- bd-2ae (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-COVERAGE): Bundle consumes ReconciliationCoverageOperationModule.
- bd-3ts (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-STATEMENT-CORRECTION): The bundle consumes the extended reconciliation.apply correction disposition.
- bd-1ui (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-AUTOMATIC-ACTIVATION): The complete reconciliation bundle proves automatic cases only after the evidence-backed policy is activated.

## In Scope
- Integration gate with no Implements link: compose evidence-aware candidate, base apply, statement correction, decision, and coverage modules.
- Produce: ReconciliationOperationBundle and composed integration tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT add reflection discovery, generic reconciliation gateway, overloaded reconcile commands, INGEST batch behavior, or raw payloads — rejected by the governing decisions.
- Do NOT call one public handler from another; compose through named stores/policies and the statement coordinator.
- Do NOT weaken conflict, ambiguity, authority, or review-required outcomes to make integration tests green.

## Success Criteria
- The bundle exposes exactly nine evidence-reconciliation descriptors once with concrete source-generated request/result/error types and explicit DI; schema inventory contains correct_existing_from_statement and no aliases or open payloads.
- A real-store sequence registers statement evidence, projects exact and differing candidates, confirms an exact fact, applies one authoritative correction with explicit carry-forward, creates a statement-only fact, preserves ambiguity, corrects an owner decision, completes coverage, and returns exact history without direct store access.
- Compatible transfer/refund replacement remains valid; incompatible relationship correction returns review-required with unchanged financial state; crash/retry and provider-neutral scans report nonzero cases and no duplicate effect.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT add reflection discovery, generic reconciliation gateway, overloaded reconcile commands, INGEST batch behavior, or raw payloads — rejected by the governing decisions.
- ❌ Do NOT call one public handler from another; compose through named stores/policies and the statement coordinator.
- ❌ Do NOT weaken conflict, ambiguity, authority, or review-required outcomes to make integration tests green.

## Approach
- Integration gate with no Implements link: compose evidence-aware candidate, base apply, statement correction, decision, and coverage modules.
- Expected output: ReconciliationOperationBundle and composed integration tests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: ReconciliationProjectionOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACTS) — Projection and candidate descriptors
- Consumes: ReconciliationApplyOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACTS) — Base reconciliation dispositions
- Consumes: StatementCorrectionOperationExtension (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACTS) — Statement-authoritative correction disposition
- Consumes: ReconciliationDecisionOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACTS) — Owner decision descriptors
- Consumes: ReconciliationCoverageOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACTS) — Coverage and exception descriptors
- Produces: ReconciliationOperationBundle (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACTS) — Exactly nine provider-neutral reconciliation descriptors

## Files (reservation globs)

- `tests/Tally.Tests/Integration/ReconciliationOperationBundleTests.cs` (test) — composed seam verification — required

## Verification

- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.Integration.ReconciliationOperationBundleTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 20 composed reconciliation, correction, coverage, crash, and schema cases run and 0 fail

title: Validate built-in policies with private owner evidence
description:
## Objective
Changed built-in policies can enter the released catalogue only after complete owner-only cases, canaries, aggregate outcomes, and owner acceptance pass without private disclosure.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-POLICY-VALIDATION: Validate analytical policies against private evidence
  - AC: Given a candidate policy set and owner-only evaluation manifest, when validation starts, then candidate versions, expected-state fingerprint, corpus fingerprint, producer contract versions, window policy, and evaluation-tool version are recorded.
  - AC: Given a completed validation run, when its result is returned, then every manifest case is accounted for as supported, unavailable, insufficient, correct signal, incorrect signal, correct severity, false severity, or disclosure-canary outcome.
  - AC: Given sparse, zero, recurring, front-loaded, irregular, refund-heavy, plan-change, category-lifecycle, classification-gap, and correction cases, when required corpus coverage is checked, then every required shape is present or validation fails before policy activation.
  - AC: Given any false high-severity Attention Item, when candidate activation is evaluated, then the Attention Item policy is rejected.
  - AC: Given a candidate Forecast, Trend, or Anomaly without a deterministic formula, explicit comparable baseline, minimum evidence rule, limitations, and named unavailable state, when activation is evaluated, then that signal policy is rejected.
  - AC: Given a candidate report shape that the owner does not accept as improving a named decision over raw Budget Position inspection, when the plan gate is evaluated, then the do-less kill criterion is reported as triggered.
  - AC: Given an unavailable private manifest, when existing released policies are used, then ordinary report generation may continue with their unchanged versions.
  - AC: Given an unavailable private manifest, when a new or changed analytical policy is proposed for release, then activation is blocked.
  - AC: Given a validation result, when it is persisted or exported, then only fingerprints, structural case identifiers, aggregate outcomes, and canary status are present; filenames, financial values, merchant text, report payloads, and expected rows are absent.
  - AC: Given the public INSIGHTS capability set, when it is enumerated, then no owner-facing policy create, edit, activate, retire, scripting, or plugin operation is present; candidate validation is a release evidence gate for built-in policies.

## Depends On
- bd-uh9 (TASK-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): The harness validates released catalogue and receipt contracts.
- bd-3ac (TASK-INSIGHTS-REPORT-COMPILER): Private cases exercise the real candidate compiler rather than a second evaluator.
- bd-21u (TASK-INSIGHTS-LIFECYCLE-CONTRACT-MODELS): The private harness emits the external aggregate PolicyValidationReceipt.

## In Scope
- Implement the internal manifest-driven validation harness and privacy-safe external receipt for active built-in policies and candidate report shape.
- Produce: InsightPolicyValidationHarness
- Produce: PrivateEvaluationManifestReader
- Produce: PolicyValidationReceiptWriter
- Produce: Privacy and coverage tests

## Out of Scope
- The durable receipt is release/test evidence outside active product state; private cases remain ignored and owner-controlled.

## Success Criteria
- InsightPolicyValidationHarness accepts the private manifest only from TALLY_INSIGHTS_PRIVATE_EVALUATION_MANIFEST, requires an owner-only regular file, and reads cases in memory without printing paths or payloads.
- Validation records candidate policy, report schema, producer contract, window, corpus, expected-state, and tool fingerprints and accounts for every structural case as supported, unavailable, insufficient, correct/incorrect signal, correct/false severity, or disclosure-canary outcome.
- Sparse, zero, recurring, front-loaded, irregular, refund-heavy, plan-change, category-lifecycle, classification-gap, correction, false-severity, and disclosure-canary shapes are required before success.
- The external PolicyValidationReceipt contains only approved structural IDs, fingerprints, aggregate outcomes, false-high-severity count, canary status, tool version, and owner acceptance; it is never inserted into insights.db.
- Missing evidence blocks changed policy release but does not disable unchanged released policies; deferred policies remain non-executable.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT add a public validation, policy CRUD, expression, script, plugin, activation, or retirement operation — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-POLICY-VALIDATION.
- ❌ Do NOT commit anonymized private examples or rely on synthetic tests alone — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-POLICY-VALIDATION.
- ❌ Do NOT persist manifest paths, filenames, financial values, merchant text, report payloads, expected rows, corpus rows, or PolicyValidationReceipt in the active store.
- ❌ Do NOT release a changed policy when any case, required shape, disclosure canary, owner acceptance, or zero-false-high-severity gate is absent or failing.

## Approach
Implement the internal manifest-driven validation harness and privacy-safe external receipt for active built-in policies and candidate report shape.
- Constraint: Ordinary report generation must not require the private corpus for unchanged released policy versions.
- Expected output: InsightPolicyValidationHarness
- Expected output: PrivateEvaluationManifestReader
- Expected output: PolicyValidationReceiptWriter
- Expected output: Privacy and coverage tests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: InsightReportCompiler.Compile (DM-INSIGHTS-INSIGHT-REPORT) — real candidate compiler
- Consumes: PolicyValidationReceipt (DM-INSIGHTS-POLICY-VALIDATION-RECEIPT) — external aggregate release evidence
- Produces: InsightPolicyValidationHarness.ValidateAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-POLICY-VALIDATION-RECEIPT) — complete private case evaluation
- Produces: PolicyValidationReceiptWriter.WriteAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-POLICY-VALIDATION-RECEIPT) — owner-only aggregate receipt writer

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/PolicyValidation/PrivateEvaluationManifestReader.cs` (create) — owner-only manifest boundary
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/PolicyValidation/InsightPolicyValidationHarness.cs` (create) — complete case and canary evaluator
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/PolicyValidation/PolicyValidationReceiptWriter.cs` (create) — aggregate external receipt writer
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/PolicyValidation/InsightPolicyValidationHarnessTests.cs` (test) — shape, false-severity, privacy, and release tests

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~InsightPolicyValidationHarnessTests` — exit 0; at least 24 permission, manifest, required-shape, complete-accounting, false-high-severity, deferred-policy, changed-policy, unchanged-policy, canary, receipt-field, no-store, no-log, and no-public-operation cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(insights): validate built-in policies with private owner evidence` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-1g7`

## Lex references
- DD-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-POLICY-VALIDATION
- DM-INSIGHTS-POLICY-CATALOGUE
- DM-INSIGHTS-POLICY-VALIDATION-RECEIPT
- EXT-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-EVALUATION-CORPUS
- FR-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-POLICY-VALIDATION
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-01-ANALYTICAL-CORE
- TASK-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-POLICY-VALIDATION
- TC-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-POLICY-VALIDATION
- Deep context: `lex context bd-1g7 --json`
