title: Restore through a verified generation and atomic activation
description:
## Objective
RestoreInsightsBackupCommand activates either one complete verified restored generation or leaves the prior authoritative generation unchanged under every fault.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-BACKUP-VERIFY-RESTORE: Back up, verify, and restore retained INSIGHTS state
  - AC: Given a valid owner-only backup target, when Backup succeeds, then all durable Report Snapshots, Restatement links, Deletion Events, idempotency outcomes, policy metadata, and storage-version evidence are captured in one attributable point-in-time artifact.
  - AC: Given Backup, when its contents are inspected, then ephemeral producer cursors, temporary reports, private evaluation rows, raw merchant text, and unrelated upstream state are absent.
  - AC: Given a backup artifact, when Verify succeeds, then cryptographic fingerprint, format and storage compatibility, ownership and permissions, complete counts, link integrity, content fingerprints, idempotency outcomes, and deletion-state consistency are independently checked.
  - AC: Given corruption, omission, incompatible version, unsafe ownership or permissions, or failed integrity, when Verify runs, then the exact failure is returned and no active store changes.
  - AC: Given a verified compatible backup, when Restore starts, then it builds and validates a separate candidate without modifying the active store.
  - AC: Given a complete verified candidate, when Restore activates, then the selected backup state becomes authoritative atomically and the prior store remains available until activation succeeds.
  - AC: Given cancellation, crash, insufficient space, permission failure, or migration failure before activation, when recovery completes, then the prior authoritative store is unchanged.
  - AC: Given a backup created before a later report deletion, when that backup is explicitly restored, then its exact captured report and deletion state are reinstated and identified in the restore result.

## Depends On
- bd-2bm (TASK-INSIGHTS-BACKUP-VERIFY): Restore reuses the independent artifact verifier.
- bd-7kn (TASK-INSIGHTS-STATE-FOUNDATION): Restore uses generation layout, migrations, integrity, and writer coordination.
- bd-2nv (TASK-INSIGHTS-IDEMPOTENCY-EXECUTOR): Restore records and replays one outcome inside the activated candidate.

## In Scope
- Implement source verification, separate generation construction, compatible migration, restore outcome recording, revalidation, expected-current locking, and atomic CURRENT replacement.
- Produce: InsightsRestoreActivator
- Produce: RestoreInsightsBackupCommand
- Produce: Atomic activation fault tests

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- Restore requires source, expected current generation fingerprint, actor, safe path identity, and idempotency and resolves an exact prior outcome before source work.
- The source is independently verified before a separate new generation is created; only compatible ordered migrations run against the candidate, and complete logical, fingerprint, permission, and integrity verification follows.
- The candidate records exactly the current Restore replay outcome and recovery event, is flushed and reverified, then activation acquires the exclusive module writer lock and compares expected current fingerprint before changing authority.
- Successful activation atomically replaces CURRENT and fsyncs its directory; startup observes either the complete old or complete new generation, Status cites durable Restore verification, and the prior generation remains a recovery point.
- Corruption, omission, incompatibility, permission, space, cancellation, migration, verification, expected-current drift, pointer replacement, or fsync failure before completed activation leaves the prior authoritative store unchanged and returns an exact stable failure.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT restore over the live database or rename individual database/WAL files — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-VERIFIED-RECOVERY-ACTIVATION.
- ❌ Do NOT activate before full candidate verification, skip expected-current revalidation, overwrite the prior generation, persist standalone Verify, or add source business data beyond the captured backup.
- ❌ Do NOT include candidate paths or report payloads in results, diagnostics, logs, or crash output.

## Approach
Implement source verification, separate generation construction, compatible migration, restore outcome recording, revalidation, expected-current locking, and atomic CURRENT replacement.
- Constraint: Only CURRENT is the authority switch; directory fsync and exclusive writer coordination are mandatory.
- Expected output: InsightsRestoreActivator
- Expected output: RestoreInsightsBackupCommand
- Expected output: Atomic activation fault tests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: InsightsBackupVerifier.VerifyAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-RECOVERY-CONTRACTS) — source and candidate verification
- Consumes: InsightsMigrations.ApplyAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE) — compatible candidate migrations
- Consumes: InsightsIdempotencyExecutor.ExecuteAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-IDEMPOTENCY) — exact replay and Restore outcome
- Produces: InsightsRestoreActivator.ActivateAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-RECOVERY-CONTRACTS) — verified atomic generation activation
- Produces: RestoreInsightsBackupCommand.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — insights.restore.activate handler

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Insights/Storage/Recovery/InsightsRestoreActivator.cs` (create) — candidate migration, validation, lock, pointer, and fsync workflow
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/Recovery/Restore/RestoreInsightsBackupCommand.cs` (create) — idempotent Restore handler
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Recovery/InsightsRestoreActivationTests.cs` (test) — source, migration, expected-current, atomic pointer, restart, and fault tests

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~InsightsRestoreActivationTests` — exit 0; at least 24 exact-replay, source-verification, incompatible-version, migration, candidate-outcome, revalidation, permission, space, cancellation, expected-current-drift, lock, pointer, fsync, restart-old-or-new, prior-generation, Status, and disclosure cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(insights): restore verified generation atomically` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-1i6`

## Lex references
- DD-INSIGHTS-VERIFIED-RECOVERY-ACTIVATION
- DIAG-INSIGHTS-RECOVERY-WORKFLOW
- DM-INSIGHTS-RECOVERY-CONTRACTS
- FR-INSIGHTS-BACKUP-VERIFY-RESTORE
- NFR-INSIGHTS-VERIFIED-RECOVERABILITY
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-03-RECOVERY
- TASK-INSIGHTS-RESTORE-ACTIVATE
- TC-INSIGHTS-RESTORE-FAILURE-ATOMICITY

title: Implement version-matched integration guidance
description:
## Objective
Keep the executable schema sufficient for every caller while optional guidance teaches only public operations and rejects incompatibility before host-file mutation.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-SKILL-COMPATIBILITY: Expose version-matched integration guidance
  - AC: Given only the executable and its discovered schemas, when Hermes or another External Orchestrator integrates with Ledger, then every public workflow is usable without installing an agent-specific skill or accessing private storage.
  - AC: Given a supported optional agent host, when Ledger integration guidance or a skill is installed, then its instructions and declared contract version are available without copying private database files, implementation APIs, or provider-specific transport semantics.
  - AC: Given guidance or a skill and executable with compatible declared contract versions, when a Ledger workflow is invoked, then only discoverable provider-neutral public operations and schemas are used.
  - AC: Given an incompatible declared contract version, when execution is attempted, then compatibility fails before any Ledger mutation and returns a stable module-scoped error.
  - AC: Given a breaking public contract change, when a release is produced, then the contract version changes and all bundled guidance or skills declare compatibility with that version.

## Depends On
- bd-2zt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE): Skill operations consume the proven registry, process, artifact, and idempotency seams.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Consumer requires ArtifactReconciler from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.
- bd-3sx (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-PROCESS-CONTRACT): Consumer requires OperationRegistry from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.

## In Scope
- Embed optional provider-neutral, version-matched integration guidance and supported agent-host bundles; expose list/check/idempotent explicit install without making a skill authoritative or required.
- Produce: Codex and Claude Code skill sources
- Produce: Embedded manifest and installer
- Produce: Skill operation contracts/module
- Produce: Parity/compatibility/install tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT make Codex, Claude Code, Hermes, or any skill a runtime dependency or authority.
- Do NOT let guidance add operations, relax validation, or introduce provider/transport fields.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-5gf (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-RECOVERY-SKILL-BUNDLE)

## Success Criteria
- TC-LEDGER-AGENT-CONTRACT-CONFORMANCE and TC-LEDGER-SKILL-COMPATIBILITY-CONTRACT complete every declared public Ledger workflow using only executable help and machine schemas, with no installed guidance, private store access, or provider adapter.
- Embedded manifest declares host format, bundle checksum, operation IDs, executable range, and contract range and is generated/validated against OperationRegistry.
- guidance list/check are read-only and classify compatible, missing, outdated, newer, or invalid bundles for supported explicit scopes.
- guidance install fails before write for unsupported host/incompatible contract/unsafe path/invalid manifest; compatible install publishes only the documented host scope then verifies checksum/version.
- Identical install replay returns original path/version/checksum; crash after publication reconciles a matching manifest; bundled content uses only public provider-neutral operations and never teaches mailbox, MIME, WhatsApp, delivery, schedule, recipient, acknowledgement, private SQLite, or file access.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT make Codex, Claude Code, Hermes, or any skill a runtime dependency or authority.
- ❌ Do NOT let guidance add operations, relax validation, or introduce provider/transport fields.

## Approach
- Embed optional provider-neutral, version-matched integration guidance and supported agent-host bundles; expose list/check/idempotent explicit install without making a skill authoritative or required.
- Expected output: Codex and Claude Code skill sources
- Expected output: Embedded manifest and installer
- Expected output: Skill operation contracts/module
- Expected output: Parity/compatibility/install tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: GuidanceOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-SYSTEM-SKILL-CONTRACTS) — list/check/install descriptors
- Produces: GuidanceService (DM-LEDGER-SYSTEM-SKILL-CONTRACTS) — optional host publication
- Consumes: OperationRegistry (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — authoritative public contract
- Consumes: ArtifactReconciler (DD-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-MUTATIONS) — atomic optional publication

## Files (reservation globs)

- `src/Tally/Guidance/Codex/SKILL.md` (create) — optional Codex host bundle — required
- `src/Tally/Guidance/ClaudeCode/SKILL.md` (create) — optional Claude Code host bundle — required
- `src/Tally/Contracts/System/GuidanceContracts.cs` (create) — public guidance contracts — required
- `src/Tally/Features/System/Guidance/GuidanceService.cs` (create) — explicit host publication — required
- `src/Tally/Features/System/Guidance/GuidanceOperationModule.cs` (create) — three guidance descriptors — required

## Verification

- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~GuidanceCompatibilityTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 16 schema-authority, compatibility, provider-neutral content, path, permission, crash, and replay cases run and 0 fail

title: Implement bounded in-process PDF extraction
description:
## Objective
Extract deterministic passive PDF evidence under Native-AOT and resource bounds without source mutation, child processes, disk extraction, or a speculative parser port.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-VARIANT-QUALIFICATION: Qualify and detect supported statement variants
  - AC: Given any of the three qualified private fixtures, when variant detection runs, then exactly one of the two versioned text-native PDF variants is selected from structural evidence rather than filename or extension.
  - AC: Given the qualified permission-encrypted fixture that is text-extractable without an opening password, when it is previewed twice, then each run selects exactly the fixture manifest variantId, reproduces every expected ordered record and reconciliation control, requires no opening password, OCR, external process, or external fetch, completes within 5 seconds and 256 MiB peak resident memory, and creates no LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a scan-only PDF, opening-password requirement, unsupported tabular export, active-content dependency, external fetch, malformed structure, ambiguous variant, or changed layout, when detection runs, then INGEST returns a stable unsupported or unsafe-source result and creates no committable manifest or LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a source exceeding any advertised adapter bound for size, pages, records, nesting, decompression, memory, or processing time, when preview is attempted, then processing stops with a stable resource-limit result before mutation.
  - AC: Given a proposed new or revised adapter, when it lacks representative fixtures that account for every source record and reconcile every required financial control deterministically, then the variant cannot be advertised as supported.

## Depends On
- bd-twx (TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): Extractor failures use the stable IngestError contract.
- bd-3k6 (TASK-INGEST-GATE-EVIDENCE-PRIVATE-FIXTURES): Fixture-dependent extractor tests consume the validated private expectation manifest.

## In Scope
- Pin the spike-proven upstream package, convert one immutable byte buffer to ordered page/glyph evidence, and define the one real format-adapter seam.
- Produce: Pinned PdfPig dependency.
- Produce: PdfStatementTextExtractor.
- Produce: PdfDocumentEvidence and IStatementAdapter seam.
- Produce: Private fixture injection helper.
- Produce: Native-AOT extraction tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- src/Tally/Tally.csproj pins package PdfPig at exactly 0.1.14 and introduces no legacy UglyToad.PdfPig package, Poppler executable, custom PDF parser, OCR library, or dynamic-code dependency.
- PdfStatementTextExtractor accepts a bounded immutable byte buffer, CancellationToken, and extraction limits and returns ordered PdfDocumentEvidence page/glyph/text/coordinate data with no PdfPig type escaping Infrastructure/Ingest/Pdf.
- The extractor never follows links, renders, executes scripts or embedded objects, performs remote fetch, writes extracted text to disk, or modifies the caller-owned source.
- Unsupported opening-password encryption, scan-only content, malformed structure, ambiguous content order, and every configured byte/page/glyph/time bound return a stable pre-mutation IngestError.
- PrivateStatementFixtureSet reads only the path named by TALLY_INGEST_PRIVATE_FIXTURE_MANIFEST; the ignored manifest supplies three source paths, variant IDs, and expected private facts, and neither paths nor facts enter source control or test output.
- All three private fixtures, including the permission-encrypted fixture, extract non-empty evidence twice with identical digests; Release linux-x64 Native-AOT publish succeeds with zero trim/dynamic-code warnings.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT use legacy UglyToad.PdfPig 1.7.0-custom-5, Poppler, a child process, or a custom parser — rejected per DD-INGEST-DOCUMENT-EXTRACTION.
- ❌ Do NOT introduce a generic PDF parser interface; PdfStatementTextExtractor remains concrete until a second real production implementation exists.
- ❌ Do NOT place source paths in process arguments, logs, test names, snapshots, or failure messages — per DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY.
- ❌ Do NOT commit fixture bytes, fixture manifests, filenames, expected private facts, or extracted text.

## Approach
Pin the spike-proven upstream package, convert one immutable byte buffer to ordered page/glyph evidence, and define the one real format-adapter seam.
- Constraint: Hard limits are checked before allocation or continued parsing where the source makes that possible; cancellation is propagated at every async boundary.
- Note: The ignored private fixture manifest is external test evidence, not a tracked deliverable.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: IngestError (DM-INGEST-ERROR-STATUS-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: PrivateFixtureExpectationManifest (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT) — Validated by TASK-INGEST-GATE-EVIDENCE-PRIVATE-FIXTURES and supplied via TALLY_INGEST_PRIVATE_FIXTURE_MANIFEST.
- Produces: PdfStatementTextExtractor.ExtractAsync (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Produces: PdfDocumentEvidence (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Produces: IStatementAdapter (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Produces: PrivateStatementFixtureSet (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT)

- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/PdfExtraction/PdfStatementTextExtractorTests.cs` (test) — determinism, bounds, and AOT evidence — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~PdfStatementTextExtractorTests` -> exit 0; at least 12 extraction, malformed-input, encrypted, bound, cancellation, and determinism cases are discovered and 0 tests fail without printing source paths or content
- **Test:** `dotnet publish src/Tally/Tally.csproj -c Release -r linux-x64 --self-contained true -p:PublishAot=true` -> exit 0; a native tally executable is produced with 0 trim or dynamic-code warnings attributable to PdfPig or INGEST

## Commit

- FR-INGEST-VARIANT-QUALIFICATION

title: Wire and prove the complete INSIGHTS public contract
description:
## Objective
A focused public-contract suite proves exactly eleven stable Public INSIGHTS Operations plus shared schema discovery are wired and published.

## Implements
- None; this task is a validation or shared-contract task with no direct FR ownership.

## Depends On

- bd-32j (TASK-INSIGHTS-REPORT-HISTORY-STATUS): Register List and Status.
- bd-6eq (TASK-INSIGHTS-RETAIN-REPORT): Register Retain.
- bd-3t2 (TASK-INSIGHTS-RESTATE-REPORT): Register Restate.
- bd-34e (TASK-INSIGHTS-DELETE-REPORT): Register Delete.
- bd-2bm (TASK-INSIGHTS-BACKUP-VERIFY): Register Backup and Verify.
- bd-1i6 (TASK-INSIGHTS-RESTORE-ACTIVATE): Register Restore.

## In Scope
- Converge all handler slices into the released local INSIGHTS registry and Native-AOT consumer contract.
- Produce: Complete published INSIGHTS contract evidence

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- InsightsExtensions explicitly registers all eleven descriptors and handlers plus compiler, mapper, state, BUDGET client, recovery, and TimeProvider services; Program composes it without reflection.
- system.schema.list/show exposes exactly eleven canonically ordered INSIGHTS descriptors plus active and deferred policy metadata with stable request, result, error, authority, mutability, idempotency, limit, and compatibility schemas and performs no financial read.
- Every invocation emits one versioned stdout envelope and documented exit category for success, malformed input, unknown field, NotFound, Deleted, conflict, unavailable, insufficient, incompatibility, integrity, resource, downstream, or sanitized internal failure.
- Complex financial input uses stdin or an owner-only file; values and private paths never enter arguments, stderr, ordinary logs, crash output, Git, or shared temporary files, and no listener or background service starts.
- Supported and one-version-incompatible Agent Host and CHANNELS fixtures prove identical released semantics and no presentation, delivery, policy, upstream, or retained-state mutation authority.
- linux-x64 Native-AOT publication reaches every handler through source-generated JSON with zero trim, reflection, dynamic-code, descriptor, or build warnings.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT use scanning, implicit discovery, a generic execute facade, HTTP/FastEndpoints, aliases, prompts, daemons, schedulers, listeners, or background processes.
- ❌ Do NOT register deferred formulas, UI services, seeds, or mutation authority for BUDGET, LEDGER, CLASSIFY, policies, or CHANNELS.
- ❌ Do NOT accept aggregate totals unless every named descriptor, handler, compatibility partition, and consumer fixture has nonzero discovery.

## Approach
Converge all handler slices into the released local INSIGHTS registry and Native-AOT consumer contract.
- Expected output: Complete published INSIGHTS contract evidence

title: RefundWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove one valid full-amount refund offsets the current category and Spend Pool assigned to the original transaction in the credit period while preserving immutable facts and exact history.

## Verifies
- FR-LEDGER-REFUND-CONFIRMATION
- FR-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-WRITES
- DD-LEDGER-FULL-AMOUNT-REFUND-RELATIONSHIP
- UC-LEDGER-010
- TC-LEDGER-REFUND-CONFIRMATION-CONTRACT

## Depends On
- bd-2jj: the complete published Ledger public contract

## In Scope
- Release-published process verification of refund confirmation, relationship detail, and dimensional actuals.
- Exact full-amount, same-account ZAR eligibility.
- Partial, over-refund, unmatched, duplicate, lifecycle, replay, concurrency, and write-failure paths.
- Later-period and negative-period exact actuals.

## Out of Scope
- Partial or cumulative refunds.
- Direct handler or SQLite invocation.
- Inferring the relationship or its category/pool from another dimension.

## Files
- tests/Tally.Tests/EndToEnd/UC010RefundWorkflowTests.cs (test)

## Verification
- dotnet build Tally.slnx
- focused UC010RefundWorkflowTests through the Release published executable

## Commit
- test(ledger): verify uc 010 workflow
- Refs: bd-1jr

## Context
- lex context bd-1jr --json
design: Verify UC-LEDGER-010 only through the Release published tally process and provider-neutral operations. The workflow proves one exact full refund, its dimensional offset, immutable source facts, history, stable replay, and fail-closed rejection of partial, over-refund, unmatched, duplicate, inactive, and competing attempts.
acceptance_criteria:
- One active same-account ZAR credit with opposite role signs and exactly equal absolute minor units creates one attributable refund relationship and reduces External Spend under the original transaction's current category and pool in the credit Effective Date period.
- Later category or pool correction moves the linked offset without rewriting either transaction or relationship; exact all-up, pool, category, and cell totals remain conserved.
- Missing, account, currency, sign, lifecycle, partial, over-refund, active-role duplicate, replay-conflict, concurrent competing-refund, and write-failure cases leave prior state unchanged.
- A semantically identical replay returns the original relationship; detail/history expose exact full-amount roles while both source transaction facts remain unchanged.
- At least 12 full-refund main and failure-path scenarios pass through the published executable.
notes: Corrected on 2026-07-22 after Dirk established the full-refund-only product rule. Multiple or cumulative partial-credit scenarios are prohibited and must not appear in UC-LEDGER-010 verification.

title: Prove the qualified adapter set
description:
## Objective
Prove exclusive detection, deterministic extraction, exact private fixture expectations, and advertised resource bounds for exactly two supported variants.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-SOURCE-RECONCILIATION: Reconcile every supported statement before approval
  - AC: Given a qualified statement, when preview completes, then every source record has exactly one accepted-candidate, exact-duplicate, excluded-non-transaction, or blocked outcome with a stable reason.
  - AC: Given any supported initial fixture, when its normalized non-zero movements are summed with its opening economic balance, then the result equals its closing economic balance exactly in minor units.
  - AC: Given a supported variant that supplies running balances, when records are normalized, then every consecutive running-balance transition reconciles exactly to its signed movement.
  - AC: Given a source control that the detected variant exposes, when preview completes, then the manifest records and verifies that control exactly.
  - AC: Given an unexplained transaction-bearing record, amount mismatch, running-balance mismatch, opening/closing mismatch, or ambiguous exclusion, when reconciliation runs, then the manifest remains unapproved and uncommittable.
  - AC: Given a control type that the source does not expose, when preview completes, then the manifest reports that control as unavailable rather than fabricating a value.
- FR-INGEST-VARIANT-QUALIFICATION: Qualify and detect supported statement variants
  - AC: Given any of the three qualified private fixtures, when variant detection runs, then exactly one of the two versioned text-native PDF variants is selected from structural evidence rather than filename or extension.
  - AC: Given the qualified permission-encrypted fixture that is text-extractable without an opening password, when it is previewed twice, then each run selects exactly the fixture manifest variantId, reproduces every expected ordered record and reconciliation control, requires no opening password, OCR, external process, or external fetch, completes within 5 seconds and 256 MiB peak resident memory, and creates no LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a scan-only PDF, opening-password requirement, unsupported tabular export, active-content dependency, external fetch, malformed structure, ambiguous variant, or changed layout, when detection runs, then INGEST returns a stable unsupported or unsafe-source result and creates no committable manifest or LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a source exceeding any advertised adapter bound for size, pages, records, nesting, decompression, memory, or processing time, when preview is attempted, then processing stops with a stable resource-limit result before mutation.
  - AC: Given a proposed new or revised adapter, when it lacks representative fixtures that account for every source record and reconcile every required financial control deterministically, then the variant cannot be advertised as supported.

## Depends On
- bd-1as (TASK-INGEST-ADAPTER-LAYOUT-A): The gate consumes PdfTextLayoutAStatementAdapter.
- bd-3c4 (TASK-INGEST-ADAPTER-LAYOUT-B): The gate consumes PdfTextLayoutBStatementAdapter.
- bd-1j2 (TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION): Consumes PdfStatementTextExtractor.ExtractAsync.

## In Scope
- Integration gate converges the extractor and both adapters into the exact explicit v1 registry before preview can consume any format behavior.
- Produce: StatementAdapterRegistry.
- Produce: QualifiedStatementAdapterSet.
- Produce: AdapterQualificationTests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- StatementAdapterRegistry explicitly contains exactly one PdfTextLayoutAStatementAdapter and one PdfTextLayoutBStatementAdapter in deterministic order; no reflection scan, plugin, fallback, third variant, or user configuration exists.
- Every one of the three private fixtures produces exactly one exact_match and the variant ID declared by the ignored private fixture manifest; zero or multiple matches return a stable unsupported result.
- Each fixture is extracted twice through the real PdfPig extractor and selected adapter with identical ordered source records, dispositions, controls, and safe digests.
- Every transaction-bearing source record is accepted, proven Exact Duplicate by fixture evidence, or blocked; non-transaction exclusions carry stable reasons; all available opening, closing, running-balance, total, and record-count controls match exactly.
- One-over-limit and malformed sources fail before state or LEDGER mutation; measured direct extraction plus adapter work stays below 5 seconds and 256 MiB peak RSS per fixture on the supported release host.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT advertise a format from filename/extension or partial fixture success.
- ❌ Do NOT add OCR, generic tables, regex templates, dynamic plugins, or best-effort fallback — rejected per both governing decisions.
- ❌ Do NOT print or persist private fixture paths, descriptions, amounts, balances, records, or expected facts.

## Approach
Integration gate converges the extractor and both adapters into the exact explicit v1 registry before preview can consume any format behavior.
- Constraint: A new or revised adapter cannot enter the registry without representative private golden evidence and exact reconciliation.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: PdfStatementTextExtractor.ExtractAsync (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Consumes: PdfTextLayoutAStatementAdapter (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Consumes: PdfTextLayoutBStatementAdapter (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Produces: StatementAdapterRegistry (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Produces: QualifiedStatementAdapterSet (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Pdf/StatementAdapterRegistry.cs` (create) — exact explicit adapter registry — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Adapters/AdapterQualificationTests.cs` (test) — cross-adapter exclusivity and golden gate — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Adapters/AdapterResourceBoundTests.cs` (test) — resource and one-over-limit gate — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~AdapterQualificationTests|FullyQualifiedName~AdapterResourceBoundTests'` -> exit 0; all 3 injected fixtures run twice, exactly 2 variants are advertised, at least 20 qualification/resource cases are discovered, and 0 tests fail

## Commit
- `test(ingest): prove qualified adapters` — footer `Refs: bd-1m0`

## Lex references
- DD-INGEST-DOCUMENT-EXTRACTION
- DD-INGEST-FORMAT-ADAPTERS
- DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE
- FA-INGEST-CONTRACT-FORMATS
- FR-INGEST-SOURCE-RECONCILIATION
- FR-INGEST-VARIANT-QUALIFICATION
- NFR-INGEST-BOUNDED-PARSING
- NFR-INGEST-DETERMINISTIC-INTEGRITY
- PLAN-INGEST-V1
- SP-INGEST-01-FORMAT-CORE
- TASK-INGEST-ADAPTER-LAYOUT-A
- TASK-INGEST-ADAPTER-LAYOUT-B
- TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-FORMAT-ADAPTERS
- TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION
- TC-INGEST-ADAPTER-GOLDEN-FIXTURES
- Deep context: `lex context bd-1m0 --json`

title: Implement passive preview and immutable manifest persistence
description:
## Objective
Deliver ingest.preview as a deterministic no-LEDGER-mutation workflow that persists only a complete immutable manifest or a stable failure state.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-SOURCE-RECONCILIATION: Reconcile every supported statement before approval
  - AC: Given a qualified statement, when preview completes, then every source record has exactly one accepted-candidate, exact-duplicate, excluded-non-transaction, or blocked outcome with a stable reason.
  - AC: Given any supported initial fixture, when its normalized non-zero movements are summed with its opening economic balance, then the result equals its closing economic balance exactly in minor units.
  - AC: Given a supported variant that supplies running balances, when records are normalized, then every consecutive running-balance transition reconciles exactly to its signed movement.
  - AC: Given a source control that the detected variant exposes, when preview completes, then the manifest records and verifies that control exactly.
  - AC: Given an unexplained transaction-bearing record, amount mismatch, running-balance mismatch, opening/closing mismatch, or ambiguous exclusion, when reconciliation runs, then the manifest remains unapproved and uncommittable.
  - AC: Given a control type that the source does not expose, when preview completes, then the manifest reports that control as unavailable rather than fabricating a value.
- FR-INGEST-STATEMENT-PREVIEW: Preview a statement without financial mutation
  - AC: Given a readable supported source and one explicitly selected active canonical account, when preview runs, then INGEST fingerprints the source content, validates statement metadata against the selected account, records adapter and LEDGER contract versions, and returns one stable batch and manifest revision.
  - AC: Given the same Source Fingerprint, selected account, adapter version, and LEDGER contract version, when preview is repeated under any filename, then INGEST identifies Exact Replay and returns the same ordered source-record outcomes, candidate facts, reconciliation evidence, and manifest identity.
  - AC: Given a missing, archived, conflicting, multi-account, or ambiguous selected account, when preview runs, then INGEST blocks the batch rather than inferring an account from the filename or untrusted statement text.
  - AC: Given a source that changes while being read or no longer matches its recorded fingerprint, when preview completes, then INGEST returns a stable source-changed failure and persists no committable manifest.
  - AC: Given any preview success or failure, when the operation ends, then the caller-owned source remains byte-for-byte unchanged and no LEDGER transaction has been created.

## Depends On
- bd-3l1 (TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION): Preview persists one atomic manifest transaction through IngestDatabase.
- bd-1m0 (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-FORMAT-ADAPTERS): Preview consumes only the qualified StatementAdapterRegistry.
- bd-2sg (TASK-INGEST-LEDGER-PUBLIC-CLIENT): Preview validates the selected account through LedgerContractClient.GetAccountAsync.
- bd-2gk (TASK-INGEST-PREVIEW-DOMAIN): Preview composes the deterministic identity, normalization, reconciliation, manifest, and overlap policies.
- bd-twx (TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): Consumes IngestOperationContracts.

## In Scope
- Compose public account lookup, bounded source snapshotting, qualified extraction, domain policies, Exact Replay/overlap checks, and one atomic preview-state transaction.
- Produce: CallerOwnedSourceReader.
- Produce: PreviewStateStore.
- Produce: PreviewHandler.
- Produce: PreviewOperationModule.
- Produce: PreviewWorkflowTests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Preview validates contract versions, selected account identifier, path schema, and advertised limits before opening the source; LedgerContractClient confirms exactly one active ZAR account and statement metadata must agree without inference.
- CallerOwnedSourceReader opens read-only, captures safe before/after metadata, reads no more than the byte limit into one immutable buffer, computes SHA-256 over those exact bytes, and returns source_changed if metadata or bytes change.
- PreviewStateStore checks the complete Exact Replay key first: Source Fingerprint, selected account, adapter version, and LEDGER contract version. An active or completed match returns the prior manifest, status, or receipt without reparsing or mutation; any changed key component requires a new preview before overlap evaluation.
- A new source runs the real extractor and exactly one registered adapter, normalizes/accounts/reconciles every record, blocks any ambiguity or overlap, canonicalizes the complete ordered manifest, and persists batch, revision, records, candidates, controls, and safe status in one INGEST transaction.
- Success returns stable batchId and manifestRevisionId; any failure creates no committable manifest; every path leaves the source byte-for-byte unchanged and invokes no ledger.transaction.record.
- PreviewOperationModule exposes a typed handler binding for ingest.preview but is not added to the global registry until GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT infer the account from filename or masked statement text, reread after manifest freeze, or mutate LEDGER — per C8/C12 and DD-INGEST-MANIFEST-IDENTITY-OVERLAP.
- ❌ Do NOT persist partial candidate/manifests outside one SQLite transaction or retain a source copy/extracted temporary.
- ❌ Do NOT call adapters through reflection/plugins or bypass StatementAdapterRegistry.
- ❌ Do NOT place parsing, reconciliation, identity, or finance policy inside PreviewHandler.

## Approach
Compose public account lookup, bounded source snapshotting, qualified extraction, domain policies, Exact Replay/overlap checks, and one atomic preview-state transaction.
- Constraint: All private payload output is confined to the explicit owner-invoked result envelope; diagnostics stay metadata-only.

An active or completed match returns the prior manifest, status, or receipt without reparsing or mutation; any changed key component requires a new preview before overlap evaluation.

title: Verify Restatement and corrected-report comparison
description:
## Objective
UC-INSIGHTS-004 creates exactly one linked child only after a complete meaningful change and otherwise returns NoChange without modifying history.

## Implements
- None; this task is a validation or shared-contract task with no direct FR ownership.

## Depends On

## In Scope

- Produce: VerifiedInsightsUc004
- Produce: UC-004 Restatement evidence

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- A compatible existing predecessor remains byte-semantically unchanged while current evidence is generated through the same coherent workflow and compared by source, policy, schema, exact metric, state, explanation, and attention sections.
- Identical identities, versions, schema, and semantic content return NoChange, create no child snapshot/link, and replay the same outcome without producer access.
- A source-, policy-, schema-, or semantic-only change creates one child with one predecessor link and returns deterministic bounded differences even when exact amounts are equal.
- Unknown, deleted, corrupt, incompatible, unavailable, incoherent, over-limit, cancellation, write, and crash paths return exact failures with no partial child, link, difference, or duplicate replay outcome.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT modify the predecessor, compare display text, omit provenance/version changes, auto-restate, store partial differences, or create a child for exact NoChange.
- ❌ Do NOT validate only value changes; equal-value source, policy, and schema changes are explicit Restatements.

## Approach
Acceptance task for UC-INSIGHTS-004; prove predecessor immutability, current coherent generation, NoChange, ordered difference, linked append, replay, and failure atomicity.
- Constraint: All reads and writes use the published Restate contract and real store.
- Expected output: VerifiedInsightsUc004
- Expected output: UC-004 Restatement evidence

title: TransactionCorrectionWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove ordinary correction retains history and never silently transfers evidence, decisions, dimensions, or relationships.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-WRITES: Make public writes idempotent
  - AC: Given any previously successful public mutation, when the same Idempotency Identity, operation, contract version, and semantically identical request are submitted again, then the original structured result is returned and no second domain, decision, link, or artifact effect occurs.
  - AC: Given a previously successful Idempotency Identity, when it is reused for another operation, contract version, or materially different request, then the same module-scoped conflict code is returned on every retry and the original effect is preserved.
  - AC: Given an invalid mutation request, when validation fails, then no domain or artifact effect occurs and the supplied Idempotency Identity remains available for a corrected request.
  - AC: Given evidence registration or linking, reconciliation application or correction, payment attribution, Pool Assignment, or pool maintenance, when its logical identity is replayed through another request identity, then the domain uniqueness rule prevents a duplicate effect independently of request replay.
  - AC: Given any public mutation without a valid caller-visible Idempotency Identity, when invocation is attempted, then the Ledger rejects it before mutation with a stable validation error.
- FR-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CORRECTION: Void or supersede erroneous transactions
  - AC: Given an active transaction and a reason, when the owner voids it, then it becomes inactive, the void event is attributable, active financial relationships retire atomically, and default actuals exclude it.
  - AC: Given an active transaction, a reason, and valid replacement facts, when the owner supersedes it, then one distinct replacement is created, the original becomes inactive, and default actuals include only explicitly established active replacement state.
  - AC: Given a corrected transaction with evidence, Reconciliation Decisions, category or Pool Assignments, or payment attribution, when void or supersession is applied, then those records remain attached to their historical subject and none silently moves to the replacement.
  - AC: Given reconciliation was affected by void or supersession, when current state is queried, then one Reconciliation Exception outcome is returned until a valid deterministic or owner-approved decision is recorded.
  - AC: Given a voided or superseded transaction, when history-inclusive detail is requested, then original facts, evidence and decision history, assignments, actor context, reason, time, and retired relationships remain queryable, and its replacement reference is returned for supersession or explicit null for void.
  - AC: Given a statement-authoritative reconciliation correction uses supersession, when it commits, then category, Spend Pool, Payment Instrument, and cardholder state may appear on the replacement only through explicit carry-forward events referencing the Reconciliation Decision; the historical state remains attached to the prior transaction and no evidence or relationship moves by overwrite.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace ordinary void/supersede separately from statement-authoritative correction.
- Produce: UC009TransactionCorrectionWorkflowTests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT conflate ordinary supersession with statement authority or accept a replacement that inherits financial meaning silently.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- Published ordinary void/supersede preserves original facts and evidence/decision/dimension history, retires incompatible relationships atomically, and counts only explicitly established replacement state.
- No-copy, invalid replacement, relationship retirement failure, stale/incompatible correction, history-inclusive detail, and replay cover every UC-LEDGER-009 failure path.
- A separate statement-correction fixture proves carry-forward occurs only through ledger.reconciliation.apply with decision-linked events, never through ordinary transaction.supersede.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT conflate ordinary supersession with statement authority or accept a replacement that inherits financial meaning silently.

## Approach
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace ordinary void/supersede separately from statement-authoritative correction.
- Constraint: Invoke the Release published tally executable; inspect stdout, stderr, exit code, and real temporary SQLite state.
- Expected output: UC009TransactionCorrectionWorkflowTests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: PublishedTallyFixture (Release published-process E2E fixture)
- Consumes: CompletePublicContract (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — Exactly 72 provider-neutral operations
- Produces: VerifiedUC009 (UC-LEDGER-009) — evidence-aware correction workflow

## Files (reservation globs)

## Verification

- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.EndToEnd.UC009TransactionCorrectionWorkflowTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 10 main/failure-path scenarios run and 0 fail

title: Define INSIGHTS analytical contracts
description:
## Objective
Every analytical INSIGHTS data shape is represented by one deterministic Native-AOT-safe contract without transport, persistence, or UI behavior.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY: Discover the Public INSIGHTS Operation contract
  - AC: Given a compatible caller with no financial input, when operation discovery is requested, then every Public INSIGHTS Operation is returned in canonical verb order with stable operation identity and contract version.
  - AC: Given one discovered operation, when its descriptor is inspected, then request and result schema fingerprints, mutability, authority, idempotency requirement, limits, stable error types, exit categories, and compatibility range are explicit.
  - AC: Given policy discovery, when the catalogue is returned, then every released Analysis Window, formula, Evidence Sufficiency, severity, explanation, and ordering policy has stable kind, version, status, and fingerprint metadata.
  - AC: Given a formally deferred Forecast, Trend, Anomaly, or Attention Item policy, when policy discovery is requested, then it is marked unsupported and no executable formula or threshold is advertised.
  - AC: Given INSIGHTS authority metadata, when it is inspected, then no LEDGER, CLASSIFY, or BUDGET mutation operation is present.
  - AC: Given an unsupported INSIGHTS contract version, when discovery is requested, then one stable compatibility failure is returned without reading financial data.
  - AC: Given discovery, help, or schema output, when all fields are inspected, then no report payload, amount, merchant text, private path, permission seed, role template, menu seed, translation key, or frontend key model is present.

## Depends On
- None; this bead is a planned execution root.

## In Scope
- Create the closed source-generated policy, coherent evidence, analytical state, explanation, and report records used before retained-state work begins.
- Produce: Native-AOT-safe analytical contract models
- Produce: Analytical schema and privacy tests

## Out of Scope
- Lifecycle, recovery, idempotency, and private-receipt records belong to TASK-INSIGHTS-LIFECYCLE-CONTRACT-MODELS.

## Success Criteria
- The contract assembly exposes closed enums and immutable records for policy descriptors, plan states, exact money and rationals, windows, metrics, pace states, evidence states, explanations, provenance, and InsightReport.
- Canonical report contracts exclude raw merchant descriptions, canonical transaction payloads, producer cursors, private evaluation rows, and dated actual members; only BoundInsightEvidence may carry dated members as ephemeral compiler input.
- All money is signed checked Int64 ZAR minor units, rationals have a positive denominator and canonical normalization, IDs are opaque stable values, and every collection documents deterministic ordering.
- The semantic content fingerprint includes every visible metric, state, explanation, provenance item, policy version, and source identity while excluding derived reportId and contentFingerprint fields.
- Source-generated serialization rejects unknown fields and produces byte-stable schema fingerprints under repeated builds.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT use string constants for system-owned states or statuses; use closed enums per ADR-CORE-0004.
- ❌ Do NOT add HTTP DTOs, frontend models, EF entities, policy scripts, arbitrary formulas, or caller-authored report payloads.
- ❌ Do NOT place raw merchant text, canonical transactions, producer cursors, or private evaluation rows in any analytical contract.
- ❌ Do NOT include reportId or contentFingerprint in the semantic payload that is hashed.

## Approach
Create the closed source-generated policy, coherent evidence, analytical state, explanation, and report records used before retained-state work begins.
- Constraint: Use file-scoped namespaces, nullable annotations, immutable records, and no reflection-based serialization.
- Expected output: Native-AOT-safe analytical contract models
- Expected output: Analytical schema and privacy tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: InsightPolicyCatalogueContracts (DM-INSIGHTS-POLICY-CATALOGUE) — active and deferred built-in policy descriptors
- Produces: BoundInsightEvidence (DM-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-EVIDENCE) — validated ephemeral producer evidence
- Produces: AnalysisWindowMetricStateAndExplanation (DM-INSIGHTS-ANALYTICAL-EVIDENCE) — exact analytical state records
- Produces: InsightReport (DM-INSIGHTS-INSIGHT-REPORT) — complete bounded analytical result

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Insights/Analysis/InsightAnalysisContracts.cs` (create) — policy evidence window metric pace explanation and report records
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Contract/InsightAnalyticalContractModelTests.cs` (test) — closed schema exact value ordering fingerprint and forbidden-field tests

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~InsightAnalyticalContractModelTests` — exit 0; at least 16 schema enum exact-value canonical-order fingerprint and forbidden-payload cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit

- DM-INSIGHTS-INSIGHT-REPORT

title: Verify immutable review and approval workflow
description:
## Objective
Prove an owner can inspect and approve exactly one committable immutable revision without editing source facts or mutating LEDGER.

## Implements
- No direct FR ownership: this is an evidence, integration, security, use-case, or completion gate.
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-MANIFEST-REVIEW-CONTRACT: Verify immutable manifest review and approval (verifies).
- Proves or constrains UC-INGEST-002: Review and approve a candidate batch (covers).

## Depends On

## In Scope
- Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-002 main scenario and every documented failure path.
- Produce: UCIngest002Evidence.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Main scenario previews, inspects the complete exact revision, verifies candidates/exclusions/duplicates/conflicts/controls/versions, approves its digest with actor attribution, and observes approved state with no Ledger transaction.
- An unexplained row, failed control, ambiguous semantic duplicate, overlap, missing fact, or unsupported finance meaning keeps approval blocked.
- Attempted edits to amount, currency, date, description, sign, account, or provenance are rejected and require corrected source/adapter plus a new preview.
- Adapter, manifest, Ledger contract, account state, or digest change invalidates the old approval and requires a compatible new revision.
- Commit with absent approval or an approval for another revision is rejected before mutation.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT query ingest.db or call handlers directly; assertions use inspect/status and public Ledger observation.
- ❌ Do NOT place manifest payloads in diagnostics or tracked snapshots.

## Approach
Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-002 main scenario and every documented failure path.
- Constraint: Sensitive manifest output is consumed in-memory by the test and never written to snapshots or logs.

## Interfaces

- Produces: UCIngest002Evidence (UC-INGEST-002)

title: Implement safe batch status and failure reporting
description:
## Objective
Deliver ingest.status with deterministic paging, mutation certainty, unresolved frontier, and safe retry/resume actions without source access.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-FAILURE-STATUS: Report blocked and failed ingestion safely
  - AC: Given any preview, review, commit, resume, or cleanup failure, when the result is returned, then it includes a stable INGEST error code and category, safe batch or candidate identifiers if available, whether any LEDGER mutation may have occurred, the last durable receipt state, and whether retry, resume, or correction is allowed.
  - AC: Given an unsupported, malformed, permission, source-change, resource-limit, compatibility, or reconciliation failure before commit, when status is inspected, then it proves no LEDGER mutation occurred and no committable manifest exists.
  - AC: Given a blocking overlap or ambiguous financial fact, when status is inspected, then it identifies the blocked source-context record safely and requires a new reviewed resolution rather than automatic continuation.
  - AC: Given a commit-stage failure after some Terminal Candidate Outcomes, when status is inspected, then it identifies the same approved manifest, completed outcomes, unresolved frontier, and safe resume operation.
  - AC: Given any expected domain failure, when diagnostics are emitted, then stdout contains one structured result, stderr contains metadata only, and no stack trace or parser exception substitutes for the stable contract.

## Depends On
- bd-twx (TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): Status consumes IngestError, BatchStatus, and ImportReceipt contracts.
- bd-3l1 (TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION): StatusStateStore reads the V001 INGEST schema.

## In Scope

- ❌ Do NOT modify durable state in StatusHandler — adapted read-only query rule per PAT-CORE-API-QUERIES.

## Approach
Read one detailed batch or a stable cursor page of retained summaries and map every expected failure to metadata-only durable-state guidance.

title: Activate evidence-proven automatic reconciliation
description:
## Objective
After OQ-LEDGER-13 is resolved from paired evidence, add the exact versioned automatic match and correction subset to the already working owner-reviewed reconciliation path. Every unproven case remains review-required.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On
- bd-339 (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION-POLICY): Automatic behavior is forbidden until paired evidence resolves OQ-LEDGER-13 and defines the bounded policy matrix.
- bd-1zm (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-PROJECTION): Automatic authority reuses the stable advisory projection and comparison basis.
- bd-37y (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-APPLY): Activation extends the proven owner-reviewed base apply path rather than creating a second writer.
- bd-3ts (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-STATEMENT-CORRECTION): Activation extends the proven owner-approved statement-correction path with only evidence-backed automatic cases.

## In Scope
- After OQ-LEDGER-13 is resolved from paired evidence, add the exact versioned automatic match and correction subset to the already working owner-reviewed reconciliation path. Every unproven case remains review-required.
- Produce: ReconciliationPolicyV1 and automatic authority integration with policy-matrix, drift, ambiguity, owner-path regression, and replay tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT infer rules beyond the resolved evidence matrix, add fuzzy description scoring, provider-specific fields, runtime tolerance configuration, or hidden tie-breaking.
- Do NOT make OQ-LEDGER-13 optional, synthesize its resolution in code, or enable automatic behavior while its gate bead is incomplete.
- Do NOT fork a second reconciliation write path; extend the existing disposition and statement-correction policies inside LedgerMutationExecutor atomicity.

## Success Criteria
- ReconciliationPolicyV1 encodes exactly the automatic match and correction cases recorded in the resolved OQ-LEDGER-13 matrix, including policy ID and version, Owned Bank Account, currency, Signed Account Amount, Effective Date, bounded tolerances if any, uniqueness requirements, conflict guards, and stable reasons.
- Automatic match or correction revalidates the Evidence Record fingerprint, current projection, policy and version, candidates, Canonical Transaction facts, lifecycle, and conflicts inside the existing writer transaction; drift or unsupported cases return a stable review-required or validation result with no mutation.
- Candidate enumeration order cannot alter a policy outcome; multiple compatible or guard candidates, conflicting Evidence Records, unresolved relationship replacement, or any case absent from the matrix cannot apply automatically.
- Owner-approved match, correction, Statement-only Transaction, ambiguity, and Reconciliation Exception behavior remains unchanged, and replay across both authority kinds returns one original effect.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT infer rules beyond the resolved evidence matrix, add fuzzy description scoring, provider-specific fields, runtime tolerance configuration, or hidden tie-breaking.
- ❌ Do NOT make OQ-LEDGER-13 optional, synthesize its resolution in code, or enable automatic behavior while its gate bead is incomplete.
- ❌ Do NOT fork a second reconciliation write path; extend the existing disposition and statement-correction policies inside LedgerMutationExecutor atomicity.

## Approach
- After OQ-LEDGER-13 is resolved from paired evidence, add the exact versioned automatic match and correction subset to the already working owner-reviewed reconciliation path. Every unproven case remains review-required.
- Expected output: ReconciliationPolicyV1 and automatic authority integration with policy-matrix, drift, ambiguity, owner-path regression, and replay tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: ReconciliationPolicyV1 (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY) — Only rules recorded by OQ-LEDGER-13Resolution
- Consumes: OQ-LEDGER-13Resolution (OQ-LEDGER-13)
- Consumes: ManualReviewProjectionV1 (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY)
- Consumes: ReconciliationDispositionPolicy (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY)
- Consumes: StatementAuthorityPolicy (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ledger/Reconciliation/ReconciliationPolicyV1.cs` (create) — evidence-proven automatic policy — required
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ledger/Reconciliation/ReconciliationDispositionPolicy.cs` (modify) — automatic match authority extension — required
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ledger/Reconciliation/StatementAuthorityPolicy.cs` (modify) — automatic correction authority extension — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Reconciliation/ReconciliationApplyHandler.cs` (modify) — transactional automatic authority integration — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Reconciliation/StatementAuthoritativeCorrectionCoordinator.cs` (modify) — automatic correction integration — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ledger/ReconciliationPolicyMatrixTests.cs` (test) — resolved policy fixture matrix — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ledger/ReconciliationApplyOperationTests.cs` (modify) — automatic match and owner regression tests — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ledger/StatementAuthoritativeCorrectionTests.cs` (modify) — automatic correction and owner regression tests — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Reconciliation --no-restore` — exit 0; the resolved automatic matrix, drift, ambiguity, owner-authority regression, correction, crash, and replay cases all run with 0 failures
- **test-evidence:** Map every enabled automatic branch to one resolved OQ-LEDGER-13 matrix row and prove every excluded row remains unapplied.
- **branch-review:** Confirm the change extends one reconciliation path and preserves owner authority, provider neutrality, statement authority, and append-only history.

## Commit

## In Scope

- Produce: IngestModuleGuardTests.
- Produce: Complete INGEST build/test/AOT/coverage evidence.

## Out of Scope

## Success Criteria

- Every security and UC evidence producer is present, every private fixture is external/ignored, git diff contains no statement filename/content, and source/hash/artifact checks are green.
- No bead refs are created by this task; planning/execution status remains owned by Lex/br pipeline stages.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT waive a failed required gate, reduce test discovery thresholds to hide missing classes, or treat zero-match filters as success.
- ❌ Do NOT ship private statements/expectations, source paths, extracted facts, temporary spike code, or generated plan input files.
- ❌ Do NOT compile beads, write execution status, or implement unapproved OQ-INGEST-4 cross-file identity.

## Approach
Final convergence gate intentionally has no Implements link: it proves build, all tests, Native-AOT publish, architecture/privacy guards, Lex coverage, and every expected implementation path after all security/use-case evidence exists.
- Constraint: All commands run from repository root with the external private fixture manifest injected only for tests that require it.

title: Implement deterministic reconciliation candidate projection
description:
## Objective
Deliver the read-only provider-neutral candidate and guard-candidate projection needed for owner review. It deterministically compares approved statement observations with active unreconciled Canonical Transactions but grants no automatic match or correction authority while OQ-LEDGER-13 remains unresolved.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-PROJECTION

## Depends On
- bd-3qf (TASK-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-REGISTRY): Projection reads normalized evidence through EvidenceStore.
- bd-84s (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTIONS-RECORD-GET): Projection reads canonical active transaction facts through TransactionStore.

## In Scope
- Deliver the read-only provider-neutral candidate and guard-candidate projection needed for owner review. It deterministically compares approved statement observations with active unreconciled Canonical Transactions but grants no automatic match or correction authority while OQ-LEDGER-13 remains unresolved.
- Produce: ManualReviewProjectionV1, projection contracts/store/handler/module, and real-SQLite deterministic projection tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT score descriptions, infer provider semantics, introduce automatic date or amount tolerances in this task, or choose a candidate; OQ-LEDGER-13 and TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-AUTOMATIC-ACTIVATION own any future automatic policy.
- Do NOT let a projection token authorize a write without transactional revalidation and explicit owner authority while automatic reconciliation is disabled.
- Do NOT mutate durable or ephemeral Ledger state from the candidates query or expose raw evidence payloads.

## Success Criteria
- For approved statement evidence and scope, the query returns active unreconciled exact candidates whose Owned Bank Account, currency, Signed Account Amount, and Effective Date equal the typed observation, plus same-account in-scope guard candidates with explicit differing fields; membership and ordering are stable across enumeration order.
- Zero exact candidates, one exact candidate, multiple exact candidates, and guard-only results are classified explicitly for owner review; no result authorizes automatic reconciliation while OQ-LEDGER-13 is unresolved.
- Inactive, reconciled, voided, superseded, wrong-account, out-of-scope, currency-conflicting, and actively statement-confirmed Canonical Transactions are excluded or surfaced with stable reasons according to the reviewed contract.
- Projection exposes a versioned advisory review token and complete privacy-safe comparison basis but creates no Evidence Record link, Reconciliation Decision, correction, Canonical Transaction, Idempotency Identity, or snapshot row.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT score descriptions, infer provider semantics, introduce automatic date or amount tolerances in this task, or choose a candidate; OQ-LEDGER-13 and TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-AUTOMATIC-ACTIVATION own any future automatic policy.
- ❌ Do NOT let a projection token authorize a write without transactional revalidation and explicit owner authority while automatic reconciliation is disabled.
- ❌ Do NOT mutate durable or ephemeral Ledger state from the candidates query or expose raw evidence payloads.

## Approach
- Deliver the read-only provider-neutral candidate and guard-candidate projection needed for owner review. It deterministically compares approved statement observations with active unreconciled Canonical Transactions but grants no automatic match or correction authority while OQ-LEDGER-13 remains unresolved.
- Expected output: ManualReviewProjectionV1, projection contracts/store/handler/module, and real-SQLite deterministic projection tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: ManualReviewProjectionV1 (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY) — Versioned advisory comparison only; never automatic authority
- Produces: ReconciliationProjectionStore (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY)
- Produces: ReconciliationProjectionOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: EvidenceStore (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECORD-LINK)
- Consumes: TransactionStore (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-FACT)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ledger/Reconciliation/ReconciliationProjectionContracts.cs` (create) — public contracts — required
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ledger/Reconciliation/ManualReviewProjectionV1.cs` (create) — versioned non-authorizing comparison rules — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Reconciliation/ReconciliationProjectionHandler.cs` (create) — query handler — required
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Storage/Reconciliation/ReconciliationProjectionStore.cs` (create) — read projection — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Reconciliation/ReconciliationProjectionOperationModule.cs` (create) — explicit descriptor and DI — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ledger/ReconciliationProjectionTests.cs` (test) — real-SQLite manual-review projection tests — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.Ledger.ReconciliationProjectionTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 20 exact, guard, excluded, order-independence, zero-write, and provider-neutral cases run and 0 fail
- **test-evidence:** Attach exact and guard candidate matrices and proof that projection performs zero writes.
- **self-review:** Confirm every result is advisory, provider-neutral, and incapable of authorizing automatic matching.

## Commit
- `feat(ledger): add manual reconciliation projection` — one commit per bead; footer `Refs: bd-1zm`

title: Prove the coherent BUDGET evidence prerequisite
description:
## Objective
A focused contract suite proves budget.insights.evidence.get is coherent, complete, bounded, versioned, and mutation-free for BoundRevision, NoBudgetPlan, and NoActiveBudgetPlanRevision.

## Implements
- None; this task is a validation or shared-contract task with no direct FR ownership.

## Depends On
- No INSIGHTS bead; this ready gate proves the external BUDGET and LEDGER prerequisites before dependent INSIGHTS work can proceed.

## In Scope
- Integration prerequisite with no INSIGHTS Implements ownership; prove the released BUDGET composite provides all three plan states from one public LEDGER snapshot before any INSIGHTS client is allowed to consume it.
- Produce: VerifiedBudgetInsightEvidenceContract
- Produce: Three-state correction-race evidence

## Out of Scope
- Failure blocks INSIGHTS and returns upstream to BUDGET; it is not repaired with private access or a second read.

## Success Criteria
- OperationRegistry exposes exactly one read-only budget.insights.evidence.get descriptor with a versioned bounded request and the three-state BudgetInsightEvidence result; no BUDGET mutation authority is reachable.
- BoundRevision returns one immutable plan revision, canonical BudgetPosition, calculation schema, complete dated members, exact ledgerBudgetActualTotal, shared snapshot identity, generation fingerprint, producer versions, and one binding fingerprint; every member reconciles exactly once to the returned position.
- NoBudgetPlan and NoActiveBudgetPlanRevision return no plan detail, BudgetPosition, or calculation schema, preserve every dated member exactly once, reconcile their checked sum to ledgerBudgetActualTotal, and prove BudgetPositionCalculator was not invoked.
- Forced corrections before and after producer capture return wholly pre-correction or wholly post-correction evidence; an unprovable binding, missing or duplicate ordinal, stale generation, incompatibility, or one-over-limit request returns a stable failure with no partial financial result.
- The test compiles using only public BUDGET and LEDGER contracts plus OperationRegistry; private database, SQL, feature-handler, repository, and domain references are inaccessible.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT replace the composite with independent budget.position.get and ledger.actuals.query calls — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-PUBLIC-EVIDENCE.
- ❌ Do NOT hand an expiring LEDGER cursor through BUDGET — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-PUBLIC-EVIDENCE.
- ❌ Do NOT open budget.db or ledger.db, call private handlers, reproduce BudgetPositionCalculator, fabricate a zero plan, or accept a partial member set.
- ❌ Do NOT mark the external dependency validated unless the real published contract and correction-race cases execute successfully.

## Approach
Integration prerequisite with no INSIGHTS Implements ownership; prove the released BUDGET composite provides all three plan states from one public LEDGER snapshot before any INSIGHTS client is allowed to consume it.
- Constraint: This task is an intentional loose execution root; its external blocked-by refs must complete operationally before its tests can pass.
- Expected output: VerifiedBudgetInsightEvidenceContract
- Expected output: Three-state correction-race evidence

## Interfaces
- Produces: VerifiedBudgetInsightEvidenceContract (DM-BUDGET-INSIGHTS-READ-CONTRACT) — proof that the released registry and public three-state composite satisfy the prerequisite

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Cli/OperationRegistry.cs` (read) — released operation registry
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Budget/Insights/BudgetInsightEvidence.cs` (read) — public coherent evidence contract
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/BudgetContract/BudgetInsightsPrerequisiteTests.cs` (test) — three-state race limit and authority prerequisite

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~BudgetInsightsPrerequisiteTests` — exit 0; at least 16 descriptor, three-plan-state, member-completeness, bound-position, absence-total, calculator-exclusion, correction-race, compatibility, limit, and mutation-exclusion cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit
- `test(insights): prove coherent budget evidence prerequisite` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-200`

## Lex references
- DD-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-PUBLIC-EVIDENCE
- DM-BUDGET-INSIGHTS-READ-CONTRACT
- EXT-INSIGHTS-BUDGET-PUBLIC-READ-CONTRACT
- EXT-INSIGHTS-LEDGER-ACTUALS-CONTRACT
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-00-FOUNDATION
- TASK-BUDGET-GATE-INT-LEDGER-CONTRACT
- TASK-BUDGET-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT
- TASK-BUDGET-INSIGHTS-READ-PROJECTION
- TASK-INSIGHTS-GATE-INT-BUDGET-EVIDENCE
- TC-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-EVIDENCE-CONCURRENT-CORRECTION
- Deep context: `lex context bd-200 --json`

title: Define INSIGHTS lifecycle and recovery contracts
description:
## Objective
Every retained-state and recovery data shape is represented by one deterministic Native-AOT-safe contract without persisting upstream truth or private evidence.

## Implements
- None; this task is a validation or shared-contract task with no direct FR ownership.

## Depends On
- None; this bead is a planned execution root.

## In Scope
- Create the closed source-generated history, recovery, idempotency, and private-receipt records used after the owner-value gate.
- Produce: Native-AOT-safe lifecycle and recovery contracts
- Produce: Lifecycle schema and privacy tests

## Out of Scope
- This task owns retained-state and recovery records only; analytical records remain in TASK-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-MODELS.

## Success Criteria
- The contract assembly exposes closed enums and immutable records for ReportSnapshot, RestatementDifference, DeletionEvent, idempotency, backup, verification, restore, status, and PolicyValidationReceipt.
- ReportSnapshot stores the canonical report payload and provenance but never dated actual members, raw merchant descriptions, canonical transactions, or producer cursors.
- PolicyValidationReceipt contains only structural identifiers, fingerprints, aggregate outcomes, canary status, tool version, and owner acceptance fields and is explicitly external to insights.db.
- Snapshot, recovery, and replay identities are opaque stable values; collections have deterministic ordering and source-generated serialization produces stable schema fingerprints.
- Content fingerprints exclude derived reportId and contentFingerprint fields and distinguish every semantic lifecycle or recovery change.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT use string constants for system-owned states or statuses; use closed enums per ADR-CORE-0004.
- ❌ Do NOT add HTTP DTOs, frontend models, EF entities, policy scripts, arbitrary formulas, or caller-authored report payloads.
- ❌ Do NOT place dated actual members, raw merchant text, producer cursors, or private evaluation rows in snapshots, recovery receipts, status, or PolicyValidationReceipt.
- ❌ Do NOT include reportId or contentFingerprint in the semantic payload that is hashed.

## Approach
Create the closed source-generated history, recovery, idempotency, and private-receipt records used after the owner-value gate.
- Constraint: Use file-scoped namespaces, nullable annotations, immutable records, and no reflection-based serialization.
- Expected output: Native-AOT-safe lifecycle and recovery contracts
- Expected output: Lifecycle schema and privacy tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: InsightHistoryContracts (DM-INSIGHTS-RETAINED-REPORT-LIFECYCLE) — immutable history and deletion models
- Produces: InsightsIdempotencyRecord (DM-INSIGHTS-IDEMPOTENCY) — module-wide replay record
- Produces: InsightsRecoveryContracts (DM-INSIGHTS-RECOVERY-CONTRACTS) — backup verification restore and status contracts
- Produces: PolicyValidationReceipt (DM-INSIGHTS-POLICY-VALIDATION-RECEIPT) — external aggregate evidence only

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Insights/History/InsightHistoryContracts.cs` (create) — snapshot restatement deletion and idempotency records
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Insights/Recovery/InsightRecoveryContracts.cs` (create) — manifest verification restore and status records
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Insights/PolicyValidation/PolicyValidationReceipt.cs` (create) — privacy-safe external release evidence
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Contract/InsightLifecycleContractModelTests.cs` (test) — closed schema identity ordering and forbidden-field tests

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~InsightLifecycleContractModelTests` — exit 0; at least 14 lifecycle schema identity canonical-order private-receipt and forbidden-payload cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(insights): define lifecycle and recovery contracts` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-21u`

## Lex references
- ADR-CORE-0004
- DD-INSIGHTS-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE
- DM-INSIGHTS-IDEMPOTENCY
- DM-INSIGHTS-POLICY-VALIDATION-RECEIPT
- DM-INSIGHTS-RECOVERY-CONTRACTS
- DM-INSIGHTS-RETAINED-REPORT-LIFECYCLE
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-00-FOUNDATION
- TASK-INSIGHTS-LIFECYCLE-CONTRACT-MODELS
- Deep context: `lex context bd-21u --json`

title: Implement the SQLite runtime and storage foundation
description:
## Objective
Provide a real-store-tested LedgerDb, schema-fragment registry, storage metadata schema, generation activation primitives, and owner-only artifact policy for complete provider-neutral Durable Ledger State.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On
- bd-3sx (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-PROCESS-CONTRACT): Storage project and package references require the solution/process scaffold.

## In Scope
- Foundation task intentionally has no Implements link: establish raw-SQLite runtime, storage/idempotency metadata, migration composition, permissions, and generation primitives; domain schema arrives through separately reviewed V001 fragments.
- Produce: LedgerDb and connection policy
- Produce: Explicit V001 schema-fragment registry and storage fragment
- Produce: Linux owner-only artifact protection
- Produce: Generation/CURRENT primitives
- Produce: Real-SQLite foundation tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT create account, category, transaction, allocation, relationship, or query-snapshot tables without their dedicated schema-fragment tasks.
- Do NOT use EF Core, PostgreSQL, SQLite REAL money, provider-neutral repositories, in-place live migration, direct DB-file rename, SQLCipher, custom cryptography, a database server, or mocked SQLite behavior.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-34k (TASK-LEDGER-ACCOUNTS)
  - bd-3j9 (TASK-LEDGER-BACKUP-VERIFY)
  - bd-34a (TASK-LEDGER-CATEGORIES)
  - bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY)

  - bd-3ja (TASK-LEDGER-DURABLE-STATE-VERIFIER)
