title: Wire and prove the complete INGEST public contract
description:
## Objective
Expose exactly eight ingest.* operations through the released registry and published Native-AOT binary while preserving exactly 38 ledger.* operations.

## Implements
- No direct FR ownership: this is an evidence, integration, security, use-case, or completion gate.

## Depends On
- bd-1of (TASK-INGEST-PREVIEW-WORKFLOW): Consumes PreviewOperationModule and qualified format/state dependencies.
- bd-37p (TASK-INGEST-REVIEW-WORKFLOW): Consumes ReviewOperationModule.
- bd-2i6 (TASK-INGEST-COMMIT-SAGA): Consumes CommitOperationModule.
- bd-3ey (TASK-INGEST-RESUME-WORKFLOW): Consumes ResumeOperationModule.
- bd-1tv (TASK-INGEST-STATUS-WORKFLOW): Consumes StatusOperationModule.
- bd-557 (TASK-INGEST-ABANDON-CLEANUP): Consumes RecoveryCleanupOperationModule.
- bd-2dg (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-LEDGER-CONTRACT): Consumes VerifiedLedgerOperationRegistry.
- bd-1m0 (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-FORMAT-ADAPTERS): Consumes StatementAdapterRegistry.

## In Scope
- Integration gate intentionally has no Implements link: it converges all six operation modules, the exact adapter registry, state/client services, and source-generated contracts into the shared process without changing any existing ledger.* operation.
- Produce: IngestExtensions.
- Produce: IngestOperationBundle.
- Produce: Eight-operation snapshot.
- Produce: Published INGEST contract tests.
- Produce: Prefix-safe shared registry inventory.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- IngestExtensions explicitly registers exactly PdfTextLayoutAStatementAdapter, PdfTextLayoutBStatementAdapter, StatementAdapterRegistry, IngestDatabase/migrator/protection, LedgerContractClient, every handler/store, and six operation modules; no reflection scan or plugin loading occurs.
- OperationRegistry contains exactly 8 unique ingest.* IDs/paths and preserves exactly 38 unique ledger.* IDs/paths; at this milestone the combined total is 46. Ledger inventory assertions are changed from global-total to ledger-prefix exactness, so no Ledger operation/schema/error/example is removed or weakened.
- Every ingest descriptor has a concrete source-generated request/result type, complete stable errors/exits, limits, versions, help/example, mutation classification, and matching handler. The registry remains the sole source for dispatch, help, schema, compatibility, and snapshots.
- tally schema list/show reports the exact eight operations and two qualified variants without reading a source or opening ingest.db; unknown operation/field/malformed input/incompatible version fails before processing.
- Published success and expected-failure invocations emit exactly one stdout business envelope, metadata-only stderr, and documented exit categories; sourcePath is accepted only inside --input - or --input @file JSON.
- Release linux-x64 self-contained Native-AOT publish succeeds offline with no trim/dynamic-code warning, network listener, daemon, child process, or missing JSON metadata.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT auto-discover services/modules, hand-maintain a second schema manifest, add aliases/generic CRUD/import/run operations, or introduce HTTP — rejected per governing decisions.
- ❌ Do NOT change the meaning or count of ledger.* operations to add INGEST; preserve 38 Ledger descriptors and scope its inventory test by prefix.
- ❌ Do NOT globally register incomplete handlers before every producer task is complete.
- ❌ Do NOT expose private store paths, SQL, PdfPig types, source content, exceptions, or financial payloads in help/errors/diagnostics.

## Approach
Integration gate intentionally has no Implements link: it converges all six operation modules, the exact adapter registry, state/client services, and source-generated contracts into the shared process without changing any existing ledger.* operation.
- Constraint: The combined registry is additive: 38 ledger.* plus 8 ingest.* at this milestone; future modules must receive their own prefix-scoped inventory.
- Note: Removal impact: replace the planned global count assertion of 38 with exact ledger-prefix count 38 plus exact ingest-prefix count 8. All 38 Ledger operations, schemas, examples, errors, and tests retain coverage; no information is lost.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: VerifiedLedgerOperationRegistry (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — Shared registry validated by the prerequisite gate
- Consumes: PreviewOperationModule (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Preview descriptor
- Consumes: ReviewOperationModule (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Inspect and approve descriptors
- Consumes: CommitOperationModule (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Commit descriptor
- Consumes: ResumeOperationModule (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Resume descriptor
- Consumes: StatusOperationModule (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Status descriptor
- Consumes: RecoveryCleanupOperationModule (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Abandon and cleanup descriptors
- Consumes: StatementAdapterRegistry (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE) — Exactly two qualified adapters
- Produces: IngestOperationBundle (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Exactly eight descriptors
- Produces: CompleteIngestPublicContract (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Published eight-operation surface
- Produces: PublishedIngestTallyFixture (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Release binary process fixture extended for INGEST

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Bootstrap/Features/IngestExtensions.cs` (create) — explicit INGEST DI/composition — required
- `src/Tally/Bootstrap/LedgerServices.cs` (modify) — call additive INGEST registration — required
- `src/Tally/Cli/OperationRegistry.cs` (modify) — compose additive INGEST descriptors — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Cli/PublicContractInventoryTests.cs` (modify) — scope exact Ledger count by prefix — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Contract/IngestPublicContractInventoryTests.cs` (test) — exact eight-operation inventory — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Process/PublishedIngestContractTests.cs` (test) — released binary contract matrix — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Cli/Snapshots/ingest-operations-v1.json` (test) — deterministic eight-operation snapshot — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~PublicContractInventoryTests|FullyQualifiedName~IngestPublicContractInventoryTests|FullyQualifiedName~PublishedIngestContractTests'` -> exit 0; at least 30 inventory/process cases run, exactly 38 ledger.* and 8 ingest.* operations are reported, and 0 tests fail
- **Test:** `dotnet publish src/Tally/Tally.csproj -c Release -r linux-x64 --self-contained true -p:PublishAot=true` -> exit 0; the native tally binary publishes with 0 warnings and schema list reports exactly 8 ingest.* operations

## Commit
- `test(ingest): prove public contract` — footer `Refs: bd-2jn`

## Lex references
- PLAN-INGEST-V1
- SP-INGEST-04-VERIFICATION
- TASK-INGEST-ABANDON-CLEANUP
- TASK-INGEST-COMMIT-SAGA
- TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-FORMAT-ADAPTERS
- TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-LEDGER-CONTRACT
- TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT
- TASK-INGEST-PREVIEW-WORKFLOW
- TASK-INGEST-RESUME-WORKFLOW
- TASK-INGEST-REVIEW-WORKFLOW
- TASK-INGEST-STATUS-WORKFLOW
- Deep context: `lex context bd-2jn --json`

4. tally schema list/show reports the exact eight operations and two qualified variants without reading a source or opening ingest.db; unknown operation/field/malformed input/incompatible version fails before processing.
5. Published success and expected-failure invocations emit exactly one stdout business envelope, metadata-only stderr, and documented exit categories; sourcePath is accepted only inside --input - or --input @file JSON.
6. Release linux-x64 self-contained Native-AOT publish succeeds offline with no trim/dynamic-code warning, network listener, daemon, child process, or missing JSON metadata.
notes: Compiled from TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT in PLAN-INGEST-V1. The executor must satisfy every required verification command and preserve all failure criteria.

title: Prove retained-state and recovery failure atomicity
description:
## Objective
One reproducible fault matrix proves restart observes exactly one complete outcome or the unchanged prior state for Retain, Restate, Delete, Backup, and Restore.

## Implements
- None; this task is a validation or shared-contract task with no direct FR ownership.

## Depends On
- bd-6eq (TASK-INSIGHTS-RETAIN-REPORT): The gate injects Retain failures.
- bd-3t2 (TASK-INSIGHTS-RESTATE-REPORT): The gate injects Restatement failures.
- bd-34e (TASK-INSIGHTS-DELETE-REPORT): The gate injects Delete failures.
- bd-2bm (TASK-INSIGHTS-BACKUP-VERIFY): The gate injects Backup and Verify failures.
- bd-1i6 (TASK-INSIGHTS-RESTORE-ACTIVATE): The gate injects candidate and activation failures.

## In Scope
- Cross-task convergence gate with no Implements ownership; inject cancellation, crash, storage, artifact, migration, and activation faults across every retained mutation and recovery boundary.
- Produce: InsightsAtomicRecoveryEvidence
- Produce: Reproducible failure-injection report

## Out of Scope
- This gate is intentionally loose and converges behavior already implemented by feature tasks.

## Success Criteria
- The matrix injects faults before and after snapshot, payload, Restatement link, DeletionEvent, replay reservation/outcome, backup candidate, current-reservation removal, artifact publication, terminal receipt, restore copy, migration, candidate outcome, verification, expected-current check, pointer replacement, and directory fsync boundaries.
- After each Retain, Restate, or Delete fault and restart, the store contains either the complete logical outcome exactly once or the unchanged pre-operation state with no partial payload, dangling link, missing event, duplicate identity, or consumed failed key.
- After each Backup fault, exact replay either resumes or reconciles the deterministic artifact and leaves at most one published verified artifact and one terminal outcome; completed failure leaves no stale reservation.
- After each Restore fault, startup observes the complete prior or complete verified candidate generation, never a mixed store, and the prior generation remains recoverable.
- The report records only fault labels, safe fingerprints, row/count deltas, exit states, and test totals and contains no financial payload, private path, or report body.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT accept aggregate test totals without nonzero discovery for every mutation and recovery fault partition.
- ❌ Do NOT repair failed state in the test, skip restart inspection, ignore WAL/pointer/fsync boundaries, weaken exact replay, or suppress a mixed/partial outcome.
- ❌ Do NOT print fixture payloads, report bodies, private paths, or raw database rows in fault evidence.

## Approach
Cross-task convergence gate with no Implements ownership; inject cancellation, crash, storage, artifact, migration, and activation faults across every retained mutation and recovery boundary.
- Constraint: Tests use real temporary SQLite, real file operations, and process-restart-equivalent store reopening; SQL and filesystem authority switches are not mocked.
- Expected output: InsightsAtomicRecoveryEvidence
- Expected output: Reproducible failure-injection report

## Interfaces
- Consumes: RetainInsightReportCommand.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — retained-generation fault surface
- Consumes: RestateInsightReportCommand.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Restatement fault surface
- Consumes: DeleteInsightReportCommand.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — deletion fault surface
- Consumes: CreateInsightsBackupCommand.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-RECOVERY-CONTRACTS) — backup saga fault surface
- Consumes: RestoreInsightsBackupCommand.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-RECOVERY-CONTRACTS) — restore activation fault surface
- Produces: InsightsAtomicRecoveryEvidence (NFR-INSIGHTS-VERIFIED-RECOVERABILITY) — complete fault-matrix proof

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Recovery/InsightsAtomicRecoveryFaultMatrixTests.cs` (test) — cross-operation injected fault and restart matrix
- `scripts/verify-insights-recovery.sh` (create) — non-vacuous recovery suite and evidence runner
- `docs/verification/insights-recovery.md` (document) — safe fault-boundary and result report

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `bash scripts/verify-insights-recovery.sh` — exit 0; every named Retain, Restate, Delete, Backup, and Restore partition discovers nonzero cases, at least 40 injected fault/restart cases execute, every outcome is complete-old or complete-new exactly once, evidence is payload-free, and 0 checks fail

## Commit
- `test(insights): prove retained-state and recovery failure atomicity` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-2ls`

- TC-INSIGHTS-RESTORE-FAILURE-ATOMICITY

title: TransactionWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove one Canonical Transaction plus generic initial evidence is atomic, privacy-safe, replay-safe, and returned with explicit unknown/unassigned dimensions and Reconciliation State.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-WRITES: Make public writes idempotent
  - AC: Given any previously successful public mutation, when the same Idempotency Identity, operation, contract version, and semantically identical request are submitted again, then the original structured result is returned and no second domain, decision, link, or artifact effect occurs.
  - AC: Given a previously successful Idempotency Identity, when it is reused for another operation, contract version, or materially different request, then the same module-scoped conflict code is returned on every retry and the original effect is preserved.
  - AC: Given an invalid mutation request, when validation fails, then no domain or artifact effect occurs and the supplied Idempotency Identity remains available for a corrected request.
  - AC: Given evidence registration or linking, reconciliation application or correction, payment attribution, Pool Assignment, or pool maintenance, when its logical identity is replayed through another request identity, then the domain uniqueness rule prevents a duplicate effect independently of request replay.
  - AC: Given any public mutation without a valid caller-visible Idempotency Identity, when invocation is attempted, then the Ledger rejects it before mutation with a stable validation error.
- FR-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-RECORDING: Record Canonical Transactions
  - AC: Given an active account, valid original description, exact base-10 signed amount, ZAR currency, transaction date, privacy-safe initial evidence, and Idempotency Identity, when recording succeeds, then one stable Canonical Transaction and one linked Evidence Record are created atomically without precision loss.
  - AC: Given a transaction with a distinct supplied posting date, when it is recorded, then both transaction date and posting date round-trip without substituting one for the other.
  - AC: Given any missing required fact, unsupported currency, zero amount, malformed date, archived account, unsupported Evidence Kind, or forbidden provider payload, when recording is attempted, then a stable validation error is returned and neither transaction nor evidence is created.
  - AC: Given a recorded transaction, when it is read later, then its account identity, original description, signed amount, currency, supplied dates, stable identifier, evidence links, current Reconciliation State, and explicit unknown attribution states round-trip without silently inferring category, pool, instrument, or cardholder.
  - AC: Given a movement on an active owned asset or liability account, when its signed amount is recorded, then a positive value means the owner's economic position improved and a negative value means it worsened: asset increases and liability reductions are positive; asset decreases and liability increases are negative.
  - AC: Given the same logical initial evidence and semantically identical Canonical Transaction facts are submitted through another request identity, when recording is attempted, then the original transaction and evidence result is returned and no duplicate effect is created.
  - AC: Given the same logical initial evidence is submitted with materially different Canonical Transaction facts, when recording is attempted, then a stable conflict is returned and the existing transaction and evidence remain unchanged.
  - AC: Given a Canonical Transaction created through the ordinary recording operation, when its initial state is returned, then its Reconciliation State is recorded-but-unreconciled until a valid deterministic or owner-approved Reconciliation Decision changes it.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace revised UC-LEDGER-002 through the Release published CLI and real SQLite state.
- Produce: UC002TransactionWorkflowTests with main/failure paths

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT use transport provenance, a single sourceReference authority, provider payloads, or inferred payment/pool/category state in the fixture.
- Do NOT accept aggregate-only atomicity evidence; inspect every durable row class.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- A discovered record schema accepts exact signed ZAR/date facts, generic evidence allowlist fields, optional stable payment IDs, and Idempotency Identity for an active account; get round-trips facts, evidence link, current Reconciliation State, payment attribution, and pool exactly.
- Provider/raw payload, full identifier, invalid fact/evidence/attribution/key, and missing/archived account failures create no transaction, evidence, link, assignment, or replay row.
- Identical request or cross-key logical evidence replay returns the existing result; conflict preserves the original; crash injection proves transaction, evidence link, unknown/unassigned defaults, and replay outcome commit together or none.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT use transport provenance, a single sourceReference authority, provider payloads, or inferred payment/pool/category state in the fixture.
- ❌ Do NOT accept aggregate-only atomicity evidence; inspect every durable row class.

## Approach
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace revised UC-LEDGER-002 through the Release published CLI and real SQLite state.
- Constraint: Invoke the Release published tally executable; inspect stdout, stderr, exit code, and real temporary SQLite state.
- Expected output: UC002TransactionWorkflowTests with main/failure paths

## Interfaces
- Consumes: PublishedTallyFixture (Release published-process E2E fixture)
- Consumes: CompletePublicContract (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — Exactly 72 provider-neutral operations
- Produces: VerifiedUC002 (UC-LEDGER-002) — canonical transaction and initial evidence workflow

## Files (reservation globs)

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.EndToEnd.UC002TransactionWorkflowTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 9 main/failure-path scenarios run and 0 fail

## Commit

title: Implement transactional mutation idempotency
description:
## Objective
Guarantee identical request and cross-key logical replay return the original effect while changed replay, invalid input, busy failure, and crash boundaries preserve exactly one effect.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-WRITES: Make public writes idempotent
  - AC: Given any previously successful public mutation, when the same Idempotency Identity, operation, contract version, and semantically identical request are submitted again, then the original structured result is returned and no second domain, decision, link, or artifact effect occurs.
  - AC: Given a previously successful Idempotency Identity, when it is reused for another operation, contract version, or materially different request, then the same module-scoped conflict code is returned on every retry and the original effect is preserved.
  - AC: Given an invalid mutation request, when validation fails, then no domain or artifact effect occurs and the supplied Idempotency Identity remains available for a corrected request.
  - AC: Given evidence registration or linking, reconciliation application or correction, payment attribution, Pool Assignment, or pool maintenance, when its logical identity is replayed through another request identity, then the domain uniqueness rule prevents a duplicate effect independently of request replay.
  - AC: Given any public mutation without a valid caller-visible Idempotency Identity, when invocation is attempted, then the Ledger rejects it before mutation with a stable validation error.

## Depends On
- bd-23m (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-STORAGE): IdempotencyStore requires the initialized SQLite schema and LedgerDb.
- bd-3sx (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-PROCESS-CONTRACT): Mutation executor consumes CommandResult and process contract primitives.

## In Scope
- Build the common mutation executor, canonical request hashing, stored outcomes, logical-effect identity, and artifact reconciliation for every public write.
- Produce: LedgerMutationExecutor and IdempotencyStore
- Produce: CanonicalRequestHasher
- Produce: ArtifactReconciler
- Produce: Crash/replay tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT scope evidence or reconciliation idempotency only to a caller-provided key.
- Do NOT consume identities on validation/busy failure or persist a partial logical effect.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-34k (TASK-LEDGER-ACCOUNTS)
  - bd-1ie (TASK-LEDGER-AGENT-SKILLS)
  - bd-3j9 (TASK-LEDGER-BACKUP-VERIFY)
  - bd-34a (TASK-LEDGER-CATEGORIES)
  - bd-3q1 (TASK-LEDGER-CATEGORY-ALLOCATIONS)
  - bd-fan (TASK-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-LINKING)
  - bd-3qf (TASK-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-REGISTRY)
  - bd-2zt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE)
  - bd-3nk (TASK-LEDGER-PAYMENT-ATTRIBUTION)
  - bd-3od (TASK-LEDGER-PAYMENT-IDENTITIES)
  - bd-2bd (TASK-LEDGER-POOL-ASSIGNMENTS)
  - bd-37y (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-APPLY)
  - bd-2ae (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-COVERAGE)
  - bd-3q4 (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-DECISIONS)
  - bd-3ts (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-STATEMENT-CORRECTION)
  - bd-10w (TASK-LEDGER-REFUNDS)
  - bd-2sb (TASK-LEDGER-RELATIONSHIP-CORRECTIONS)
  - bd-3vc (TASK-LEDGER-SPEND-POOLS)
  - bd-2rh (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CORRECTIONS)
  - bd-84s (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTIONS-RECORD-GET)
  - bd-xq6 (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSFERS)

## Success Criteria
- Request identity is contract version + operation ID + caller key; hash includes normalized input and actor.
- Identical request replay returns the stored result/entity/artifact with no volatile replay field and no second effect; changed operation/version/actor/payload returns LEDGER-IDEMPOTENCY-001 and preserves the first outcome.
- Evidence registration and Reconciliation Decisions also claim closed logical-effect identities, so identical evidence or decision submitted under another caller key returns the existing outcome and conflicting semantic input fails closed.
- Validation and busy/resource failures occur before key or logical identity consumption; domain changes, histories, links, stored result, request identity, and logical identity commit in one transaction.
- Artifact publication uses deterministic identity, private staging, checksum, flush, atomic publication, and retry reconciliation.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT scope evidence or reconciliation idempotency only to a caller-provided key.
- ❌ Do NOT consume identities on validation/busy failure or persist a partial logical effect.

## Approach
- Build the common mutation executor, canonical request hashing, stored outcomes, logical-effect identity, and artifact reconciliation for every public write.
- Expected output: LedgerMutationExecutor and IdempotencyStore
- Expected output: CanonicalRequestHasher
- Expected output: ArtifactReconciler
- Expected output: Crash/replay tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync (DM-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENCY-RECORD)
- Produces: IdempotencyStore (DM-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENCY-RECORD)
- Produces: CanonicalRequestHasher
- Produces: ArtifactReconciler (DD-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-MUTATIONS)
- Consumes: LedgerDb (DM-LEDGER-STORE-GENERATION)
- Consumes: CommandResult<TResult> (ADR-CORE-0010)

## Verification

- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~LedgerMutationExecutorTests|FullyQualifiedName~ArtifactReconcilerTests' --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 16 replay/conflict/crash cases run and 0 fail

title: Implement module-wide retained-operation replay
description:
## Objective
InsightsIdempotencyExecutor returns prior logical outcomes for exact retries, rejects changed input, and never consumes failed request identities or leaks deleted content.

## Implements
- None; this task is a validation or shared-contract task with no direct FR ownership.

## Depends On
- bd-7kn (TASK-INSIGHTS-STATE-FOUNDATION): The replay executor uses the idempotency table and caller-owned SQLite transactions.

## In Scope
- Create the transactional Attempting and Succeeded replay coordinator consumed by Retain, Restate, Delete, Backup, and Restore.
- Produce: InsightsIdempotencyExecutor
- Produce: Replay conflict and crash-state tests

## Out of Scope
- Recovery handlers use the same identity model but own their external artifact saga.

## Success Criteria
- The unique replay identity is operationId plus contractVersion plus keyDigest; the canonical request fingerprint includes every logical input, actor, reason, target or predecessor, selected policies, and expected-current fingerprint; Backup and Restore additionally include safe normalized path identity.
- A Succeeded record with the same key and fingerprint returns the exact prior logical outcome before TimeProvider, producer, or write access; the same key with a changed fingerprint returns IdempotencyConflict and changes nothing.
- New write attempts are reserved transactionally; success stores one immutable outcome reference; validation, cancellation, transient, generation, and persistence failure leave no successful outcome and remove any completed-failure reservation.
- If a retained snapshot outcome was later deleted, replay resolves to metadata-only Deleted evidence and never recreates or discloses the removed payload.
- Backup-specific Attempting reconciliation can retain deterministic artifact identity across a crash while the copied artifact excludes the current non-terminal reservation.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT store full requests, reports, private paths, or caller idempotency keys; persist digests, fingerprints, safe identities, and outcome refs only.
- ❌ Do NOT treat expected failures as exceptions, consume a failed request identity, create a second logical outcome, or replay deleted content.
- ❌ Do NOT implement a generic event-sourcing system — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-RETENTION-RESTATEMENT-LIFECYCLE.

## Approach
Create the transactional Attempting and Succeeded replay coordinator consumed by Retain, Restate, Delete, Backup, and Restore.
- Constraint: The executor participates in the caller-owned SQLite transaction and has one production implementation.
- Expected output: InsightsIdempotencyExecutor
- Expected output: Replay conflict and crash-state tests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: InsightsStateStore (DM-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE) — transaction and replay rows
- Produces: InsightsIdempotencyExecutor.ExecuteAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-IDEMPOTENCY) — new, replay, conflict, or failed reservation result

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Insights/Storage/Idempotency/InsightsIdempotencyExecutor.cs` (create) — transactional replay coordinator
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Storage/InsightsIdempotencyExecutorTests.cs` (test) — same, conflict, failure, deleted, and recovery reservation tests

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~InsightsIdempotencyExecutorTests` — exit 0; at least 18 exact-replay, changed-input conflict, validation failure, cancellation, rollback, crash reservation, deleted-outcome, path-digest, payload-exclusion, and concurrent-key cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit

title: Validate relationship spend policy
description:
## Objective
Lock the owner-approved transfer, refund, reversal, and fee rules without deciding cash-withdrawal treatment.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-REFUND-CONFIRMATION: Confirm refunds and reversals
  - AC: Given active same-account ZAR transactions with opposite signs, when the owner explicitly confirms one transaction as a refund or reversal of the other with a reason, then one active attributable refund relationship is created without inferring the relationship from description or date alone.
  - AC: Given an active refund relationship, when pool and category actuals are queried, then the refund offsets the original transaction's current active pool and category in the refund's own Effective Date period, including explicit unassigned buckets.
  - AC: Given one or more active partial refunds, when another refund is proposed, then confirmation succeeds only if cumulative active refunded magnitude does not exceed the original transaction magnitude.
  - AC: Given same-sign, different-account, different-currency, inactive, over-refund, transfer-linked, refund-credit-as-original, or refund-credit-reuse participants, when confirmation is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and creates no relationship.
  - AC: Given a valid refund credit, when it is confirmed, then its entire magnitude participates in exactly one active refund relationship to one original; an original may have multiple active refund credits only while their cumulative magnitude remains at most the original magnitude.
- FR-LEDGER-TRANSFER-CONFIRMATION: Confirm owned-account transfers
  - AC: Given two active transactions on distinct active owned accounts with ZAR equal-and-opposite economic movements, when the owner confirms them as a transfer with a reason, then one active attributable transfer relationship is created.
  - AC: Given an active confirmed transfer, when default External Spend actuals are queried, then the paired principal contributes exactly zero while both transaction legs remain queryable.
  - AC: Given qualifying legs with different supplied dates, when the owner explicitly confirms the relationship, then the date difference alone does not prevent confirmation.
  - AC: Given same-account legs, same-sign legs, unequal principal magnitudes, different currencies, inactive legs, or either leg already participating in any active transfer or refund relationship, when transfer confirmation is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and creates no relationship.
  - AC: Given a bank fee recorded separately from the equal-and-opposite principal legs, when the transfer is confirmed, then the fee remains an unlinked transaction and retains its own spend treatment.
  - AC: Given a confirmed transfer whose legs have category or Spend Pool Assignments, when pool, category, or pool-by-category actuals are queried, then principal contributes exactly zero to every bucket while attribution remains queryable.

## Depends On
- None — this is a compile root.

## In Scope
- Validation-only task with no Implements link: verify the direct owner decision recorded for OQ-LEDGER-5 before relationship and actuals implementation.
- Produce: Resolved relationship policy evidence and OQ-LEDGER-5Resolution

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT treat owned-account transfer principal as spend or remove either bank transaction — per the resolved OQ-LEDGER-5 policy.
- Do NOT infer transfer or refund relationships from description, provider wording, or date alone — per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- Do NOT decide cash-withdrawal treatment in this task; OQ-LEDGER-16 owns that separate gate.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-xq6 (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSFERS)

## Success Criteria
- OQ-LEDGER-5 is resolved from direct owner instruction: principal moved between owned accounts contributes zero External Spend and Budget Actual while both account transactions remain queryable.
- Linked refunds and reversals offset the original transaction's current category and Spend Pool in the refund Effective Date period; a separately recorded bank fee remains ordinary spend.
- The redacted validation report names the approved rules, excludes raw financial or transport payloads, and routes cash-withdrawal treatment exclusively to OQ-LEDGER-16.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT treat owned-account transfer principal as spend or remove either bank transaction — per the resolved OQ-LEDGER-5 policy.
- ❌ Do NOT infer transfer or refund relationships from description, provider wording, or date alone — per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- ❌ Do NOT decide cash-withdrawal treatment in this task; OQ-LEDGER-16 owns that separate gate.

## Approach
- Validation-only task with no Implements link: verify the direct owner decision recorded for OQ-LEDGER-5 before relationship and actuals implementation.
- Constraint: Only privacy-safe structural conclusions are committed; no raw email or statement content.
- Expected output: Resolved relationship policy evidence and OQ-LEDGER-5Resolution

## Interfaces
- Produces: OQ-LEDGER-5Resolution (OQ-LEDGER-5) — Owner-approved transfer, refund, reversal, and fee policy

## Files (reservation globs)
- `docs/validation/ledger/oq-5-relationship-policy.md` (read) — owner decision evidence — required — Verify the resolved structural policy
- `.lexicon/graph/LEDGER/open-question/OQ-LEDGER-5.json` (read) — canonical gate state — required — Read through lex open-question show

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `test -s docs/validation/ledger/oq-5-relationship-policy.md && lex open-question show OQ-LEDGER-5 --json` — exit 0; the report exists and command output shows a non-empty resolved decision with zero-spend owned-account transfers

title: TransferWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove UC-LEDGER-004 through observable process results and durable-state invariants.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-WRITES: Make public writes idempotent
  - AC: Given any previously successful public mutation, when the same Idempotency Identity, operation, contract version, and semantically identical request are submitted again, then the original structured result is returned and no second domain, decision, link, or artifact effect occurs.
  - AC: Given a previously successful Idempotency Identity, when it is reused for another operation, contract version, or materially different request, then the same module-scoped conflict code is returned on every retry and the original effect is preserved.
  - AC: Given an invalid mutation request, when validation fails, then no domain or artifact effect occurs and the supplied Idempotency Identity remains available for a corrected request.
  - AC: Given evidence registration or linking, reconciliation application or correction, payment attribution, Pool Assignment, or pool maintenance, when its logical identity is replayed through another request identity, then the domain uniqueness rule prevents a duplicate effect independently of request replay.
  - AC: Given any public mutation without a valid caller-visible Idempotency Identity, when invocation is attempted, then the Ledger rejects it before mutation with a stable validation error.
- FR-LEDGER-TRANSFER-CONFIRMATION: Confirm owned-account transfers
  - AC: Given two active transactions on distinct active owned accounts with ZAR equal-and-opposite economic movements, when the owner confirms them as a transfer with a reason, then one active attributable transfer relationship is created.
  - AC: Given an active confirmed transfer, when default External Spend actuals are queried, then the paired principal contributes exactly zero while both transaction legs remain queryable.
  - AC: Given qualifying legs with different supplied dates, when the owner explicitly confirms the relationship, then the date difference alone does not prevent confirmation.
  - AC: Given same-account legs, same-sign legs, unequal principal magnitudes, different currencies, inactive legs, or either leg already participating in any active transfer or refund relationship, when transfer confirmation is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and creates no relationship.
  - AC: Given a bank fee recorded separately from the equal-and-opposite principal legs, when the transfer is confirmed, then the fee remains an unlinked transaction and retains its own spend treatment.
  - AC: Given a confirmed transfer whose legs have category or Spend Pool Assignments, when pool, category, or pool-by-category actuals are queried, then principal contributes exactly zero to every bucket while attribution remains queryable.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task intentionally has no Implements link: trace UC-LEDGER-004 main scenario and every failure path through the published CLI.
- Produce: UC004TransferWorkflowTests with main/failure paths

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT infer transfer from dates/descriptions or force an embedded fee pair.
- Do NOT omit any UC-LEDGER-004 role/conservation failure.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- Two distinct-account equal-opposite active legs with explicit roles create one transfer; both remain queryable and principal contributes zero External Spend.
- Different leg dates are accepted; a separate fee remains unlinked with ordinary spend treatment.
- Same account/currency mismatch/same sign/unequal magnitude/inactive/missing/already-related/embedded-fee cases return stable no-relationship outcomes.
- Injected write failure leaves neither leg partially related and replay returns one relationship.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT infer transfer from dates/descriptions or force an embedded fee pair.
- ❌ Do NOT omit any UC-LEDGER-004 role/conservation failure.

## Approach
- Verification-only task intentionally has no Implements link: trace UC-LEDGER-004 main scenario and every failure path through the published CLI.
- Constraint: Invoke the Release published tally executable; inspect stdout, stderr, exit code, and real temporary SQLite state.

title: Implement the V001 transaction schema fragment
description:
## Objective
Create exact immutable transaction and correction-history tables plus independent current/historical category, payment, and pool dimensions without implementing handlers.

## Implements

## Depends On

## In Scope

- Produce: V001TransactionSchema
- Produce: LedgerTransactionSchemaTests real-SQLite constraint tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT infer payment attribution or pool from account, category, description, evidence provider, or one another.
- Do NOT place evidence or reconciliation records in this fragment.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-195 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-RECONCILIATION-AUTHORITY)
  - bd-2zt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE)

## Success Criteria
- V001TransactionSchema creates every linked fact, lifecycle, category allocation, instrument/Cardholder Attribution, and Pool Assignment field with enum/date/money checks, RESTRICT foreign keys, terminal lifecycle rules, and one-current-record constraints.
- signedAmountMinor is nonzero Int64, currency is literal ZAR, facts are insert-only by contract, initial payment attribution is explicitly unknown, and initial Pool Assignment is explicitly unassigned.
- Source identity is not embedded as transport provenance or a single sourceReference; generic evidence and reconciliation schema is owned by V001EvidenceReconciliationSchema.
- Applying the fragment twice in one migration plan is rejected before SQL; an injected statement failure rolls back every transaction, lifecycle, and dimensional-history table/index.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT infer payment attribution or pool from account, category, description, evidence provider, or one another.
- ❌ Do NOT place evidence or reconciliation records in this fragment.

## Approach
- Schema foundation intentionally has no Implements link: translate immutable transaction facts, lifecycle events, category-allocation, payment-attribution, and Spend Pool Assignment history into one independently verified V001 fragment.
- Constraint: Implement an explicit V001 fragment only; TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE owns complete-schema composition.

title: Implement transaction void and supersession
description:
## Objective
Count only the valid active transaction outcome while keeping ordinary correction and statement-authoritative composition semantically distinct.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CORRECTION: Void or supersede erroneous transactions
  - AC: Given an active transaction and a reason, when the owner voids it, then it becomes inactive, the void event is attributable, active financial relationships retire atomically, and default actuals exclude it.
  - AC: Given an active transaction, a reason, and valid replacement facts, when the owner supersedes it, then one distinct replacement is created, the original becomes inactive, and default actuals include only explicitly established active replacement state.
  - AC: Given a corrected transaction with evidence, Reconciliation Decisions, category or Pool Assignments, or payment attribution, when void or supersession is applied, then those records remain attached to their historical subject and none silently moves to the replacement.
  - AC: Given reconciliation was affected by void or supersession, when current state is queried, then one Reconciliation Exception outcome is returned until a valid deterministic or owner-approved decision is recorded.
  - AC: Given a voided or superseded transaction, when history-inclusive detail is requested, then original facts, evidence and decision history, assignments, actor context, reason, time, and retired relationships remain queryable, and its replacement reference is returned for supersession or explicit null for void.
  - AC: Given a statement-authoritative reconciliation correction uses supersession, when it commits, then category, Spend Pool, Payment Instrument, and cardholder state may appear on the replacement only through explicit carry-forward events referencing the Reconciliation Decision; the historical state remains attached to the prior transaction and no evidence or relationship moves by overwrite.

## Depends On
- bd-xq6 (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSFERS): Transaction correction consumes RelationshipStore.RetireForTransactionAsync.
- bd-10w (TASK-LEDGER-REFUNDS): Transaction correction must retire both transfer and refund roles.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Consumer requires LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.
- bd-84s (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTIONS-RECORD-GET): Consumer requires TransactionBaseOperationModule from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.
- bd-3q4 (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-DECISIONS): Corrections consume ReconciliationStateReducer to retain decisions and derive current exceptions.

## In Scope
- Deliver ordinary attributable void/supersede behavior and a reconciliation-only supersession primitive while preserving source facts and history.
- Produce: Transaction lifecycle contracts/domain/handlers
- Produce: Transaction store/module correction support
- Produce: Atomic correction tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT move or silently reconfirm evidence, decisions, category, pool, payment attribution, or relationships onto an ordinary replacement.
- Do NOT expose AppendStatementSupersessionAsync publicly or let it commit outside StatementAuthoritativeCorrectionCoordinator — per DD-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT.
- Do NOT overwrite original transaction columns or delete historical decisions.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-2ve (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CATALOGUE-TRANSACTION-BUNDLE)
  - bd-3pa (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-STATEMENT-CORRECTION-EFFECT-WRITER)

## Success Criteria
- Ordinary void appends an attributable lifecycle event, retires active transfer/refund relationships atomically, preserves evidence/decision/dimension history, and exposes affected confirmations as current Reconciliation Exceptions.
- Ordinary supersede creates one independent replacement with only explicitly supplied new evidence or attribution; it never copies category, pool, payment attribution, relationships, or confirmation.
- AppendStatementSupersessionAsync is internal and may run only inside an authorized reconciliation writer transaction; it creates the typed statement-derived replacement and supersession while leaving all carry-forward and relationship decisions to the coordinator.
- Any replacement validation, relationship retirement, Reconciliation Exception, or write failure leaves the original state unchanged; history returns complete original/replacement facts and retained associations.
- Identical correction replay returns the original outcome; an incompatible later correction returns stable lifecycle/conflict.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT move or silently reconfirm evidence, decisions, category, pool, payment attribution, or relationships onto an ordinary replacement.
- ❌ Do NOT expose AppendStatementSupersessionAsync publicly or let it commit outside StatementAuthoritativeCorrectionCoordinator — per DD-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT.
- ❌ Do NOT overwrite original transaction columns or delete historical decisions.

## Approach
- Deliver ordinary attributable void/supersede behavior and a reconciliation-only supersession primitive while preserving source facts and history.
- Expected output: Transaction lifecycle contracts/domain/handlers
- Expected output: Transaction store/module correction support
- Expected output: Atomic correction tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: TransactionOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CONTRACTS) — Public void and supersede descriptors
- Consumes: TransactionBaseOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CONTRACTS) — Record/get composition
- Produces: TransactionStore.VoidAsync (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-HISTORY) — Atomic ordinary void
- Produces: TransactionStore.SupersedeAsync (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-HISTORY) — Independent ordinary replacement
- Produces: TransactionStore.AppendStatementSupersessionAsync (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-HISTORY) — Internal statement-derived replacement primitive
- Consumes: RelationshipStore.RetireForTransactionAsync (DM-LEDGER-FINANCIAL-RELATIONSHIP) — Retire active roles for ordinary correction
- Consumes: ReconciliationStateReducer (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY) — Preserve history and expose current exception
- Consumes: LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync (DM-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENCY-RECORD) — Atomic replay boundary

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ledger/Transactions/TransactionContracts.cs` (modify) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ledger/Transactions/TransactionLifecycle.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Transactions/TransactionCorrectionHandlers.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Storage/Transactions/TransactionStore.cs` (modify) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Transactions/TransactionOperationModule.cs` (modify) — implementation — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Features/Ledger/Transactions/TransactionCorrectionTests.cs` (test) — implementation — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.Features.Ledger.Transactions.TransactionCorrectionTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 14 void/supersede/retirement/rollback/history/replay cases run and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(ledger): implement transaction void and supersession` — one commit per bead; footer `Refs: bd-2rh`

## Lex references
- DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY
- DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CONTRACTS
- FR-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CORRECTION
- PLAN-LEDGER-V1
- SP-LEDGER-03-RELATIONSHIPS-ACTUALS

- TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTIONS-RECORD-GET
- TASK-LEDGER-TRANSFERS
- TC-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CORRECTION-CONTRACT

title: RelationshipCorrectionWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove UC-LEDGER-011 through observable process results and durable-state invariants.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-WRITES: Make public writes idempotent
  - AC: Given any previously successful public mutation, when the same Idempotency Identity, operation, contract version, and semantically identical request are submitted again, then the original structured result is returned and no second domain, decision, link, or artifact effect occurs.
  - AC: Given a previously successful Idempotency Identity, when it is reused for another operation, contract version, or materially different request, then the same module-scoped conflict code is returned on every retry and the original effect is preserved.
  - AC: Given an invalid mutation request, when validation fails, then no domain or artifact effect occurs and the supplied Idempotency Identity remains available for a corrected request.
  - AC: Given evidence registration or linking, reconciliation application or correction, payment attribution, Pool Assignment, or pool maintenance, when its logical identity is replayed through another request identity, then the domain uniqueness rule prevents a duplicate effect independently of request replay.
  - AC: Given any public mutation without a valid caller-visible Idempotency Identity, when invocation is attempted, then the Ledger rejects it before mutation with a stable validation error.
- FR-LEDGER-RELATIONSHIP-CORRECTION: Revoke or replace financial relationships
  - AC: Given an active transfer or refund relationship and a reason, when the owner revokes it, then it becomes retired, the event is attributable, and active actuals are recalculated atomically.
  - AC: Given an active relationship and proposed replacement participants, when the owner replaces it with a reason, then role exclusivity, cardinality, amount conservation, account, currency, sign, and lifecycle rules are revalidated before the prior relationship is retired and exactly one valid replacement is activated atomically.
  - AC: Given an inactive relationship, when it is revoked again, then the Ledger returns a stable lifecycle error and changes no actuals.
  - AC: Given any retired relationship, when history-inclusive detail is requested, then its participants, type, lifecycle reason, actor context, and time remain queryable, and its replacement reference is returned when the retirement event records one or explicit null otherwise.
  - AC: Given a transfer or refund relationship is revoked or replaced, when pool, category, and pool-by-category actuals are queried, then the retired effect is removed and only the valid active replacement effect is applied atomically and exactly.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task intentionally has no Implements link: trace UC-LEDGER-011 main scenario and every failure path through the published CLI.
- Produce: UC011RelationshipCorrectionWorkflowTests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT edit old relationships in place or retire before validating replacement.
- Do NOT omit UC-LEDGER-011 aggregate fail-closed and atomic rollback paths.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- Revoke removes active spend effect and appends actor/reason; replace validates all rules before atomically retiring old and activating one new relationship.
- History exposes full chain and before/after spend effects reconcile exactly.
- Absent reason/key, retired relationship, invalid replacement, retirement/replacement interruption, and aggregate mismatch preserve prior active state.
- Identical replay returns original lifecycle result; changed retry conflicts.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT edit old relationships in place or retire before validating replacement.
- ❌ Do NOT omit UC-LEDGER-011 aggregate fail-closed and atomic rollback paths.

## Approach
- Verification-only task intentionally has no Implements link: trace UC-LEDGER-011 main scenario and every failure path through the published CLI.
- Constraint: Invoke the Release published tally executable; inspect stdout, stderr, exit code, and real temporary SQLite state.
- Expected output: UC011RelationshipCorrectionWorkflowTests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: PublishedTallyFixture (Release published-process E2E fixture)
- Consumes: CompletePublicContract (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — Exactly 72 provider-neutral operations
- Produces: VerifiedUC011 (UC-LEDGER-011) — workflow verification

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/EndToEnd/UC011RelationshipCorrectionWorkflowTests.cs` (test) — verification — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.EndToEnd.UC011RelationshipCorrectionWorkflowTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 9 main/failure-path scenarios run and 0 fail

## Commit

title: Implement relationship revoke and replace
description:
## Objective
Retire or replace one active financial relationship atomically only after typed policies prove the resulting roles and spend effect valid.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-RELATIONSHIP-CORRECTION
- DD-LEDGER-FULL-AMOUNT-REFUND-RELATIONSHIP
- DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY

## Depends On
- bd-10w: closed; provides the full-amount RefundPolicy
- bd-2ne: closed; provides LedgerMutationExecutor
- bd-xq6: closed; provides TransferPolicy and RelationshipStore

## In Scope
- Public relationship revoke and replace.
- Internal statement-correction replacement.
- Append-only attributable lifecycle history.
- Revalidate transfer invariants or exact same-account ZAR full-refund equality before retiring the current relationship.
- Preserve one active relationship per participant.

## Out of Scope
- Partial or cumulative refunds.
- In-place relationship edits or retirement before replacement validation.
- Bypassing TransferPolicy or RefundPolicy.
- Exposing statement replacement as an independent public command.

## Files
- src/Tally/Contracts/Ledger/Relationships/RelationshipLifecycleContracts.cs (create)
- src/Tally/Domain/Ledger/Relationships/RelationshipLifecycle.cs (create)
- src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Relationships/RelationshipLifecycleHandlers.cs (create)
- src/Tally/Infrastructure/Storage/Relationships/RelationshipStore.cs (modify)
- src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Relationships/RelationshipLifecycleOperationModule.cs (create)
- src/Tally/Contracts/Common/ProcessContracts.cs (modify)
- src/Tally/Bootstrap/LedgerServices.cs (modify)
- src/Tally/Cli/OperationRegistry.cs (modify)
- src/Tally/Cli/TallyProcess.cs (modify)
- tests/Tally.Tests/Features/Ledger/Relationships/RelationshipLifecycleTests.cs (test)

## Verification
- focused RelationshipLifecycleTests
- dotnet build
- dotnet test

## Commit
- feat(ledger): implement relationship revoke and replace
- Refs: bd-2sb

## Context
- lex context bd-2sb --json
design: Implement append-only relationship revoke/replace using the existing lifecycle table and transaction boundary. Replacement validates the complete proposed transfer or refund before retiring the active relationship. Refund replacement reuses RefundPolicy and therefore requires same-account active ZAR transactions, correct signs, and exact absolute full-amount equality; partial, cumulative, or over-refund replacements are rejected.
acceptance_criteria:
- Public revoke appends an attributable lifecycle event, retires one active transfer or refund atomically, and removes its spend effect.
- Public replace validates the complete proposal before retirement and leaves the old relationship active on any failure.
- Replacement revalidates lifecycle, roles, cardinality, exact amount, account, currency, signs, full-refund equality, and participant exclusivity.
- Statement correction replaces only the authorized transaction and preserves every invariant, including exact full-refund equality, or returns review-required before any write.
- History retains participants, type, reason, actor/time, reconciliation decision, and replacement reference; identical replay returns the original outcome.
- No path supports partial or cumulative refunds.
notes: Corrected on 2026-07-22 after Dirk established the full-refund-only product rule. This bead consumes DD-LEDGER-FULL-AMOUNT-REFUND-RELATIONSHIP and RefundPolicy from bd-10w; cumulative-refund semantics are superseded. Pre-flight added the four established CLI integration files to the task and bead boundary so the promised typed public operations can be composed and exposed without scope drift. ASSUMPTION: Public lifecycle mutation results return the retired relationship, lifecycle event ID, and optional active replacement, following the repository's existing lifecycle-result convention.
close_reason: Implemented and verified: typed transfer/refund revoke and replace, decision-authorized statement replacement, append-only history, full-refund-only validation, and stable replay. Gates: 21 focused, 71 relationship, 568 full, build/format/AOT green.

title: Implement the concrete public LEDGER client
description:
## Objective
Provide one concrete LedgerContractClient that preserves released schemas, errors, versions, cancellation, and idempotency unchanged.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-APPROVED-BATCH-COMMIT: Commit approved candidates through public LEDGER operations
  - AC: Given an approved immutable manifest revision whose adapter and discovered LEDGER versions remain compatible, when commit starts, then INGEST validates its fingerprint, approval, versions, account state, and candidate identities before the first mutation.
  - AC: Given an accepted candidate, when it is committed, then INGEST invokes only the public LEDGER transaction-recording operation with the frozen request, statement-import provenance, and a stable candidate idempotency identity.
  - AC: Given an Exact Duplicate outcome, when commit traverses the manifest, then INGEST records the prior canonical reference and invokes no new LEDGER write for that candidate.
  - AC: Given a successful LEDGER result, when INGEST records the outcome, then the returned stable transaction reference round-trips through the public LEDGER read contract with the approved immutable facts.
  - AC: Given a LEDGER validation, compatibility, or idempotency conflict for a candidate, when commit receives it, then INGEST durably records the stable outcome and stops before unsafe continuation.

## Depends On
- bd-2dg (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-LEDGER-CONTRACT): The prerequisite gate proves every operation and contract consumed by the client.
- bd-twx (TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): The client consumes FrozenLedgerRecordRequest and stable INGEST compatibility errors.

## In Scope
- Use the shared typed operation executor for account lookup and transaction record/get; expose no transport abstraction and no private LEDGER implementation.
- Produce: LedgerContractClient.
- Produce: Public-contract client and architecture tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- LedgerContractClient.GetAccountAsync invokes ledger.account.get through OperationRegistry and returns the released AccountDetail without inferring or translating account identity, class, currency, or lifecycle.
- RecordTransactionAsync invokes ledger.transaction.record with the persisted FrozenLedgerRecordRequest, provenance kind statement_import, sourceReference and idempotency key derived by INGEST; GetTransactionAsync invokes ledger.transaction.get for round-trip verification.
- Unsupported Ledger contract versions fail before source parsing or mutation; all public stable errors and exits are preserved without translation to weaker generic failures.
- Cancellation propagates into the shared executor; the client neither spawns a process nor catches an unexpected exception to expose it as payload.
- Architecture tests reject references to LedgerDb, Infrastructure/Storage/Ledger, Features/Ledger handlers, private Ledger domain types, SQL, or storage paths.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT open LedgerDb/SQL or call feature handlers directly — rejected per DD-INGEST-LEDGER-PUBLIC-INTEGRATION.
- ❌ Do NOT spawn the CLI as a child process or add an ILedgerTransport interface with one implementation — rejected alternatives per DD-INGEST-LEDGER-PUBLIC-INTEGRATION.
- ❌ Do NOT duplicate or weaken LEDGER account, money, date, provenance, source-reference, or idempotency validation.

## Approach
Use the shared typed operation executor for account lookup and transaction record/get; expose no transport abstraction and no private LEDGER implementation.
- Constraint: Only released operation IDs, common envelopes, Ledger contract records, and stable errors may cross this boundary.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: VerifiedLedgerOperationRegistry (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — Validated by the local prerequisite gate
- Consumes: VerifiedLedgerAccountDetail (DM-LEDGER-ACCOUNT-CATEGORY-CONTRACTS) — Validated external AccountDetail contract
- Consumes: VerifiedLedgerTransactionDetail (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CONTRACTS) — Validated external TransactionDetail contract
- Consumes: LedgerIngestContractPrerequisite (DM-INGEST-LEDGER-COMMIT-CONTRACT) — Upstream gate evidence
- Consumes: FrozenLedgerRecordRequest (DM-INGEST-LEDGER-COMMIT-CONTRACT) — Frozen INGEST public request
- Produces: LedgerContractClient.GetAccountAsync (DM-LEDGER-ACCOUNT-CATEGORY-CONTRACTS) — Public account lookup
- Produces: LedgerContractClient.RecordTransactionAsync (DM-INGEST-LEDGER-COMMIT-CONTRACT) — Public idempotent write
- Produces: LedgerContractClient.GetTransactionAsync (DM-INGEST-LEDGER-COMMIT-CONTRACT) — Public canonical verification

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Integration/Ledger/LedgerContractClient.cs` (create) — public operation executor client — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/LedgerContract/LedgerContractClientTests.cs` (test) — account/record/get contract tests — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/LedgerContract/LedgerBoundaryArchitectureTests.cs` (test) — private-reference guards — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~LedgerContractClientTests|FullyQualifiedName~LedgerBoundaryArchitectureTests'` -> exit 0; at least 14 account, version, record/get, replay, cancellation, error-preservation, and private-reference cases are discovered and 0 tests fail

## Commit
- `feat(ingest): implement public ledger client` — footer `Refs: bd-2sg`

## Lex references
- DD-INGEST-LEDGER-PUBLIC-INTEGRATION
- FR-INGEST-APPROVED-BATCH-COMMIT
- PLAN-INGEST-V1
- SP-INGEST-02-PREVIEW-REVIEW
- TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION
- TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-LEDGER-CONTRACT
- TASK-INGEST-LEDGER-PUBLIC-CLIENT
- TC-INGEST-LEDGER-PUBLIC-CONFORMANCE
- Deep context: `lex context bd-2sg --json`

description:

- Produce: Exact-set, hierarchy-contract, and provider-neutral composition tests

title: Prove the process-to-storage core seam
description:
## Objective
Compose and verify the complete current schema, V001 upgrade path, process contract, exact values, privacy, and idempotent storage on linux-x64.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On
- bd-23m (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-STORAGE): Core gate exercises the real store and generation policy.
- bd-1b2 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-MONEY-DATES): Core gate proves exact values through SQLite.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Core gate proves transactional replay and crash behavior.
- bd-3sx (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-PROCESS-CONTRACT): Consumer requires TallyProcess.RunAsync from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.
- bd-2i5 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-CATALOGUE): Consumes V001CatalogueSchema.
- bd-2qj (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-TRANSACTIONS): Consumes V001TransactionSchema.
- bd-193 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-RELATIONSHIPS-ACTUALS): Consumes V001RelationshipActualsSchema.
- bd-35j (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION): CompleteV001Schema consumes the evidence and reconciliation fragment.
- bd-195 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-RECONCILIATION-AUTHORITY): The proven complete schema must include the V002 statement-authority upgrade.

## In Scope
- Integration-only task with no Implements link: compose every V001 fragment plus the additive V002 statement-authority upgrade and prove the published process-to-store seam.
- Produce: CompleteLedgerSchema composition
- Produce: CompleteLedgerSchemaTests, CoreRuntimeStorageTests, PublishedBinaryCoreTests, and scripts/verify-ledger-core.sh

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT edit committed V001EvidenceReconciliationSchema, substitute mocks/partial schema/Debug-only proof, or skip the V001-to-V002 upgrade test.
- Do NOT clear while value-semantics gates or OQ-LEDGER-16 invalidate the selected schema.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-34k (TASK-LEDGER-ACCOUNTS)
  - bd-1ie (TASK-LEDGER-AGENT-SKILLS)
  - bd-34a (TASK-LEDGER-CATEGORIES)
  - bd-3qf (TASK-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-REGISTRY)
  - bd-3od (TASK-LEDGER-PAYMENT-IDENTITIES)
  - bd-3vc (TASK-LEDGER-SPEND-POOLS)

## Success Criteria
- CompleteLedgerSchema explicitly registers all five V001 fragments and V002StatementAuthoritySchema in dependency-safe version/name order; a fresh store and a committed V001 fixture reach the same current schema and migration metadata.
- Schema tests compare every table, column, index, foreign key, check, hierarchy rule, evidence privacy allowlist, statement-authority reference, and user_version; missing/duplicate fragments or injected SQL failure leave no partial schema.
- Published tally initializes a private current generation, executes a typed idempotent probe through TallyProcess, emits one stdout envelope, and leaves no child process or network listener.
- Identical request/logical replay returns the original effect; pre-commit crash leaves zero domain/idempotency rows; changed replay conflicts.
- Money/date round-trip is exact, hierarchy is acyclic, integrity is ok, foreign-key violations are zero, artifact modes are 0700/0600, and provider/raw-payload canaries never appear.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT edit committed V001EvidenceReconciliationSchema, substitute mocks/partial schema/Debug-only proof, or skip the V001-to-V002 upgrade test.

title: Resolve cash-withdrawal spend treatment
description:
## Objective
Choose and record whether cash withdrawal is immediate External Spend or a transfer to a tracked cash account.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On
- None — this is a compile root.

## In Scope
- Validation-only task with no Implements link: resolve OQ-LEDGER-16 before account, transfer, and actuals contracts harden.
- Produce: docs/validation/ledger/oq-16-cash-withdrawal-policy.md with the owner decision
- Produce: OQ-LEDGER-16 resolved or an explicit lex:design handoff

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT infer cash treatment from provider wording, transaction description, or ATM code.
- Do NOT classify an external cash withdrawal as an owned-account transfer unless a tracked cash account and balanced transfer semantics are explicitly designed.
- Do NOT mark this gate complete while OQ-LEDGER-16 is open or while the chosen rule contradicts C22 exact actual conservation.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-34k (TASK-LEDGER-ACCOUNTS)
  - bd-3hy (TASK-LEDGER-ACTUALS-PROJECTION)
  - bd-2i5 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-CATALOGUE)
  - bd-xq6 (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSFERS)

## Success Criteria
- The owner chooses exactly one rule: cash withdrawal is immediate spend, or it transfers value to an explicitly tracked cash account until later cash spending is recorded.
- The validation report tests the chosen rule against withdrawal fees, later cash purchases, refunds, account movement, External Spend, Budget Actual, and replay without retaining raw bank or email payloads.
- OQ-LEDGER-16 resolves with the exact rule and affected design entities; if tracked cash requires a new account class or relationship invariant, the gate records that impact and returns to lex:design before downstream code.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT infer cash treatment from provider wording, transaction description, or ATM code.
- ❌ Do NOT classify an external cash withdrawal as an owned-account transfer unless a tracked cash account and balanced transfer semantics are explicitly designed.
- ❌ Do NOT mark this gate complete while OQ-LEDGER-16 is open or while the chosen rule contradicts C22 exact actual conservation.

## Approach
- Validation-only task with no Implements link: resolve OQ-LEDGER-16 before account, transfer, and actuals contracts harden.
- Constraint: Only privacy-safe structural conclusions are committed; graph state changes use lex commands.
- Expected output: docs/validation/ledger/oq-16-cash-withdrawal-policy.md with the owner decision
- Expected output: OQ-LEDGER-16 resolved or an explicit lex:design handoff

## Interfaces
- Produces: OQ-LEDGER-16Resolution (OQ-LEDGER-16) — Exact cash-withdrawal spend rule or design-return result

## Files (reservation globs)

- `.lexicon/graph/LEDGER/open-question/OQ-LEDGER-16.json` (read) — canonical gate state — required — Update through lex open-question commands only

## Verification

title: List metadata history and report store status
description:
## Objective
ListInsightReportsQuery and GetInsightsStatusQuery expose deterministic safe metadata, integrity, counts, durable recovery evidence, and supported policies only.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-HISTORY: Inspect retained report history and status
  - AC: Given a known retained identity, when Get is requested, then the exact immutable Report Snapshot and its complete provenance are returned.
  - AC: Given an unknown retained identity, when Get is requested, then Not Found is returned without other report metadata.
  - AC: Given a deleted retained identity, when Get is requested, then Deleted and metadata-only Deletion Event evidence are returned without report content.
  - AC: Given a bounded List request, when it succeeds, then stable report identity, predecessor identity, Budget Period, trusted creation time, actor, policy versions, content fingerprint, and deletion state are returned without financial payload.
  - AC: Given retained history, when List ordering is inspected, then it is trusted creation time descending followed by stable report identity descending.
  - AC: Given a List request over the released count or output limit, when it is validated, then resource failure is returned rather than silent truncation.
  - AC: Given Status, when it is requested, then store version, integrity state, retained count, deleted count, latest successful backup/verification evidence, and supported policy versions are returned without amounts, merchant text, report bodies, or private paths.
  - AC: Given Get, List, or Status success or failure, when retained history is inspected afterwards, then no Report Snapshot, Deletion Event, or idempotency outcome changed.

## Depends On
- bd-7kn (TASK-INSIGHTS-STATE-FOUNDATION): Both queries use normalized metadata and integrity primitives.

## In Scope
- Implement bounded metadata-only List and Status queries without report payload reads or state changes.
- Produce: ListInsightReportsQuery
- Produce: GetInsightsStatusQuery
- Produce: Metadata-only query tests

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- List accepts only documented BudgetPeriod, trusted creation-range, actor, and count filters; one-over-limit input returns ResourceLimit rather than truncating silently.
- List returns snapshot identity, predecessor identity, BudgetPeriod, trusted creation time, actor, policy versions, content fingerprint, and deletion state ordered by creation time descending then stable identity descending, with no report payload or amount.
- Status returns active generation fingerprint, storage version, integrity state, retained count, deleted count, supported policy versions, and latest durable successful Backup or Restore verification without payloads, private paths, or merchant text.
- Standalone Verify results do not appear in Status and List or Status success and failure change no retained or replay state.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT deserialize report payloads for List or Status, silently truncate, record standalone Verify, or use display text for ordering.
- ❌ Do NOT add paging cursors, search infrastructure, HTTP routes, automatic cleanup, or read-side materialized replicas — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE.
- ❌ Do NOT expose amounts, explanations, private paths, or artifact filenames in metadata queries.

## Approach
Implement bounded metadata-only List and Status queries without report payload reads or state changes.
- Constraint: Queries use normalized metadata columns and short read connections only.
- Expected output: ListInsightReportsQuery
- Expected output: GetInsightsStatusQuery
- Expected output: Metadata-only query tests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: InsightsStateStore (DM-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE) — metadata-only queries
- Produces: ListInsightReportsQuery.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — insights.report.list handler
- Produces: GetInsightsStatusQuery.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-RECOVERY-CONTRACTS) — insights.status.get handler

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/History/List/ListInsightReportsQuery.cs` (create) — bounded metadata list handler
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/History/Status/GetInsightsStatusQuery.cs` (create) — metadata-only store status handler
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/History/InsightReportHistoryStatusTests.cs` (test) — filters, order, limits, status, and no-payload tests

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~InsightReportHistoryStatusTests` — exit 0; at least 18 filter, deterministic-order, exact-limit, one-over-limit, deleted-metadata, payload-read-canary, integrity, durable-backup, durable-restore, standalone-verify-exclusion, privacy, no-write, and cancellation cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(insights): list metadata history and report store status` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-32j`

## Lex references
- DD-INSIGHTS-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE
- DD-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE
- DD-INSIGHTS-VERIFIED-RECOVERY-ACTIVATION
- DM-INSIGHTS-RECOVERY-CONTRACTS
- DM-INSIGHTS-RETAINED-REPORT-LIFECYCLE
- FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-HISTORY
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-02-RETAINED-HISTORY
- TASK-INSIGHTS-REPORT-HISTORY-STATUS
- TC-INSIGHTS-REPORT-HISTORY-STATUS
- Deep context: `lex context bd-32j --json`

title: Validate deterministic reconciliation policy
description:
## Objective
Produce a bounded versioned automatic match matrix while keeping explicit owner-approved statement correction available independently.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On
- None — this is a compile root.

## In Scope
- Validation-only task with no Implements link: resolve only the automatic matching/correction subset of OQ-LEDGER-13 from paired private agent-capture and statement-row evidence.
- Produce: docs/validation/ledger/oq-13-reconciliation-policy.md with redacted policy matrix and explicit excluded cases
- Produce: OQ-LEDGER-13 resolved with the exact ReconciliationPolicyV1 decision on the only completing path

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT invent tolerance, fuzzy description scoring, provider fields, or hidden tie-breaking — per DD-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT.
- Do NOT treat agent capture as co-equal authority or retain raw statement/email/MIME content; statement evidence is authoritative only through typed decisions.
- Do NOT enable an automatic case with multiple compatible or guard candidates; unresolved automatic cases remain review-required.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-1zm (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-PROJECTION)

## Success Criteria
- A redacted paired-evidence matrix covers exact facts, differing authoritative statement facts, shifted dates, duplicate amounts, competing candidates, conflicts, reconciled facts, and zero-candidate rows without retaining raw rows or descriptions.
- Each permitted automatic rule names exact account, currency, signed amount, date/tolerance, uniqueness, conflict guards, authority basis, and stable reason; every unproven case is review-required.
- OQ-LEDGER-13 resolves only when the matrix proves bounded ReconciliationPolicyV1 matching and any automatic correction subset; otherwise this task remains incomplete while explicit owner-approved correction remains a separately valid runtime path.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT invent tolerance, fuzzy description scoring, provider fields, or hidden tie-breaking — per DD-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT.
- ❌ Do NOT treat agent capture as co-equal authority or retain raw statement/email/MIME content; statement evidence is authoritative only through typed decisions.
- ❌ Do NOT enable an automatic case with multiple compatible or guard candidates; unresolved automatic cases remain review-required.

## Approach
- Validation-only task with no Implements link: resolve only the automatic matching/correction subset of OQ-LEDGER-13 from paired private agent-capture and statement-row evidence.
- Expected output: docs/validation/ledger/oq-13-reconciliation-policy.md with redacted policy matrix and explicit excluded cases
- Expected output: OQ-LEDGER-13 resolved with the exact ReconciliationPolicyV1 decision on the only completing path

## Interfaces
- Produces: OQ-LEDGER-13Resolution (OQ-LEDGER-13) — Produced only after bounded policy evidence resolves the question
- Produces: ReconciliationPolicyMatrixEvidence (TC-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-POLICY-MATRIX) — Redacted executable policy matrix and invariant result

## Files (reservation globs)
- `docs/statements/` (read) — private evidence — required — Never stage or copy source material
- `docs/validation/ledger/oq-13-reconciliation-policy.md` (document) — redacted evidence — required
- `.lexicon/graph/LEDGER/open-question/OQ-LEDGER-13.json` (modify) — canonical gate state — required

## Verification

- **Test:** `test -s docs/validation/ledger/oq-13-reconciliation-policy.md && lex open-question show OQ-LEDGER-13 --json | jq -e '.status == "resolved" and (.decision | length > 0)'` — exit 0; redacted matrix is non-empty and OQ-LEDGER-13 contains a non-empty resolved decision

## Commit
