- DD-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT

- OQ-LEDGER-13

title: Verify on-demand Insight Report generation
description:
## Objective
UC-INSIGHTS-001 succeeds end to end only for one complete coherent deterministic report and leaves all upstream and INSIGHTS durable state unchanged.

## Implements
- None; this task is a validation or shared-contract task with no direct FR ownership.

## Depends On
- bd-1jf (TASK-INSIGHTS-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): UC-001 invokes the published Generate surface.
- bd-200 (TASK-INSIGHTS-GATE-INT-BUDGET-EVIDENCE): UC-001 requires the real coherent producer prerequisite.

## In Scope

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- A compatible caller discovers Generate and its limits, submits one bounded BudgetPeriod request, and receives one report using a single frozen Johannesburg As-of Instant and one coherent BUDGET result.
- The report accounts for exact day, Monday-week, and month-to-date totals and categories, preserves Unbudgeted and Uncategorized, returns exact Linear Pace or its named unavailable state, accounts for every requested kind, and cites complete producer/policy provenance and limitations.
- Identical trusted time, normalized request, evidence identities, and policy versions reproduce semantically identical ordered output and fingerprint.
- NoBudgetPlan and NoActiveBudgetPlanRevision preserve exact actual metrics and return pace unavailable without an invented plan; current correction races yield wholly pre/post evidence or SourceStateChanged with no report.
- Malformed, incompatible, unsupported-window, over-limit, incomplete, incoherent, overflow, integrity, and cancellation paths return one stable failure and change no BUDGET, LEDGER, CLASSIFY, or durable INSIGHTS state.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT accept a partial report, mixed producer state, implicit clock, rolling window, floating-point pace, hidden signal omission, automatic retention, or upstream mutation.
- ❌ Do NOT validate only aggregate totals; every named failure partition and all three plan states require nonzero discovery.

## Approach

- Constraint: Run against the published registry and real BUDGET contract seam, not private handlers or a test-only second evaluator.

- A compatible caller discovers Generate and its limits, submits one bounded BudgetPeriod request, and receives one report using a single frozen Johannesburg As-of Instant and one coherent BUDGET result.
- The report accounts for exact day, Monday-week, and month-to-date totals and categories, preserves Unbudgeted and Uncategorized, returns exact Linear Pace or its named unavailable state, accounts for every requested kind, and cites complete producer/policy provenance and limitations.
- Identical trusted time, normalized request, evidence identities, and policy versions reproduce semantically identical ordered output and fingerprint.
- NoBudgetPlan and NoActiveBudgetPlanRevision preserve exact actual metrics and return pace unavailable without an invented plan; current correction races yield wholly pre/post evidence or SourceStateChanged with no report.
- Malformed, incompatible, unsupported-window, over-limit, incomplete, incoherent, overflow, integrity, and cancellation paths return one stable failure and change no BUDGET, LEDGER, CLASSIFY, or durable INSIGHTS state.
notes: No soft checkpoint; execute the complete graph contract and rejection criteria.

title: Implement Spend Category catalogue operations
description:
## Objective
Maintain stable category identities, deterministic ancestry, and append-only parent/lifecycle history.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-CATEGORY-CATALOGUE: Maintain the Spend Category catalogue
  - AC: Given a valid category name and optional active parent Category ID, when the owner creates a category, then the Ledger returns one active category with a stable identifier and its current ancestry path.
  - AC: Given an active category hierarchy, when any node is inspected or listed, then each category exposes its stable ID, optional parent ID, depth, active state, and deterministic ancestry path without requiring callers to reconstruct the tree from names.
  - AC: Given an existing category, when the owner renames it, then its stable identifier, parent relationship, child relationships, and historical transaction assignments remain unchanged.
  - AC: Given an active category and an active proposed parent, when the owner reparents it with a reason, then one append-only parent-change event becomes current and category roll-ups use the new acyclic hierarchy.
  - AC: Given a category is proposed as its own parent or descendant, a proposed parent is archived, or the move would create a cycle, when reparenting is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and preserves the current hierarchy.
  - AC: Given any category with historical transaction, budget, or hierarchy references, when physical deletion is requested, then the Ledger rejects it and retains the stable category and history; archival is the supported lifecycle action.
  - AC: Given an archived category with no invalid archived ancestor, when the owner reactivates it, then the same stable identifier becomes active and may receive new assignments or active children.
  - AC: Given Spend Categories and Spend Pools, when either catalogue is maintained, then its identity, lifecycle, and naming remain independent and no value silently creates or selects a value in the other catalogue.
  - AC: Given an active category with active children, when archival is requested, then the Ledger rejects it without mutation; given no active children, archival succeeds and historical assignments and parent events remain queryable.

## Depends On
- bd-2zt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE): Category slice consumes the proven core seam.
- bd-sz5 (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-EVIDENCE-CATEGORIES): Category contracts cannot start until OQ-LEDGER-6 resolves.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Consumer requires LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.
- bd-23m (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-STORAGE): Consumer requires LedgerDb from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.

## In Scope
- Deliver the owner-approved hierarchical category catalogue and attributable lifecycle/parent operations.
- Produce: Category contracts/domain/store/handlers/module
- Produce: Real-SQLite and handler tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT implement a flat catalogue, materialized-path overwrite, nested-set rewrite, split Category Assignment, hard delete, or in-place lifecycle edit — per DD-LEDGER-CATEGORY-HIERARCHY and DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- Do NOT create budget amounts, infer Spend Pool, or implement BUDGET exact-node/subtree overlap policy.
- Do NOT add HTTP endpoints, generic CRUD save/update/delete commands, or provider wording.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3q1 (TASK-LEDGER-CATEGORY-ALLOCATIONS)
  - bd-2ve (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CATALOGUE-TRANSACTION-BUNDLE)

## Success Criteria
- Create accepts an optional active parent and returns stable ID, parent ID, depth, and ancestry; normalized names are unique only among active siblings.
- Rename preserves ID, parent, children, and assignments; reparent appends one reasoned parent event and preserves identity while current ancestry changes.
- Self-parent, descendant cycle, archived parent, archived target, and active-child archive requests return stable no-mutation errors; valid reactivation preserves identity and requires active ancestry.
- List/get return deterministic ancestry-path, name, and ID ordering for requested children, subtree, or all scope; all six mutations including reparent are idempotent.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT implement a flat catalogue, materialized-path overwrite, nested-set rewrite, split Category Assignment, hard delete, or in-place lifecycle edit — per DD-LEDGER-CATEGORY-HIERARCHY and DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- ❌ Do NOT create budget amounts, infer Spend Pool, or implement BUDGET exact-node/subtree overlap policy.
- ❌ Do NOT add HTTP endpoints, generic CRUD save/update/delete commands, or provider wording.

## Approach
- Deliver the owner-approved hierarchical category catalogue and attributable lifecycle/parent operations.
- Expected output: Category contracts/domain/store/handlers/module
- Expected output: Real-SQLite and handler tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: CategoryOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-ACCOUNT-CATEGORY-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: CategoryStore (DM-LEDGER-SPEND-CATEGORY)
- Produces: CategoryDetail (DM-LEDGER-ACCOUNT-CATEGORY-CONTRACTS)

title: Delete one unreferenced report payload safely
description:
## Objective
DeleteInsightReportCommand removes only an unreferenced target payload and outgoing link atomically while preserving surviving Restatement chains and attributable deletion evidence.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-DELETION: Delete retained report payloads dependency-safely
  - AC: Given a retained leaf Report Snapshot and a valid actor, reason, idempotency identity, and explicit report identity, when Delete succeeds, then its report payload and Restatement link are removed atomically.
  - AC: Given successful deletion, when the Deletion Event is inspected, then report identity, actor, reason, trusted time, idempotency outcome, and dependency result are present and no financial value or report content remains.
  - AC: Given a Report Snapshot referenced by a surviving Restatement, when Delete is requested, then dependency conflict is returned and no payload or link changes.
  - AC: Given a Restatement chain, when the owner deletes reports from leaves to root, then each unreferenced target can be deleted independently and surviving predecessors remain immutable until targeted.
  - AC: Given an unknown report identity, when Delete is requested, then Not Found is returned and no Deletion Event is created.
  - AC: Given an already deleted report and semantically identical idempotent Delete retry, when retried, then the prior logical deletion result is returned and no second event is created.
  - AC: Given no explicit Delete request, when time passes or report limits are approached, then INSIGHTS performs no automatic expiry, cleanup, or payload deletion.
  - AC: Given deletion after a backup was created, when the delete result is returned, then it states that existing point-in-time backups are unchanged and an explicit restore may reinstate their captured state.

## Depends On
- bd-7kn (TASK-INSIGHTS-STATE-FOUNDATION): Delete uses snapshot, link, payload, and event tables.
- bd-2nv (TASK-INSIGHTS-IDEMPOTENCY-EXECUTOR): Delete retries must resolve without a second event.

## In Scope
- Implement explicit replay-safe RESTRICT leaf deletion with one metadata-only DeletionEvent and no automatic cleanup.
- Produce: DeleteInsightReportCommand
- Produce: Dependency-safe deletion tests

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- Delete requires explicit snapshot identity, trusted P1 actor, non-blank reason, and idempotency identity; unknown target returns NotFound and creates no event.
- A target referenced by any surviving child returns DependencyConflict and changes no payload, link, event, or replay outcome.
- A leaf deletion atomically removes its canonical payload and outgoing predecessor link and appends one DeletionEvent containing identities, actor, reason, trusted time, dependency result, and replay reference but no amount, explanation, or report content.
- Leaves can be deleted toward the root while each surviving predecessor remains immutable; exact retry of an already deleted target returns the prior metadata-only result and creates no second event.
- Existing backups remain unchanged and the result states an explicit restore may reinstate their captured state; time and limits never trigger automatic deletion.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT cascade-delete referenced predecessors, delete descendants, rewrite chains, return deleted payloads, or create an event for NotFound — per ADR-CORE-0020 and DD-INSIGHTS-RETENTION-RESTATEMENT-LIFECYCLE.
- ❌ Do NOT add automatic expiry, retention cleanup, background jobs, or backup mutation.
- ❌ Do NOT split payload, link, event, and replay changes across transactions or log deleted report content.

## Approach
Implement explicit replay-safe RESTRICT leaf deletion with one metadata-only DeletionEvent and no automatic cleanup.
- Constraint: Deletion remains explicit, leaf-only, attributable, idempotent, and metadata-only.
- Expected output: DeleteInsightReportCommand
- Expected output: Dependency-safe deletion tests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: InsightsIdempotencyExecutor.ExecuteAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-IDEMPOTENCY) — same/conflicting Delete replay
- Consumes: InsightsStateStore (DM-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE) — dependency check and atomic deletion transaction
- Produces: DeleteInsightReportCommand.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — insights.report.delete handler

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/History/Delete/DeleteInsightReportCommand.cs` (create) — RESTRICT leaf deletion handler
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/History/DeleteInsightReportCommandTests.cs` (test) — dependency, replay, event, backup, and no-cleanup tests

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~DeleteInsightReportCommandTests` — exit 0; at least 18 unknown, referenced conflict, leaf, chain-order, event-minimization, exact replay, changed replay, cancellation, rollback, existing-backup, no-auto-cleanup, deleted-read, and payload-log cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(insights): delete unreferenced report payload safely` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-34e`

## Lex references
- ADR-CORE-0020
- DD-INSIGHTS-RETENTION-RESTATEMENT-LIFECYCLE
- DD-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE
- DIAG-INSIGHTS-REPORT-STATE
- DM-INSIGHTS-RETAINED-REPORT-LIFECYCLE
- FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-DELETION
- NFR-INSIGHTS-ATOMIC-DURABLE-REPORT-STATE
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-02-RETAINED-HISTORY
- TASK-INSIGHTS-DELETE-REPORT
- TC-INSIGHTS-REPORT-DELETION-DEPENDENCIES

title: Implement account lifecycle operations
description:
## Objective
Maintain stable masked asset and liability accounts with attributable append-only lifecycle history.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-ACCOUNT-MAINTENANCE: Maintain Owned Bank Accounts
  - AC: Given a valid unique institution name, owner label, supported asset or liability account type, ZAR currency, display label, and masked distinguishing identifier, when the owner creates an account, then the Ledger returns one active account with a stable identifier and stores no online-banking credential or full account number.
  - AC: Given an existing account, when the owner renames its display label, then its stable identifier and previously recorded transaction references remain unchanged.
  - AC: Given an account referenced by transactions, when the owner archives it, then its history remains queryable and the Ledger rejects new transactions against it with a stable validation error.
  - AC: Given active and archived accounts, when the owner lists accounts with an explicit lifecycle-state filter, then each matching account is returned once with its stable identifier and masked identity fields.
  - AC: Given an owned account, Payment Instruments, cardholders, Spend Pools, and categories, when account maintenance occurs, then no account value silently creates, selects, or changes another dimension.

## Depends On
- bd-2zt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE): Account slice consumes the proven core seam.
- bd-31r (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-EVIDENCE-CASH-WITHDRAWALS): Account operations must know whether tracked cash is an in-scope account class.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Account mutations consume LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync.
- bd-23m (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-STORAGE): Account persistence consumes LedgerDb.

## In Scope
- Deliver create/get/list/rename/archive account behavior through typed handlers, real SQLite storage, and a self-contained operation module.
- Produce: Account contracts/domain/store/handlers/module
- Produce: Real-SQLite and handler tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT delete or overwrite account history; retain RESTRICT-linked append-only events per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- Do NOT add repositories, mediator, HTTP, credentials, or full account identifiers per DD-LEDGER-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-2ve (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CATALOGUE-TRANSACTION-BUNDLE)
  - bd-84s (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTIONS-RECORD-GET)

## Success Criteria
- Valid unique ZAR input creates one active asset or liability account with a stable ULID, masked identifier, and no credential or full account number.
- Rename and archive append attributable lifecycle events; archived accounts remain queryable and reject new transactions with LEDGER-ACCOUNT-ARCHIVED and no write.
- Get and list apply history, lifecycle, and institution filters with deterministic ordering and one row per account.
- Every mutation uses LedgerMutationExecutor; identical replay returns the original result and changed replay conflicts.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT delete or overwrite account history; retain RESTRICT-linked append-only events per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- ❌ Do NOT add repositories, mediator, HTTP, credentials, or full account identifiers per DD-LEDGER-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE.

## Approach
- Deliver create/get/list/rename/archive account behavior through typed handlers, real SQLite storage, and a self-contained operation module.
- Expected output: Account contracts/domain/store/handlers/module
- Expected output: Real-SQLite and handler tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: AccountOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-ACCOUNT-CATEGORY-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: AccountStore (DM-LEDGER-ACCOUNT)
- Produces: AccountDetail (DM-LEDGER-ACCOUNT-CATEGORY-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync (DM-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENCY-RECORD)
- Consumes: LedgerDb (DM-LEDGER-STORE-GENERATION)

## Verification

title: Verify preview and qualification workflow
description:
## Objective
Prove an owner can preview each qualified statement into one reconciled immutable manifest with no source or LEDGER mutation.

## Implements
- No direct FR ownership: this is an evidence, integration, security, use-case, or completion gate.
- Proves or constrains NFR-INGEST-BOUNDED-PARSING: Bound passive statement processing (satisfies).
- Proves or constrains NFR-INGEST-DETERMINISTIC-INTEGRITY: Preserve deterministic extraction and financial integrity (satisfies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-FINANCIAL-NORMALIZATION-CONTRACT: Verify exact financial normalization (verifies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-SOURCE-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT: Verify complete source reconciliation (verifies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-STATEMENT-PREVIEW-CONTRACT: Verify deterministic statement preview (verifies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-VARIANT-QUALIFICATION-CONTRACT: Verify supported variant qualification (verifies).
- Proves or constrains UC-INGEST-001: Preview and qualify a local statement (covers).

## Depends On
- bd-2jn (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): UC verification exercises the complete published INGEST contract.
- bd-1j2 (TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION): Consumes PrivateStatementFixtureSet.

## In Scope
- Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-001 main scenario and every documented failure path using the published binary and injected private fixtures.
- Produce: UCIngest001Evidence.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Main scenario discovers the contract, supplies one private source and explicit active account, selects exactly one qualified adapter, normalizes every record, verifies every available control, freezes one immutable revision, and returns preview with zero ledger.transaction.record calls.
- Each of the three fixtures run twice with identical ordered outcomes, candidate facts, controls, and manifest identity within 5 seconds and 256 MiB peak RSS; before/after source hashes match.
- Unreadable/source-changed input returns stable source failure with no committable manifest; conflicting/missing/ambiguous account blocks without inference.
- Unsupported, password-required, scan-only, active/external, ambiguous-variant, malformed, or over-limit input fails closed with no mutation.
- Missing/ambiguous amount, currency, sign, date, description, provenance, identity, or row boundary blocks rather than guesses.
- Unexplained records or any opening/closing/running/source-control mismatch leaves the manifest uncommittable.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT inspect internal handlers/stores to assert success; use published operations and public Ledger observations only.
- ❌ Do NOT print or commit private fixture paths, content, or facts.

## Approach
Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-001 main scenario and every documented failure path using the published binary and injected private fixtures.
- Constraint: The test fails loudly with a safe diagnostic when the external private fixture manifest is unavailable.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CompleteIngestPublicContract (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: PrivateStatementFixtureSet (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT)
- Produces: UCIngest001Evidence (UC-INGEST-001)

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`

## Commit

title: Account for evidence states and render explanations
description:
## Objective
Every requested catalogue kind has exactly one evidence state and an explanation that cites the evaluated rule, provenance, permitted next action, and limitations.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-EVIDENCE-STATE-ACCOUNTING: Account for every evidence state
  - AC: Given a requested metric or signal kind, when a complete report is returned, then exactly one Supported, Unavailable, Insufficient Evidence, Not Started, or Deferred state accounts for it.
  - AC: Given No Budget Plan, No Active Budget Plan Revision, Unsupported Policy, Insufficient Horizon, Incomplete Coverage, Incomparable Baseline, or Not Started, when the state is returned, then the reason is a stable typed value with cited evidence and policy version.
  - AC: Given an unavailable or insufficient derived signal, when exact metrics remain supported, then the complete report preserves those exact metrics and records the signal-local state.
  - AC: Given a signal-local unavailable state, when amounts are inspected, then zero, baseline, confidence, or severity values are absent unless zero is the exact supported observed metric.
  - AC: Given cross-source incoherence, contract incompatibility, integrity failure, exact-arithmetic failure, or incomplete pagination, when generation is attempted, then no Insight Report is returned.
  - AC: Given multiple requested kinds, when report accounting is inspected, then every kind appears exactly once in deterministic catalogue order and no unsupported kind disappears silently.
- FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-EXPLANATION: Explain report evidence deterministically
  - AC: Given a supported exact metric, when its explanation is inspected, then exact value, window bounds, category or total identity, source evidence references, and arithmetic rule are returned.
  - AC: Given a supported Linear Pace state, when its explanation is inspected, then formula version, elapsed and total days, exact plan and actual inputs, exact comparison result, and uniform-distribution limitation are returned.
  - AC: Given an unavailable, insufficient, not-started, or deferred state, when its explanation is inspected, then the typed reason, evaluated evidence, unmet rule, and permitted next action are returned without an invented result.
  - AC: Given a retained Report Snapshot identity, when Get or Explain is requested, then the exact immutable stored explanation and provenance are returned rather than recomputing current evidence.
  - AC: Given an unknown retained identity, when Get or Explain is requested, then Not Found is returned without leaking other report metadata.
  - AC: Given a deleted retained identity with a Deletion Event, when Get or Explain is requested, then Deleted is returned with metadata-only deletion evidence and no prior report payload.
  - AC: Given explanatory text, when it is compared across identical structured states and policy versions, then it is semantically identical, template-based, channel-neutral, and contains no persuasion, recommendation, translation, or delivery wording.

## Depends On
- bd-1qz (TASK-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-MODELS): Both policies produce the analytical evidence and explanation contracts.

## In Scope
- Implement closed state accounting and deterministic channel-neutral explanation templates for every requested metric or signal.
- Produce: EvidenceStateAccountant
- Produce: InsightExplanationRenderer
- Produce: State and template tests

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- Every requested active or deferred catalogue kind appears exactly once in catalogue order as Supported, Unavailable, InsufficientEvidence, NotStarted, or Deferred with a stable typed reason.
- Signal-local unavailability preserves supported exact metrics and omits zero, baseline, confidence, and severity unless zero is an exact observed value.
- Coherence, compatibility, integrity, incomplete evidence, and exact-arithmetic failures remain whole-report failures and cannot be rendered as a report state.
- Supported explanations cite exact value, window, identity, evidence references, arithmetic rule, version, and limitations; unavailable explanations cite evaluated evidence, unmet rule, and permitted next action without inventing a result.
- Identical structured state and template version yield semantically identical channel-neutral text with no persuasion, recommendation, translation, notification, delivery wording, or raw merchant expansion.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT silently omit unsupported kinds, invent confidence or severity, or collapse whole-report failures into local states.
- ❌ Do NOT make explanations caller-authored, localized, persuasive, advisory, notification-oriented, or dependent on display-text ordering.
- ❌ Do NOT expose one public service per metric or signal — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-DETERMINISTIC-REPORT-COMPILER.

## Approach
Implement closed state accounting and deterministic channel-neutral explanation templates for every requested metric or signal.
- Constraint: Templates consume structured values only and must not inspect raw source descriptions.
- Expected output: EvidenceStateAccountant
- Expected output: InsightExplanationRenderer
- Expected output: State and template tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: EvidenceStateAccountant.Account (DM-INSIGHTS-ANALYTICAL-EVIDENCE) — one state per requested kind
- Produces: InsightExplanationRenderer.Render (DM-INSIGHTS-ANALYTICAL-EVIDENCE) — deterministic structured and rendered explanation

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Domain/Insights/Policies/EvidenceStateAccountant.cs` (create) — complete requested-kind accounting
- `src/Tally/Domain/Insights/Reports/InsightExplanationRenderer.cs` (create) — deterministic channel-neutral templates
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Policies/EvidenceStateAccountantTests.cs` (test) — state accounting and whole-report boundary tests
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Reports/InsightExplanationRendererTests.cs` (test) — template, provenance, limitation, and forbidden-wording tests

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~EvidenceStateAccountantTests|FullyQualifiedName~InsightExplanationRendererTests"` — exit 0; both named classes are discovered with at least 12 cases each, every state and whole-report boundary is exercised, forbidden wording and raw-description canaries are absent, and 0 tests fail

## Commit
- `feat(insights): account for evidence states and render explanations` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-358`

## Lex references
- DD-INSIGHTS-DETERMINISTIC-REPORT-COMPILER
- DM-INSIGHTS-ANALYTICAL-EVIDENCE
- FA-INSIGHTS-REPORT-EXPLANATION
- FR-INSIGHTS-EVIDENCE-STATE-ACCOUNTING
- FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-EXPLANATION
- NFR-INSIGHTS-DETERMINISTIC-ANALYTICAL-INTEGRITY
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-01-ANALYTICAL-CORE
- TASK-INSIGHTS-EVIDENCE-EXPLANATION
- TC-INSIGHTS-EVIDENCE-STATE-ACCOUNTING
- TC-INSIGHTS-REPORT-EXPLANATION-CONTRACT
- Deep context: `lex context bd-358 --json`

title: Implement the V001 evidence and reconciliation schema fragment
description:
## Objective
Register one real-SQLite V001 fragment whose constraints preserve provider-neutral evidence identity, one active confirmation, append-only decisions, and complete statement coverage.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On
- bd-23m (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-STORAGE): The fragment registers with LedgerSchemaFragmentRegistry and is exercised through LedgerDb.

## In Scope
- Shared-schema task with no Implements link: create the constraints required by evidence, reconciliation, decisions, coverage, and crash-atomic replay slices.
- Produce: V001EvidenceReconciliationSchema
- Produce: Real-SQLite constraint tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT store raw source payloads, arbitrary metadata JSON, transport identifiers, or mutable reconciliation flags — per DD-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT and DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- Do NOT introduce EF Core, a repository abstraction, or a second database — rejected per DD-LEDGER-EMBEDDED-STORAGE.
- Do NOT implement feature policy in migration SQL beyond structural constraints.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-195 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-RECONCILIATION-AUTHORITY)
  - bd-3qf (TASK-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-REGISTRY)
  - bd-2zt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE)

## Success Criteria
- A fresh real SQLite generation contains Evidence Record/observation/link, Reconciliation Decision/current projection, statement scope/coverage/exception, and required unique/index/foreign-key structures.
- Constraints reject duplicate logical evidence with changed content, multiple active confirming targets, broken decision predecessors, mutable-history replacement, and coverage entries outside their scope.
- V001 composition remains deterministic and idempotent and every table participates in integrity/count manifest discovery.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT store raw source payloads, arbitrary metadata JSON, transport identifiers, or mutable reconciliation flags — per DD-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT and DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- ❌ Do NOT introduce EF Core, a repository abstraction, or a second database — rejected per DD-LEDGER-EMBEDDED-STORAGE.
- ❌ Do NOT implement feature policy in migration SQL beyond structural constraints.

## Approach
- Shared-schema task with no Implements link: create the constraints required by evidence, reconciliation, decisions, coverage, and crash-atomic replay slices.
- Expected output: V001EvidenceReconciliationSchema
- Expected output: Real-SQLite constraint tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: V001EvidenceReconciliationSchema (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY)
- Consumes: LedgerSchemaFragmentRegistry (DD-LEDGER-EMBEDDED-STORAGE)
- Consumes: LedgerDb (DM-LEDGER-STORE-GENERATION)

## Files (reservation globs)

- `tests/Tally.Tests/Infrastructure/Storage/LedgerEvidenceReconciliationSchemaTests.cs` (test) — real SQLite schema verification — required

## Verification

- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.Infrastructure.Storage.LedgerEvidenceReconciliationSchemaTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 14 real-SQLite schema and constraint cases run and 0 fail

title: Implement trusted Johannesburg Analysis Windows
description:
## Objective
AnalysisWindowPolicy returns the exact approved calendar bounds or a named NotStarted or Unsupported state without implicit clocks or locale behavior.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-TRUSTED-ANALYSIS-WINDOWS: Apply trusted calendar Analysis Windows
  - AC: Given any report generation, when processing starts, then one trusted TimeProvider instant is frozen before producer reads and returned as the As-of Instant in Source Provenance.
  - AC: Given any As-of Instant, when its local date is derived, then the fixed IANA timezone Africa/Johannesburg is used and host locale is ignored.
  - AC: Given a day window, when its bounds are returned, then it contains the single As-of local calendar date intersected with the selected Budget Period.
  - AC: Given a week window, when its bounds are returned, then it begins Monday, ends Sunday, and is clipped to the selected Budget Period.
  - AC: Given a month-to-date window, when its bounds are returned, then it begins on the Budget Period start and ends on the earlier of the As-of local date or period end, inclusive.
  - AC: Given a Budget Period completed before the As-of local date, when the month window is returned, then the full Budget Period is selected.
  - AC: Given a Budget Period starting after the As-of local date, when elapsed-time metrics are requested, then Not Started is returned and no zero-spend or pace interpretation is fabricated.
  - AC: Given a rolling-day or rolling-week policy request, when it is validated, then Unsupported Analysis Window is returned before financial processing.
  - AC: Given transaction membership in any window, when actuals are grouped, then LEDGER Effective Date controls membership and posting time or invocation time does not move a transaction between dates.
  - AC: Given a historical Budget Period, when a report is generated now, then the report states that current corrected producer evidence was used and does not claim to reconstruct the source state that existed at a past As-of Instant.

## Depends On
- bd-1qz (TASK-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-MODELS): The policy returns AnalysisWindow and named evidence-state contracts.

## In Scope
- Derive deterministic inclusive day, Monday-week, and month-to-date windows from one frozen TimeProvider instant and Budget Period.
- Produce: AnalysisWindowPolicy
- Produce: Calendar conformance tests

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- The caller freezes TimeProvider.GetUtcNow once before producer reads and AnalysisWindowPolicy converts it with fixed IANA Africa/Johannesburg independent of host locale.
- Day is the As-of local date intersected with the Budget Period; week is Monday through Sunday clipped to the period; month-to-date is period start through the earlier of local As-of date or period end, inclusive.
- A completed historical period returns the full period, a future period returns NotStarted with no zero-spend interpretation, and rolling-day or rolling-week requests fail before financial processing.
- Window membership uses LEDGER Effective Date and historical generation states that current corrected evidence was used rather than claiming source-state reconstruction.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT accept a caller-supplied historical As-of Instant — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-TRUSTED-WINDOW-PACE-POLICY.
- ❌ Do NOT use host locale, posting time, invocation time per member, rolling windows, or inferred daily/weekly sub-budgets.
- ❌ Do NOT read TimeProvider more than once for one report or hide NotStarted as an empty successful window.

## Approach
Derive deterministic inclusive day, Monday-week, and month-to-date windows from one frozen TimeProvider instant and Budget Period.
- Constraint: Use DateOnly boundaries and explicit inclusive semantics.
- Expected output: AnalysisWindowPolicy
- Expected output: Calendar conformance tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: AnalysisWindowPolicy.Evaluate (DM-INSIGHTS-ANALYTICAL-EVIDENCE) — ordered window bounds and named states

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Domain/Insights/Time/AnalysisWindowPolicy.cs` (create) — trusted calendar policy
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Time/AnalysisWindowPolicyTests.cs` (test) — timezone, clipping, future, and historical cases

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~AnalysisWindowPolicyTests` — exit 0; at least 18 Johannesburg conversion, month-boundary, Monday-week, clipping, historical, future, rolling-rejection, Effective-Date, and single-clock cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(insights): implement trusted johannesburg analysis windows` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-36r`

## Lex references
- DD-INSIGHTS-TRUSTED-WINDOW-PACE-POLICY
- DM-INSIGHTS-ANALYTICAL-EVIDENCE
- FR-INSIGHTS-TRUSTED-ANALYSIS-WINDOWS
- NFR-INSIGHTS-DETERMINISTIC-ANALYTICAL-INTEGRITY
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-01-ANALYTICAL-CORE
- TASK-INSIGHTS-ANALYSIS-WINDOWS
- TC-INSIGHTS-ANALYSIS-WINDOW-CONFORMANCE
- Deep context: `lex context bd-36r --json`

- The caller freezes TimeProvider.GetUtcNow once before producer reads and AnalysisWindowPolicy converts it with fixed IANA Africa/Johannesburg independent of host locale.

title: Implement immutable inspect and approval
description:
## Objective
Deliver ingest.inspect and ingest.approve with immutable revision, compatibility, actor, and approval invalidation guarantees.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-MANIFEST-REVIEW: Review and approve an immutable manifest revision
  - AC: Given a completed preview, when the manifest is inspected, then it exposes safe batch identity, source fingerprint, selected account, adapter and LEDGER contract versions, ordered source-record outcomes, candidate facts, exclusions, exact duplicates, overlap conflicts, validation results, and reconciliation evidence.
  - AC: Given a manifest whose transaction-bearing records are all accepted or proven exact duplicates and whose required controls reconcile, when the owner explicitly approves that exact revision, then its approval state and actor are recorded without LEDGER mutation.
  - AC: Given any blocked record, unresolved overlap, failed control, incompatible version, or missing required fact, when approval is attempted, then INGEST rejects approval and preserves the uncommittable manifest.
  - AC: Given an attempted change to amount, currency, date, original description, sign, or provenance, when review is performed, then INGEST rejects inline editing and requires a corrected source or adapter plus a new preview.
  - AC: Given a changed account decision, duplicate disposition, adapter version, or LEDGER contract version, when the batch is reconsidered, then INGEST creates a new manifest revision and invalidates approval of prior revisions for commit.

## Depends On
- bd-1of (TASK-INGEST-PREVIEW-WORKFLOW): Inspect and approve consume persisted immutable manifests created by preview.
- bd-twx (TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): Consumes ImportManifestContract.
- bd-2gk (TASK-INGEST-PREVIEW-DOMAIN): Consumes ManifestCanonicalizer.
- bd-3l1 (TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION): Consumes IngestDatabase.

## In Scope
- Expose the exact sensitive manifest to its owner and record approval only for one committable digest without editing source-derived facts or mutating LEDGER.
- Produce: InspectHandler.
- Produce: ApproveHandler.
- Produce: ReviewStateStore.
- Produce: ReviewOperationModule.
- Produce: ReviewWorkflowTests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- ingest.inspect requires exact batchId plus manifestRevisionId and returns the complete owner-only manifest view: identities, versions, selected account, ordered record outcomes, immutable candidates, exclusions, duplicates, conflicts, controls, validations, and approval state.
- Inspect performs no mutation and deterministic repeated reads return semantically identical ordered content.
- Approve rehashes the stored canonical manifest, checks exact digest/revision, committable state, every record/control, active selected account, compatible adapter/manifest/Ledger versions, and trusted actor attribution before one atomic approval insert.
- Any blocked record, overlap, failed control, incompatible version, missing fact, digest mismatch, stale account, or absent actor returns a stable failure and leaves the revision unapproved.
- Requests cannot update amount, currency, date, originalDescription, sign, provenance, account, adapter, duplicate disposition, or version; changed meaning requires a new preview/revision and prior approval is inactive for commit.
- ReviewOperationModule produces inspect/approve descriptor bindings but global registration waits for GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT implement a generic save/update or inline manifest editor — rejected by DD-INGEST-CLI-OPERATION-CONTRACT and DD-INGEST-MANIFEST-IDENTITY-OVERLAP.
- ❌ Do NOT approve by batchId alone, mutable latest revision, or unchecked digest.
- ❌ Do NOT mutate Ledger or reread/reparse the source during inspect or approve.
- ❌ Do NOT log or send the sensitive manifest to stderr/telemetry; it is allowed only in explicit stdout business output.

## Approach
Expose the exact sensitive manifest to its owner and record approval only for one committable digest without editing source-derived facts or mutating LEDGER.
- Constraint: Inspect is a query; approve is a narrow CommandResult transition with expected domain failures, not an EF/HTTP CRUD save.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: ImportManifestContract (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST)
- Consumes: IngestDatabase (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Consumes: ManifestCanonicalizer (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST)
- Consumes: PreviewStateStore (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Produces: ReviewStateStore (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Produces: ReviewOperationModule (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Review/InspectQuery.cs` (create) — exact manifest query — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Review/InspectHandler.cs` (create) — sensitive owner result mapping — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Review/ApproveCommand.cs` (create) — exact revision transition — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Review/ApproveHandler.cs` (create) — approval orchestration — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Review/ReviewOperationModule.cs` (create) — inspect/approve descriptor bindings — required
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Storage/ReviewStateStore.cs` (create) — manifest read and approval SQL — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Review/ReviewWorkflowTests.cs` (test) — inspect/approval state tests — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~ReviewWorkflowTests` -> exit 0; at least 16 exact-inspect, deterministic-order, approval, blocked, digest, version, actor, inline-edit rejection, invalidation, and no-Ledger-mutation cases are discovered and 0 tests fail

## Commit
- `feat(ingest): implement review workflow` — footer `Refs: bd-37p`

## Lex references
- DD-INGEST-MANIFEST-IDENTITY-OVERLAP
- DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST
- FR-INGEST-MANIFEST-REVIEW
- PLAN-INGEST-V1
- SP-INGEST-02-PREVIEW-REVIEW
- TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION
- TASK-INGEST-PREVIEW-DOMAIN
- TASK-INGEST-PREVIEW-WORKFLOW
- TASK-INGEST-REVIEW-WORKFLOW
- TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION
- TC-INGEST-MANIFEST-REVIEW-CONTRACT
- Deep context: `lex context bd-37p --json`

Constraints:

Interfaces:
1. consumes: ImportManifestContract [DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST]

4. Any blocked record, overlap, failed control, incompatible version, missing fact, digest mismatch, stale account, or absent actor returns a stable failure and leaves the revision unapproved.
5. Requests cannot update amount, currency, date, originalDescription, sign, provenance, account, adapter, duplicate disposition, or version; changed meaning requires a new preview/revision and prior approval is inactive for commit.
6. ReviewOperationModule produces inspect/approve descriptor bindings but global registration waits for GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT.

title: Implement atomic reconciliation dispositions
description:
## Objective
Deliver owner-approved match_existing and create_statement_only plus record_ambiguous and record_exception. Automatic matching remains a stable unsupported-policy result until OQ-LEDGER-13 is resolved and the later activation task completes; statement correction remains the later composite.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-STATEMENT-RECONCILIATION

## Depends On
- bd-3qf (TASK-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-REGISTRY): Apply consumes normalized evidence identity and storage.
- bd-84s (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTIONS-RECORD-GET): Match and statement-only outcomes consume canonical TransactionStore semantics.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Every disposition commits through LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync.
- bd-1zm (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-PROJECTION): Owner-reviewed apply revalidates the advisory projection token and candidate or guard membership.

## In Scope
- Deliver owner-approved match_existing and create_statement_only plus record_ambiguous and record_exception. Automatic matching remains a stable unsupported-policy result until OQ-LEDGER-13 is resolved and the later activation task completes; statement correction remains the later composite.
- Produce: Owner-reviewed reconciliation apply contracts/policy/store/handler/module and crash-atomic integration tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT implement correct_existing_from_statement in this base task; TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-STATEMENT-CORRECTION owns the cross-feature composite.
- Do NOT guess among candidates, treat an advisory token as authority, or accept automatic matching while OQ-LEDGER-13 remains unresolved.
- Do NOT implement INGEST batch/manifests/previews/approvals/receipts, use transport IDs as authority, or overwrite facts, evidence, or decisions.

## Success Criteria
- match_existing requires typed owner authority, actor, reason, statement Evidence Record fingerprint, expected advisory projection token, and one target Canonical Transaction; it revalidates current fact, lifecycle, candidate or guard membership, conflicts, and evidence exclusivity inside one BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction before appending the Reconciliation Decision and confirming link.
- create_statement_only requires typed canonical facts equal to the normalized statement observation plus explicit owner authorization after review; it atomically creates one Statement-only Transaction with explicit unknown Payment Instrument and Cardholder Attribution, an unassigned Pool Assignment, Reconciliation Decision, confirming link, and replay result.
- record_ambiguous preserves the complete reviewed candidate set and applies no confirming link or financial effect; record_exception preserves one Reconciliation Exception with stable code and reason; stale, conflicting, changed replay, or unsupported automatic authority preserves prior state.
- Crash injection at every base write boundary leaves the full outcome absent or committed; identical logical statement Evidence Record and disposition replay returns the existing outcome.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT implement correct_existing_from_statement in this base task; TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-STATEMENT-CORRECTION owns the cross-feature composite.
- ❌ Do NOT guess among candidates, treat an advisory token as authority, or accept automatic matching while OQ-LEDGER-13 remains unresolved.

title: Consume the public BUDGET insight evidence operation
description:
## Objective
INSIGHTS invokes exactly one compatible budget.insights.evidence.get operation through the shared executor and exposes its typed result without private or mutation access.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-PUBLIC-EVIDENCE: Bind one coherent public evidence set
  - AC: Given a valid Budget Period with a selected plan revision, when evidence is bound, then the immutable plan identity, revision status, exact entries, exact totals, category lifecycle evidence, and contract provenance come from the released BUDGET read contract.
  - AC: Given a valid Budget Period with no Budget Plan, when evidence is bound, then No Budget Plan is explicit and no invented plan, zero plan, or Not Found result is substituted.
  - AC: Given a valid Budget Period with a plan but no Active revision and no explicit revision selector, when evidence is bound, then No Active Budget Plan Revision is explicit and no revision is inferred.
  - AC: Given date-granular actual evidence, when it is consumed, then every page belongs to one public snapshot or authoritative state binding and every ordinal is present exactly once.
  - AC: Given a complete evidence set with a Budget Position, when actual members are reconciled, then each member is assigned exactly once to Budgeted, Zero Budget, Unbudgeted, or Uncategorized and every exact bucket and total equals the cited Budget Position.
  - AC: Given a correction or producer write concurrent with evidence acquisition, when one coherent state cannot be proven, then generation returns Source State Changed and emits no Insight Report or Report Snapshot.
  - AC: Given cursor expiry, missing or duplicate ordinal, incompatible version, stale generation, unknown category identity, or reconciliation mismatch, when evidence binding fails, then no partial report or retained state is produced.
  - AC: Given CLASSIFY coverage is incomplete, when evidence is bound, then current LEDGER category allocation is used and Uncategorized Spend remains explicit without a direct CLASSIFY call.
  - AC: Given evidence acquisition, when dependency boundaries are inspected, then INSIGHTS invoked only released public operations, performed no upstream mutation, read no private store, and did not reproduce BUDGET position calculation.

## Depends On
- bd-uh9 (TASK-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): The client returns stable INSIGHTS errors and uses source-generated process contracts.
- bd-200 (TASK-INSIGHTS-GATE-INT-BUDGET-EVIDENCE): The public producer seam must be proven before INSIGHTS consumes it.

## In Scope
- Implement the single concrete public-operation client used by INSIGHTS to acquire one bounded BudgetInsightEvidence result.
- Produce: BudgetContractClient
- Produce: Public-operation compatibility tests

## Out of Scope
- The client transports producer evidence; CoherentEvidenceBinder owns INSIGHTS validation.

## Success Criteria
- BudgetContractClient resolves the released budget.insights.evidence.get descriptor from OperationRegistry and invokes it once through the shared public operation executor with CancellationToken.
- Supported BoundRevision, NoBudgetPlan, and NoActiveBudgetPlanRevision results deserialize through source-generated contracts without changing their plan-state, member, total, snapshot, generation, version, or binding evidence.
- Unsupported BUDGET or LEDGER versions, Source State Changed, resource limits, malformed results, and executor failures return stable typed INSIGHTS failures before report output or retained writes.
- The client exposes no mutation descriptor and performs no retry that could join evidence from separate producer captures.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT add an IBudgetContractClient interface or transport adapter — the concrete client over the existing shared executor is selected per DD-INSIGHTS-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE.
- ❌ Do NOT call budget.position.get plus ledger.actuals.query, resume a cursor, or calculate Budget Position — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-PUBLIC-EVIDENCE.
- ❌ Do NOT open BUDGET or LEDGER storage, import private feature handlers or domain types, grant mutation authority, or log request/result payloads.

## Approach
Implement the single concrete public-operation client used by INSIGHTS to acquire one bounded BudgetInsightEvidence result.
- Constraint: Consume only the verified descriptor and DTOs established by TASK-INSIGHTS-GATE-INT-BUDGET-EVIDENCE.
- Expected output: BudgetContractClient
- Expected output: Public-operation compatibility tests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: VerifiedBudgetInsightEvidenceContract (DM-BUDGET-INSIGHTS-READ-CONTRACT) — proved public prerequisite
- Produces: BudgetContractClient.GetInsightEvidenceAsync (DM-BUDGET-INSIGHTS-READ-CONTRACT) — one typed composite result through the released registry

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Application/OperationAbstractions.cs` (read) — shared executor abstraction
- `src/Tally/Integration/Budget/BudgetContractClient.cs` (create) — single public BUDGET operation client
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Evidence/BudgetContractClientTests.cs` (test) — descriptor, compatibility, and single-call tests

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~BudgetContractClientTests` — exit 0; at least 12 descriptor, three-state deserialization, single-call, cancellation, compatibility, source-change, limit, mutation-exclusion, private-access, and payload-log cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(insights): consume budget insight evidence operation` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-388`

## Lex references
- DD-INSIGHTS-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE
- DD-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-PUBLIC-EVIDENCE
- DM-BUDGET-INSIGHTS-READ-CONTRACT
- EXT-INSIGHTS-BUDGET-PUBLIC-READ-CONTRACT
- FR-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-PUBLIC-EVIDENCE
- NFR-INSIGHTS-PUBLIC-CONTRACT-COMPATIBILITY
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1

- TASK-INSIGHTS-BUDGET-EVIDENCE-CLIENT

title: Verify agent contract discovery and invocation
description:
## Objective
Prove a zero-context AI Agent Host can discover and safely drive every INGEST transition without parsing prose or inventing identity.

## Implements
- No direct FR ownership: this is an evidence, integration, security, use-case, or completion gate.
- Proves or constrains NFR-INGEST-AGENT-OPERABILITY: Keep agent invocation deterministic and inspectable (satisfies).
- Proves or constrains NFR-INGEST-PUBLIC-CONTRACT-COMPATIBILITY: Preserve public contract compatibility boundaries (satisfies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY-CONTRACT: Verify INGEST contract discovery (verifies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-PUBLISHED-CONTRACT-MATRIX: Verify the published INGEST operation contract (verifies).
- Proves or constrains UC-INGEST-006: Discover and invoke the INGEST agent contract (covers).

## Depends On
- bd-2jn (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): UC verification begins from the complete published descriptor-generated contract.

## In Scope
- Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-006 schema discovery, structured invocation, compatibility, stable exits, and recovery-directed agent behavior.
- Produce: UCIngest006Evidence.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- A clean client discovers exactly eight operations, request/result schemas, two variant descriptors, limits, versions, stable errors/exits, examples, and next-operation semantics without source/store access.
- The client constructs valid stdin/file JSON, invokes preview through cleanup non-interactively, receives exactly one stdout envelope per call, uses metadata-only stderr, and branches on structured outcome/exit rather than prose.
- Unknown operation/schema returns help plus stable usage failure; malformed/unknown-field input fails before source processing.
- Incompatible client/INGEST/adapter/manifest/Ledger versions fail before parsing or mutation.
- Expected domain failures return module-scoped stable error and non-zero documented exit without stack trace.
- After output loss the client cannot construct a new identity to continue; status/resume with the original batch recovers safely and no daemon, prompt, network, or source reread is required.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT use internal types, database inspection, interactive prompts, prose scraping, or reconstructed candidate IDs in the agent harness.
- ❌ Do NOT add bundled skill content not generated from the same descriptors.

## Approach
Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-006 schema discovery, structured invocation, compatibility, stable exits, and recovery-directed agent behavior.
- Constraint: The harness starts with only tally on PATH plus generated schema output and runs with network access disabled.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CompleteIngestPublicContract (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: UCIngest006Evidence (UC-INGEST-006)

title: Compile one deterministic immutable Insight Report
description:
## Objective
InsightReportCompiler returns one complete bounded deterministic report or one whole-report failure from normalized input, frozen time, validated evidence, and released policies.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-GENERATION: Generate one deterministic Insight Report
  - AC: Given a valid generation request and coherent evidence, when generation succeeds, then exactly one ephemeral Insight Report is returned and no durable INSIGHTS state changes.
  - AC: Given omitted optional policy selectors, when generation succeeds, then the released default window, formula, Evidence Sufficiency, explanation, and ordering versions are explicit in the result.
  - AC: Given one report, when its content is inspected, then report identity, Budget Period, trusted As-of Instant, exact windows, plan state, exact metrics, every requested signal state, limitations, and Source Provenance are present.
  - AC: Given identical trusted As-of Instant, normalized request values, coherent source identities, and policy versions, when generation is repeated, then semantically identical ordered content and the same content fingerprint are returned.
  - AC: Given categories, metrics, states, and explanations, when report ordering is inspected, then it follows the released deterministic ordering policy and never depends on localized display text.
  - AC: Given one report, when Source Provenance is inspected, then all producer contract versions, authoritative state or snapshot identities, plan revision, category contract, report schema, policy versions, and content fingerprints are present.
  - AC: Given a requested horizon, category count, metric count, or output size over a released limit, when validation occurs, then resource failure is returned before producer reads or report output.
  - AC: Given a successful report, when its claims are inspected, then it describes recorded Tally evidence and does not claim complete bank activity, financial advice, enforcement, or historical source reconstruction.

## Depends On
- bd-2a8 (TASK-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-EVIDENCE-BINDER): The compiler accepts only BoundInsightEvidence.
- bd-36r (TASK-INSIGHTS-ANALYSIS-WINDOWS): The compiler composes trusted windows.
- bd-mbq (TASK-INSIGHTS-EXACT-METRICS): The compiler composes exact metric results.
- bd-29y (TASK-INSIGHTS-LINEAR-PACE): The compiler composes the active pace policy.
- bd-358 (TASK-INSIGHTS-EVIDENCE-EXPLANATION): The compiler requires complete state accounting and explanations.

## In Scope
- Orchestrate the pure evidence, window, metric, pace, state, explanation, ordering, provenance, canonicalization, and fingerprint policies without storage or producer access.
- Produce: InsightReportCompiler
- Produce: InsightReportCanonicalizer
- Produce: Determinism and failure-atomicity tests

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- InsightReportCompiler accepts only normalized request values, one frozen As-of Instant, one BoundInsightEvidence, and released policy descriptors; it performs no producer or storage call.
- The complete report contains budget period, trusted time, exact windows, plan state, exact metrics, every requested signal state, deterministic explanations, limitations, and complete source and policy provenance in released order.
- Repeated compilation with identical inputs produces semantically identical ordered content, contentFingerprint, and reportId; collection order never depends on localized display text.
- InsightReportCanonicalizer serializes the semantic payload without reportId and contentFingerprint, hashes canonical UTF-8 JSON with SHA-256, assigns that hash as contentFingerprint, then derives reportId.
- Any coherence, compatibility, integrity, completeness, exact-arithmetic, canonicalization, or output-limit failure returns no report and no partial financial payload.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT embed all calculation inside Generate or expose per-metric orchestration to callers — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-DETERMINISTIC-REPORT-COMPILER.
- ❌ Do NOT read a producer, store state, use reflection serialization, hash derived identity fields into themselves, depend on display text, or emit partial reports.
- ❌ Do NOT add Forecast, Trend, Anomaly, or Attention Item formulas while their FRs remain deferred.

## Approach
Orchestrate the pure evidence, window, metric, pace, state, explanation, ordering, provenance, canonicalization, and fingerprint policies without storage or producer access.
- Constraint: The compiler and all collaborators remain concrete pure classes; no IReportCompiler or policy interfaces are introduced.
- Expected output: InsightReportCompiler
- Expected output: InsightReportCanonicalizer
- Expected output: Determinism and failure-atomicity tests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CoherentEvidenceBinder.Bind (DM-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-EVIDENCE) — validated evidence
- Consumes: AnalysisWindowPolicy.Evaluate (DM-INSIGHTS-ANALYTICAL-EVIDENCE) — trusted windows
- Consumes: ExactMetricCalculator.Calculate (DM-INSIGHTS-ANALYTICAL-EVIDENCE) — exact metrics
- Consumes: LinearPaceCalculator.Calculate (DM-INSIGHTS-ANALYTICAL-EVIDENCE) — pace state
- Consumes: EvidenceStateAccountant.Account (DM-INSIGHTS-ANALYTICAL-EVIDENCE) — complete state inventory
- Consumes: InsightExplanationRenderer.Render (DM-INSIGHTS-ANALYTICAL-EVIDENCE) — template evidence
- Produces: InsightReportCanonicalizer.Canonicalize (DM-INSIGHTS-INSIGHT-REPORT) — ordered canonical report and derived identity
- Produces: InsightReportCompiler.Compile (DM-INSIGHTS-INSIGHT-REPORT) — complete report or whole-report failure

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/Reports/InsightReportCompiler.cs` (create) — deep report orchestration boundary
- `src/Tally/Domain/Insights/Reports/InsightReportCanonicalizer.cs` (create) — stable ordering, canonical JSON, fingerprint, and report identity
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Reports/InsightReportCompilerTests.cs` (test) — complete report, determinism, limits, and whole-report failure tests
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Reports/InsightReportCanonicalizerTests.cs` (test) — ordering and non-self-referential hash tests

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~InsightReportCompilerTests|FullyQualifiedName~InsightReportCanonicalizerTests"` — exit 0; both named classes are discovered with at least 14 cases each, identical-input fingerprints are byte-stable, derived identity fields are excluded from their hash, every requested kind is accounted for, all whole-report failures produce no report, and 0 tests fail

## Commit

title: SpendPoolWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove Spend Pool lifecycle, assignment, unassigned behavior, and exact pool/category conservation across finance corrections.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-POOL-ASSIGNMENT: Assign and correct transaction Spend Pools
  - AC: Given an active spend transaction, active Spend Pool, reason, actor context, and Idempotency Identity, when the pool is assigned, then exactly one current Pool Assignment is recorded independently from category, account, Payment Instrument, and cardholder.
  - AC: Given a current Pool Assignment and an active replacement pool or explicit unassigned state, when it is corrected with a reason, then the prior assignment is retired, the new state becomes current, and both remain attributable in history.
  - AC: Given an inactive transaction, archived pool, or missing correction reason, when assignment is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and changes no pool history or actuals.
  - AC: Given a confirmed transfer, when pool actuals are queried, then its principal contributes exactly zero to every pool even if either leg has historical pool attribution.
  - AC: Given an active linked refund or reversal, when pool and pool-by-category actuals are queried, then its magnitude offsets the original transaction's current pool and category in the refund's own Effective Date period; unassigned originals offset the explicit unassigned-pool bucket.
- FR-LEDGER-SPEND-POOL-CATALOGUE: Maintain the Spend Pool catalogue
  - AC: Given a valid unique pool name such as company-paid discretionary or personal after-tax, when the owner creates a Spend Pool, then one active pool with a stable identifier is returned.
  - AC: Given an existing Spend Pool, when the owner renames it, then its stable identifier and historical transaction assignments remain unchanged.
  - AC: Given a referenced Spend Pool, when the owner archives it, then history remains queryable and new assignments are rejected until the same identity is reactivated.
  - AC: Given account, Payment Instrument, cardholder, category, and pool records, when any is created or changed, then no value in one dimension silently creates, selects, renames, or archives a value in another.
  - AC: Given active and archived pools, when they are listed with an explicit lifecycle filter, then each matching pool appears once with stable identity and assignment counts.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace UC-LEDGER-018 through the Release published CLI.
- Produce: UC018SpendPoolWorkflowTests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT infer pool from any other dimension or hide unassigned transactions.
- Do NOT accept totals that reconcile only in aggregate but fail per pool/category cell.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- The published workflow creates/archives/reactivates pools, assigns/corrects active transactions, preserves explicit unassigned state, and queries exact pool, category, and pool-by-category totals.
- Pool conflict/archive, identical/changed replay, inactive transaction, stale assignment, write failure, and unassigned query cover every UC-LEDGER-018 failure path.
- Refunds offset the original current pool/category in their own period, transfers contribute zero spend, and void/supersession/correction histories preserve exact all-up equals grouped totals.

## Failure Criteria

title: StatementReconciliationWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove exact confirmation, authoritative correction, statement-only creation, and ambiguity leave at most one active economic effect.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-STATEMENT-RECONCILIATION: Apply statement reconciliation outcomes
  - AC: Given one unique compatible unreconciled transaction whose canonical facts equal the registered statement observation, when a supported deterministic or owner-approved match is applied, then statement evidence links to that existing transaction, one decision is appended, its state becomes statement-reconciled, and no transaction is created.
  - AC: Given one owner-approved or policy-supported compatible prior event whose canonical facts differ from authoritative statement facts, when correction from statement is applied, then one statement-derived replacement fact, supersession, decision, confirming link, explicit valid dimension carry-forward events, and any invariant-preserving relationship replacement commit atomically; the prior fact becomes historical and exactly one replacement remains active.
  - AC: Given no compatible prior transaction, registered statement evidence, approved canonical facts, and explicit authorization to create a statement-only outcome, when reconciliation is applied, then exactly one statement-only Canonical Transaction is created and linked atomically.
  - AC: Given multiple compatible or guard candidates, when reconciliation is requested, then one ambiguous current outcome records stable evidence and candidate identities while no link, correction, or Canonical Transaction is applied.
  - AC: Given statement evidence conflicts with an existing active confirmation, cannot be associated with one economic event, or cannot support an internally valid replacement, when reconciliation is requested, then one Reconciliation Exception with stable reasons is appended and no active financial effect changes.
  - AC: Given an unsupported automatic policy or a candidate that fails independent compatibility or lifecycle validation, when automatic reconciliation is requested, then a stable validation error is returned and no decision, link, correction, or transaction is created.
  - AC: Given a request whose supplied candidates, policy identity, observation fingerprint, authoritative statement facts, or match basis differs from the current Ledger projection, when it is applied, then the Ledger rejects the stale request without mutation.
  - AC: Given the same logical statement evidence and reconciliation request is replayed, when it is submitted again, then the original transaction, replacement if any, decisions, links, and carry-forward results are returned with zero duplicate effects.
  - AC: Given the prior transaction participates in an active transfer or refund relationship, when statement correction would violate role, account, sign, currency, principal, or refund conservation, then the Ledger returns a review-required exception and applies no replacement; when the relationship remains valid with the replacement, one explicit attributable relationship replacement commits with it.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace statement-authoritative match-first reconciliation through the Release CLI.
- Produce: UC014StatementReconciliationWorkflowTests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT bypass the published CLI, apply ambiguity, overwrite the agent-capture fact, create two active effects, or omit the relationship review block.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- An exact row confirms the existing transaction without creation; one compatible event with differing facts creates one statement-derived replacement and supersedes the provisional fact with explicit category/pool/payment carry-forward.
- Compatible active transfer/refund relationships are explicitly replaced and remain valid; incompatible replacement returns review-required and leaves the prior financial state unchanged.
- Approved zero-candidate evidence creates one statement-only fact with unknown payment and unassigned pool; multiple/guard candidates remain ambiguous without link or financial effect.
- Replay, unsupported policy, missing owner authority/basis, candidate conflict, stale authoritative facts, active confirmation conflict, write/crash failure, and changed idempotency request cover every failure path.
- Process output and history expose prior/active IDs, authority basis, decision, link, carry-forward, relationship events, and exact before/after actuals; raw email/statement payloads never enter Tally.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT bypass the published CLI, apply ambiguity, overwrite the agent-capture fact, create two active effects, or omit the relationship review block.

## Approach
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace statement-authoritative match-first reconciliation through the Release CLI.
- Expected output: UC014StatementReconciliationWorkflowTests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: PublishedTallyFixture
- Consumes: CompletePublicContract (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — 72 provider-neutral operations
- Produces: VerifiedUC014 (UC-LEDGER-014)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/EndToEnd/UC014StatementReconciliationWorkflowTests.cs` (test) — published-process verification — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.EndToEnd.UC014StatementReconciliationWorkflowTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 14 match, statement-only, ambiguity, conflict, crash, and replay scenarios run and 0 fail

## Commit
