title: Implement the qualified layout B adapter
description:
## Objective
Produce deterministic layout B statement evidence and normalized source records without a generic template or fallback parser.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-FINANCIAL-NORMALIZATION: Normalize exact source financial facts
  - AC: Given a transaction-bearing source record with complete evidence, when it is normalized, then the candidate preserves the selected account, original description, exact base-10 amount, ZAR, transaction date, optional distinct posting date, adapter version, source fingerprint, source-context record identity, and source reference without precision loss.
  - AC: Given an asset or liability statement movement, when its signed amount is normalized, then positive means the owner economic position improved and negative means it worsened: asset increases and liability reductions are positive; asset decreases and liability increases are negative.
  - AC: Given a source transaction date that includes its year, when it is normalized, then that exact calendar date is the Effective Date.
  - AC: Given a source row with zero monetary movement, when it is accounted for, then it is retained as an explicitly excluded non-transaction record with a stable reason and no zero-amount LEDGER candidate is created.
  - AC: Given a missing or ambiguous amount, sign, currency, date, description, record boundary, account meaning, or provenance field, when normalization is attempted, then the source record is blocked without inventing or editing a value.
  - AC: Given a row date without a year in the qualified yearless layout, when exactly one matching calendar date falls inside the explicit statement period, then that date is the Effective Date; otherwise the batch is blocked.
- FR-INGEST-SOURCE-RECONCILIATION: Reconcile every supported statement before approval
  - AC: Given a qualified statement, when preview completes, then every source record has exactly one accepted-candidate, exact-duplicate, excluded-non-transaction, or blocked outcome with a stable reason.
  - AC: Given any supported initial fixture, when its normalized non-zero movements are summed with its opening economic balance, then the result equals its closing economic balance exactly in minor units.
  - AC: Given a supported variant that supplies running balances, when records are normalized, then every consecutive running-balance transition reconciles exactly to its signed movement.
  - AC: Given a source control that the detected variant exposes, when preview completes, then the manifest records and verifies that control exactly.
  - AC: Given an unexplained transaction-bearing record, amount mismatch, running-balance mismatch, opening/closing mismatch, or ambiguous exclusion, when reconciliation runs, then the manifest remains unapproved and uncommittable.
  - AC: Given a control type that the source does not expose, when preview completes, then the manifest reports that control as unavailable rather than fabricating a value.
- FR-INGEST-VARIANT-QUALIFICATION: Qualify and detect supported statement variants
  - AC: Given any of the three qualified private fixtures, when variant detection runs, then exactly one of the two versioned text-native PDF variants is selected from structural evidence rather than filename or extension.
  - AC: Given the qualified permission-encrypted fixture that is text-extractable without an opening password, when it is previewed twice, then each run selects exactly the fixture manifest variantId, reproduces every expected ordered record and reconciliation control, requires no opening password, OCR, external process, or external fetch, completes within 5 seconds and 256 MiB peak resident memory, and creates no LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a scan-only PDF, opening-password requirement, unsupported tabular export, active-content dependency, external fetch, malformed structure, ambiguous variant, or changed layout, when detection runs, then INGEST returns a stable unsupported or unsafe-source result and creates no committable manifest or LEDGER transaction.
  - AC: Given a source exceeding any advertised adapter bound for size, pages, records, nesting, decompression, memory, or processing time, when preview is attempted, then processing stops with a stable resource-limit result before mutation.
  - AC: Given a proposed new or revised adapter, when it lacks representative fixtures that account for every source record and reconcile every required financial control deterministically, then the variant cannot be advertised as supported.

## Depends On
- bd-1j2 (TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION): The adapter implements IStatementAdapter over PdfDocumentEvidence and uses the private fixture helper.

## In Scope
- Encode only the structural detection, row boundaries, financial semantics, exclusions, and source controls proven by the private layout B fixture, including permission encryption and yearless dates.
- Produce: PdfTextLayoutBStatementAdapter.
- Produce: LayoutBStatementAdapterTests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Descriptor advertises exactly pdf-text-layout-b-v1, its adapter/extraction/manifest versions, media type, and hard limits.
- Probe selects exact_match only from layout B structural evidence, including the permission-encrypted but opening-password-free fixture, and returns no_match for layout A and unsupported fixtures.
- Extract accounts for every layout B source record in order, preserves raw evidence privately, distinguishes transaction and non-transaction records, and exposes all variant controls.
- A yearless source date resolves only when exactly one calendar date lies inside the explicit statement period; zero or multiple matches block the record and batch.
- Layout B account-class, owner-economic sign, zero-movement exclusion, amount, description, source-reference, and control rules reproduce every private expected fact in integer minor units.
- Two runs produce identical ordered evidence and structural positions; password-required, scan-only, malformed, or drifted structures fail closed.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT add conditional branches for layout A or future banks — rejected single-parser alternative per DD-INGEST-FORMAT-ADAPTERS.
- ❌ Do NOT guess a year, use filename/extension, fuzzy-match rows, or fall back to OCR/template configuration.
- ❌ Do NOT commit private fixtures, expected facts, filenames, extracted text, or payload snapshots.

## Approach
Encode only the structural detection, row boundaries, financial semantics, exclusions, and source controls proven by the private layout B fixture, including permission encryption and yearless dates.
- Constraint: All private expected facts come through PrivateStatementFixtureSet and remain outside source control.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: IStatementAdapter (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Consumes: PdfDocumentEvidence (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)
- Consumes: PrivateStatementFixtureSet (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT)
- Produces: PdfTextLayoutBStatementAdapter (DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Pdf/PdfTextLayoutBStatementAdapter.cs` (create) — layout B probe and extraction — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Adapters/LayoutBStatementAdapterTests.cs` (test) — private golden-fixture tests — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~LayoutBStatementAdapterTests` -> exit 0; at least 12 probe, encryption, yearless-date, drift, row-accounting, financial-fact, control, and repeatability cases are discovered and 0 tests fail

## Commit

## Lex references

- DM-INGEST-FORMAT-EVIDENCE
- FR-INGEST-FINANCIAL-NORMALIZATION
- FR-INGEST-SOURCE-RECONCILIATION
- FR-INGEST-VARIANT-QUALIFICATION
- NFR-INGEST-DETERMINISTIC-INTEGRITY
- PLAN-INGEST-V1
- SP-INGEST-01-FORMAT-CORE

- TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION
- TC-INGEST-ADAPTER-GOLDEN-FIXTURES

title: CategoryCatalogueWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove stable category identity and deterministic acyclic ancestry across every lifecycle operation.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-CATEGORY-CATALOGUE: Maintain the Spend Category catalogue
  - AC: Given a valid category name and optional active parent Category ID, when the owner creates a category, then the Ledger returns one active category with a stable identifier and its current ancestry path.
  - AC: Given an active category hierarchy, when any node is inspected or listed, then each category exposes its stable ID, optional parent ID, depth, active state, and deterministic ancestry path without requiring callers to reconstruct the tree from names.
  - AC: Given an existing category, when the owner renames it, then its stable identifier, parent relationship, child relationships, and historical transaction assignments remain unchanged.
  - AC: Given an active category and an active proposed parent, when the owner reparents it with a reason, then one append-only parent-change event becomes current and category roll-ups use the new acyclic hierarchy.
  - AC: Given a category is proposed as its own parent or descendant, a proposed parent is archived, or the move would create a cycle, when reparenting is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and preserves the current hierarchy.
  - AC: Given any category with historical transaction, budget, or hierarchy references, when physical deletion is requested, then the Ledger rejects it and retains the stable category and history; archival is the supported lifecycle action.
  - AC: Given an archived category with no invalid archived ancestor, when the owner reactivates it, then the same stable identifier becomes active and may receive new assignments or active children.
  - AC: Given Spend Categories and Spend Pools, when either catalogue is maintained, then its identity, lifecycle, and naming remain independent and no value silently creates or selects a value in the other catalogue.
  - AC: Given an active category with active children, when archival is requested, then the Ledger rejects it without mutation; given no active children, archival succeeds and historical assignments and parent events remain queryable.
- FR-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-WRITES: Make public writes idempotent
  - AC: Given any previously successful public mutation, when the same Idempotency Identity, operation, contract version, and semantically identical request are submitted again, then the original structured result is returned and no second domain, decision, link, or artifact effect occurs.
  - AC: Given a previously successful Idempotency Identity, when it is reused for another operation, contract version, or materially different request, then the same module-scoped conflict code is returned on every retry and the original effect is preserved.
  - AC: Given an invalid mutation request, when validation fails, then no domain or artifact effect occurs and the supplied Idempotency Identity remains available for a corrected request.
  - AC: Given evidence registration or linking, reconciliation application or correction, payment attribution, Pool Assignment, or pool maintenance, when its logical identity is replayed through another request identity, then the domain uniqueness rule prevents a duplicate effect independently of request replay.
  - AC: Given any public mutation without a valid caller-visible Idempotency Identity, when invocation is attempted, then the Ledger rejects it before mutation with a stable validation error.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace hierarchical category maintenance through the Release CLI.
- Produce: UC008CategoryCatalogueWorkflowTests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT bypass the CLI, infer hierarchy from names, omit cycle/active-child cases, or accept flat-catalogue behavior.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- Published create/get/list/rename/reparent/archive/reactivate calls prove roots/children, sibling uniqueness, deterministic ancestry/depth, stable identity, and append-only lifecycle/parent history.

title: Implement deterministic interrupted-commit resume
description:
## Objective
Deliver ingest.resume so repeated recovery converges without a second canonical transaction or a changed request.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-DURABLE-RECEIPT-RESUME: Record durable outcomes and resume interrupted commit
  - AC: Given an approved batch commit, when any candidate receives a structured LEDGER result, then INGEST durably records its accepted, Exact Duplicate, conflicted, rejected, or unresolved receipt outcome before attempting the next candidate.
  - AC: Given interruption after a durable receipt outcome, when resume runs, then it skips terminal candidates and reuses the exact frozen request and original idempotency identity only for unresolved candidates.
  - AC: Given interruption after LEDGER committed but before INGEST recorded the outcome, when resume replays the original request and identity, then LEDGER returns the original result and INGEST records it without a second transaction.
  - AC: Given any resume or status request, when it runs, then it uses the frozen manifest and durable receipt rather than rereading or reparsing the caller-owned source.
  - AC: Given every candidate has a Terminal Candidate Outcome, when commit or resume completes, then INGEST returns one complete receipt with exact outcome counts and canonical references and marks no candidate merely processed.

## Depends On
- bd-2i6 (TASK-INGEST-COMMIT-SAGA): Resume reuses CandidateCommitSaga, BatchCommitLock, and CommitStateStore.

## In Scope
- Reuse CandidateCommitSaga for only unresolved frozen requests and prove every before/after Ledger and receipt-durability crash window.
- Produce: ResumeHandler.
- Produce: ResumeOperationModule.
- Produce: Fault-injection crash matrix tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Resume requires one batchId, acquires the same BatchCommitLock, loads only the frozen approved manifest/requests/keys and durable receipt, and never rereads or reparses the source.
- Terminal accepted, exact_duplicate, conflicted, and rejected candidates are skipped only after any stored canonical reference still validates; pending, attempting, or unresolved candidates replay the exact stored request/key in original order.
- Fault injection before Ledger call, after Ledger commit, before INGEST receipt durability, after receipt durability, and between candidates produces a recoverable durable frontier and stops further candidates.
- A crash after Ledger commit but before receipt durability replays the original key and records the original public result with no second transaction.
- Changed request bytes, manifest digest, candidate identity, adapter/manifest/Ledger compatibility, canonical detail, or reused key with changed facts returns stable conflict and does not continue.
- Repeated resume converges to one complete receipt with exact terminal counts; concurrent resume/commit is rejected by the per-batch lock.
- ResumeOperationModule binds ingest.resume but global registration waits for the public-contract gate.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT generate a new idempotency key/request, choose a latest manifest, reread the source, retry terminal candidates blindly, or proceed after mismatch.
- ❌ Do NOT add a second recovery engine; call CandidateCommitSaga.ExecuteAsync with resume mode — per DD-INGEST-COMMIT-RECOVERY.
- ❌ Do NOT use an in-memory cursor or hold a database transaction across public Ledger execution.

## Approach
Reuse CandidateCommitSaga for only unresolved frozen requests and prove every before/after Ledger and receipt-durability crash window.
- Constraint: Fault hooks are internal test seams only and must not become public configuration or production plugin ports.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CandidateCommitSaga.ExecuteAsync (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-RECEIPT)
- Consumes: BatchCommitLock (DD-INGEST-COMMIT-RECOVERY)
- Consumes: CommitStateStore (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Produces: ResumeOperationModule (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Commit/ResumeCommand.cs` (create) — batch resume input — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Commit/ResumeHandler.cs` (create) — resume validation and saga invocation — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Commit/ResumeOperationModule.cs` (create) — resume descriptor binding — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/CommitRecovery/CommitFaultInjector.cs` (test) — internal crash-point harness — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/CommitRecovery/ResumeCrashMatrixTests.cs` (test) — full recovery matrix — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~ResumeCrashMatrixTests` -> exit 0; all 5 required interruption boundaries plus changed-request, incompatible-version, concurrent-lock, canonical-mismatch, and repeated-resume cases run; at least 16 cases are discovered and 0 tests fail with zero second transactions

## Commit
- `feat(ingest): implement commit resume` — footer `Refs: bd-3ey`

## Lex references
- DD-INGEST-COMMIT-RECOVERY
- DD-INGEST-MANIFEST-IDENTITY-OVERLAP

- FA-INGEST-RECOVERY-CLEANUP
- FR-INGEST-DURABLE-RECEIPT-RESUME
- NFR-INGEST-INTERRUPTED-COMMIT-RECOVERY
- PLAN-INGEST-V1
- SP-INGEST-03-COMMIT-RECOVERY
- TASK-INGEST-COMMIT-SAGA
- TASK-INGEST-RESUME-WORKFLOW
- TC-INGEST-COMMIT-RECOVERY-MATRIX

title: StorageEvolutionWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove isolated storage evolution preserves complete provider-neutral durable state and only activates a verified candidate after explicit authorization.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-WRITES: Make public writes idempotent
  - AC: Given any previously successful public mutation, when the same Idempotency Identity, operation, contract version, and semantically identical request are submitted again, then the original structured result is returned and no second domain, decision, link, or artifact effect occurs.
  - AC: Given a previously successful Idempotency Identity, when it is reused for another operation, contract version, or materially different request, then the same module-scoped conflict code is returned on every retry and the original effect is preserved.
  - AC: Given an invalid mutation request, when validation fails, then no domain or artifact effect occurs and the supplied Idempotency Identity remains available for a corrected request.
  - AC: Given evidence registration or linking, reconciliation application or correction, payment attribution, Pool Assignment, or pool maintenance, when its logical identity is replayed through another request identity, then the domain uniqueness rule prevents a duplicate effect independently of request replay.
  - AC: Given any public mutation without a valid caller-visible Idempotency Identity, when invocation is attempted, then the Ledger rejects it before mutation with a stable validation error.
- FR-LEDGER-SAFE-STORAGE-EVOLUTION: Upgrade or migrate Ledger storage safely
  - AC: Given a supported source Ledger, when an upgrade or migration is requested, then compatibility, integrity, permissions, available space, and required recovery conditions are checked before source mutation.
  - AC: Given successful preflight, when evolution begins, then an independently verified complete-state recovery point is established before candidate activation.
  - AC: Given a completed candidate, when validation runs, then target storage version, every Durable Ledger State type, evidence and reconciliation link, category and pool history, attribution, financial relationship, idempotency outcome, per-type count, cardinality rule, integrity result, and exact pool/category total matches the source and declared migration outcome before activation.
  - AC: Given successful candidate validation, when activation occurs, then it is atomic and the new version becomes authoritative exactly once.
  - AC: Given any preflight, conversion, validation, or activation failure, when the operation ends, then the original Ledger remains authoritative and no partially evolved store is exposed.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace revised UC-LEDGER-012 through Release storage status and evolution operations.
- Produce: UC012StorageEvolutionWorkflowTests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT compare only schema version, row totals, or aggregate money.
- Do NOT transform the authoritative generation in place.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- Preflight validates source integrity, versions, reconciliation policy, permissions, space, host protection, and creates an independently verified complete-state recovery point before transformation.
- The isolated candidate comparison covers every fact/history/dimension/evidence/reconciliation/relationship/request-and-logical-replay type, per-type count, privacy rule, integrity result, and exact named/dimensional total.
- Unsupported, corrupt, incomplete, privacy-invalid, interrupted, unauthorized, or mismatching candidates never replace CURRENT; authorized activation is atomic, retains recovery generation, and is recoverable after post-activation failure.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT compare only schema version, row totals, or aggregate money.
- ❌ Do NOT transform the authoritative generation in place.

## Approach
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace revised UC-LEDGER-012 through Release storage status and evolution operations.
- Constraint: Invoke the Release published tally executable; inspect stdout, stderr, exit code, and real temporary SQLite state.
- Expected output: UC012StorageEvolutionWorkflowTests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: PublishedTallyFixture (Release published-process E2E fixture)
- Consumes: CompletePublicContract (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — Exactly 72 provider-neutral operations
- Produces: VerifiedUC012 (UC-LEDGER-012) — complete-state evolution workflow

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/EndToEnd/UC012StorageEvolutionWorkflowTests.cs` (test) — verification — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.EndToEnd.UC012StorageEvolutionWorkflowTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 12 main/failure-path scenarios run and 0 fail

## Commit

title: Implement exact actuals projection and totals
description:
## Objective
Return exact dimensional actuals with one active economic effect and zero owned-transfer principal.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-ACTUALS-QUERY: Query exact Ledger actuals
  - AC: Given explicit filters for account, inclusive date range, category, Spend Pool, Payment Instrument, cardholder, Evidence Kind, Reconciliation State, transfer state, refund state, categorization state, Pool Assignment state, and transaction lifecycle state, when actuals are queried, then only transactions matching every supplied filter are returned.
  - AC: Given no lifecycle-state override, when actuals are queried, then each active economic event contributes through exactly one current Canonical Transaction; voided or superseded facts and retired relationships or assignments do not affect default totals.
  - AC: Given any selected transaction set, when Net Account Movement is returned, then it equals the exact base-10 sum of each selected active transaction's Signed Account Amount, with inflows positive and outflows negative.
  - AC: Given a selected transaction set, when External Spend is returned, then it adds positive magnitudes of qualifying active outflows, subtracts active linked refund or reversal magnitudes in their own Effective Date periods, excludes active owned-transfer principal and unrelated inflows, includes separately recorded fees, and never silently chooses a cash-withdrawal policy while OQ-LEDGER-16 is unresolved.
  - AC: Given category grouping with scope exact or subtree, when Budget Actual is returned, then each qualifying active transaction contributes once to its direct category or uncategorized bucket and, for subtree scope, once to each requested ancestor roll-up; pool and pool-by-category totals reconcile exactly without hierarchy double counting.
  - AC: Given an active linked refund or reversal, when grouped actuals are queried, then it offsets the original transaction's current pool and category, including explicit unassigned buckets, without inheriting account, instrument, or cardholder as a pool rule.
  - AC: Given no matching transactions, when actuals are queried, then the Ledger returns a successful empty collection and zero exact totals rather than a not-found error.
  - AC: Given unchanged Ledger state and identical filters, when the query is repeated, then membership, ordering, reconciliation and attribution state, and exact aggregates are identical.
  - AC: Given an approved statement-authoritative correction changes account, amount, or Effective Date, when current actuals are queried, then the superseded notification-derived fact contributes zero and the active statement-derived replacement contributes exactly once under its explicitly carried-forward or corrected dimensions.

## Depends On
- bd-1b2 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-MONEY-DATES): Consumer requires Money from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.
- bd-2ae (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-COVERAGE): Actuals filters consume current evidence/reconciliation coverage state.
- bd-31r (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-EVIDENCE-CASH-WITHDRAWALS): External Spend and Budget Actual require the resolved cash rule.
- bd-3q1 (TASK-LEDGER-CATEGORY-ALLOCATIONS): Actuals consumes CategoryAllocationStore for current category membership.
- bd-3nk (TASK-LEDGER-PAYMENT-ATTRIBUTION): Actuals consumes PaymentAttributionStore for instrument and cardholder filters.
- bd-2bd (TASK-LEDGER-POOL-ASSIGNMENTS): Actuals consumes PoolAssignmentStore for current pool membership.
- bd-xq6 (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSFERS): Actuals consumes RelationshipStore for transfer and refund state.

## In Scope
- Project exact active membership and named totals across current hierarchy, pools, payment attribution, evidence, reconciliation, and financial relationships.
- Produce: ActualsFilter/Item/Calculator
- Produce: ActualsProjectionStore
- Produce: Formula/filter/performance tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT count transfer principal as spend or infer pool/category/cardholder/cash treatment.
- Do NOT double count direct and ancestor rows or include superseded facts.
- Do NOT move budget-plan overlap semantics into LEDGER.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-1dt (TASK-LEDGER-ACTUALS-SNAPSHOT)
  - bd-3ja (TASK-LEDGER-DURABLE-STATE-VERIFIER)

## Success Criteria
- Account/date, category exact-or-subtree, pool, instrument/cardholder, evidence, reconciliation, relationship, categorization, and lifecycle filters compose conjunctively with explicit unknown and unassigned buckets.
- Net Account Movement sums signed active facts; External Spend includes qualifying outflows and fees, excludes owned-transfer principal, and applies linked refunds on their Effective Date to the original current category and pool.
- Direct category, subtree, pool, and pool-by-category groups reconcile exactly to all-up Budget Actual; hierarchy moves change subtree membership without rewriting assignments or double counting.
- Statement correction excludes the superseded capture and includes one active statement replacement with explicit carried or corrected dimensions.
- Cash withdrawals follow OQ-LEDGER-16Resolution; empty and uncategorized results remain explicit and exact.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT count transfer principal as spend or infer pool/category/cardholder/cash treatment.
- ❌ Do NOT double count direct and ancestor rows or include superseded facts.
- ❌ Do NOT move budget-plan overlap semantics into LEDGER.

## Approach
- Project exact active membership and named totals across current hierarchy, pools, payment attribution, evidence, reconciliation, and financial relationships.
- Expected output: ActualsFilter/Item/Calculator
- Expected output: ActualsProjectionStore
- Expected output: Formula/filter/performance tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: ActualsFilter (DM-LEDGER-RELATIONSHIP-ACTUALS-CONTRACTS) — canonical multi-dimensional filters
- Produces: ActualsCalculator.Calculate (DM-LEDGER-RELATIONSHIP-ACTUALS-CONTRACTS) — exact named totals and cells
- Produces: ActualsProjectionStore (DM-LEDGER-QUERY-SNAPSHOT) — current membership projection

- Consumes: RelationshipStore (DM-LEDGER-FINANCIAL-RELATIONSHIP) — transfer/refund state
- Consumes: CategoryAllocationStore (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-HISTORY) — current category
- Consumes: PoolAssignmentStore (DM-LEDGER-SPEND-POOL-ASSIGNMENT) — current pool
- Consumes: PaymentAttributionStore (DM-LEDGER-PAYMENT-ATTRIBUTION) — instrument/cardholder filters
- Consumes: ReconciliationCoverageStore (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY) — evidence/reconciliation filters

title: Verify retained generation and retrieval
description:
## Objective
UC-INSIGHTS-003 produces exactly one immutable snapshot per successful logical retained-generation request and keeps all retrieval operations read-only.

## Implements
- None; this task is a validation or shared-contract task with no direct FR ownership.

## Depends On
- bd-1jf (TASK-INSIGHTS-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): UC-003 invokes the complete published retained-report surface.

## In Scope
- Acceptance task for UC-INSIGHTS-003; prove explicit Retain, exact replay, conflict, immutable Get, and bounded metadata List as one workflow.
- Produce: VerifiedInsightsUc003
- Produce: UC-003 retained workflow evidence

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- Retain validates bounded analysis input, actor, reason, idempotency, permissions, compatibility, and policies, generates internally, and atomically stores one immutable snapshot without accepting caller-authored report content.
- Exact retry returns the same logical snapshot before producer access, changed normalized input conflicts, and validation/generation/storage/cancellation/crash failure leaves no partial snapshot or successful key.
- Get returns the exact immutable snapshot and provenance; bounded List returns deterministic metadata-only history and never includes payload except through explicit Get.
- Ordinary Generate, Get, List, and their failures create no durable state, and later corrections or policy changes cannot modify the retained snapshot.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT auto-retain Generate, accept uploaded report content, duplicate a retry, silently truncate List, deserialize payloads for metadata, or mutate stored bytes after source change.
- ❌ Do NOT skip fault/restart inspection or infer atomicity from a successful command alone.

## Approach
Acceptance task for UC-INSIGHTS-003; prove explicit Retain, exact replay, conflict, immutable Get, and bounded metadata List as one workflow.
- Constraint: Use the published process surface and real temporary SQLite.
- Expected output: VerifiedInsightsUc003
- Expected output: UC-003 retained workflow evidence

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CompleteInsightsPublicContract (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Generate, Retain, Get, and List
- Produces: VerifiedInsightsUc003 (UC-INSIGHTS-003) — retained workflow proof

## Verification

- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~RetainAndRetrieveInsightReportUseCaseTests` — exit 0; at least 18 authority, internal-generation, no-caller-payload, exact replay, changed conflict, cancellation, write/crash atomicity, Get, List order, List limit, metadata-only, ordinary-Generate-no-write, later-correction immutability, and privacy cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit
- `test(insights): verify retained generation and retrieval` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-3iu`

- DD-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE

title: Create and independently verify Ledger backups
description:
## Objective
Publish one owner-only backup only after complete-state, privacy, replay, relationship, hierarchy, reconciliation, and exact-total verification.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-BACKUP-VERIFICATION: Create and verify Ledger backups
  - AC: Given a healthy Ledger, when the owner creates a backup, then one version-identifiable consistent artifact containing the complete Durable Ledger State is produced with owner-only host permissions and the live Ledger is unchanged.
  - AC: Given a backup artifact, when it is verified independently, then compatibility, integrity, storage metadata, accounts, categories, Spend Pools, transactions and correction chains, category and pool history, Payment Instrument and Cardholder Attribution, Evidence Records and links, reconciliation scopes, decisions and exceptions, financial relationships, idempotency outcomes, per-type counts, cardinality rules, and exact pool/category totals all validate before success.
  - AC: Given a corrupt, incomplete, unsupported, or permission-inaccessible artifact, when verification is attempted, then a stable failure is returned and neither the artifact nor live Ledger is mutated.
  - AC: Given backup or verification output and diagnostics, when inspected, then no credential, raw evidence or provider payload, unmasked account or instrument identifier, or transaction description is emitted.

## Depends On
- bd-3ja (TASK-LEDGER-DURABLE-STATE-VERIFIER): Consumes DurableLedgerVerifier.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Consumes ArtifactReconciler.
- bd-23m (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-STORAGE): Consumes IHostArtifactProtection.

## In Scope
- Implement versioned backup operations on DurableLedgerVerifier; publication requires complete hierarchy and statement-authority verification.
- Produce: Backup contracts/service/module
- Produce: Verified manifest publication
- Produce: Artifact/replay/security tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT copy a live WAL file, overwrite a target, include QuerySnapshot, weaken permissions, rely only on database idempotency, or publish before complete verification.
- Do NOT omit V002 authority metadata, hierarchy ancestry, carry-forward, or relationship replacement from the manifest fingerprint.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-5gf (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-RECOVERY-SKILL-BUNDLE)
  - bd-4zp (TASK-LEDGER-STORAGE-EVOLUTION)

## Success Criteria
- backup.create under the writer lock uses SQLite online backup to private staging, invokes DurableLedgerVerifier, writes its normalized fingerprint/counts/direct-subtree-pool totals/schema/storage/reconciliation-policy manifest, flushes, and atomically publishes one 0600 artifact.
- backup.verify independently checks artifact checksum and compatibility, then returns the same complete report including category ancestry and statement-replacement chains.
- Corrupt, cyclic, incomplete-correction, invalid-relationship, unsupported, privacy-invalid, inaccessible, unsafe, or existing target fails and mutates neither artifact nor live store.
- Diagnostics expose no descriptions, raw evidence, email/statement payloads, credentials, or full identifiers; identical replay reconciles the artifact manifest while changed requests conflict.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT copy a live WAL file, overwrite a target, include QuerySnapshot, weaken permissions, rely only on database idempotency, or publish before complete verification.
- ❌ Do NOT omit V002 authority metadata, hierarchy ancestry, carry-forward, or relationship replacement from the manifest fingerprint.

## Approach
- Implement versioned backup operations on DurableLedgerVerifier; publication requires complete hierarchy and statement-authority verification.
- Constraint: Use the exact backup operation and stable-error contract in DM-LEDGER-RECOVERY-STORAGE-CONTRACTS.
- Expected output: Backup contracts/service/module
- Expected output: Verified manifest publication
- Expected output: Artifact/replay/security tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: BackupOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-RECOVERY-STORAGE-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: BackupService (DM-LEDGER-RECOVERY-STORAGE-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: DurableLedgerVerifier (DM-LEDGER-STORE-GENERATION)
- Consumes: ArtifactReconciler (DD-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-MUTATIONS)
- Consumes: IHostArtifactProtection (DD-LEDGER-EMBEDDED-STORAGE)

## Files (reservation globs)

- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Recovery/BackupService.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Recovery/BackupOperationModule.cs` (create) — implementation — required

- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.Features.Ledger.Recovery.BackupTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 14 publication/path/permission/replay/verifier-boundary cases run and 0 fail

title: Build the complete Durable Ledger State verifier
description:
## Objective
Produce one normalized real-SQLite report required before backup publication, restore activation, or storage-evolution activation.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On
- bd-1dt (TASK-LEDGER-ACTUALS-SNAPSHOT): Complete-state verification uses the final actuals projection while excluding snapshots.
- bd-23m (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-STORAGE): Consumes LedgerDb.
- bd-3hy (TASK-LEDGER-ACTUALS-PROJECTION): Durable state verification consumes ActualsCalculator.Calculate.

## In Scope
- Shared recovery foundation with no Implements link: verify every durable fact, hierarchy, evidence, reconciliation correction, dimension, relationship, idempotency effect, count, and exact total.
- Produce: DurableLedgerReport
- Produce: DurableLedgerVerifier
- Produce: Complete-state and recovery-drill tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT treat PRAGMA integrity_check, checksum, or row counts alone as complete-state proof.
- Do NOT omit hierarchy history, statement replacement/carry-forward, relationship replacement, or idempotency state; do not include QuerySnapshot or mutate the inspected store.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3j9 (TASK-LEDGER-BACKUP-VERIFY)
  - bd-fup (TASK-LEDGER-RESTORE-ACTIVATE)
  - bd-4zp (TASK-LEDGER-STORAGE-EVOLUTION)

## Success Criteria
- Verification opens a non-live store read-only and checks checksum metadata, SQLite integrity, foreign keys, current schema/storage/reconciliation-policy compatibility before domain reconciliation.
- The report enumerates facts/lifecycles; category nodes, parent history, acyclicity and current ancestry; pool/payment/category histories; evidence/links; confirmed-existing and corrected-from-statement decisions; supersession/carry-forward/relationship chains; coverage/exceptions; idempotency/logical effects; counts and exact totals. QuerySnapshot is excluded.
- Any missing type, cycle, orphan, invalid lifecycle/cardinality, privacy violation, duplicate active effect, incomplete statement correction, invalid relationship replacement, replay mismatch, count mismatch, overflow, hierarchy double-count, or total/cell mismatch fails closed.
- A verified backup restored into a fresh private generation reproduces the normalized report exactly apart from generation metadata; deterministic corruptions prove each rejection.
- Diagnostics contain safe type names, identifiers, and counts only, never descriptions, raw evidence, email/statement payloads, credentials, full identifiers, or serialized financial objects.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT treat PRAGMA integrity_check, checksum, or row counts alone as complete-state proof.
- ❌ Do NOT omit hierarchy history, statement replacement/carry-forward, relationship replacement, or idempotency state; do not include QuerySnapshot or mutate the inspected store.

## Approach
- Shared recovery foundation with no Implements link: verify every durable fact, hierarchy, evidence, reconciliation correction, dimension, relationship, idempotency effect, count, and exact total.
- Constraint: Use exact Int64 minor-unit arithmetic with checked overflow.
- Expected output: DurableLedgerReport
- Expected output: DurableLedgerVerifier
- Expected output: Complete-state and recovery-drill tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: DurableLedgerVerifier (DM-LEDGER-STORE-GENERATION)
- Consumes: LedgerDb (DM-LEDGER-STORE-GENERATION)
- Consumes: ActualsCalculator.Calculate (DM-LEDGER-RELATIONSHIP-ACTUALS-CONTRACTS)

- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.Infrastructure.Recovery.DurableLedgerVerifierTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 16 complete-state/corruption/reconciliation/recovery cases run and 0 fail

title: Prepare and validate private statement evidence
description:
## Objective
Provide a complete, machine-readable, private fixture expectation contract without copying statement names, paths, content, balances, transactions, or extracted text into Git, Lex, logs, or test output.

## Implements
- No direct FR ownership: this is an evidence, integration, security, use-case, or completion gate.
- Proves or constrains DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY: Memory-only extraction and owner-only payload handling (governed-by).
- Proves or constrains EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT: Owner-Supplied Bank Statement (references).

## Depends On
- None. This bead is an intentional execution root.

## In Scope
- Evidence-only gate that inventories the owner-supplied ignored statements and records their expected facts in one ignored manifest before any fixture-dependent implementation begins; it implements no product requirement.
- Produce: Ignored private fixture expectation manifest.
- Produce: Validated PrivateFixtureExpectationManifest interface.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- docs/statements remains ignored and contains exactly the three owner-supplied readable PDF fixtures already assessed during discovery; this gate neither renames nor modifies them.
- docs/statements/.ingest-fixture-manifest.json is ignored JSON schemaVersion 1 with exactly three fixture objects and exactly two distinct supported variantId values.
- Each fixture object contains a repository-relative sourcePath below docs/statements plus expected statementPeriod, accountEvidence, orderedRecords, and reconciliation controls sufficient to assert every source row, exact available balance control, and variant identity; absolute paths are prohibited and all values remain private.
- Every manifest sourcePath is repository-relative, stays below docs/statements, resolves to one readable file, and appears only once; no statement filename, path, content, amount, balance, record, or expected fact is emitted by verification.
- The manifest is supplied to fixture-dependent tests through TALLY_INGEST_PRIVATE_FIXTURE_MANIFEST; another worktree or agent must receive that environment variable rather than reconstructing private facts.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT add, force-add, stage, commit, export, or paste statement files or the private manifest into any tracked artifact.
- ❌ Do NOT infer expected facts from the production parser under test; transcribe and independently reconcile them against the source evidence.
- ❌ Do NOT continue to fixture-dependent tasks when a source is absent, unreadable, duplicated, unsupported, or not fully accounted for.

## Approach
Evidence-only gate that inventories the owner-supplied ignored statements and records their expected facts in one ignored manifest before any fixture-dependent implementation begins; it implements no product requirement.
- Constraint: Only structural schema and counts may appear in command output; private values stay in the ignored manifest.
- Note: This gate is deliberately non-landable evidence preparation: the durable plan records the contract while the private artifact remains owner-controlled and ignored.

## Interfaces
- Produces: PrivateFixtureExpectationManifest (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT) — schemaVersion 1; three fixtures; two variants; private expected facts

## Files (reservation globs)
- `.gitignore` (read) — prove the private statement directory remains ignored — required
- `docs/statements/**` (read) — owner-supplied immutable private evidence — required — Never print or track filenames or contents.
- `docs/statements/.ingest-fixture-manifest.json` (create) — ignored private expectation manifest — required — Private execution input; never commit.

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `test -s docs/statements/.ingest-fixture-manifest.json && jq -e '(.schemaVersion == 1) and (.fixtures[0] != null) and (.fixtures[1] != null) and (.fixtures[2] != null) and (.fixtures[3] == null) and all(.fixtures[]; (.variantId == "pdf-text-layout-a-v1" or .variantId == "pdf-text-layout-b-v1") and (.sourcePath != null) and (.expected.statementPeriod != null) and (.expected.accountEvidence != null) and (.expected.orderedRecords != null) and (.expected.controls != null)) and any(.fixtures[]; .variantId == "pdf-text-layout-a-v1") and any(.fixtures[]; .variantId == "pdf-text-layout-b-v1") and (.fixtures[0].sourcePath != .fixtures[1].sourcePath) and (.fixtures[0].sourcePath != .fixtures[2].sourcePath) and (.fixtures[1].sourcePath != .fixtures[2].sourcePath)' docs/statements/.ingest-fixture-manifest.json >/dev/null` -> exit 0 with no stdout; schemaVersion, exactly three unique fixtures, exactly two supported variants, and all required expected-fact structures are present
- **Test:** `fixture_root="$(realpath -e docs/statements 2>/dev/null)" || exit 1; while IFS= read -r fixture_path; do resolved_path="$(realpath -e "$fixture_path" 2>/dev/null)" || exit 1; if [[ "$resolved_path" != "$fixture_root"/* ]]; then exit 1; fi; if [[ "${resolved_path##*.}" != [Pp][Dd][Ff] ]]; then exit 1; fi; if ! test -r "$resolved_path"; then exit 1; fi; done < <(jq -er '.fixtures[].sourcePath' docs/statements/.ingest-fixture-manifest.json)` -> exit 0 with no stdout; every privately consumed source path canonicalizes below docs/statements, names a PDF, and resolves to a readable file
- **Test:** `git check-ignore -q docs/statements/.ingest-fixture-manifest.json && test -z "$(git ls-files -- docs/statements)"` -> exit 0 with no stdout; the private manifest is ignored and no file below docs/statements is tracked

## Commit
- `test(ingest): prepare private fixture evidence` — footer `Refs: bd-3k6`

## Lex references
- DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY
- EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT
- PLAN-INGEST-V1
- SP-INGEST-00-FOUNDATION
- TASK-INGEST-GATE-EVIDENCE-PRIVATE-FIXTURES
- Deep context: `lex context bd-3k6 --json`
design:
Evidence-only gate that inventories the owner-supplied ignored statements and records their expected facts in one ignored manifest before any fixture-dependent implementation begins; it implements no product requirement.

Constraints:
1. Only structural schema and counts may appear in command output; private values stay in the ignored manifest.

Interfaces:
1. produces: PrivateFixtureExpectationManifest [EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT]
acceptance_criteria:
1. docs/statements remains ignored and contains exactly the three owner-supplied readable PDF fixtures already assessed during discovery; this gate neither renames nor modifies them.
2. docs/statements/.ingest-fixture-manifest.json is ignored JSON schemaVersion 1 with exactly three fixture objects and exactly two distinct supported variantId values.
3. Each fixture object contains a repository-relative sourcePath below docs/statements plus expected statementPeriod, accountEvidence, orderedRecords, and reconciliation controls sufficient to assert every source row, exact available balance control, and variant identity; absolute paths are prohibited and all values remain private.
4. Every manifest sourcePath is repository-relative, stays below docs/statements, resolves to one readable file, and appears only once; no statement filename, path, content, amount, balance, record, or expected fact is emitted by verification.
5. The manifest is supplied to fixture-dependent tests through TALLY_INGEST_PRIVATE_FIXTURE_MANIFEST; another worktree or agent must receive that environment variable rather than reconstructing private facts.
notes: Compiled from TASK-INGEST-GATE-EVIDENCE-PRIVATE-FIXTURES in PLAN-INGEST-V1. The executor must satisfy every required verification command and preserve all failure criteria.

title: Prove bounded personal-scale INSIGHTS performance
description:
## Objective
Generate, Retain, Restate, Get, List, Status, and Delete meet their p95 and memory targets without truncation, approximation, hidden background work, or integrity loss.

## Implements
- None; this task is a validation or shared-contract task with no direct FR ownership.

## Depends On
- bd-1jf (TASK-INSIGHTS-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Benchmarks invoke the complete published operation surface.

## In Scope
- Performance convergence gate with no Implements ownership; benchmark all public analytical and retained-state paths at the approved dataset and output limits with exact result checks.
- Produce: InsightsPerformanceGateEvidence
- Produce: Payload-free benchmark report

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- The fixture contains exactly 100000 active LEDGER transactions through the public BUDGET composite, 1000 category positions, 1000 retained ReportSnapshots, and every active v1 policy without private owner payloads in the report.
- After warm-up, at least 100 measured invocations per operation record p50, p95, maximum duration, peak resident memory, exact result/fingerprint reconciliation, output size, and environment fingerprint.
- Generate and Retain each complete within 5 seconds p95 and 256 MiB peak resident memory; Restate completes within 6 seconds p95; Get, Status, Delete, and a 1000-item metadata List each complete within 1 second p95.
- One-over-limit horizon, member, category, metric, signal, list, or output request fails before financial output, producer over-read, partial report, or retained change.
- Network remains denied, no backup/migration overlaps, List and Status avoid payload reads, exact arithmetic and fingerprints reconcile, and every benchmark class has nonzero discovery.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT reduce dataset sizes, measured samples, active policies, category count, or retained history to manufacture a pass.
- ❌ Do NOT cache mutable producer truth, weaken exact arithmetic, skip result checks, silently truncate, deserialize payloads for metadata reads, or run a daemon/background worker.
- ❌ Do NOT include financial fixture content or private paths in benchmark output.

## Approach
Performance convergence gate with no Implements ownership; benchmark all public analytical and retained-state paths at the approved dataset and output limits with exact result checks.
- Constraint: Run on the supported release host with Release build and record the exact environment fingerprint.
- Expected output: InsightsPerformanceGateEvidence
- Expected output: Payload-free benchmark report

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CompleteInsightsPublicContract (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — all measured operations
- Produces: InsightsPerformanceGateEvidence (NFR-INSIGHTS-PERSONAL-SCALE-PERFORMANCE) — threshold and exact-result proof

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Performance/InsightsPersonalScalePerformanceTests.cs` (test) — approved-scale benchmark and exact result assertions
- `scripts/verify-insights-performance.sh` (create) — Release benchmark, discovery, threshold, and report runner
- `docs/verification/insights-performance.md` (document) — safe p50/p95/max/memory/output/environment report

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `bash scripts/verify-insights-performance.sh` — exit 0; each measured operation has at least 100 samples, Generate and Retain p95 are <=5s, Restate p95 is <=6s, Get/List/Status/Delete p95 are <=1s, peak RSS for Generate and Retain is <=256 MiB, every result and fingerprint reconciles, all one-over-limit cases fail before output/write, and 0 discovery or benchmark checks fail

## Commit
- `test(insights): prove bounded personal-scale performance` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-3kn`

## Lex references
- DD-INSIGHTS-DETERMINISTIC-REPORT-COMPILER
- DD-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE
- NFR-INSIGHTS-PERSONAL-SCALE-PERFORMANCE
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-04-ACCEPTANCE
- TASK-INSIGHTS-GATE-PERFORMANCE
- TC-INSIGHTS-PERSONAL-SCALE-PERFORMANCE
- Deep context: `lex context bd-3kn --json`

- The fixture contains exactly 100000 active LEDGER transactions through the public BUDGET composite, 1000 category positions, 1000 retained ReportSnapshots, and every active v1 policy without private owner payloads in the report.
- After warm-up, at least 100 measured invocations per operation record p50, p95, maximum duration, peak resident memory, exact result/fingerprint reconciliation, output size, and environment fingerprint.
- Generate and Retain each complete within 5 seconds p95 and 256 MiB peak resident memory; Restate completes within 6 seconds p95; Get, Status, Delete, and a 1000-item metadata List each complete within 1 second p95.
- One-over-limit horizon, member, category, metric, signal, list, or output request fails before financial output, producer over-read, partial report, or retained change.
- Network remains denied, no backup/migration overlaps, List and Status avoid payload reads, exact arithmetic and fingerprints reconcile, and every benchmark class has nonzero discovery.
notes: No soft checkpoint; execute the complete graph contract and rejection criteria.

title: Create the protected INGEST SQLite foundation
description:
## Objective
Provide an owner-only ingest.db with deterministic schema evolution and no access path to LedgerDb.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-ARTIFACT-CLEANUP: Abandon and clean up ingestion artifacts safely
  - AC: Given preview, commit, failure, or abandonment reaches a safe boundary, when temporary extraction data is no longer required, then INGEST removes it deterministically and retains no caller-owned source copy.
  - AC: Given a crash left known temporary artifacts, when the next safe INGEST invocation starts, then cleanup identifies and removes only orphaned INGEST temporaries without touching sources, manifests required for resume, receipts, or LEDGER data.
  - AC: Given an incomplete batch, when the owner explicitly abandons it, then INGEST records abandonment, prevents future commit or resume for that manifest revision, removes disposable temporaries, leaves prior canonical transactions unchanged, and leaves the source untouched.
  - AC: Given an incomplete manifest or receipt still required for review or resume, when cleanup is requested, then INGEST rejects destructive cleanup with a stable retained-for-recovery result.
  - AC: Given a completed metadata-only receipt or safely abandoned batch, when the owner explicitly requests cleanup, then INGEST removes only the selected retained INGEST artifacts and reports the result without deleting any canonical transaction or caller-owned source.

## Depends On
- bd-2dg (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-LEDGER-CONTRACT): The shared Tally solution and data-root conventions must exist before the separate INGEST store is added.

## In Scope
- Create only the separate raw-SQLite connection, immutable V001 schema, migration runner, and owner-only artifact enforcement used by feature-owned SQL stores.
- Produce: IngestDatabase.
- Produce: IngestMigrationV001 and migrator.
- Produce: IngestArtifactProtection.
- Produce: Real-SQLite foundation tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- IngestDatabase opens only <TallyDataRoot>/ingest/ingest.db, enables foreign_keys, WAL, synchronous=FULL, and bounded busy handling, and never opens or attaches LedgerDb.
- IngestMigrationV001 creates every table, key, uniqueness rule, and relationship in DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE with snake_case identifiers; PRAGMA user_version advances atomically from 0 to 1.
- Repeated migration is idempotent, unknown newer user_version fails with a stable compatibility result, and interruption leaves either the complete prior or complete V001 schema.
- IngestArtifactProtection creates directories as 0700 and state, WAL, SHM, lock, and atomic artifacts as 0600 where supported; inability to establish protection blocks persistence.
- Real temporary SQLite tests enforce immutable revision numbering, unique idempotency keys, foreign keys, and concurrent writer bounds; architecture guards find no LedgerDb or private LEDGER storage reference.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT share LedgerDb or LEDGER tables, attach databases, or use private LEDGER storage — rejected per DD-INGEST-STATE-STORE.
- ❌ Do NOT replace raw Microsoft.Data.Sqlite with EF Core, PostgreSQL, JSON batch files, or a generic repository — rejected per DD-INGEST-STATE-STORE and DD-INGEST-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE.
- ❌ Do NOT persist when owner-only permissions cannot be established — per DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY.
- ❌ Do NOT modify V001 after release; add a new immutable migration version.

## Approach
Create only the separate raw-SQLite connection, immutable V001 schema, migration runner, and owner-only artifact enforcement used by feature-owned SQL stores.
- Constraint: Use explicit SQL transactions and propagate CancellationToken through every asynchronous SQLite call.

## Interfaces
- Produces: IngestDatabase (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Produces: IngestSchemaMigrator.ApplyAsync (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Produces: IngestArtifactProtection.EnsureOwnerOnly (DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY)
- Consumes: LedgerIngestContractPrerequisite (DM-INGEST-LEDGER-COMMIT-CONTRACT) — Shared host prerequisite; no shared store

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Storage/IngestDatabase.cs` (create) — separate connection boundary — required
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Storage/IngestSchemaMigrator.cs` (create) — migration runner — required
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Storage/Migrations/IngestMigrationV001.cs` (create) — immutable V001 schema — required
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Storage/IngestArtifactProtection.cs` (create) — owner-only protection — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Storage/IngestDatabaseFoundationTests.cs` (test) — real SQLite conformance — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Security/IngestArtifactProtectionTests.cs` (test) — permission/isolation tests — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~IngestDatabaseFoundationTests|FullyQualifiedName~IngestArtifactProtectionTests'` -> exit 0; at least 18 migration, constraint, concurrency, permission, and isolation cases are discovered against real temporary SQLite stores and 0 tests fail

## Commit
- `feat(ingest): create protected sqlite state` — footer `Refs: bd-3l1`

## Lex references
- DD-INGEST-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE
- DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY
- DD-INGEST-STATE-STORE
- DIAG-INGEST-STATE-ER
- DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE

- FR-INGEST-ARTIFACT-CLEANUP

- PLAN-INGEST-V1
- SP-INGEST-00-FOUNDATION
- TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-LEDGER-CONTRACT
- TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION

title: Verify commit and resume workflow
description:
## Objective
Prove an approved batch commits each candidate once and every interruption resumes from the exact durable frontier.

## Implements
- No direct FR ownership: this is an evidence, integration, security, use-case, or completion gate.
- Proves or constrains NFR-INGEST-INTERRUPTED-COMMIT-RECOVERY: Recover every interrupted commit deterministically (satisfies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-APPROVED-BATCH-COMMIT-CONTRACT: Verify approved batch commit through LEDGER (verifies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-COMMIT-RECOVERY-MATRIX: Verify every candidate commit crash window (verifies).
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-DURABLE-RECEIPT-RESUME-CONTRACT: Verify durable receipt and interrupted resume (verifies).
- Proves or constrains UC-INGEST-003: Commit and resume an approved batch (covers).

## Depends On
- bd-2jn (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): UC verification exercises published commit/resume/status and public Ledger operations.

## In Scope
- Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-003 main scenario and all public-Ledger/receipt interruption paths.
- Produce: UCIngest003Evidence.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Main scenario commits the exact approved revision in frozen order through public ledger.transaction.record, round-trips each accepted transaction through ledger.transaction.get, and returns one complete receipt with exact counts/references.
- Manifest integrity, approval, account, adapter, manifest-schema, or Ledger compatibility failure creates no new mutation.
- A Ledger validation/identity conflict becomes a durable stable candidate outcome and stops before the next candidate.
- Receipt persistence failure stops further candidates and status exposes the last durable frontier.
- Interruption after Ledger commit but before receipt update resumes with the same request/key, obtains the original result, and creates no second transaction.
- A materially different retry request/key/manifest preserves the original effect and returns stable conflict; repeated resume converges.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT assert via LedgerDb or ingest.db; use public Ledger transaction get/list observations and INGEST receipt/status.
- ❌ Do NOT weaken fault injection or reset durable state between interruption and resume.

## Approach
Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-003 main scenario and all public-Ledger/receipt interruption paths.
- Constraint: Each crash case uses an isolated real data root and records canonical transaction IDs only as safe evidence.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CompleteIngestPublicContract (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Published INGEST and shared Ledger operations
- Produces: UCIngest003Evidence (UC-INGEST-003) — Commit and resume workflow proof

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/E2E/CommitResumeWorkflowTests.cs` (test) — UC-INGEST-003 black-box gate — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~CommitResumeWorkflowTests` -> exit 0; main flow plus all 5 interruption boundaries and at least 8 conflict/failure cases run, at least 14 cases are discovered, 0 tests fail, and no case creates a second canonical transaction

## Commit

title: ActualsWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove exact membership and totals across transfers, refunds, hierarchy, dimensions, and statement correction.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-ACTUALS-QUERY: Query exact Ledger actuals
  - AC: Given explicit filters for account, inclusive date range, category, Spend Pool, Payment Instrument, cardholder, Evidence Kind, Reconciliation State, transfer state, refund state, categorization state, Pool Assignment state, and transaction lifecycle state, when actuals are queried, then only transactions matching every supplied filter are returned.
  - AC: Given no lifecycle-state override, when actuals are queried, then each active economic event contributes through exactly one current Canonical Transaction; voided or superseded facts and retired relationships or assignments do not affect default totals.
  - AC: Given any selected transaction set, when Net Account Movement is returned, then it equals the exact base-10 sum of each selected active transaction's Signed Account Amount, with inflows positive and outflows negative.
  - AC: Given a selected transaction set, when External Spend is returned, then it adds positive magnitudes of qualifying active outflows, subtracts active linked refund or reversal magnitudes in their own Effective Date periods, excludes active owned-transfer principal and unrelated inflows, includes separately recorded fees, and never silently chooses a cash-withdrawal policy while OQ-LEDGER-16 is unresolved.
  - AC: Given category grouping with scope exact or subtree, when Budget Actual is returned, then each qualifying active transaction contributes once to its direct category or uncategorized bucket and, for subtree scope, once to each requested ancestor roll-up; pool and pool-by-category totals reconcile exactly without hierarchy double counting.
  - AC: Given an active linked refund or reversal, when grouped actuals are queried, then it offsets the original transaction's current pool and category, including explicit unassigned buckets, without inheriting account, instrument, or cardholder as a pool rule.
  - AC: Given no matching transactions, when actuals are queried, then the Ledger returns a successful empty collection and zero exact totals rather than a not-found error.
  - AC: Given unchanged Ledger state and identical filters, when the query is repeated, then membership, ordering, reconciliation and attribution state, and exact aggregates are identical.
  - AC: Given an approved statement-authoritative correction changes account, amount, or Effective Date, when current actuals are queried, then the superseded notification-derived fact contributes zero and the active statement-derived replacement contributes exactly once under its explicitly carried-forward or corrected dimensions.
- FR-LEDGER-SNAPSHOT-PAGINATION: Preserve query snapshots across pages
  - AC: Given a filtered actuals query requiring multiple pages, when the first page is requested, then the result includes a snapshot-bound opaque cursor, deterministic ordering, evidence and Reconciliation State, current category, pool, instrument and Cardholder Attribution, and totals for the full selected set.
  - AC: Given a valid cursor, when a later page is requested after unrelated Ledger mutations, then membership, state, attribution, and pool, category, and pool-by-category totals remain those of the first-page snapshot.
  - AC: Given a cursor with altered filters, invalid encoding, unsupported version, or expired snapshot, when a page is requested, then a stable cursor error is returned and no partial page or live-state fallback is exposed.
  - AC: Given all pages from one snapshot, when their stable transaction identifiers are combined, then each selected Canonical Transaction appears exactly once and page aggregates reconcile to the first-page full-set totals.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace exact hierarchy/pool/reconciliation actuals and snapshot pagination through the Release CLI.
- Produce: UC005ActualsWorkflowTests with main/failure paths

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT accept aggregate-only assertions, live second-page reads, stale hierarchy, duplicated ancestors, both replacement facts, or private-store calculation.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- Published actuals queries prove every filter and explicit unknown/uncategorized/unassigned bucket with deterministic membership and exact Net Account Movement, External Spend, and Budget Actual.
- Owned-transfer principal contributes zero; separate fees remain spend; refunds offset original current category/pool in refund date; cash follows OQ-LEDGER-16Resolution.
- Category-direct, subtree, pool, and matrix totals reconcile without double counting before and after hierarchy moves.
- Statement correction excludes the superseded fact and includes the replacement once; later snapshot pages retain frozen ancestry, Reconciliation State, membership, ordering, and totals despite writes.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT accept aggregate-only assertions, live second-page reads, stale hierarchy, duplicated ancestors, both replacement facts, or private-store calculation.

## Approach
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace exact hierarchy/pool/reconciliation actuals and snapshot pagination through the Release CLI.
- Constraint: Invoke the Release published tally executable; inspect stdout, stderr, exit code, and real temporary SQLite state.
- Expected output: UC005ActualsWorkflowTests with main/failure paths

## Interfaces
- Consumes: PublishedTallyFixture (Release published-process E2E fixture)
- Consumes: CompletePublicContract (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — Exactly 72 provider-neutral operations
- Produces: VerifiedUC005 (UC-LEDGER-005) — multi-dimensional actuals workflow

## Files (reservation globs)

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.EndToEnd.UC005ActualsWorkflowTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 12 main/failure-path scenarios run and 0 fail

## Commit

title: Implement transaction payment attribution
description:
## Objective
Assign or correct payment attribution independently and support statement correction without guessing incompatible identity.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-PAYMENT-ATTRIBUTION: Maintain Payment Instrument and Cardholder Attribution
  - AC: Given a privacy-safe unique label and optional masked identifier, when a Payment Instrument or cardholder is created, then one active provider-neutral identity is returned and no full card number, credential, or provider payload is stored.
  - AC: Given an active transaction, active Payment Instrument or cardholder identity, reason, actor context, and Idempotency Identity, when attribution is assigned, then at most one current identity per dimension is recorded without changing account, category, pool, evidence, or source facts.
  - AC: Given an existing attribution and a valid replacement or explicit unknown state, when it is corrected, then the prior attribution is retired, the replacement or unknown state becomes current, and both remain attributable in history.
  - AC: Given an archived identity, incompatible declared account association, inactive transaction, or missing correction reason, when attribution is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and changes no attribution.
  - AC: Given an identity is renamed or archived, when current and historical transactions are queried, then stable references remain resolvable and archival prevents only new assignments.

## Depends On
- bd-3od (TASK-LEDGER-PAYMENT-IDENTITIES): Attribution validates local instrument and cardholder identities through PaymentIdentityStore.
- bd-84s (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTIONS-RECORD-GET): Attribution consumes TransactionStore and its atomically initialized unknown projection.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Assignment and correction consume LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync.

## In Scope
- Deliver independent instrument/cardholder assignment, correction, and reconciliation-only compatible carry-forward over explicit unknown states.
- Produce: Payment attribution contracts, policy, store, handlers, operation module, and real-SQLite tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT infer attribution from account, pool, category, description, Evidence Kind, or provider wording — rejected per DD-LEDGER-DIMENSIONAL-ATTRIBUTION.
- Do NOT overwrite attribution rows, copy incompatible identity, or inherit attribution across ordinary supersession — per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- Do NOT expose carry-forward publicly or accept full provider identifiers.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3hy (TASK-LEDGER-ACTUALS-PROJECTION)
  - bd-2ve (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CATALOGUE-TRANSACTION-BUNDLE)
  - bd-3pa (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-STATEMENT-CORRECTION-EFFECT-WRITER)

## Success Criteria
- Every transaction exposes independent instrument and cardholder projections initialized as unknown; public assign/correct sets either dimension known or unknown without changing account, category, pool, evidence, or reconciliation.
- Missing/inactive transaction, absent/archived/incompatible identity, same-as-active correction, stale prior state, or conflicting replay preserves the current attribution and returns the declared stable outcome.
- CarryForwardOrUnknownAsync is internal to statement correction: compatible current identity appends carry_forward; incompatible account/identity appends explicit unknown initialization plus review metadata; both reference source, replacement, and Reconciliation Decision.
- Get/history returns the active projection and complete initialize/assign/correct/carry_forward chain with actor, reason, trusted time, and predecessor.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT infer attribution from account, pool, category, description, Evidence Kind, or provider wording — rejected per DD-LEDGER-DIMENSIONAL-ATTRIBUTION.
- ❌ Do NOT overwrite attribution rows, copy incompatible identity, or inherit attribution across ordinary supersession — per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- ❌ Do NOT expose carry-forward publicly or accept full provider identifiers.

## Approach
- Deliver independent instrument/cardholder assignment, correction, and reconciliation-only compatible carry-forward over explicit unknown states.

title: Implement Payment Instrument and cardholder catalogues
description:
## Objective
Create, inspect, list, rename, archive, and reactivate Payment Instruments and cardholders using local ULIDs and attributable lifecycle history.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-PAYMENT-ATTRIBUTION: Maintain Payment Instrument and Cardholder Attribution
  - AC: Given a privacy-safe unique label and optional masked identifier, when a Payment Instrument or cardholder is created, then one active provider-neutral identity is returned and no full card number, credential, or provider payload is stored.
  - AC: Given an active transaction, active Payment Instrument or cardholder identity, reason, actor context, and Idempotency Identity, when attribution is assigned, then at most one current identity per dimension is recorded without changing account, category, pool, evidence, or source facts.
  - AC: Given an existing attribution and a valid replacement or explicit unknown state, when it is corrected, then the prior attribution is retired, the replacement or unknown state becomes current, and both remain attributable in history.
  - AC: Given an archived identity, incompatible declared account association, inactive transaction, or missing correction reason, when attribution is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and changes no attribution.
  - AC: Given an identity is renamed or archived, when current and historical transactions are queried, then stable references remain resolvable and archival prevents only new assignments.

## Depends On
- bd-2zt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE): The catalogue uses the proven process, SQLite, exact IDs, and mutation executor.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Every catalogue mutation consumes LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync.

## In Scope
- Deliver stable local privacy-safe identity operations independently from transaction attribution.
- Produce: Payment identity contracts, policies, stores, handlers, operation modules, and focused tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT use provider IDs as primary identity, retain full card/account numbers, or infer cardholder from wording — rejected per DD-LEDGER-DIMENSIONAL-ATTRIBUTION.
- Do NOT physically delete referenced identities or overwrite labels/history — rejected per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- Do NOT add a provider adapter or transport vocabulary to contracts.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-2ve (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CATALOGUE-TRANSACTION-BUNDLE)

- Duplicate active identity and conflicting idempotency requests return stable conflicts while identical replay returns the original result.

## Failure Criteria

- ❌ Do NOT physically delete referenced identities or overwrite labels/history — rejected per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- ❌ Do NOT add a provider adapter or transport vocabulary to contracts.

## Approach
- Deliver stable local privacy-safe identity operations independently from transaction attribution.
