title: Implement Category Assignment and correction
description:
## Objective
Apply one attributable category decision and expose exact direct/current-ancestor membership.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-CATEGORY-ASSIGNMENT: Assign and correct transaction categories
  - AC: Given an active transaction and any active category node, when the owner assigns the category, then exactly one active assignment is returned and the transaction facts remain unchanged.
  - AC: Given a categorized active transaction, an active replacement category, and a reason, when the owner corrects the assignment, then the prior assignment is retired, the replacement becomes active, and both remain attributable in history.
  - AC: Given an inactive transaction or archived category, when an assignment or correction is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and changes no assignment.
  - AC: Given one transaction assigned to a category node, when exact category actuals are queried for that node, then it contributes once to the direct total and once to each requested ancestor roll-up, never to a sibling and never more than once in an all-up total.
  - AC: Given a category rename or attributable reparenting, when current hierarchy roll-ups are queried, then the same transaction assignment ID is retained and the current path is used without rewriting historical assignment events.
  - AC: Given a Category Assignment or correction, when it is applied, then account, Payment Instrument, cardholder, Spend Pool, evidence, and Reconciliation State remain unchanged unless changed through their own explicit operations.

## Depends On
- bd-84s (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTIONS-RECORD-GET): Allocations require TransactionStore and transaction identity.
- bd-34a (TASK-LEDGER-CATEGORIES): Allocations require active category identity and lifecycle policy.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Consumer requires LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.

## In Scope
- Deliver zero-or-one Category Assignment, correction, and reconciliation-only carry-forward against any active hierarchy node without rewriting facts.
- Produce: Allocation contracts/domain/store/handlers/module
- Produce: Real-SQLite allocation history tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT split an amount across categories, copy category state into transaction facts, or infer category from account, pool, evidence, or description — per DD-LEDGER-CATEGORY-HIERARCHY.
- Do NOT rewrite assignment history after category rename or reparent; resolve current ancestry at query time.
- Do NOT expose carry-forward as a public command or silently inherit category during ordinary void/supersession — per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3hy (TASK-LEDGER-ACTUALS-PROJECTION)
  - bd-2ve (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CATALOGUE-TRANSACTION-BUNDLE)
  - bd-3pa (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-STATEMENT-CORRECTION-EFFECT-WRITER)

## Success Criteria
- Assigning any active root, intermediate, or leaf category to an active transaction creates exactly one current assignment and changes no transaction fact or independent dimension.
- Correction retires the prior event and appends one replacement with reason/actor; identical replay returns the original result and simultaneous split assignment returns a stable cardinality error.
- Inactive transaction, archived category, or invalid hierarchy target changes nothing; category rename or reparent preserves the assignment ID and current roll-ups use the current ancestry.
- Direct membership contains the transaction once at the assigned node; requested ancestor membership contains it once per ancestor and all-up membership never duplicates it.
- CarryForwardAsync is internal to an authorized statement-correction transaction: it copies the current category to the replacement through one append-only carry_forward event referencing source, replacement, and Reconciliation Decision; ordinary supersession never invokes it.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT split an amount across categories, copy category state into transaction facts, or infer category from account, pool, evidence, or description — per DD-LEDGER-CATEGORY-HIERARCHY.
- ❌ Do NOT rewrite assignment history after category rename or reparent; resolve current ancestry at query time.
- ❌ Do NOT expose carry-forward as a public command or silently inherit category during ordinary void/supersession — per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.

## Approach
- Deliver zero-or-one Category Assignment, correction, and reconciliation-only carry-forward against any active hierarchy node without rewriting facts.
- Expected output: Allocation contracts/domain/store/handlers/module
- Expected output: Real-SQLite allocation history tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: CategoryAllocationOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CONTRACTS) — Public assign and correct operations
- Produces: CategoryAllocationStore (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-HISTORY) — Append-only assignment persistence
- Produces: CategoryAllocationStore.CarryForwardAsync (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-HISTORY) — Reconciliation-only explicit carry-forward
- Consumes: TransactionStore (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-FACT) — Active transaction identity
- Consumes: CategoryStore (DM-LEDGER-SPEND-CATEGORY) — Active hierarchy and ancestry
- Consumes: LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync (DM-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENCY-RECORD) — Atomic replay boundary

- `src/Tally/Domain/Ledger/Transactions/CategoryAllocation.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Transactions/CategoryAllocationHandlers.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Storage/Transactions/CategoryAllocationStore.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Transactions/CategoryAllocationOperationModule.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Features/Ledger/Transactions/CategoryAllocationTests.cs` (test) — implementation — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.Features.Ledger.Transactions.CategoryAllocationTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 12 assignment/correction/history/restriction/replay cases run and 0 fail

title: Implement Reconciliation Decision correction lifecycle
description:
## Objective
Resolve ambiguity and correct decisions through explicit owner intent while retaining statement-authority detail and one active confirmation target.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-DECISION-LIFECYCLE: Resolve and correct Reconciliation Decisions
  - AC: Given ambiguous statement evidence, compatible candidates, an explicit owner selection, reason, actor context, and Idempotency Identity, when the owner confirms one candidate, then one owner-confirmed decision and active evidence link are created atomically and no other candidate is changed.
  - AC: Given ambiguous or unmatched evidence and an explicit owner rejection of all candidates with a reason, when the decision is recorded, then one attributable rejected decision is appended without linking the evidence or creating a Canonical Transaction.
  - AC: Given an active Reconciliation Decision and a valid correction request, when the owner revokes it or replaces it with one compatible candidate, then the prior decision remains immutable, the active evidence link changes atomically, and the current state reflects only the new decision.
  - AC: Given an inactive or incompatible candidate, an Evidence Record already actively confirming another transaction, or a correction without explicit reason and actor context, when a decision is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation or conflict error and changes no link or decision.
  - AC: Given a transaction is voided or superseded, when it has reconciliation history, then no evidence or confirmation silently moves to the replacement and one Reconciliation Exception current outcome remains until another valid decision is recorded.

## Depends On
- bd-37y (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-APPLY): Decision corrections operate on active decisions and links created by apply.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Every decision transition is an idempotent mutation.
- bd-3qf (TASK-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-REGISTRY): Decision correction consumes EvidenceStore for evidence identity and links.
- bd-84s (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTIONS-RECORD-GET): Decision correction consumes TransactionStore for active compatible targets.

## In Scope
- Deliver owner confirm/reject and attributable get/revoke/replace transitions over immutable decision and evidence-link history.
- Produce: Decision contracts/state reducer/store/handlers/module and lifecycle tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT treat Hermes clarification as implicit owner intent; require typed owner assertion fields.
- Do NOT overwrite/delete decisions or links, silently inherit across ordinary supersession, or repair duplicates by editing facts — per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- Do NOT bypass logical decision idempotency or erase statement authority/carry-forward references.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-1f1 (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-RECONCILIATION-BUNDLE)
  - bd-2ae (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-COVERAGE)
  - bd-3pa (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-STATEMENT-CORRECTION-EFFECT-WRITER)
  - bd-2rh (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CORRECTIONS)

## Success Criteria
- decision.get returns ordered history, prior and active transaction IDs, confirmed_existing or corrected_from_statement detail, deterministic-policy or owner authority, statement authority basis, actor, reason, policy/version, links, carry-forward IDs, relationship replacement IDs, and current state.
- confirm/reject require typed owner actor and reason; revoke/replace require the expected active predecessor and atomically retire/create links and decisions.
- Missing/inactive/incompatible candidate, evidence confirmed elsewhere, stale predecessor, conflicting replay, or write failure preserves prior active state with a stable result.
- Correction never deletes history, edits a fact, or permits one statement Evidence Record to confirm multiple active transactions.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT treat Hermes clarification as implicit owner intent; require typed owner assertion fields.
- ❌ Do NOT overwrite/delete decisions or links, silently inherit across ordinary supersession, or repair duplicates by editing facts — per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- ❌ Do NOT bypass logical decision idempotency or erase statement authority/carry-forward references.

## Approach
- Deliver owner confirm/reject and attributable get/revoke/replace transitions over immutable decision and evidence-link history.
- Expected output: Decision contracts/state reducer/store/handlers/module and lifecycle tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: ReconciliationStateReducer (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY)

title: Implement provider-neutral evidence registration
description:
## Objective
Register one privacy-safe generic Evidence Record per logical item and return identical cross-key replay without provider payload retention.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-REGISTRATION: Register and link generic evidence
  - AC: Given a supported generic Evidence Kind, logical evidence identity, Opaque External Reference, privacy-safe metadata or fingerprint, and Idempotency Identity, when evidence is registered, then one stable Evidence Record is returned without storing raw provider, mailbox, MIME, messaging, document, or delivery payloads.
  - AC: Given a registered Evidence Record, an active Canonical Transaction, actor context, and reason, when the evidence is linked, then one attributable active link is appended and the transaction may retain multiple independently queryable Evidence Records.
  - AC: Given the same logical evidence registration or link is replayed with semantically identical input, when it is submitted again, then the original result is returned and no second Evidence Record or link is created.
  - AC: Given an existing logical evidence identity or active confirmation link, when conflicting metadata is supplied or a direct link to a second Canonical Transaction is requested, then the Ledger returns a stable conflict and preserves the existing state.
  - AC: Given newly registered or linked evidence with no valid Reconciliation Decision, when current state is queried, then evidence presence alone does not mark the transaction statement-reconciled or owner-confirmed.
  - AC: Given statement-row evidence with no valid Reconciliation Decision, when a caller requests a confirming transaction link directly, then the Ledger rejects the link and preserves the evidence as unconfirmed.

## Depends On
- bd-2zt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE): Evidence consumes the complete V001 schema, process contract, and real store.
- bd-35j (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION): EvidenceStore requires the evidence tables and uniqueness constraints.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Register is a public idempotent mutation.

## In Scope
- Deliver logical evidence identity, privacy allowlisting, register/get operations, and the reusable transaction-scoped evidence store primitive.
- Produce: Evidence registry contracts, identity policy, store, handlers, module, and privacy/replay tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT introduce a transport-specific provenance enum, single sourceReference field, open metadata bag, or provider adapter — rejected per DD-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT and DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- Do NOT rely only on request-key replay; enforce logical-effect identity per DD-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-MUTATIONS.
- Do NOT confirm a transaction through evidence.register.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-fan (TASK-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-LINKING)
  - bd-2ve (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CATALOGUE-TRANSACTION-BUNDLE)
  - bd-37y (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-APPLY)
  - bd-3q4 (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-DECISIONS)
  - bd-1zm (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-PROJECTION)
  - bd-84s (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTIONS-RECORD-GET)

## Success Criteria
- Register accepts only closed Evidence Kinds, privacy-safe logical identity/content fingerprints, Opaque External Reference, actor, and allowlisted observation fields; get returns safe metadata and history.
- Identical logical evidence under the same or another request key returns the existing record; changed observation/content conflicts and preserves the original.
- Forbidden arbitrary metadata, source payload, provider cursor, mailbox/MIME/message/delivery fields, credentials, or full identifiers fail before any evidence/idempotency row is retained.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT introduce a transport-specific provenance enum, single sourceReference field, open metadata bag, or provider adapter — rejected per DD-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT and DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- ❌ Do NOT rely only on request-key replay; enforce logical-effect identity per DD-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-MUTATIONS.
- ❌ Do NOT confirm a transaction through evidence.register.

## Approach
- Deliver logical evidence identity, privacy allowlisting, register/get operations, and the reusable transaction-scoped evidence store primitive.
- Expected output: Evidence registry contracts, identity policy, store, handlers, module, and privacy/replay tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: EvidenceIdentity (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECORD-LINK)
- Produces: EvidenceStore (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECORD-LINK)
- Produces: EvidenceStore.RegisterInitialAsync (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECORD-LINK) — Caller-scoped transaction primitive used by canonical transaction capture
- Produces: EvidenceRegistryOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync (DM-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENCY-RECORD)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ledger/Evidence/EvidenceRegistryContracts.cs` (create) — public contracts — required
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ledger/Evidence/EvidenceIdentity.cs` (create) — logical identity and allowlist — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Evidence/EvidenceRegistryHandlers.cs` (create) — commands and queries — required
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Storage/Evidence/EvidenceStore.cs` (create) — SQLite evidence and transaction-scoped primitive — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Evidence/EvidenceRegistryOperationModule.cs` (create) — explicit descriptors and DI — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ledger/EvidenceRegistryOperationTests.cs` (test) — contract, privacy, and persistence tests — required

## Verification

- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.Ledger.EvidenceRegistryOperationTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 18 Evidence Kind, allowlist, logical replay, conflict, and privacy cases run and 0 fail

title: Compose the relationship and actuals operation bundle
description:
## Objective
Prove one eight-operation relationship/actuals bundle conserves exact spend across hierarchy, pools, corrections, and statements.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On

- bd-1dt (TASK-LEDGER-ACTUALS-SNAPSHOT): Consumes ActualsOperationModule.

## In Scope
- Convergence gate with no Implements link: compose transfer, refund, relationship lifecycle, statement-corrected transactions, hierarchical actuals, and snapshots.
- Produce: RelationshipActualsOperationBundle
- Produce: Exact-set composition tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT duplicate financial formulas in composition, flatten category scope, count transfer principal, or count both superseded and replacement facts.
- Do NOT accept count-only inventory or infer cash, pool, category, or payment state from another dimension.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT)

## Success Criteria
- The bundle contains exactly eight unique transfer, refund, relationship, and actuals descriptors; schemas retain exact/subtree category scope, unknown/unassigned buckets, statement-correction state, and cash-policy result.
- Descriptor order and every source field remain byte-for-byte stable after canonical serialization; missing module, duplicate path, incompatible version, lost hierarchy/reconciliation field, or unresolved cash gate fails before dispatch.
- Real-store tests prove all-up, category-direct, category-subtree, pool, and matrix conservation through transfer, fee, refund, hierarchy move, relationship replacement, ordinary correction, and statement correction.
- The published actuals path processes 100000 transactions with measured p95 below 2000 ms and exact totals unchanged.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT duplicate financial formulas in composition, flatten category scope, count transfer principal, or count both superseded and replacement facts.
- ❌ Do NOT accept count-only inventory or infer cash, pool, category, or payment state from another dimension.

## Approach
- Convergence gate with no Implements link: compose transfer, refund, relationship lifecycle, statement-corrected transactions, hierarchical actuals, and snapshots.
- Constraint: Use explicit compile-time module composition.
- Expected output: RelationshipActualsOperationBundle
- Expected output: Exact-set composition tests

title: Build the self-contained process and operation contract
description:
## Objective
Produce one offline tally executable whose explicit foundation registry drives dispatch, help, schemas, streams, errors, exits, compatibility, and version reporting.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY: Discover the Ledger command contract
  - AC: Given the self-contained executable, when a caller requests top-level or Ledger help non-interactively, then available provider-neutral public operations, required inputs, output modes, compatibility metadata, and examples are returned without starting a daemon or mutation.
  - AC: Given any public Ledger operation, when its machine schema is requested, then a versioned schema describes request fields, result shape, every expected module-scoped error and exit code, and its idempotency requirements; if its descriptor declares ambiguity or review states, the schema enumerates every such closed outcome.
  - AC: Given a schema request is repeated for the same contract version, when results are compared, then their semantic content is deterministic.
  - AC: Given an operation or field outside the public contract, including provider transport, mailbox, MIME, recipient, schedule, delivery, or acknowledgement concerns, when it is requested, then a stable unknown-operation or validation error is returned with no internal storage detail and no mutation.
  - AC: Given Hermes or another External Orchestrator uses the published schema, when it invokes Ledger, then no provider-specific adapter, private database access, or bundled agent skill is required.
- FR-LEDGER-STRUCTURED-INVOCATION: Invoke Ledger operations non-interactively
  - AC: Given a structured public operation, when valid JSON input is supplied through the documented non-interactive input path, then exactly one structured result is written to stdout and the process exits with the documented success code.
  - AC: Given an expected validation, conflict, lifecycle, compatibility, cursor, not-found, ambiguous-match, stale-projection, or review-required failure, when the operation completes, then one machine-readable module-scoped error is emitted through the documented structured channel, safe diagnostics remain on stderr, and the documented non-zero exit is stable.
  - AC: Given malformed JSON, unknown fields forbidden by the schema, or provider transport, mailbox, MIME, recipient, schedule, delivery, or acknowledgement fields, when invocation is attempted, then validation fails before any mutation.
  - AC: Given a successful or expected-failure invocation, when stdout and stderr are inspected, then stderr contains no transaction description, raw evidence, full account or instrument identifier, credential, or serialized request or response body.
  - AC: Given output is lost after a committed mutation, when the External Orchestrator retries the same Idempotency Identity and semantic input, then the original structured outcome is recoverable without duplicate effects.

## Depends On
- None — this is a compile root.

## In Scope
- Create the .NET 10 Native-AOT solution and provider-neutral descriptor-driven process boundary every Ledger slice extends.
- Produce: net10.0 solution/projects
- Produce: Native-AOT process host and source-generated common contracts
- Produce: OperationDescriptor registry with schema/version operations
- Produce: Focused contract tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT add FastEndpoints, Controllers, Minimal APIs, Aspire, PostgreSQL, EF Core, a daemon/listener, mediator, event bus, generic repository, reflection scanning, or plugin runtime - per DD-LEDGER-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE.
- Do NOT add Hermes, AgentMail, WhatsApp, provider transport, recipient, schedule, delivery, acknowledgement, or orchestration behavior - per ADR-CORE-0030.
- Do NOT expose generic invoke/save/update/delete/SQL/database operations or aliases - rejected per DD-LEDGER-CLI-OPERATION-CONTRACT.
- Do NOT throw expected domain outcomes or place finance policy in TallyProcess or OperationRegistry.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-1ie (TASK-LEDGER-AGENT-SKILLS)
  - bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY)
  - bd-1b2 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-MONEY-DATES)
  - bd-23m (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-STORAGE)
  - bd-2zt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE)
  - bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT)
  - bd-112 (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-SECURITY)

## Success Criteria
- Release linux-x64 publish contains one native tally executable and adjacent SQLite library; invocations start no daemon/listener and leave no child process.
- schema list/show, version, and help return deterministic descriptor-derived request/result/error/exit/compatibility/example metadata without store access or installed guidance.
- Valid JSON through --input - or @file writes one result envelope to stdout; malformed JSON, unknown fields/operations, and provider/delivery fields fail before mutation.
- Expected and sanitized unexpected failures keep diagnostics on stderr with metadata only and never serialize financial/evidence payloads, full identifiers, or stack traces.
- Every async process/handler boundary propagates CancellationToken and registration is explicit; this completed foundation supplied the prior descriptor scaffold, while TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT owns the revised exact 73-operation release inventory after category.reparent is added.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT add FastEndpoints, Controllers, Minimal APIs, Aspire, PostgreSQL, EF Core, a daemon/listener, mediator, event bus, generic repository, reflection scanning, or plugin runtime - per DD-LEDGER-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE.
- ❌ Do NOT add Hermes, AgentMail, WhatsApp, provider transport, recipient, schedule, delivery, acknowledgement, or orchestration behavior - per ADR-CORE-0030.
- ❌ Do NOT expose generic invoke/save/update/delete/SQL/database operations or aliases - rejected per DD-LEDGER-CLI-OPERATION-CONTRACT.
- ❌ Do NOT throw expected domain outcomes or place finance policy in TallyProcess or OperationRegistry.

## Approach
- Create the .NET 10 Native-AOT solution and provider-neutral descriptor-driven process boundary every Ledger slice extends.
- Constraint: Treat compiler warnings as errors and enable nullable.
- Constraint: Use source-generated JSON metadata compatible with Native AOT.
- Expected output: net10.0 solution/projects
- Expected output: Native-AOT process host and source-generated common contracts
- Expected output: OperationDescriptor registry with schema/version operations
- Expected output: Focused contract tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: TallyProcess.RunAsync (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR)
- Produces: OperationRegistry (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR)
- Produces: OperationDescriptor (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR)
- Produces: ICommandHandler<TCommand,TResult>
- Produces: IQueryHandler<TQuery,TResult>
- Produces: CommandResult<TResult> (ADR-CORE-0010)

- `src/Tally/Tally.csproj` (create) — executable — required

- `src/Tally/Features/System/Contract/SystemOperationModule.cs` (create) — handlers — required

title: Append explicit Restatements and deterministic differences
description:
## Objective
RestateInsightReportCommand creates one immutable child only when provenance, version, schema, or semantic content changes and returns ordered bounded differences.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-RESTATEMENT: Create an explicit linked Restatement
  - AC: Given an existing compatible Report Snapshot and a valid actor-attributed idempotent Restate request, when current coherent evidence is available, then one new Report Snapshot is generated without modifying the predecessor.
  - AC: Given a successful Restatement, when its provenance is inspected, then exactly one predecessor identity and the new current source and policy identities are explicit.
  - AC: Given predecessor and restated reports, when the difference result is returned, then Source Provenance, policy-version, exact-metric, signal-state, explanation, and Attention Item differences are separated and deterministically ordered.
  - AC: Given identical predecessor source identities, policy versions, and semantic content, when Restate is requested, then No Change is returned and no new Report Snapshot is created.
  - AC: Given any changed producer state or snapshot identity, policy version, report schema version, or semantic report content, when Restate succeeds, then one new linked Report Snapshot is retained even when exact metric values remain equal.
  - AC: Given an unknown, deleted, corrupted, or incompatible predecessor, when Restate is requested, then the exact stable failure is returned and no new report is created.
  - AC: Given unavailable or incoherent current evidence, when Restate is requested, then no partial Restatement or difference payload is retained.
  - AC: Given the same idempotency identity and same normalized Restate request, when retried, then the prior logical outcome is returned without a second Restatement.

## Depends On
- bd-7kn (TASK-INSIGHTS-STATE-FOUNDATION): Restate reads verified predecessors and atomically writes child and link rows.
- bd-2nv (TASK-INSIGHTS-IDEMPOTENCY-EXECUTOR): Restate resolves replay before current producer access.
- bd-1cr (TASK-INSIGHTS-GENERATE-REPORT): Restate reuses the approved current report path.

## In Scope
- Implement replay-safe current-evidence generation, predecessor comparison, NoChange, and atomic linked snapshot creation without rewriting history.
- Produce: InsightReportDiffer
- Produce: RestateInsightReportCommand
- Produce: Restatement and NoChange tests

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- Restate requires one compatible existing predecessor plus actor, reason, normalized selectors, and idempotency; unknown, deleted, corrupt, or incompatible predecessors fail before producer access.
- Exact replay resolves before current evidence; a new request generates through the existing Generate path and never changes the predecessor.
- InsightReportDiffer compares source identities, policy versions, report schema, exact metrics, evidence states, explanations, and deferred Attention Item collection in fixed section and item order.
- Identical source identities, versions, schema, and semantic content return NoChange and store only the replay outcome; any changed source, version, schema, or content appends one child snapshot and one predecessor link atomically even when visible amounts remain equal.
- Current-evidence, comparison, cancellation, or write failure returns no partial difference, snapshot, link, or successful outcome; same-key replay never creates a second Restatement.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT update or silently replace the predecessor, auto-restate after corrections, store arbitrary caller reports, or event-source every calculation — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-RETENTION-RESTATEMENT-LIFECYCLE.
- ❌ Do NOT compare display text, omit provenance/version-only changes, expose unrelated transactions, or store a difference without its complete child snapshot.
- ❌ Do NOT duplicate the Generate pipeline or call producer operations after an exact replay is found.

## Approach
Implement replay-safe current-evidence generation, predecessor comparison, NoChange, and atomic linked snapshot creation without rewriting history.
- Constraint: One child has exactly one predecessor; a predecessor may have multiple surviving children.
- Expected output: InsightReportDiffer
- Expected output: RestateInsightReportCommand
- Expected output: Restatement and NoChange tests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: GenerateInsightReportQuery.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-INSIGHT-REPORT) — current complete evidence
- Consumes: InsightsIdempotencyExecutor.ExecuteAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-IDEMPOTENCY) — replay before producer access
- Consumes: InsightsStateStore (DM-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE) — predecessor read and atomic child/link append
- Produces: InsightReportDiffer.Compare (DM-INSIGHTS-RETAINED-REPORT-LIFECYCLE) — ordered bounded differences
- Produces: RestateInsightReportCommand.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — insights.report.restate handler

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Domain/Insights/Reports/InsightReportDiffer.cs` (create) — deterministic bounded report comparison
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/History/Restate/RestateInsightReportCommand.cs` (create) — replay-safe linked restatement handler
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/History/RestateInsightReportCommandTests.cs` (test) — predecessor, difference, NoChange, replay, and atomicity tests

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~RestateInsightReportCommandTests` — exit 0; at least 22 unknown, deleted, corrupt, incompatible, replay-before-read, no-change, source-only, policy-only, schema-only, equal-amount semantic-change, ordered-difference, cancellation, write, crash, predecessor-immutability, and duplicate-prevention cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(insights): append deterministic report restatements` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-3t2`

## Lex references
- DD-INSIGHTS-DETERMINISTIC-REPORT-COMPILER
- DD-INSIGHTS-RETENTION-RESTATEMENT-LIFECYCLE
- DIAG-INSIGHTS-REPORT-STATE
- DM-INSIGHTS-RETAINED-REPORT-LIFECYCLE
- FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-RESTATEMENT
- NFR-INSIGHTS-ATOMIC-DURABLE-REPORT-STATE
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-02-RETAINED-HISTORY
- TASK-INSIGHTS-RESTATE-REPORT

- TC-INSIGHTS-RESTATEMENT-CONTRACT

title: Compose statement-authoritative reconciliation correction
description:
## Objective
Extend ledger.reconciliation.apply with the owner-approved composite correction that replaces provisional agent-capture facts from approved authoritative statement evidence. Automatic correction remains disabled until the later OQ-LEDGER-13 activation task.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-STATEMENT-RECONCILIATION

## Depends On
- bd-3lf (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-STATEMENT-CORRECTION-PREREQUISITES): The correction composite consumes the prerequisite seam only after every producer interface and the V002 schema have been proven together.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Correction consumes LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync.
- bd-3pa (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-STATEMENT-CORRECTION-EFFECT-WRITER): The public composite consumes StatementCorrectionEffectWriter.AppendAsync.
- bd-37y (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-APPLY): The public correction extends ReconciliationApplyContracts and ReconciliationApplyOperationModule.

## In Scope
- Extend ledger.reconciliation.apply with the owner-approved composite correction that replaces provisional agent-capture facts from approved authoritative statement evidence. Automatic correction remains disabled until the later OQ-LEDGER-13 activation task.
- Produce: StatementAuthoritativeCorrectionCoordinator
- Produce: Reconciliation apply contract with correct_existing_from_statement
- Produce: Crash-atomic correction and replay tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT overwrite the notification-derived fact, create a second active spend effect, or move Evidence Records, Category Assignments, Pool Assignments, Payment Instrument or Cardholder Attribution, or relationships in place.
- Do NOT accept raw email, MIME, statement document, provider payload, message ID semantics, or arbitrary metadata.
- Do NOT preserve an invalid relationship or silently drop an existing Category Assignment or Pool Assignment; an invalid relationship blocks, while category and pool preservation is explicit.
- Do NOT enable automatic correction until OQ-LEDGER-13 resolves and TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-AUTOMATIC-ACTIVATION supplies the exact supported policy; explicit owner-approved correction remains available.

## Success Criteria
- Given one prior economic event selected through the advisory projection and explicit owner authority, correct_existing_from_statement revalidates the Evidence Record, target and guard membership, owner actor and reason, authoritative facts, active dimensions, and relationships inside one BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction.
- A valid correction appends one statement-derived fact, supersession, Reconciliation Decision, confirming link, Category Assignment and Pool Assignment carry-forward, compatible Payment Instrument and Cardholder Attribution carry-forward or explicit unknown initialization, and any invariant-preserving transfer or refund relationship replacement; exactly one fact remains active.
- The original agent-capture fact, its supporting Evidence Record, prior Category Assignment, prior Payment Instrument and Cardholder Attribution, Pool Assignment, and prior relationship history remain queryable; every new event references the Reconciliation Decision and source and replacement Canonical Transaction IDs.
- If Payment Instrument or Cardholder Attribution is incompatible, explicit unknown state and review metadata commit; if an active transfer or refund would violate role, account, sign, currency, principal, or refund conservation, the operation returns review-required and commits no replacement or Idempotency Identity outcome.
- An automatic correction request returns the stable unsupported-policy result until TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-AUTOMATIC-ACTIVATION completes; crash injection before and after every write yields either the complete owner-approved outcome once or unchanged prior state.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT overwrite the notification-derived fact, create a second active spend effect, or move Evidence Records, Category Assignments, Pool Assignments, Payment Instrument or Cardholder Attribution, or relationships in place.
- ❌ Do NOT accept raw email, MIME, statement document, provider payload, message ID semantics, or arbitrary metadata.
- ❌ Do NOT preserve an invalid relationship or silently drop an existing Category Assignment or Pool Assignment; an invalid relationship blocks, while category and pool preservation is explicit.
- ❌ Do NOT enable automatic correction until OQ-LEDGER-13 resolves and TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-AUTOMATIC-ACTIVATION supplies the exact supported policy; explicit owner-approved correction remains available.

## Approach
- Extend ledger.reconciliation.apply with the owner-approved composite correction that replaces provisional agent-capture facts from approved authoritative statement evidence. Automatic correction remains disabled until the later OQ-LEDGER-13 activation task.
- Constraint: All financial and idempotency effects commit inside one LedgerMutationExecutor writer transaction.
- Note: This vertical composite follows the base reconciliation and ordinary correction slices to avoid a dependency cycle and keep ordinary supersession semantics distinct.
- Expected output: StatementAuthoritativeCorrectionCoordinator
- Expected output: Reconciliation apply contract with correct_existing_from_statement
- Expected output: Crash-atomic correction and replay tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: StatementAuthoritativeCorrectionCoordinator (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY)
- Produces: StatementAuthorityPolicy (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY) — Owner authority first; automatic authority remains unsupported until activation
- Produces: StatementCorrectionOperationExtension (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: StatementCorrectionEffectWriter.AppendAsync (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY)
- Consumes: ReconciliationApplyOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync (DM-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENCY-RECORD)
- Consumes: VerifiedStatementCorrectionPrerequisites (DM-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-HISTORY)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ledger/Reconciliation/ReconciliationApplyContracts.cs` (modify) — public correction contract — required
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ledger/Reconciliation/StatementAuthorityPolicy.cs` (create) — correction policy — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Reconciliation/StatementAuthoritativeCorrectionCoordinator.cs` (create) — atomic composite — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Reconciliation/ReconciliationApplyOperationModule.cs` (modify) — public operation extension — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ledger/StatementAuthoritativeCorrectionTests.cs` (test) — contract and invariant tests — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Integration/ReconciliationCrashAtomicityTests.cs` (test) — crash matrix extension — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~StatementAuthoritativeCorrectionTests --no-restore && dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~ReconciliationCrashAtomicityTests --no-restore` — exit 0 from both commands; at least 36 owner-authority, replacement, unsupported-policy, carry-forward, relationship, crash, rollback, and replay cases run in total and 0 fail
- **test-evidence:** Show exactly one active replacement, complete historical lineage, explicit dimensions, relationship safety, all crash points, and replay.
- **branch-review:** Confirm no transport, INGEST batch, raw-payload, overwrite, or duplicate-spend behavior entered LEDGER.

## Commit
- `feat(ledger): add statement-authoritative correction` — one commit per bead; footer `Refs: bd-3ts`

- DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY

title: CategoryAllocationWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove one Category Assignment remains attributable and rolls up exactly through the current hierarchy.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-CATEGORY-ASSIGNMENT: Assign and correct transaction categories
  - AC: Given an active transaction and any active category node, when the owner assigns the category, then exactly one active assignment is returned and the transaction facts remain unchanged.
  - AC: Given a categorized active transaction, an active replacement category, and a reason, when the owner corrects the assignment, then the prior assignment is retired, the replacement becomes active, and both remain attributable in history.
  - AC: Given an inactive transaction or archived category, when an assignment or correction is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and changes no assignment.
  - AC: Given one transaction assigned to a category node, when exact category actuals are queried for that node, then it contributes once to the direct total and once to each requested ancestor roll-up, never to a sibling and never more than once in an all-up total.
  - AC: Given a category rename or attributable reparenting, when current hierarchy roll-ups are queried, then the same transaction assignment ID is retained and the current path is used without rewriting historical assignment events.
  - AC: Given a Category Assignment or correction, when it is applied, then account, Payment Instrument, cardholder, Spend Pool, evidence, and Reconciliation State remain unchanged unless changed through their own explicit operations.
- FR-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-WRITES: Make public writes idempotent
  - AC: Given any previously successful public mutation, when the same Idempotency Identity, operation, contract version, and semantically identical request are submitted again, then the original structured result is returned and no second domain, decision, link, or artifact effect occurs.
  - AC: Given a previously successful Idempotency Identity, when it is reused for another operation, contract version, or materially different request, then the same module-scoped conflict code is returned on every retry and the original effect is preserved.
  - AC: Given an invalid mutation request, when validation fails, then no domain or artifact effect occurs and the supplied Idempotency Identity remains available for a corrected request.
  - AC: Given evidence registration or linking, reconciliation application or correction, payment attribution, Pool Assignment, or pool maintenance, when its logical identity is replayed through another request identity, then the domain uniqueness rule prevents a duplicate effect independently of request replay.
  - AC: Given any public mutation without a valid caller-visible Idempotency Identity, when invocation is attempted, then the Ledger rejects it before mutation with a stable validation error.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace hierarchical UC-LEDGER-003 through the Release CLI.
- Produce: UC003CategoryAllocationWorkflowTests with main/failure paths

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT bypass the public CLI, assert only stored category ID, or omit split, lifecycle, hierarchy-move, and replay paths.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- Published calls assign root, intermediate, and leaf nodes and correct between them; each transaction has zero or one active assignment and facts/other dimensions remain unchanged.
- Archived target, inactive transaction, simultaneous split request, stale correction, and conflicting replay produce documented no-mutation outcomes.
- After category rename and reparent, the assignment ID/history remains stable while direct and current-ancestor membership each include the transaction exactly once.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT bypass the public CLI, assert only stored category ID, or omit split, lifecycle, hierarchy-move, and replay paths.

## Approach
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace hierarchical UC-LEDGER-003 through the Release CLI.
- Constraint: Invoke the Release published tally executable; inspect stdout, stderr, exit code, and real temporary SQLite state.
- Expected output: UC003CategoryAllocationWorkflowTests with main/failure paths

## Interfaces
- Consumes: PublishedTallyFixture (Release published-process E2E fixture)
- Consumes: CompletePublicContract (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — Exactly 72 provider-neutral operations
- Produces: VerifiedUC003 (UC-LEDGER-003) — workflow verification

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/EndToEnd/UC003CategoryAllocationWorkflowTests.cs` (test) — verification — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.EndToEnd.UC003CategoryAllocationWorkflowTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 7 main/failure-path scenarios run and 0 fail

## Commit

title: Implement Spend Pool catalogue operations
description:
## Objective
Create, inspect, list, rename, archive, and reactivate Spend Pools with stable identity and historical RESTRICT behavior.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-SPEND-POOL-CATALOGUE: Maintain the Spend Pool catalogue
  - AC: Given a valid unique pool name such as company-paid discretionary or personal after-tax, when the owner creates a Spend Pool, then one active pool with a stable identifier is returned.
  - AC: Given an existing Spend Pool, when the owner renames it, then its stable identifier and historical transaction assignments remain unchanged.
  - AC: Given a referenced Spend Pool, when the owner archives it, then history remains queryable and new assignments are rejected until the same identity is reactivated.
  - AC: Given account, Payment Instrument, cardholder, category, and pool records, when any is created or changed, then no value in one dimension silently creates, selects, renames, or archives a value in another.
  - AC: Given active and archived pools, when they are listed with an explicit lifecycle filter, then each matching pool appears once with stable identity and assignment counts.

## Depends On
- bd-z7p (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-EVIDENCE-POOL-CARDINALITY): Pool implementation is forbidden until OQ-LEDGER-15 validates one-active-pool cardinality.
- bd-2zt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CORE): The catalogue uses the proven process and SQLite seam.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Every catalogue mutation consumes LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync.

## In Scope
- Deliver the first-class provider-neutral pool catalogue independently from accounts, payment identities, and categories.
- Produce: Spend Pool contracts, lifecycle, store, handlers, operation module, and focused tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT encode pools as categories or derive them from account, instrument, cardholder, or description — rejected per DD-LEDGER-DIMENSIONAL-ATTRIBUTION.
- Do NOT physically delete referenced pools or rewrite lifecycle history — rejected per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- Do NOT add budget amounts or performance policy to LEDGER.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-2ve (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CATALOGUE-TRANSACTION-BUNDLE)
  - bd-2bd (TASK-LEDGER-POOL-ASSIGNMENTS)

## Success Criteria
- Create/list/get/rename/archive/reactivate expose stable local pool ULIDs, owner labels, active state, assignment counts, actor, reason, and history without mutating any other dimension.

title: Implement safe storage evolution and status
description:
## Objective
Upgrade supported storage exactly once while failure leaves the original complete generation authoritative.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-SAFE-STORAGE-EVOLUTION: Upgrade or migrate Ledger storage safely
  - AC: Given a supported source Ledger, when an upgrade or migration is requested, then compatibility, integrity, permissions, available space, and required recovery conditions are checked before source mutation.
  - AC: Given successful preflight, when evolution begins, then an independently verified complete-state recovery point is established before candidate activation.
  - AC: Given a completed candidate, when validation runs, then target storage version, every Durable Ledger State type, evidence and reconciliation link, category and pool history, attribution, financial relationship, idempotency outcome, per-type count, cardinality rule, integrity result, and exact pool/category total matches the source and declared migration outcome before activation.
  - AC: Given successful candidate validation, when activation occurs, then it is atomic and the new version becomes authoritative exactly once.
  - AC: Given any preflight, conversion, validation, or activation failure, when the operation ends, then the original Ledger remains authoritative and no partially evolved store is exposed.

## Depends On
- bd-fup (TASK-LEDGER-RESTORE-ACTIVATE): Evolution reuses the verified candidate activation seam.
- bd-3j9 (TASK-LEDGER-BACKUP-VERIFY): Consumer requires DurableLedgerVerifier from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.
- bd-3ja (TASK-LEDGER-DURABLE-STATE-VERIFIER): Consumes DurableLedgerVerifier.

## In Scope
- Deliver status plus isolated prepare/activate evolution with recovery point, full hierarchy/authority validation, and atomic generation switch.
- Produce: Storage status/evolution contracts/service/module
- Produce: Migration candidate builder
- Produce: Preflight/equivalence/activation tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT migrate live state in place, edit committed migrations, skip verified recovery, activate stale candidates, expose partial candidates, permit downgrade, or invent a generic migration abstraction.
- Do NOT accept a candidate with category cycles, duplicate active effects, incomplete statement correction, invalid relationship replacement, or mismatched exact totals.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-5gf (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-RECOVERY-SKILL-BUNDLE)

## Success Criteria
- storage.status returns contract/storage/reconciliation-policy versions, current generation/fingerprint, permissions, integrity, and host protection without financial/evidence payload.
- evolution.prepare validates source integrity, compatibility, permissions, free space, protection, and verified recovery before isolated transformation.
- Candidate equivalence covers every durable fact/history/dimension/category ancestry/evidence/reconciliation replacement/carry-forward/relationship/replay type, count, and exact direct/subtree/pool total.
- evolution.activate requires expected current fingerprint and authorization, revalidates under lock, switches once atomically, and retains recovery generation; every failure leaves the original current.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT migrate live state in place, edit committed migrations, skip verified recovery, activate stale candidates, expose partial candidates, permit downgrade, or invent a generic migration abstraction.
- ❌ Do NOT accept a candidate with category cycles, duplicate active effects, incomplete statement correction, invalid relationship replacement, or mismatched exact totals.

## Approach
- Deliver status plus isolated prepare/activate evolution with recovery point, full hierarchy/authority validation, and atomic generation switch.
- Expected output: Storage status/evolution contracts/service/module
- Expected output: Migration candidate builder
- Expected output: Preflight/equivalence/activation tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: StorageEvolutionOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-RECOVERY-STORAGE-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: StorageEvolutionService (DM-LEDGER-RECOVERY-STORAGE-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: MigrationCandidateBuilder (DM-LEDGER-STORE-GENERATION)
- Consumes: BackupService (DM-LEDGER-RECOVERY-STORAGE-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: DurableLedgerVerifier (DM-LEDGER-STORE-GENERATION)
- Consumes: AuthoritativeStoreActivator (DD-LEDGER-CANDIDATE-ACTIVATION)

## Verification

title: Implement safe abandon, compaction, and cleanup
description:
## Objective
Deliver ingest.abandon, ingest.cleanup, and safe startup cleanup without deleting sources, recoverable manifests, receipts, or Ledger data.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-ARTIFACT-CLEANUP: Abandon and clean up ingestion artifacts safely
  - AC: Given preview, commit, failure, or abandonment reaches a safe boundary, when temporary extraction data is no longer required, then INGEST removes it deterministically and retains no caller-owned source copy.
  - AC: Given a crash left known temporary artifacts, when the next safe INGEST invocation starts, then cleanup identifies and removes only orphaned INGEST temporaries without touching sources, manifests required for resume, receipts, or LEDGER data.
  - AC: Given an incomplete batch, when the owner explicitly abandons it, then INGEST records abandonment, prevents future commit or resume for that manifest revision, removes disposable temporaries, leaves prior canonical transactions unchanged, and leaves the source untouched.
  - AC: Given an incomplete manifest or receipt still required for review or resume, when cleanup is requested, then INGEST rejects destructive cleanup with a stable retained-for-recovery result.
  - AC: Given a completed metadata-only receipt or safely abandoned batch, when the owner explicitly requests cleanup, then INGEST removes only the selected retained INGEST artifacts and reports the result without deleting any canonical transaction or caller-owned source.

## Depends On
- bd-2i6 (TASK-INGEST-COMMIT-SAGA): Abandon and compaction consume BatchCommitLock and durable candidate outcomes.
- bd-3l1 (TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION): RecoveryStateStore uses protected ingest.db and artifact enforcement.
- bd-twx (TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION): Consumes ImportReceipt.

## In Scope
- Make incomplete state terminal only after lock release, preserve prior canonical effects, compact sensitive state, and delete only state-proven disposable INGEST artifacts.
- Produce: Abandon and cleanup handlers.
- Produce: RecoveryStateStore.
- Produce: StartupIngestCleanup.
- Produce: RecoveryCleanupOperationModule.
- Produce: Cleanup tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- Abandon requires an incomplete non-terminal batch, exact durable state, explicit reason, and an unheld BatchCommitLock; it atomically marks abandoned, blocks future commit/resume, preserves prior Ledger transaction references/counts, and compacts sensitive state to a metadata tombstone.
- A completed batch compacts manifest rows, descriptions, amounts, balances, controls, evidence, and frozen requests only after every terminal canonical reference verifies; CompletedMetadataReceipt retains stable identities, versions, outcome categories, safe refs, Ledger refs, counts, and completedAt.
- Cleanup rejects active, approved, committing, interrupted-but-recoverable, locked, ambiguous, or mismatched expectedTerminalStatus targets with retained_for_recovery; it removes only the selected completed receipt or abandoned tombstone/artifacts.
- StartupIngestCleanup inspects only known INGEST lock/atomic artifact names, verifies no live lock and durable disposability, and removes stale artifacts; unknown files, sources, manifests required for resume, receipts, and Ledger data are untouched.
- Every success, expected failure, simulated crash, abandon, cleanup, and startup path leaves no extraction temporary and leaves caller-owned source bytes unchanged.
- RecoveryCleanupOperationModule binds ingest.abandon and ingest.cleanup but global registration waits for the public-contract gate.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT cascade-delete canonical Ledger effects, caller-owned sources, active/recoverable state, or unknown files — RESTRICT by default per ADR-CORE-0020 and DD-INGEST-STATE-STORE.
- ❌ Do NOT abandon while the batch lock is held or erase evidence needed to describe prior Ledger effects.
- ❌ Do NOT use broad globs, directory recursion, filename similarity, or age alone to choose cleanup targets.
- ❌ Do NOT create extraction temporary as part of normal PdfPig processing.

## Approach
Make incomplete state terminal only after lock release, preserve prior canonical effects, compact sensitive state, and delete only state-proven disposable INGEST artifacts.
- Constraint: Deletion targets are explicit state-derived paths beneath the validated INGEST data root; sources and Ledger paths are never eligible.
- Note: Removal impact: completed sensitive state is replaced by a metadata-only receipt; abandoned sensitive state is replaced by a terminal tombstone, so replay prevention and prior-effect evidence are retained.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: ImportReceipt (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-RECEIPT)
- Consumes: IngestDatabase (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Consumes: IngestArtifactProtection.EnsureOwnerOnly (DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY)
- Consumes: BatchCommitLock (DD-INGEST-COMMIT-RECOVERY)
- Produces: RecoveryStateStore (DM-INGEST-STATE-STORE)
- Produces: StartupIngestCleanup (DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY)
- Produces: RecoveryCleanupOperationModule (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Recovery/AbandonOperation.cs` (create) — abandon command and handler — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Recovery/CleanupOperation.cs` (create) — cleanup command and handler — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Recovery/RecoveryCleanupOperationModule.cs` (create) — abandon/cleanup descriptor bindings — required
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Ingest/Storage/RecoveryStateStore.cs` (create) — abandon/compaction/cleanup SQL — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Recovery/StartupIngestCleanup.cs` (create) — state-checked stale artifact cleanup — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Recovery/AbandonCleanupTests.cs` (test) — terminal, compaction, restrict tests — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Recovery/StartupCleanupTests.cs` (test) — crash residue and target-isolation tests — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~AbandonCleanupTests|FullyQualifiedName~StartupCleanupTests'` -> exit 0; at least 22 lock, abandon, prior-effect, compaction, retained-for-recovery, terminal cleanup, stale-known-artifact, unknown-file, source-preservation, and crash cases are discovered and 0 tests fail

## Commit
- `feat(ingest): implement abandon and cleanup` — footer `Refs: bd-557`

- DD-INGEST-ARTIFACT-SECURITY
- FR-INGEST-ARTIFACT-CLEANUP
- PLAN-INGEST-V1
- SP-INGEST-03-COMMIT-RECOVERY
- TASK-INGEST-ABANDON-CLEANUP
- TASK-INGEST-COMMIT-SAGA
- TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION
- TASK-INGEST-STATE-FOUNDATION
- TC-INGEST-ARTIFACT-CLEANUP-CONTRACT
- Deep context: `lex context bd-557 --json`

title: Compose the recovery and integration-guidance bundle
description:
## Objective
Produce one deterministic 13-operation recovery/system/guidance bundle, rejecting missing, duplicate, incompatible, provider-leaking, or skill-authoritative descriptors before dispatch.

## Implements

## Depends On

- bd-4zp (TASK-LEDGER-STORAGE-EVOLUTION): Consumes StorageEvolutionOperationModule.
- bd-1ie (TASK-LEDGER-AGENT-SKILLS): Consumes GuidanceOperationModule while preserving schema authority.

## In Scope
- Convergence gate intentionally has no Implements link: compose backup, restore, storage-evolution, schema/version, and optional guidance modules without changing their contracts.
- Produce: RecoveryGuidanceOperationBundle with exactly 13 descriptors
- Produce: Exact-set, schema-authority, and optional-guidance composition tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT perform recovery, install skills, discover modules through reflection, or duplicate descriptor schemas in composition.
- Do NOT accept a count-only assertion without exact set equality.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT)

## Success Criteria

- Descriptor order is operationId ordinal and every source field is retained byte-for-byte; discovery and invocation remain complete when no guidance bundle is installed.
- A missing module, duplicate operationId/path, incompatible range, provider/transport field, or guidance-only capability fails composition before dispatch.
- Focused tests compare the 13-operation inventory with all source modules and prove no descriptor is added, dropped, aliased, or rewritten.

## Failure Criteria

- ❌ Do NOT accept a count-only assertion without exact set equality.

## Approach
- Convergence gate intentionally has no Implements link: compose backup, restore, storage-evolution, schema/version, and optional guidance modules without changing their contracts.
- Constraint: Use explicit compile-time module composition.
- Expected output: RecoveryGuidanceOperationBundle with exactly 13 descriptors

title: Retain an internally generated immutable report
description:
## Objective
RetainInsightReportCommand appends exactly one complete immutable ReportSnapshot and replay outcome without accepting caller-authored report content.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-REPORT-RETENTION: Retain an immutable Report Snapshot explicitly
  - AC: Given an ordinary Generate request, when it succeeds, then no Report Snapshot, idempotency outcome, or retained payload is created.
  - AC: Given a valid Retain request with analysis inputs, actor, reason, and idempotency identity, when it succeeds, then INSIGHTS generates one complete report and atomically appends one immutable Report Snapshot.
  - AC: Given a Retain request, when input is validated, then no arbitrary caller-authored report payload is accepted as the stored result.
  - AC: Given a retained snapshot, when its identity is inspected, then actor, reason, trusted creation time, analysis request fingerprint, source and policy versions, content fingerprint, and report schema version are present.
  - AC: Given the same idempotency identity and semantically identical normalized request, when Retain is retried, then the prior logical Report Snapshot is returned and no duplicate is created.
  - AC: Given the same idempotency identity with different normalized input, when Retain is attempted, then conflict is returned and retained history is unchanged.
  - AC: Given validation, generation, persistence, cancellation, or crash failure, when Retain ends, then no partial Report Snapshot or successful idempotency outcome exists.
  - AC: Given later source corrections or policy changes, when a retained snapshot is read, then its stored content and fingerprint remain unchanged.

## Depends On
- bd-7kn (TASK-INSIGHTS-STATE-FOUNDATION): Retain writes the snapshot and payload schema.
- bd-2nv (TASK-INSIGHTS-IDEMPOTENCY-EXECUTOR): Retain must resolve and reserve replay before producer access.
- bd-1cr (TASK-INSIGHTS-GENERATE-REPORT): Retain reuses the complete approved generation path.

## In Scope
- Implement the explicit owner-attributed idempotent Retain slice over the ephemeral Generate path and one atomic store transaction.
- Produce: RetainInsightReportCommand
- Produce: Atomic retained-generation tests

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- Retain requires normalized analysis input, trusted P1 actor, non-blank reason, and idempotency identity and accepts no report payload, reportId, snapshotId, contentFingerprint, or provenance authored by the caller.
- Exact replay resolves before TimeProvider and producer access; a new request invokes the existing Generate path, allocates one opaque ULID snapshotId, and appends snapshot metadata, canonical report payload, fingerprint, actor, reason, and Succeeded outcome in one BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction.
- The retained identity records analysis-request fingerprint, source and policy versions, report schema, content fingerprint, trusted creation time, actor, and reason; later producer or policy change cannot alter it.
- Changed-input replay conflicts; validation, generation, cancellation, SQLite, disk, or commit failure leaves no partial snapshot, payload, or successful replay outcome.
- Logs and errors contain only safe stable IDs, fingerprints, states, and counts.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT retain ordinary Generate results automatically, accept caller-authored report content, update an existing report, or store upstream member rows — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-RETENTION-RESTATEMENT-LIFECYCLE.

- ❌ Do NOT add automatic expiry, cleanup, background work, multiple writers, or payload-bearing diagnostics.

## Approach
Implement the explicit owner-attributed idempotent Retain slice over the ephemeral Generate path and one atomic store transaction.
- Constraint: Reuse GenerateInsightReportQuery and InsightsIdempotencyExecutor; duplicate analytical pipelines are forbidden.
- Expected output: RetainInsightReportCommand
- Expected output: Atomic retained-generation tests

- Consumes: InsightsIdempotencyExecutor.ExecuteAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-IDEMPOTENCY) — replay before producer reads
- Consumes: InsightsStateStore (DM-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE) — atomic snapshot and payload append
- Produces: RetainInsightReportCommand.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — insights.report.retain handler

title: Build the owner-only INSIGHTS SQLite generation store
description:
## Objective
InsightsStateStore provides atomic normalized metadata plus canonical report-payload storage without copying upstream truth or private validation evidence.

## Implements
- None; this task is a validation or shared-contract task with no direct FR ownership.

## Depends On
- bd-1qz (TASK-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-MODELS): The store maps immutable report, history, idempotency, and recovery contracts.
- bd-gsb (TASK-INSIGHTS-GATE-EVIDENCE-OWNER-VALUE): The monitored owner-value gate must pass before retained INSIGHTS state is built.
- bd-21u (TASK-INSIGHTS-LIFECYCLE-CONTRACT-MODELS): The state store maps lifecycle idempotency and recovery contracts.

## In Scope
- Persistence foundation with no direct Implements ownership; create one versioned raw-SQLite generation store, schema, SQL, row mapping, transactions, integrity checks, and owner-only layout after the owner-value gate passes.
- Produce: InsightsStateStore
- Produce: InsightsStoreLayout
- Produce: InsightsMigrations
- Produce: InsightsRowMapper
- Produce: Real-SQLite schema and protection tests

## Out of Scope
- This task is intentionally loose because it provides the shared persistence substrate; feature tasks own behavior.

## Success Criteria
- One owner-only CURRENT pointer selects one insights.db inside an owner-only generation directory; directories use 0700 and database, WAL, SHM, pointer, lock, manifest, backup, and recognized candidate files use 0600 where supported.
- Immutable ordered migrations create store_metadata, report_snapshot, report_payload, restatement_link, deletion_event, insights_idempotency_record, recovery_receipt, and restore_event with foreign keys, uniqueness, RESTRICT semantics, and PRAGMA user_version.
- Connections enable foreign_keys, WAL, synchronous FULL, and bounded busy_timeout; writers use BEGIN IMMEDIATE and cancellation-safe rollback; queries use short read connections.
- InsightsRowMapper is a concrete pure mapper between rows and immutable contracts, List and Status metadata paths do not deserialize payloads, and Get verifies schema plus content fingerprints.
- The database never stores dated actual members, raw merchant text, canonical transactions, producer cursors, private corpus rows, arbitrary formulas, PolicyValidationReceipt, report-list payloads, or upstream replicas; tests use real temporary SQLite and never mock SQL.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT use JSON files, fully normalize analytical report bodies, add EF Core or a hosted database, or place state in BUDGET/LEDGER — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE.
- ❌ Do NOT add IInsightsStore, generic repository, provider-neutral unit of work, reflection mapping, multiple writers, or automatic cleanup.
- ❌ Do NOT claim custom encryption; host-managed encrypted-volume evidence remains a release gate.
- ❌ Do NOT implement this task unless TASK-INSIGHTS-GATE-EVIDENCE-OWNER-VALUE completed successfully.

## Approach
Persistence foundation with no direct Implements ownership; create one versioned raw-SQLite generation store, schema, SQL, row mapping, transactions, integrity checks, and owner-only layout after the owner-value gate passes.
- Constraint: Raw SQLite only; use explicit SQL and transactions with CancellationToken.
- Expected output: InsightsStateStore
- Expected output: InsightsStoreLayout
- Expected output: InsightsMigrations
- Expected output: InsightsRowMapper
- Expected output: Real-SQLite schema and protection tests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: VerifiedInsightsOwnerValueGate (DM-INSIGHTS-POLICY-VALIDATION-RECEIPT) — persistence kill gate
- Consumes: InsightHistoryContracts (DM-INSIGHTS-RETAINED-REPORT-LIFECYCLE) — immutable snapshot restatement and deletion records
- Consumes: InsightsIdempotencyRecord (DM-INSIGHTS-IDEMPOTENCY) — transactional replay records
- Consumes: InsightsRecoveryContracts (DM-INSIGHTS-RECOVERY-CONTRACTS) — recovery receipts and status records
- Produces: InsightsStoreLayout (DM-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE) — owner-only generation layout
- Produces: InsightsMigrations.ApplyAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE) — compatible immutable migrations
- Produces: InsightsRowMapper (DM-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE) — concrete pure row mapper
- Produces: InsightsStateStore (DM-INSIGHTS-STATE-STORE) — single logical raw-SQLite store

## Files (reservation globs)

- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Insights/Storage/InsightsMigrations.cs` (create) — immutable ordered schema evolution

title: Implement immutable transaction record and get
description:
## Objective
Atomically record at most one immutable Canonical Transaction and its privacy-safe initial Evidence Record while round-tripping every accepted fact and link exactly.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-RECORDING: Record Canonical Transactions
  - AC: Given an active account, valid original description, exact base-10 signed amount, ZAR currency, transaction date, privacy-safe initial evidence, and Idempotency Identity, when recording succeeds, then one stable Canonical Transaction and one linked Evidence Record are created atomically without precision loss.
  - AC: Given a transaction with a distinct supplied posting date, when it is recorded, then both transaction date and posting date round-trip without substituting one for the other.
  - AC: Given any missing required fact, unsupported currency, zero amount, malformed date, archived account, unsupported Evidence Kind, or forbidden provider payload, when recording is attempted, then a stable validation error is returned and neither transaction nor evidence is created.
  - AC: Given a recorded transaction, when it is read later, then its account identity, original description, signed amount, currency, supplied dates, stable identifier, evidence links, current Reconciliation State, and explicit unknown attribution states round-trip without silently inferring category, pool, instrument, or cardholder.
  - AC: Given a movement on an active owned asset or liability account, when its signed amount is recorded, then a positive value means the owner's economic position improved and a negative value means it worsened: asset increases and liability reductions are positive; asset decreases and liability increases are negative.
  - AC: Given the same logical initial evidence and semantically identical Canonical Transaction facts are submitted through another request identity, when recording is attempted, then the original transaction and evidence result is returned and no duplicate effect is created.
  - AC: Given the same logical initial evidence is submitted with materially different Canonical Transaction facts, when recording is attempted, then a stable conflict is returned and the existing transaction and evidence remain unchanged.
  - AC: Given a Canonical Transaction created through the ordinary recording operation, when its initial state is returned, then its Reconciliation State is recorded-but-unreconciled until a valid deterministic or owner-approved Reconciliation Decision changes it.

## Depends On
- bd-34k (TASK-LEDGER-ACCOUNTS): Transactions require AccountStore and archived-account policy.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Consumer requires LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.
- bd-1b2 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-MONEY-DATES): Consumer requires Money from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.
- bd-3qf (TASK-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-REGISTRY): Atomic transaction capture consumes EvidenceStore.RegisterInitialAsync.

## In Scope
- Deliver ledger.transaction.record/get over exact source facts, generic initial evidence, account state, explicit unknown attribution/unassigned pool, idempotency, and real SQLite.
- Produce: Transaction contracts/domain/store/handlers/module
- Produce: Real-SQLite source-fact tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT update source facts, embed provider provenance, store raw evidence payloads, use a single sourceReference authority, use floating point, or silently normalize bank dates.
- Do NOT infer Payment Instrument, cardholder, Spend Pool, category, or reconciliation confirmation.
- Do NOT accept credentials or expose unmasked account/payment identifiers in results or diagnostics.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3q1 (TASK-LEDGER-CATEGORY-ALLOCATIONS)
  - bd-fan (TASK-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-LINKING)
  - bd-3nk (TASK-LEDGER-PAYMENT-ATTRIBUTION)
  - bd-2bd (TASK-LEDGER-POOL-ASSIGNMENTS)
  - bd-37y (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-APPLY)
  - bd-2ae (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-COVERAGE)
  - bd-3q4 (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-DECISIONS)
  - bd-1zm (TASK-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-PROJECTION)
  - bd-10w (TASK-LEDGER-REFUNDS)
  - bd-2rh (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CORRECTIONS)
  - bd-xq6 (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSFERS)

## Success Criteria
- Valid active asset or liability account, canonical nonzero owner-economic signedAmount, ZAR, required dates/description, closed generic Evidence Kind, Opaque External Reference, privacy-safe observation, actor, optional stable payment IDs, and key create one immutable TransactionDetail and one linked Evidence Record.
- TransactionDate and distinct PostingDate round-trip independently; Effective Date is TransactionDate; missing payment identities remain explicitly unknown and pool remains explicitly unassigned.
- Missing/zero/malformed/unsupported input, archived/missing account, incompatible attribution, or forbidden evidence field returns the documented stable error and creates no transaction, evidence link, assignment, or idempotency row.
- Get returns unchanged facts plus safe evidence links, current Reconciliation State, payment attribution, Pool Assignment, and requested history without mutating.
- Identical request or logical evidence replay returns the existing transaction; conflicting key or evidence identity preserves the original; crash injection commits transaction, initial evidence, link, defaults, and replay outcome together or none.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT update source facts, embed provider provenance, store raw evidence payloads, use a single sourceReference authority, use floating point, or silently normalize bank dates.
- ❌ Do NOT infer Payment Instrument, cardholder, Spend Pool, category, or reconciliation confirmation.
- ❌ Do NOT accept credentials or expose unmasked account/payment identifiers in results or diagnostics.

## Approach
- Deliver ledger.transaction.record/get over exact source facts, generic initial evidence, account state, explicit unknown attribution/unassigned pool, idempotency, and real SQLite.
- Expected output: Transaction contracts/domain/store/handlers/module
- Expected output: Real-SQLite source-fact tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: TransactionBaseOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CONTRACTS) — record and get descriptors
- Produces: TransactionStore (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-FACT) — atomic transaction/default-dimension writes
- Produces: TransactionDetail (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CONTRACTS) — facts and linked current state
- Consumes: AccountStore (DM-LEDGER-ACCOUNT) — account authority
- Consumes: Money (DD-LEDGER-FINANCIAL-REPRESENTATION) — exact values
- Consumes: EvidenceStore.RegisterInitialAsync (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECORD-LINK) — transaction-scoped evidence primitive
- Consumes: LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync (DM-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENCY-RECORD) — atomic replay

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ledger/Transactions/TransactionContracts.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Domain/Ledger/Transactions/TransactionFact.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Transactions/TransactionRecordHandlers.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Infrastructure/Storage/Transactions/TransactionStore.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Transactions/TransactionOperationModule.cs` (create) — implementation — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Features/Ledger/Transactions/TransactionRecordingTests.cs` (test) — implementation — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Infrastructure/Storage/TransactionStoreTests.cs` (test) — implementation — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~TransactionRecordingTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 14 exactness, date, evidence privacy, default dimension, account, and logical replay cases run and 0 fail
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~TransactionStoreTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 10 real-SQLite uniqueness, atomicity, and crash cases run and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(ledger): implement immutable transaction record and get` — one commit per bead; footer `Refs: bd-84s`

## Lex references
- DD-LEDGER-FINANCIAL-REPRESENTATION
- DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY
- DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CONTRACTS
- FR-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-RECORDING
- PLAN-LEDGER-V1
- SP-LEDGER-02-CATALOGUE-TRANSACTIONS
- TASK-LEDGER-ACCOUNTS

- TASK-LEDGER-CORE-MONEY-DATES

title: Complete the INSIGHTS v1 module gate
description:
## Objective
A clean checkout proves INSIGHTS v1 satisfies the approved design and active PRD with no hidden scope, unverified class, partial state, private disclosure, or graph drift.

## Implements
