- Produce: CompleteInsightsV1
- Produce: Clean-checkout module evidence

## Out of Scope
- Execution completion is recorded in br after bead compilation; this planning_state never represents runtime progress.

## Success Criteria
- From a clean checkout, restore, warning-as-error build, full test suite, and linux-x64 self-contained Native-AOT publish complete with zero failures, warnings, trim issues, reflection/dynamic-code gaps, or missing native SQLite artifact.
- Every task verification class and every public operation has nonzero discovery; contract, BUDGET seam, private owner-value, recovery, security, performance, six UC, and graph scripts all exit 0 with their exact thresholds.
- lex coverage, decision paths, link suggestions, endpoint heuristics, dependency evidence, plan coverage/audit/status, context budgets, graph integrity, and sync match the approved INSIGHTS graph with no unexplained gap or drift.

- ❌ Do NOT stage private manifests, reports, databases, WAL/SHM, backups, paths, statement data, merchant text, expected rows, or financial fixtures.
- ❌ Do NOT add deferred signals, UI, HTTP, hosted services, arbitrary policies, automatic retention/cleanup, or upstream mutation while closing the module.

title: Verify retained history deletion and recovery
description:
## Objective
UC-INSIGHTS-005 produces one attributable complete maintenance outcome or leaves the prior authoritative store and surviving history unchanged.

## Implements
- None; this task is a validation or shared-contract task with no direct FR ownership.

## Depends On
- bd-1jf (TASK-INSIGHTS-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): UC-005 invokes the complete published maintenance surface.
- bd-2ls (TASK-INSIGHTS-GATE-ATOMIC-RECOVERY): UC-005 requires completed retained and recovery fault evidence.

## In Scope
- Acceptance task for UC-INSIGHTS-005; exercise metadata history, RESTRICT deletion, complete Backup, independent Verify, separate-target Restore, atomic activation, and Status.
- Produce: VerifiedInsightsUc005
- Produce: UC-005 maintenance and recovery evidence

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- List and Status show bounded metadata, exact counts, integrity, supported policies, and durable successful Backup/Restore verification without payloads or private paths.
- Delete removes only an explicit unreferenced leaf payload and outgoing link with one metadata-only event; referenced targets conflict, leaves-to-root deletion preserves surviving predecessors, and no automatic cleanup occurs.
- Backup captures and independently verifies every durable object and prior terminal outcome with owner-only permissions; standalone Verify changes neither active state nor Status.
- Restore verifies the selected source, builds and migrates a separate candidate, atomically activates exact point-in-time state, can reinstate a pre-delete report, and keeps the prior generation until success.
- Every corrupt, omitted, incompatible, unsafe, no-space, cancellation, migration, expected-current, pointer, and crash path leaves the prior authoritative store and surviving history unchanged and returns one stable failure.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT cascade deletion, alter existing backups, use an unverified copy, persist standalone Verify, restore over live state, skip expected-current, or accept mixed old/new generations.
- ❌ Do NOT expose report payloads, amounts, merchant text, or artifact paths in maintenance results, Status, diagnostics, or evidence.

## Approach
Acceptance task for UC-INSIGHTS-005; exercise metadata history, RESTRICT deletion, complete Backup, independent Verify, separate-target Restore, atomic activation, and Status.
- Constraint: Use real owner-only files and real temporary SQLite; do not mock authority-switch behavior.
- Expected output: VerifiedInsightsUc005
- Expected output: UC-005 maintenance and recovery evidence

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CompleteInsightsPublicContract (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — List, Status, Delete, Backup, Verify, Restore, and Get
- Consumes: InsightsAtomicRecoveryEvidence (NFR-INSIGHTS-VERIFIED-RECOVERABILITY) — fault and restart proof
- Produces: VerifiedInsightsUc005 (UC-INSIGHTS-005) — maintenance/recovery workflow proof

title: BackupRecoveryWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove recovery reproduces every current and historical financial meaning before activation.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-BACKUP-VERIFICATION: Create and verify Ledger backups
  - AC: Given a healthy Ledger, when the owner creates a backup, then one version-identifiable consistent artifact containing the complete Durable Ledger State is produced with owner-only host permissions and the live Ledger is unchanged.
  - AC: Given a backup artifact, when it is verified independently, then compatibility, integrity, storage metadata, accounts, categories, Spend Pools, transactions and correction chains, category and pool history, Payment Instrument and Cardholder Attribution, Evidence Records and links, reconciliation scopes, decisions and exceptions, financial relationships, idempotency outcomes, per-type counts, cardinality rules, and exact pool/category totals all validate before success.
  - AC: Given a corrupt, incomplete, unsupported, or permission-inaccessible artifact, when verification is attempted, then a stable failure is returned and neither the artifact nor live Ledger is mutated.
  - AC: Given backup or verification output and diagnostics, when inspected, then no credential, raw evidence or provider payload, unmasked account or instrument identifier, or transaction description is emitted.
- FR-LEDGER-SAFE-RESTORE: Restore the Ledger safely
  - AC: Given a compatible independently verified backup and a separate empty target, when restore is requested, then the candidate is restored and validated without mutating the current authoritative Ledger.
  - AC: Given a restored candidate, when validation completes, then every Durable Ledger State type, evidence and reconciliation link, category and pool history, attribution, relationship, idempotency outcome, per-type count, cardinality rule, and exact pool/category derived total matches the verified backup report.
  - AC: Given any compatibility, integrity, permission, space, or validation failure, when restore ends, then the current Ledger remains authoritative and no partial replacement is activated.
  - AC: Given a validated candidate and explicit replacement authorization, when activation succeeds, then replacement is atomic and the prior Ledger remains available as the documented recovery point.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace complete hierarchy- and statement-aware backup/restore through the Release CLI.
- Produce: UC007BackupRecoveryWorkflowTests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT accept file-copy-only, PRAGMA-only, count-only, or current-state-only recovery proof.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- Create/verify/prepare/activate through the published CLI reproduces facts, category hierarchy/parent history, dimensions, evidence, exact and corrected statement decisions, carry-forward, relationship replacement, coverage, idempotency, and exact direct/subtree/pool totals.
- Checksum, permission, schema, cyclic hierarchy, incomplete correction, invalid relationship, count, exact-total, stale activation, and injected crash failures leave the prior generation current.
- QuerySnapshot is excluded and diagnostics contain no raw email/statement/provider payload or full identifiers.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT accept file-copy-only, PRAGMA-only, count-only, or current-state-only recovery proof.

## Approach
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace complete hierarchy- and statement-aware backup/restore through the Release CLI.
- Constraint: Invoke the Release published tally executable; inspect stdout, stderr, exit code, and real temporary SQLite state.
- Expected output: UC007BackupRecoveryWorkflowTests

## Interfaces

- Consumes: CompletePublicContract (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — Exactly 72 provider-neutral operations
- Produces: VerifiedUC007 (UC-LEDGER-007) — complete-state backup and restore workflow

## Files (reservation globs)

## Verification

- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.EndToEnd.UC007BackupRecoveryWorkflowTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 11 main/failure-path scenarios run and 0 fail

title: EvidenceLinkWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove UC-LEDGER-013 through the published CLI without retaining provider payloads or mutating reconciliation.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-REGISTRATION: Register and link generic evidence
  - AC: Given a supported generic Evidence Kind, logical evidence identity, Opaque External Reference, privacy-safe metadata or fingerprint, and Idempotency Identity, when evidence is registered, then one stable Evidence Record is returned without storing raw provider, mailbox, MIME, messaging, document, or delivery payloads.
  - AC: Given a registered Evidence Record, an active Canonical Transaction, actor context, and reason, when the evidence is linked, then one attributable active link is appended and the transaction may retain multiple independently queryable Evidence Records.
  - AC: Given the same logical evidence registration or link is replayed with semantically identical input, when it is submitted again, then the original result is returned and no second Evidence Record or link is created.
  - AC: Given an existing logical evidence identity or active confirmation link, when conflicting metadata is supplied or a direct link to a second Canonical Transaction is requested, then the Ledger returns a stable conflict and preserves the existing state.
  - AC: Given newly registered or linked evidence with no valid Reconciliation Decision, when current state is queried, then evidence presence alone does not mark the transaction statement-reconciled or owner-confirmed.
  - AC: Given statement-row evidence with no valid Reconciliation Decision, when a caller requests a confirming transaction link directly, then the Ledger rejects the link and preserves the evidence as unconfirmed.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- null
- Produce: UC013EvidenceLinkWorkflowTests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT bypass the published CLI except read-only inspection or omit cross-key replay and unchanged-reconciliation assertions.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- The published CLI links multiple generic evidence records to one active transaction, returns privacy-safe history, leaves reconciliation and actuals unchanged, and persists neither source payloads nor full identifiers.
- Invalid identity, payload, transaction, conflict, replay, and injected-failure paths return specified errors with no partial or duplicate effect.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT bypass the published CLI except read-only inspection or omit cross-key replay and unchanged-reconciliation assertions.

## Approach
- null
- Expected output: UC013EvidenceLinkWorkflowTests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: PublishedTallyFixture
- Consumes: CompletePublicContract (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR)
- Produces: VerifiedUC013 (UC-LEDGER-013)

## Files (reservation globs)

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.EndToEnd.UC013EvidenceLinkWorkflowTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 11 main/failure-path scenarios run and 0 fail

title: Implement supporting evidence linkage
description:
## Objective
Append or replay a supporting evidence link to an active transaction without changing Reconciliation State or creating a duplicate fact.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-REGISTRATION: Register and link generic evidence
  - AC: Given a supported generic Evidence Kind, logical evidence identity, Opaque External Reference, privacy-safe metadata or fingerprint, and Idempotency Identity, when evidence is registered, then one stable Evidence Record is returned without storing raw provider, mailbox, MIME, messaging, document, or delivery payloads.
  - AC: Given a registered Evidence Record, an active Canonical Transaction, actor context, and reason, when the evidence is linked, then one attributable active link is appended and the transaction may retain multiple independently queryable Evidence Records.
  - AC: Given the same logical evidence registration or link is replayed with semantically identical input, when it is submitted again, then the original result is returned and no second Evidence Record or link is created.
  - AC: Given an existing logical evidence identity or active confirmation link, when conflicting metadata is supplied or a direct link to a second Canonical Transaction is requested, then the Ledger returns a stable conflict and preserves the existing state.
  - AC: Given newly registered or linked evidence with no valid Reconciliation Decision, when current state is queried, then evidence presence alone does not mark the transaction statement-reconciled or owner-confirmed.
  - AC: Given statement-row evidence with no valid Reconciliation Decision, when a caller requests a confirming transaction link directly, then the Ledger rejects the link and preserves the evidence as unconfirmed.

## Depends On
- bd-3qf (TASK-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-REGISTRY): Linking consumes EvidenceStore and registered evidence identity.
- bd-84s (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTIONS-RECORD-GET): Linking validates canonical transaction state through TransactionStore.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Linking is an idempotent public mutation.

## In Scope
- Deliver the explicit non-confirming link operation after Canonical Transaction and evidence registry behavior exist.
- Produce: Evidence link contract, handler, operation module, and transactional linkage tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT use a supporting link as a Reconciliation Decision or allow one evidence item to be silently moved — rejected per DD-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT.
- Do NOT overwrite evidence/link history — rejected per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- Do NOT access provider payloads or private INGEST state.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-2ve (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-CATALOGUE-TRANSACTION-BUNDLE)

## Success Criteria
- link-supporting validates active transaction, evidence identity, compatibility, reason, and idempotency before appending one attributable supporting link.
- Identical link replay returns the original result; inactive/missing transaction, incompatible evidence, active link conflict, or changed replay returns a stable error and preserves prior state.
- Supporting linkage never creates confirming statement state, changes amounts/dimensions, or moves a link across supersession.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT use a supporting link as a Reconciliation Decision or allow one evidence item to be silently moved — rejected per DD-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACT.
- ❌ Do NOT overwrite evidence/link history — rejected per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- ❌ Do NOT access provider payloads or private INGEST state.

## Approach
- Deliver the explicit non-confirming link operation after Canonical Transaction and evidence registry behavior exist.
- Expected output: Evidence link contract, handler, operation module, and transactional linkage tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: EvidenceLinkOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECONCILIATION-CONTRACTS)
- Consumes: EvidenceStore (DM-LEDGER-EVIDENCE-RECORD-LINK)
- Consumes: TransactionStore (DM-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-FACT)
- Consumes: LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync (DM-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENCY-RECORD)

## Files (reservation globs)

- `src/Tally/Features/Ledger/Evidence/EvidenceLinkHandlers.cs` (create) — command — required

title: Implement safe restore prepare and activation
description:
## Objective
Recover from a Verified Ledger Backup without changing CURRENT until every durable type, history, privacy rule, replay outcome, relationship/reconciliation rule, count, and exact total is revalidated against the expected fingerprint.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-SAFE-RESTORE: Restore the Ledger safely
  - AC: Given a compatible independently verified backup and a separate empty target, when restore is requested, then the candidate is restored and validated without mutating the current authoritative Ledger.
  - AC: Given a restored candidate, when validation completes, then every Durable Ledger State type, evidence and reconciliation link, category and pool history, attribution, relationship, idempotency outcome, per-type count, cardinality rule, and exact pool/category derived total matches the verified backup report.
  - AC: Given any compatibility, integrity, permission, space, or validation failure, when restore ends, then the current Ledger remains authoritative and no partial replacement is activated.
  - AC: Given a validated candidate and explicit replacement authorization, when activation succeeds, then replacement is atomic and the prior Ledger remains available as the documented recovery point.

## Depends On
- bd-23m (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-STORAGE): Consumer requires StoreGenerationManager from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.
- bd-3ja (TASK-LEDGER-DURABLE-STATE-VERIFIER): Consumes DurableLedgerVerifier.

## In Scope
- Deliver separate-target candidate restore, complete-state validation, explicit authorized atomic activation, and retained prior generation.
- Produce: Restore contracts/service/module
- Produce: Concrete AuthoritativeStoreActivator
- Produce: Activation crash/fingerprint tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT restore over the live database, use two database renames, activate an unverified/stale/unauthorized candidate, or delete prior recovery generation — rejected per DD-LEDGER-CANDIDATE-ACTIVATION.
- Do NOT trust prepare-time validation without activate-time revalidation.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-5gf (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-RECOVERY-SKILL-BUNDLE)
  - bd-112 (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-SECURITY)
  - bd-4zp (TASK-LEDGER-STORAGE-EVOLUTION)

## Success Criteria
- restore.prepare restores a compatible verified backup into a distinct private generation and returns candidate/source normalized fingerprints plus complete verification without changing CURRENT.
- Candidate validation matches every durable type/history, evidence privacy rule, reconciliation/relationship invariant, request/logical replay outcome, count, and exact named/dimensional total from the verified backup.
- Any compatibility, integrity, permission, space, privacy, or complete-state failure leaves the current generation authoritative and exposes no partial candidate as current.
- restore.activate requires candidateId, expectedCurrentFingerprint, authorizeReplacement=true; under exclusive lock it revalidates, flushes, atomically replaces CURRENT, fsyncs the directory, and retains the previous generation.
- Crash before pointer replacement leaves old current; after successful replacement the candidate is current with original request and logical replay outcomes recoverable on retry.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT restore over the live database, use two database renames, activate an unverified/stale/unauthorized candidate, or delete prior recovery generation — rejected per DD-LEDGER-CANDIDATE-ACTIVATION.
- ❌ Do NOT trust prepare-time validation without activate-time revalidation.

## Approach
- Deliver separate-target candidate restore, complete-state validation, explicit authorized atomic activation, and retained prior generation.
- Expected output: Restore contracts/service/module
- Expected output: Concrete AuthoritativeStoreActivator
- Expected output: Activation crash/fingerprint tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: RestoreOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-RECOVERY-STORAGE-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: RestoreService (DM-LEDGER-RECOVERY-STORAGE-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: AuthoritativeStoreActivator (DD-LEDGER-CANDIDATE-ACTIVATION)
- Consumes: IAuthoritativeStoreActivator (DD-LEDGER-CANDIDATE-ACTIVATION)
- Consumes: DurableLedgerVerifier (DM-LEDGER-STORE-GENERATION)
- Consumes: StoreGenerationManager (DM-LEDGER-STORE-GENERATION)

title: ExternalOrchestratorContractWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove Hermes, another orchestrator, or a direct caller can discover and invoke all capabilities from schemas alone, with stable replay and no transport coupling or mandatory skill.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY: Discover the Ledger command contract
  - AC: Given the self-contained executable, when a caller requests top-level or Ledger help non-interactively, then available provider-neutral public operations, required inputs, output modes, compatibility metadata, and examples are returned without starting a daemon or mutation.
  - AC: Given any public Ledger operation, when its machine schema is requested, then a versioned schema describes request fields, result shape, every expected module-scoped error and exit code, and its idempotency requirements; if its descriptor declares ambiguity or review states, the schema enumerates every such closed outcome.
  - AC: Given a schema request is repeated for the same contract version, when results are compared, then their semantic content is deterministic.
  - AC: Given an operation or field outside the public contract, including provider transport, mailbox, MIME, recipient, schedule, delivery, or acknowledgement concerns, when it is requested, then a stable unknown-operation or validation error is returned with no internal storage detail and no mutation.
  - AC: Given Hermes or another External Orchestrator uses the published schema, when it invokes Ledger, then no provider-specific adapter, private database access, or bundled agent skill is required.
- FR-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-WRITES: Make public writes idempotent
  - AC: Given any previously successful public mutation, when the same Idempotency Identity, operation, contract version, and semantically identical request are submitted again, then the original structured result is returned and no second domain, decision, link, or artifact effect occurs.
  - AC: Given a previously successful Idempotency Identity, when it is reused for another operation, contract version, or materially different request, then the same module-scoped conflict code is returned on every retry and the original effect is preserved.
  - AC: Given an invalid mutation request, when validation fails, then no domain or artifact effect occurs and the supplied Idempotency Identity remains available for a corrected request.
  - AC: Given evidence registration or linking, reconciliation application or correction, payment attribution, Pool Assignment, or pool maintenance, when its logical identity is replayed through another request identity, then the domain uniqueness rule prevents a duplicate effect independently of request replay.
  - AC: Given any public mutation without a valid caller-visible Idempotency Identity, when invocation is attempted, then the Ledger rejects it before mutation with a stable validation error.
- FR-LEDGER-SKILL-COMPATIBILITY: Expose version-matched integration guidance
  - AC: Given only the executable and its discovered schemas, when Hermes or another External Orchestrator integrates with Ledger, then every public workflow is usable without installing an agent-specific skill or accessing private storage.
  - AC: Given a supported optional agent host, when Ledger integration guidance or a skill is installed, then its instructions and declared contract version are available without copying private database files, implementation APIs, or provider-specific transport semantics.
  - AC: Given guidance or a skill and executable with compatible declared contract versions, when a Ledger workflow is invoked, then only discoverable provider-neutral public operations and schemas are used.
  - AC: Given an incompatible declared contract version, when execution is attempted, then compatibility fails before any Ledger mutation and returns a stable module-scoped error.
  - AC: Given a breaking public contract change, when a release is produced, then the contract version changes and all bundled guidance or skills declare compatibility with that version.
- FR-LEDGER-STRUCTURED-INVOCATION: Invoke Ledger operations non-interactively
  - AC: Given a structured public operation, when valid JSON input is supplied through the documented non-interactive input path, then exactly one structured result is written to stdout and the process exits with the documented success code.
  - AC: Given an expected validation, conflict, lifecycle, compatibility, cursor, not-found, ambiguous-match, stale-projection, or review-required failure, when the operation completes, then one machine-readable module-scoped error is emitted through the documented structured channel, safe diagnostics remain on stderr, and the documented non-zero exit is stable.
  - AC: Given malformed JSON, unknown fields forbidden by the schema, or provider transport, mailbox, MIME, recipient, schedule, delivery, or acknowledgement fields, when invocation is attempted, then validation fails before any mutation.
  - AC: Given a successful or expected-failure invocation, when stdout and stderr are inspected, then stderr contains no transaction description, raw evidence, full account or instrument identifier, credential, or serialized request or response body.
  - AC: Given output is lost after a committed mutation, when the External Orchestrator retries the same Idempotency Identity and semantic input, then the original structured outcome is recoverable without duplicate effects.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace revised UC-LEDGER-006 through the Release published provider-neutral CLI.
- Produce: UC006ExternalOrchestratorContractWorkflowTests with main and failure paths

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT instantiate Hermes or any provider adapter inside Tally tests.
- Do NOT treat optional guidance as an operation authority or test prerequisite.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- The Release binary discovers version, help, compatibility, and exactly 73 provider-neutral operation schemas deterministically without store access or installed guidance.
- Valid structured input produces one stdout result and documented exit; domain/review/incompatibility errors are structured and safe; lost output is recovered by identical retry while changed replay conflicts.
- Mailbox, MIME, message, recipient, schedule, delivery, acknowledgement, WhatsApp, AgentMail, raw email/statement payload, and private database fields are absent and rejected before mutation.
- Optional guidance matches the registry but removing it leaves category.reparent, statement correction, and every other public workflow discoverable and invocable.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT instantiate Hermes or any provider adapter inside Tally tests.
- ❌ Do NOT treat optional guidance as an operation authority or test prerequisite.

## Approach
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace revised UC-LEDGER-006 through the Release published provider-neutral CLI.
- Constraint: Invoke the Release published tally executable; inspect stdout, stderr, exit code, and real temporary SQLite state.
- Expected output: UC006ExternalOrchestratorContractWorkflowTests with main and failure paths

## Interfaces

- Consumes: CompletePublicContract (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — Exactly 73 provider-neutral operations
- Produces: VerifiedUC006 (UC-LEDGER-006) — External-orchestrator contract workflow

title: Prove active report and retention value privately
description:
## Objective
Private aggregate evidence proves exact metrics, Linear Pace, explanations, and optional retention improve at least one named owner decision without false high severity or disclosure, or downstream retained scope stops.

## Implements
- None; this task is a validation or shared-contract task with no direct FR ownership.

## Depends On
- bd-1cr (TASK-INSIGHTS-GENERATE-REPORT): The kill gate evaluates the real active report path.
- bd-1g7 (TASK-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-POLICY-VALIDATION): The kill gate consumes the real manifest reader, harness, canaries, and receipt.

## In Scope
- Validation-only monitored kill gate with no Implements ownership; run the real Generate and policy-validation paths over representative owner-only evidence before any INSIGHTS persistence task begins.
- Produce: VerifiedInsightsOwnerValueGate
- Produce: Aggregate-only private evidence report

## Out of Scope
- This gate intentionally remains loose. A failing outcome stops retained INSIGHTS scope and routes to the do-less design; it is not an implementation defect to patch around.

## Success Criteria
- An owner-only manifest supplied through TALLY_INSIGHTS_PRIVATE_EVALUATION_MANIFEST contains every required evidence shape, time-separated cases, disclosure canaries, one named owner decision, a raw BudgetPosition comparison, and explicit owner judgments for report usefulness, explanation sufficiency, and retention privacy cost.
- The real Generate path and InsightPolicyValidationHarness account for every case and emit a privacy-safe receipt with zero false high-severity outcomes and all canaries contained.
- The owner accepts at least one active exact-metric plus Linear-Pace report shape as materially improving the named decision over raw BudgetPosition inspection, accepts deterministic explanations for v1, and accepts optional immutable retention as worth its privacy cost.
- The tracked verification report contains only counts, fingerprints, threshold outcomes, policy/schema/tool versions, environment identity, timestamp, and owner acceptance booleans; no private path or financial payload appears.
- A failed or unevidenced usefulness, explanation, retention, privacy, or canary criterion exits nonzero and blocks every task in SP-INSIGHTS-02-RETAINED-HISTORY and SP-INSIGHTS-03-RECOVERY; the do-less path is reported rather than weakened.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT continue to retained-state implementation when the monitored owner-value criterion fails or remains unevidenced.
- ❌ Do NOT cherry-pick cases, weaken canaries, infer owner acceptance, fabricate representative data, or replace the real compiler with a second evaluator.
- ❌ Do NOT commit the private manifest, source names, paths, values, merchant text, expected rows, report bodies, or raw BudgetPosition comparison.
- ❌ Do NOT activate deferred Forecast, Trend, Anomaly, or Attention Item scope to manufacture value.

## Approach
Validation-only monitored kill gate with no Implements ownership; run the real Generate and policy-validation paths over representative owner-only evidence before any INSIGHTS persistence task begins.
- Constraint: Execution requires TALLY_INSIGHTS_PRIVATE_EVALUATION_MANIFEST to name a 0600 owner file outside tracked paths.
- Expected output: VerifiedInsightsOwnerValueGate
- Expected output: Aggregate-only private evidence report

## Interfaces
- Consumes: GenerateInsightReportQuery.HandleAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-INSIGHT-REPORT) — real active report path
- Consumes: InsightPolicyValidationHarness.ValidateAsync (DM-INSIGHTS-POLICY-VALIDATION-RECEIPT) — real policy gate
- Produces: VerifiedInsightsOwnerValueGate (DM-INSIGHTS-POLICY-VALIDATION-RECEIPT) — privacy-safe approval or fail-closed do-less outcome

## Files (reservation globs)
- `.gitignore` (modify) — exclude local INSIGHTS evaluation artifacts
- `scripts/verify-insights-private-evidence.sh` (create) — fail-closed owner-value and disclosure runner
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/PrivateEvidence/PrivateInsightValueGateTests.cs` (test) — representativeness, usefulness, explanation, retention, and canary gate
- `docs/verification/insights-private-evidence.md` (document) — aggregate-only evidence report

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `bash scripts/verify-insights-private-evidence.sh` — exit 0; the runner discovers at least 16 permission, required-shape, time-separation, raw-position comparison, named-decision, report-usefulness, explanation-acceptance, retention-cost, zero-false-high-severity, canary, aggregate-only-output, no-Git, and locality checks and writes one payload-free evidence report

## Commit
- `test(insights): prove active report and retention value privately` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-gsb`

## Lex references
- DD-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-POLICY-VALIDATION
- DM-INSIGHTS-POLICY-VALIDATION-RECEIPT
- EXT-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-EVALUATION-CORPUS
- NFR-INSIGHTS-LOCAL-DATA-PROTECTION
- OQ-INSIGHTS-10
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-01-ANALYTICAL-CORE
- TASK-INSIGHTS-GATE-EVIDENCE-OWNER-VALUE
- TC-INSIGHTS-PRIVATE-POLICY-VALIDATION

title: Verify replay and overlap safety workflow
description:
## Objective
Prove repeated or overlapping statements never create an unjustified duplicate suppression or second canonical transaction.

## Implements
- No direct FR ownership: this is an evidence, integration, security, use-case, or completion gate.
- Proves or constrains TC-INGEST-REPLAY-OVERLAP-SAFETY-CONTRACT: Verify replay and overlap safety (verifies).
- Proves or constrains UC-INGEST-004: Re-import a repeated or overlapping statement safely (covers).

## Depends On
- bd-2jn (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): UC verification exercises the complete published preview/commit/status contract.
- bd-1j2 (TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION): Consumes PrivateStatementFixtureSet.

## In Scope
- Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-004 Exact Replay, distinct tuple, identity conflict, and fail-closed overlap behavior.
- Produce: UCIngest004Evidence.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- The same Source Fingerprint, selected account, adapter version, and LEDGER contract version under any filename returns the prior active or completed batch or receipt before reparsing and creates no new Ledger effect; changing any key component requires a new preview.
- A familiar filename with changed bytes is a new preview, never Exact Replay.
- Identical date-description-amount rows at distinct source positions remain separate candidates; tuple equality never collapses them.
- Only variant-proven exact identity with equal immutable facts may be Exact Duplicate; unavailable/incompatible provenance never claims automatic deduplication.
- An exact identity reused with changed immutable facts returns stable conflict and preserves the original transaction.
- A different source with an overlapping account/date window and no cross-file identity returns blocking Overlap Conflict and remains uncommitted.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT alter private fixtures or tracked expectations to manufacture identity evidence.
- ❌ Do NOT inspect internal hashes/stores; use public preview/status/receipt and Ledger observations.

## Approach
Black-box use-case gate for UC-INGEST-004 Exact Replay, distinct tuple, identity conflict, and fail-closed overlap behavior.
- Constraint: OQ-INGEST-4 remains fail-closed: no v1 test may infer cross-file identity from similarity.

## Interfaces
- Consumes: CompleteIngestPublicContract (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Published INGEST and shared Ledger operations
- Consumes: PrivateStatementFixtureSet (EXT-INGEST-OWNER-BANK-STATEMENT) — External private fixture injection
- Produces: UCIngest004Evidence (UC-INGEST-004) — Replay and overlap workflow proof

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/E2E/ReplayOverlapWorkflowTests.cs` (test) — UC-INGEST-004 black-box gate — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~ReplayOverlapWorkflowTests` -> exit 0; at least 10 renamed-replay, changed-content, duplicate-tuple, exact-identity, incompatible-provenance, changed-facts, and overlap cases are discovered and 0 tests fail with unchanged canonical counts on blocked/replay paths

## Commit

## Lex references
- PLAN-INGEST-V1
- SP-INGEST-04-VERIFICATION
- TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT
- TASK-INGEST-PDF-EXTRACTION
- TASK-INGEST-VERIFY-UC-004
- TC-INGEST-REPLAY-OVERLAP-SAFETY-CONTRACT

title: Calculate exact Analysis Window and category metrics
description:
## Objective
ExactMetricCalculator accounts for every member exactly once and returns reconciled signed ZAR metrics for each requested window.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-EXACT-WINDOW-METRICS: Calculate exact Analysis Window metrics
  - AC: Given a complete coherent evidence set, when exact metrics are calculated, then one total actual metric is returned for each requested day, week, and month-to-date window.
  - AC: Given a requested category breakdown, when exact metrics are calculated, then every stable category present in plan or actual evidence is accounted for in deterministic category-identity order.
  - AC: Given plan entries with zero amount, actual categories omitted from the plan, or uncategorized actuals, when metrics are returned, then Zero Budget, Unbudgeted, and Uncategorized remain distinct explicit buckets.
  - AC: Given refunds or reversals that make a window or category net actual negative, when metrics are returned, then the signed exact value is preserved and is not clamped to zero.
  - AC: Given no matching recorded actuals in a started window, when metrics are returned, then the exact value is zero with a successful evidence state rather than Not Found.
  - AC: Given no Budget Plan, when exact actual metrics are available, then they are returned with No Budget Plan on every plan-relative field.
  - AC: Given exact category and bucket values, when totals are returned, then total actual equals the checked exact sum of Budgeted, Zero Budget, Unbudgeted, and Uncategorized values.
  - AC: Given any Int64 overflow, undefined exact operation, or reconciliation failure, when metrics are calculated, then the entire report fails with no partial financial output.

## Depends On
- bd-1qz (TASK-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-MODELS): The calculator consumes evidence and produces exact analytical contracts.

## In Scope
- Make one deterministic checked pass over complete dated members for total, category, and bucket metrics.
- Produce: ExactMetricCalculator
- Produce: Exact arithmetic property matrix

## Out of Scope
- No sibling handoff beyond the explicit Depends On and Failure Criteria contracts.

## Success Criteria
- Each requested started day, week, and month-to-date window returns one exact total and, when requested, categories in stable identity order.
- Budgeted, ZeroBudget, Unbudgeted, and Uncategorized are distinct; NoBudgetPlan preserves exact actuals with NoBudgetPlan on plan-relative fields.
- Signed refunds and reversals remain negative, an empty matching set returns exact zero, and total actual equals the checked exact sum of all buckets.
- Every member contributes exactly once per applicable metric; duplicate accounting, overflow, undefined exact operation, or reconciliation mismatch aborts the whole report.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT use floating point, clamp refunds to zero, suppress zero results, merge Uncategorized with Unbudgeted, or invent a plan.
- ❌ Do NOT expose per-metric public services or move orchestration to callers — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-DETERMINISTIC-REPORT-COMPILER.
- ❌ Do NOT emit partial metric collections after arithmetic or reconciliation failure.

## Approach
Make one deterministic checked pass over complete dated members for total, category, and bucket metrics.
- Constraint: Use checked Int64 minor units and deterministic catalogue/category order.
- Expected output: ExactMetricCalculator
- Expected output: Exact arithmetic property matrix

## Interfaces
- Consumes: BoundInsightEvidence (DM-INSIGHTS-COHERENT-EVIDENCE) — complete validated dated members
- Produces: ExactMetricCalculator.Calculate (DM-INSIGHTS-ANALYTICAL-EVIDENCE) — ordered exact MetricResult collection

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Domain/Insights/Metrics/ExactMetricCalculator.cs` (create) — single-pass exact metric engine
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Metrics/ExactMetricCalculatorTests.cs` (test) — bucket, refund, zero, overflow, and reconciliation properties

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~ExactMetricCalculatorTests` — exit 0; at least 24 total, category, four-bucket, no-plan, refund, zero, stable-order, duplicate-accounting, Int64-boundary, overflow, and reconciliation cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(insights): calculate exact analysis window and category metrics` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-mbq`

## Lex references
- DD-INSIGHTS-DETERMINISTIC-REPORT-COMPILER
- DD-INSIGHTS-TRUSTED-WINDOW-PACE-POLICY
- DM-INSIGHTS-ANALYTICAL-EVIDENCE
- FR-INSIGHTS-EXACT-WINDOW-METRICS
- NFR-INSIGHTS-DETERMINISTIC-ANALYTICAL-INTEGRITY
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-01-ANALYTICAL-CORE
- TASK-INSIGHTS-EXACT-METRICS
- TC-INSIGHTS-EXACT-METRICS-CONFORMANCE
- Deep context: `lex context bd-mbq --json`

title: AccountWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove UC-LEDGER-001 through observable process results and durable-state invariants.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-ACCOUNT-MAINTENANCE: Maintain Owned Bank Accounts
  - AC: Given a valid unique institution name, owner label, supported asset or liability account type, ZAR currency, display label, and masked distinguishing identifier, when the owner creates an account, then the Ledger returns one active account with a stable identifier and stores no online-banking credential or full account number.
  - AC: Given an existing account, when the owner renames its display label, then its stable identifier and previously recorded transaction references remain unchanged.
  - AC: Given an account referenced by transactions, when the owner archives it, then its history remains queryable and the Ledger rejects new transactions against it with a stable validation error.
  - AC: Given active and archived accounts, when the owner lists accounts with an explicit lifecycle-state filter, then each matching account is returned once with its stable identifier and masked identity fields.
  - AC: Given an owned account, Payment Instruments, cardholders, Spend Pools, and categories, when account maintenance occurs, then no account value silently creates, selects, or changes another dimension.
- FR-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-WRITES: Make public writes idempotent
  - AC: Given any previously successful public mutation, when the same Idempotency Identity, operation, contract version, and semantically identical request are submitted again, then the original structured result is returned and no second domain, decision, link, or artifact effect occurs.
  - AC: Given a previously successful Idempotency Identity, when it is reused for another operation, contract version, or materially different request, then the same module-scoped conflict code is returned on every retry and the original effect is preserved.
  - AC: Given an invalid mutation request, when validation fails, then no domain or artifact effect occurs and the supplied Idempotency Identity remains available for a corrected request.
  - AC: Given evidence registration or linking, reconciliation application or correction, payment attribution, Pool Assignment, or pool maintenance, when its logical identity is replayed through another request identity, then the domain uniqueness rule prevents a duplicate effect independently of request replay.
  - AC: Given any public mutation without a valid caller-visible Idempotency Identity, when invocation is attempted, then the Ledger rejects it before mutation with a stable validation error.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task intentionally has no Implements link: trace UC-LEDGER-001 main scenario and every failure path through the published CLI.
- Produce: UC001AccountWorkflowTests with main/failure paths

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT weaken credential/full-identifier rejection or substitute hard delete for archive.
- Do NOT omit any UC-LEDGER-001 failure path.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- The agent discovers account schema, creates one masked account, renames/lists/archives it, and stable ID/history remain while archived record rejects new transactions.
- Missing fields, duplicate masked identity, credential-like/full identifier input, and physical-delete intent return stable errors with no mutation.
- Identical mutation retries reproduce original results and changed retries conflict.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT weaken credential/full-identifier rejection or substitute hard delete for archive.
- ❌ Do NOT omit any UC-LEDGER-001 failure path.

## Approach
- Verification-only task intentionally has no Implements link: trace UC-LEDGER-001 main scenario and every failure path through the published CLI.
- Constraint: Invoke the Release published tally executable; inspect stdout, stderr, exit code, and real temporary SQLite state.
- Expected output: UC001AccountWorkflowTests with main/failure paths

## Interfaces
- Consumes: PublishedTallyFixture (Release published-process E2E fixture)
- Consumes: CompletePublicContract (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — Exactly 72 provider-neutral operations
- Produces: VerifiedUC001 (UC-LEDGER-001) — workflow verification

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/EndToEnd/UC001AccountWorkflowTests.cs` (test) — verification — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.EndToEnd.UC001AccountWorkflowTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 7 main/failure-path scenarios run and 0 fail

## Commit

title: Validate Category Assignment policy
description:
## Objective
Lock hierarchical Spend Categories with zero or one active Category Assignment per transaction.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On
- None — this is a compile root.

## In Scope
- Validation-only task with no Implements link: verify the direct owner decision recorded for OQ-LEDGER-6 and DD-LEDGER-CATEGORY-HIERARCHY before category implementation.
- Produce: Resolved hierarchy policy evidence and OQ-LEDGER-6Resolution

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT implement a flat catalogue, split category allocations, or hierarchy encoded in names — rejected by DD-LEDGER-CATEGORY-HIERARCHY.
- Do NOT decide BUDGET parent/descendant overlap semantics in LEDGER.
- Do NOT store raw statements, email bodies, full identifiers, or transaction descriptions in validation evidence.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-34a (TASK-LEDGER-CATEGORIES)
  - bd-2i5 (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-SCHEMA-CATALOGUE)

## Success Criteria
- OQ-LEDGER-6 is resolved from direct owner instruction, A5 is validated, and the former flat-catalogue assumption A10 is invalidated.
- DD-LEDGER-CATEGORY-HIERARCHY is accepted and specifies zero or one active parent, append-only parent history, acyclicity, sibling-name uniqueness, assignment to any active node, and direct versus subtree roll-ups.
- The redacted validation report records one category per transaction and delegates budget exact-node versus subtree semantics to BUDGET through OQ-BUDGET-10.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT implement a flat catalogue, split category allocations, or hierarchy encoded in names — rejected by DD-LEDGER-CATEGORY-HIERARCHY.
- ❌ Do NOT decide BUDGET parent/descendant overlap semantics in LEDGER.
- ❌ Do NOT store raw statements, email bodies, full identifiers, or transaction descriptions in validation evidence.

## Approach
- Validation-only task with no Implements link: verify the direct owner decision recorded for OQ-LEDGER-6 and DD-LEDGER-CATEGORY-HIERARCHY before category implementation.
- Constraint: Only privacy-safe structural conclusions are committed; graph changes use lex commands.
- Expected output: Resolved hierarchy policy evidence and OQ-LEDGER-6Resolution

## Interfaces
- Produces: OQ-LEDGER-6Resolution (OQ-LEDGER-6) — Owner-approved hierarchy and one-category policy
- Produces: CategoryHierarchyPolicy (DD-LEDGER-CATEGORY-HIERARCHY) — Acyclic adjacency history with direct and subtree roll-ups

## Files (reservation globs)

- `.lexicon/graph/LEDGER/open-question/OQ-LEDGER-6.json` (read) — canonical gate state — required — Read through lex open-question show
- `.lexicon/graph/LEDGER/decision/DD-LEDGER-CATEGORY-HIERARCHY.json` (read) — accepted design — required — Read through lex decision show

## Verification

- **Test:** `test -s docs/validation/ledger/oq-6-category-policy.md && lex open-question show OQ-LEDGER-6 --json && lex decision show DD-LEDGER-CATEGORY-HIERARCHY --json` — exit 0; the report exists, OQ output shows resolved, and decision output shows accepted

title: Define the INGEST operation and business contracts
description:
## Objective
Produce the typed Native-AOT-safe contract foundation for all eight INGEST operations without registering incomplete handlers.

## Implements
- FR-INGEST-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY: Discover the INGEST command contract
  - AC: Given a compatible Tally executable, when the client requests INGEST help or schema discovery, then it returns the versioned operations preview, inspect, approve, commit, resume, status, abandon, and cleanup with their input/result schemas, supported Format Variants, safe limits, stable errors, and exit categories without reading a source or mutating LEDGER.
  - AC: Given any INGEST operation, when a valid request completes, then exactly one structured business result is written to stdout, metadata-only diagnostics use stderr, and the documented exit category matches the result.
  - AC: Given an unknown operation, malformed input, or forbidden unknown field, when invocation is attempted, then INGEST returns a stable usage or validation error before source processing or LEDGER mutation.
  - AC: Given incompatible INGEST, adapter, or LEDGER contract versions, when compatibility is checked, then INGEST returns a stable compatibility failure before source processing or mutation.

## Depends On
- bd-2dg (TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-LEDGER-CONTRACT): The common envelope and public Ledger contract types must exist before INGEST contract records compile.

## In Scope
- Create source-generated public records, stable errors, operation IDs, manifest/receipt views, and compatibility metadata used identically by every later slice.
- Produce: Eight-operation contract set.
- Produce: Stable errors and status records.
- Produce: Manifest and receipt contracts.
- Produce: Source-generated IngestJsonContext.

## Out of Scope
- Changes outside the listed reservation globs.
- Reimplementation of named consumed interfaces; use their producing bead contracts.
- Any behavior prohibited under Failure Criteria.

## Success Criteria
- IngestOperationIds contains exactly ingest.preview, ingest.inspect, ingest.approve, ingest.commit, ingest.resume, ingest.status, ingest.abandon, and ingest.cleanup with no generic operation.
- Concrete request/result records match DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS, use closed enums for finite states, carry explicit contract versions, and expose sourcePath only inside PreviewRequest.
- IngestError exposes category, mutationPossibility none|possible|confirmed, retryAction, safe identifiers, and durable state but cannot carry source paths, rows, amounts, balances, full bank identifiers, raw requests, manifests, stack traces, or parser exceptions.
- Manifest and receipt records preserve stable identifiers and immutable facts while CompletedMetadataReceipt excludes descriptions, amounts, balances, evidence, controls, and frozen requests.
- IngestJsonContext contains source-generated metadata for every contract type, rejects unknown fields, and produces byte-identical logical JSON across repeated serialization.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT hide transitions behind a generic action discriminator — rejected per DD-INGEST-CLI-OPERATION-CONTRACT.
- ❌ Do NOT introduce HTTP DTOs, FastEndpoints, controllers, mutable CRUD DTOs, or UI models — rejected per DD-INGEST-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE.
- ❌ Do NOT expose SQLite, PdfPig, private LEDGER, filesystem, exception, or parser types in public contracts.
- ❌ Do NOT use reflection-based JSON metadata or runtime property-order hashing.

## Approach
Create source-generated public records, stable errors, operation IDs, manifest/receipt views, and compatibility metadata used identically by every later slice.
- Constraint: All complex requests use the common structured input envelope; sourcePath is never a named command-line argument.

## Interfaces
- Produces: IngestOperationIds (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: IngestOperationContracts (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: IngestError (DM-INGEST-ERROR-STATUS-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: BatchStatusSummary (DM-INGEST-ERROR-STATUS-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: ImportManifestContract (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-MANIFEST)
- Produces: ImportReceipt (DM-INGEST-IMPORT-RECEIPT)
- Produces: FrozenLedgerRecordRequest (DM-INGEST-LEDGER-COMMIT-CONTRACT)
- Produces: IngestJsonContext (DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ingest/IngestOperationContracts.cs` (create) — eight request/result records — required
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ingest/IngestErrorContracts.cs` (create) — stable errors and status records — required
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ingest/IngestManifestContracts.cs` (create) — manifest and candidate views — required
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ingest/IngestReceiptContracts.cs` (create) — receipt and frozen Ledger records — required
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Ingest/IngestJsonContext.cs` (create) — source-generated JSON metadata — required
- `src/Tally/Features/Ingest/Contract/IngestOperationIds.cs` (create) — stable operation/version constants — required
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Ingest/Contract/IngestContractModelTests.cs` (test) — contract determinism tests — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~IngestContractModelTests` -> exit 0; at least 16 schema, deterministic serialization, unknown-field, and payload-exclusion cases are discovered and 0 tests fail

## Commit
- `feat(ingest): define operation contracts` — footer `Refs: bd-twx`

## Lex references
- DD-INGEST-CLI-OPERATION-CONTRACT
- DM-INGEST-OPERATION-CONTRACTS
- FR-INGEST-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY
- PLAN-INGEST-V1
- SP-INGEST-00-FOUNDATION
- TASK-INGEST-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION
- TASK-INGEST-GATE-INT-LEDGER-CONTRACT
- TC-INGEST-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY-CONTRACT
- Deep context: `lex context bd-twx --json`

title: Publish the eleven-operation INSIGHTS contract foundation
description:
## Objective
The shared registry can describe every Public INSIGHTS Operation and policy without reading financial or retained data.

## Implements
- FR-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY: Discover the Public INSIGHTS Operation contract
  - AC: Given a compatible caller with no financial input, when operation discovery is requested, then every Public INSIGHTS Operation is returned in canonical verb order with stable operation identity and contract version.
  - AC: Given one discovered operation, when its descriptor is inspected, then request and result schema fingerprints, mutability, authority, idempotency requirement, limits, stable error types, exit categories, and compatibility range are explicit.
  - AC: Given policy discovery, when the catalogue is returned, then every released Analysis Window, formula, Evidence Sufficiency, severity, explanation, and ordering policy has stable kind, version, status, and fingerprint metadata.
  - AC: Given a formally deferred Forecast, Trend, Anomaly, or Attention Item policy, when policy discovery is requested, then it is marked unsupported and no executable formula or threshold is advertised.
  - AC: Given INSIGHTS authority metadata, when it is inspected, then no LEDGER, CLASSIFY, or BUDGET mutation operation is present.
  - AC: Given an unsupported INSIGHTS contract version, when discovery is requested, then one stable compatibility failure is returned without reading financial data.
  - AC: Given discovery, help, or schema output, when all fields are inspected, then no report payload, amount, merchant text, private path, permission seed, role template, menu seed, translation key, or frontend key model is present.
- FR-INSIGHTS-STRUCTURED-INVOCATION: Invoke Public INSIGHTS Operations non-interactively
  - AC: Given valid structured input through stdin or an approved owner-only file, when a public operation succeeds, then exactly one versioned structured business-result envelope is written to stdout and the process exits with the documented success category.
  - AC: Given an expected validation, Not Found, Deleted, conflict, unavailable, insufficient-evidence, compatibility, integrity, resource, or downstream failure, when invocation completes, then exactly one module-scoped machine-readable error or state envelope is returned with the documented exit category.
  - AC: Given malformed input or a schema-forbidden unknown field, when invocation is attempted, then validation fails before any financial read or retained-state write.
  - AC: Given an unsupported INSIGHTS, BUDGET, or LEDGER contract version, when invocation is attempted, then compatibility fails before financial processing.
  - AC: Given Retain, Restate, Delete, Backup, or Restore without required P1 actor attribution or idempotency identity, when invocation is attempted, then authority validation fails before retained-state change.
  - AC: Given any operation, when it runs, then it requires no interactive prompt, network call, daemon, watcher, scheduler, service, or background Tally process.
  - AC: Given success or expected failure, when stderr, logs, telemetry, crash output, process arguments, and shared temporary locations are inspected, then they contain metadata only and no serialized financial request, report, or response body.

## Depends On
- bd-1qz (TASK-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-MODELS): Requests, results, JSON metadata, descriptors, and mapper reference the analytical and lifecycle contract records.
- bd-21u (TASK-INSIGHTS-LIFECYCLE-CONTRACT-MODELS): The eleven public operations include retained-history recovery status and private-receipt contract types.

## In Scope
- Create versioned requests, results, stable errors, source-generated JSON metadata, eleven descriptors, and the pure contract mapper over the shared Tally operation abstractions.
- Produce: InsightsOperationModule
- Produce: InsightsJsonContext
- Produce: InsightsContractMapper
- Produce: Operation contract tests

## Out of Scope
- Public-contract gate owns registration and Program wiring.

## Success Criteria
- InsightsOperationModule contains exactly insights.report.generate, insights.report.explain, insights.report.get, insights.report.list, insights.report.retain, insights.report.restate, insights.report.delete, insights.status.get, insights.backup.create, insights.backup.verify, and insights.restore.activate in canonical verb order; Discover remains shared system.schema.list/show.
- Every descriptor publishes deterministic request/result fingerprints, compatibility range, authority, mutability, idempotency requirement, released limits, stable errors, and exit categories without reading a store or producer.
- Retain, Restate, Delete, Backup, and Restore require actor attribution and idempotency; Generate, Explain, Get, List, Status, and Verify are read-only; unsupported versions and unknown fields fail before financial reads.
- Complex input is accepted only through stdin or an approved owner-only file boundary, exactly one business envelope uses stdout, and diagnostics are metadata-only.
- The released policy catalogue marks Forecast, Trend, Anomaly, and Attention Item as Deferred and exposes no executable formula, threshold, severity, or mutation operation for them.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT replace the eleven operations with a generic action-discriminator facade or arbitrary insights.execute — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-CLI-OPERATION-CONTRACT.
- ❌ Do NOT add HTTP or FastEndpoints routes — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-CLI-OPERATION-CONTRACT.
- ❌ Do NOT add repository, store, compiler, calculator, clock, mapper, mediator, event, or transport interfaces — rejected per DD-INSIGHTS-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE.
- ❌ Do NOT use reflection scanning, runtime-generated schemas, interactive prompts, financial process arguments, or serialized request/response logging.
- ❌ Do NOT create Save, Edit, Refresh, Alert, Notify, policy CRUD, permission, role, menu, translation, or frontend-key contracts.

## Approach
Create versioned requests, results, stable errors, source-generated JSON metadata, eleven descriptors, and the pure contract mapper over the shared Tally operation abstractions.
- Constraint: Reuse OperationRegistry, OperationDescriptor, ICommandHandler, IQueryHandler, CommandResult, and process envelopes from TASK-LEDGER-CORE-PROCESS-CONTRACT.
- Expected output: InsightsOperationModule
- Expected output: InsightsJsonContext
- Expected output: InsightsContractMapper
- Expected output: Operation contract tests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: InsightReport (DM-INSIGHTS-INSIGHT-REPORT) — Generate operation result contract
- Consumes: InsightHistoryContracts (DM-INSIGHTS-RETAINED-REPORT-LIFECYCLE) — retain restate delete and history result contracts
- Consumes: InsightsRecoveryContracts (DM-INSIGHTS-RECOVERY-CONTRACTS) — backup verify restore and status result contracts
- Produces: InsightsOperationContracts (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — all eleven versioned requests and results
- Produces: InsightsOperationModule (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — canonical descriptor inventory
- Produces: InsightsJsonContext (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — Native-AOT serialization metadata
- Produces: InsightsContractMapper (DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS) — concrete pure mapper

## Files (reservation globs)
- `src/Tally/Application/OperationAbstractions.cs` (read) — shared handler and result abstractions
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Insights/Operations/InsightsRequests.cs` (create) — versioned operation requests
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Insights/Operations/InsightsResults.cs` (create) — versioned operation results
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Insights/Operations/InsightsErrors.cs` (create) — stable failures and exits
- `src/Tally/Contracts/Insights/InsightsJsonContext.cs` (create) — source-generated JSON metadata
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/Contract/InsightsOperationModule.cs` (create) — eleven operation descriptors
- `src/Tally/Features/Insights/Contract/InsightsContractMapper.cs` (create) — pure domain-to-contract mapping root
- `tests/Tally.Tests/Insights/Contract/InsightsOperationContractTests.cs` (test) — descriptor, schema, authority, and no-read tests

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~InsightsOperationContractTests` — exit 0; at least 32 exact-inventory, schema, ordering, compatibility, authority, idempotency, limit, deferred-policy, unknown-field, no-data-read, and forbidden-alias cases are discovered and 0 fail

## Commit
- `feat(insights): publish eleven-operation contract foundation` — one commit per bead; footer line `Refs: bd-uh9`

## Lex references

- DD-INSIGHTS-APPLICATION-ARCHITECTURE
- DD-INSIGHTS-CLI-OPERATION-CONTRACT
- DM-INSIGHTS-OPERATION-CONTRACTS
- FR-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY
- FR-INSIGHTS-STRUCTURED-INVOCATION
- PLAN-INSIGHTS-V1
- SP-INSIGHTS-00-FOUNDATION
- TASK-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-FOUNDATION
- TASK-LEDGER-CORE-PROCESS-CONTRACT
- TC-INSIGHTS-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY

Field: contradicts:TASK-INGEST-GATE-EVIDENCE-PRIVATE-FIXTURES

Quoted text:
Carry sourcePath only inside private structured input; open read-only; read a bounded immutable byte buffer; fingerprint the exact bytes; and never persist the path or a source copy.
Each fixture object contains a sourcePath plus expected statementPeriod, accountEvidence, orderedRecords, and reconciliation controls sufficient to assert every source row, exact available balance control, and variant identity; values remain private.

title: Implement owned-account transfer confirmation
description:
## Objective
Create one attributable active transfer only when distinct owned-account legs satisfy exact role, sign, currency, amount, account, and lifecycle rules.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-TRANSFER-CONFIRMATION: Confirm owned-account transfers
  - AC: Given two active transactions on distinct active owned accounts with ZAR equal-and-opposite economic movements, when the owner confirms them as a transfer with a reason, then one active attributable transfer relationship is created.
  - AC: Given an active confirmed transfer, when default External Spend actuals are queried, then the paired principal contributes exactly zero while both transaction legs remain queryable.
  - AC: Given qualifying legs with different supplied dates, when the owner explicitly confirms the relationship, then the date difference alone does not prevent confirmation.
  - AC: Given same-account legs, same-sign legs, unequal principal magnitudes, different currencies, inactive legs, or either leg already participating in any active transfer or refund relationship, when transfer confirmation is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and creates no relationship.
  - AC: Given a bank fee recorded separately from the equal-and-opposite principal legs, when the transfer is confirmed, then the fee remains an unlinked transaction and retains its own spend treatment.
  - AC: Given a confirmed transfer whose legs have category or Spend Pool Assignments, when pool, category, or pool-by-category actuals are queried, then principal contributes exactly zero to every bucket while attribution remains queryable.

## Depends On
- bd-84s (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTIONS-RECORD-GET): Transfer confirmation requires immutable transaction legs.
- bd-2pt (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-EVIDENCE-RELATIONSHIPS): Relationship policy cannot start until OQ-LEDGER-5 resolves.
- bd-2ne (TASK-LEDGER-CORE-IDEMPOTENCY): Consumer requires LedgerMutationExecutor.ExecuteAsync from its producing task; direct compile edge enforces the declared interface contract.
- bd-31r (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-EVIDENCE-CASH-WITHDRAWALS): Transfer invariants must not assume cash is external before OQ-LEDGER-16 resolves.

## In Scope
- Deliver explicit transfer confirmation and relationship detail over immutable transaction legs.
- Produce: Transfer contracts/policy/store/handler/module
- Produce: Real-SQLite transfer invariant tests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT infer transfers from descriptions/dates, mutate transaction facts, link embedded fees, or use a generic polymorphic relationship create — per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- Do NOT implement before OQ-LEDGER-5 resolves consistently.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3hy (TASK-LEDGER-ACTUALS-PROJECTION)
  - bd-3sd (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-RELATIONSHIP-ACTUALS-BUNDLE)
  - bd-10w (TASK-LEDGER-REFUNDS)
  - bd-2sb (TASK-LEDGER-RELATIONSHIP-CORRECTIONS)
  - bd-2rh (TASK-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-CORRECTIONS)

## Success Criteria
- Two active ZAR transactions on distinct active owned accounts with explicit outflow/inflow roles and equal-opposite principal create one attributable active transfer.
- Different supplied dates do not block a valid explicit confirmation; a separately recorded fee stays unlinked.
- Same account/sign, unequal magnitude, different currency, inactive leg, or either leg already in any active transfer/refund returns its documented stable error and creates no relationship.
- Relationship get returns roles, exact principal, status, actor/time, and optional history while source transactions remain unchanged.
- Identical replay returns the original relationship; competing role assignment creates at most one active relationship.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT infer transfers from descriptions/dates, mutate transaction facts, link embedded fees, or use a generic polymorphic relationship create — per DD-LEDGER-IMMUTABLE-HISTORY.
- ❌ Do NOT implement before OQ-LEDGER-5 resolves consistently.

## Approach
- Deliver explicit transfer confirmation and relationship detail over immutable transaction legs.
- Expected output: Transfer contracts/policy/store/handler/module
- Expected output: Real-SQLite transfer invariant tests

## Interfaces
- Produces: TransferOperationModule (DM-LEDGER-RELATIONSHIP-ACTUALS-CONTRACTS)
- Produces: TransferPolicy (DM-LEDGER-FINANCIAL-RELATIONSHIP)
- Produces: RelationshipStore (DM-LEDGER-FINANCIAL-RELATIONSHIP)

## Verification

- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.Features.Ledger.Relationships.TransferTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 14 conservation/role/date/fee/atomicity/replay cases run and 0 fail

title: ReconciliationDecisionWorkflow
description:
## Objective
Prove explicit owner decisions resolve or correct reconciliation without deleting history or duplicating actuals.

## Implements
- FR-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-DECISION-LIFECYCLE: Resolve and correct Reconciliation Decisions
  - AC: Given ambiguous statement evidence, compatible candidates, an explicit owner selection, reason, actor context, and Idempotency Identity, when the owner confirms one candidate, then one owner-confirmed decision and active evidence link are created atomically and no other candidate is changed.
  - AC: Given ambiguous or unmatched evidence and an explicit owner rejection of all candidates with a reason, when the decision is recorded, then one attributable rejected decision is appended without linking the evidence or creating a Canonical Transaction.
  - AC: Given an active Reconciliation Decision and a valid correction request, when the owner revokes it or replaces it with one compatible candidate, then the prior decision remains immutable, the active evidence link changes atomically, and the current state reflects only the new decision.
  - AC: Given an inactive or incompatible candidate, an Evidence Record already actively confirming another transaction, or a correction without explicit reason and actor context, when a decision is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation or conflict error and changes no link or decision.
  - AC: Given a transaction is voided or superseded, when it has reconciliation history, then no evidence or confirmation silently moves to the replacement and one Reconciliation Exception current outcome remains until another valid decision is recorded.

## Depends On
- bd-2jj (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT): Use-case verification invokes the fully wired published public contract.

## In Scope
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace UC-LEDGER-015 through the Release published CLI.
- Produce: UC015ReconciliationDecisionWorkflowTests

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT simulate owner intent implicitly or edit decision/link rows in test setup after the workflow begins.
- Do NOT omit stale-predecessor and one-active-confirmation assertions.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-3bx (TASK-LEDGER-GATE-MODULE)

## Success Criteria
- The published workflow confirms one compatible candidate, rejects all candidates, revokes a match, replaces it, and returns immutable history plus one active confirming target.
- Missing evidence/candidate/intent/reason/key, incompatible or inactive target, other-link conflict, identical replay, changed replay, stale predecessor, and injected atomic failure cover every UC-LEDGER-015 failure path.
- Actuals contain no duplicate canonical outcome before or after correction.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT simulate owner intent implicitly or edit decision/link rows in test setup after the workflow begins.
- ❌ Do NOT omit stale-predecessor and one-active-confirmation assertions.

## Approach
- Verification-only task with no Implements link: trace UC-LEDGER-015 through the Release published CLI.
- Expected output: UC015ReconciliationDecisionWorkflowTests

## Interfaces
- Consumes: PublishedTallyFixture
- Consumes: CompletePublicContract (DM-LEDGER-OPERATION-DESCRIPTOR) — 72 provider-neutral operations
- Produces: VerifiedUC015 (UC-LEDGER-015)

## Files (reservation globs)
- `tests/Tally.Tests/EndToEnd/UC015ReconciliationDecisionWorkflowTests.cs` (test) — published-process verification — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `dotnet test tests/Tally.Tests/Tally.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Tally.Tests.EndToEnd.UC015ReconciliationDecisionWorkflowTests --no-restore` — exit 0; at least 13 owner-decision, correction, conflict, replay, and atomicity scenarios run and 0 fail

## Commit
- `test(ledger): verify uc 015 workflow` — one commit per bead; footer `Refs: bd-xt9`

## Lex references
- FR-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-DECISION-LIFECYCLE
- PLAN-LEDGER-V1
- SP-LEDGER-05-VERIFICATION
- TASK-LEDGER-GATE-INT-PUBLIC-CONTRACT
- TASK-LEDGER-VERIFY-UC-015
- TC-LEDGER-RECONCILIATION-DECISION-LIFECYCLE-CONTRACT
- UC-LEDGER-015

title: Validate Spend Pool cardinality
description:
## Objective
Prove that explicit unassigned or one active Spend Pool per transaction covers the owner finance cases, or stop for design revision.

## Implements
- None — this task is a foundation, evidence, or integration contract with no direct FR implementation.

## Depends On
- None — this is a compile root.

## In Scope
- Validation-only task with no Implements link: resolve OQ-LEDGER-15 before Pool Assignment and pool-aware actuals implementation.
- Produce: docs/validation/ledger/oq-15-pool-cardinality.md with redacted scenario matrix
- Produce: OQ-LEDGER-15 resolved with a non-empty cardinality decision on the only completing path

## Out of Scope
- Do NOT encode pools as categories, infer them from another dimension, or silently split a transaction — rejected per DD-LEDGER-DIMENSIONAL-ATTRIBUTION.
- Do NOT commit raw transaction rows, descriptions, full identifiers, or owner-sensitive mapping data.
- Do NOT resolve the gate by excluding mixed purchases; a demonstrated split-pool need invalidates the current design.
- Downstream integration remains owned by:
  - bd-2bd (TASK-LEDGER-POOL-ASSIGNMENTS)
  - bd-3vc (TASK-LEDGER-SPEND-POOLS)

## Success Criteria
- A redacted matrix covers company-paid discretionary spend, personal after-tax spend, unassigned spend, corrections, refunds, reversals, transfers, voids, supersessions, and at least one candidate mixed purchase.
- For every supported case the report proves one active pool or explicit unassigned state and exact pool/category conservation without deriving pool from account, instrument, cardholder, category, or description.
- OQ-LEDGER-15 resolves only if no representative transaction requires simultaneous split pools; otherwise leave it open, keep this task incomplete, and return to lex:design before pool implementation.

## Failure Criteria
- ❌ Do NOT encode pools as categories, infer them from another dimension, or silently split a transaction — rejected per DD-LEDGER-DIMENSIONAL-ATTRIBUTION.
- ❌ Do NOT commit raw transaction rows, descriptions, full identifiers, or owner-sensitive mapping data.
- ❌ Do NOT resolve the gate by excluding mixed purchases; a demonstrated split-pool need invalidates the current design.

## Approach
- Validation-only task with no Implements link: resolve OQ-LEDGER-15 before Pool Assignment and pool-aware actuals implementation.
- Expected output: docs/validation/ledger/oq-15-pool-cardinality.md with redacted scenario matrix
- Expected output: OQ-LEDGER-15 resolved with a non-empty cardinality decision on the only completing path

## Interfaces
- Produces: OQ-LEDGER-15Resolution (OQ-LEDGER-15) — Produced only when one-pool cardinality is validated

## Files (reservation globs)
- `docs/statements/` (read) — private evidence — required — Never stage or copy source material
- `docs/validation/ledger/oq-15-pool-cardinality.md` (document) — redacted evidence — required
- `.lexicon/graph/LEDGER/open-question/OQ-LEDGER-15.json` (modify) — canonical gate state — required

## Verification
- **Build:** `dotnet build Tally.slnx`
- **Test:** `test -s docs/validation/ledger/oq-15-pool-cardinality.md && lex open-question show OQ-LEDGER-15 --json | jq -e '.status == "resolved" and (.decision | length > 0)'` — exit 0; redacted matrix is non-empty and OQ-LEDGER-15 contains a non-empty resolved decision

## Commit
